structures.desc: Deep revision of past continuous (was/were + -ing) for background actions, interrupted actions, and simultaneous past actions, contrasted with past simple.

rules.t: Use past continuous for a longer action interrupted by a shorter one (past simple). Pattern: While X was happening, Y happened.
rules.td: Verwende das Past Continuous für eine längere Handlung, die von einer kürzeren (Past Simple) unterbrochen wird. Muster: While X was happening, Y happened.

ex.en: While his mum was reading, Johnny gave her a surprise.
ex.de: Während seine Mutter las, überraschte Johnny sie.

ex.en: I was walking home when it started to rain.
ex.de: Ich ging gerade nach Hause, als es zu regnen begann.

rules.t: Use past continuous for an ongoing action at a specific time in the past.
rules.td: Verwende das Past Continuous für eine andauernde Handlung zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt in der Vergangenheit.

ex.en: From 2014 onwards, everything was getting better for Ireland.
ex.de: Ab 2014 wurde alles besser für Irland.

ex.en: At 8 o'clock last night, I was doing my homework.
ex.de: Um 8 Uhr gestern Abend machte ich gerade meine Hausaufgaben.

rules.t: Use past continuous for two longer actions happening at the same time. Pattern: While X was doing..., Y was doing...
rules.td: Verwende das Past Continuous für zwei längere Handlungen, die gleichzeitig stattfanden. Muster: While X was doing..., Y was doing...

ex.en: While many people were starving, the landlords were sending grain to England.
ex.de: Während viele Menschen hungerten, schickten die Grundbesitzer Getreide nach England.

ex.en: She was cooking while he was cleaning the house.
ex.de: Sie kochte, während er das Haus putzte.

errs.d: Using past simple for both actions instead of past continuous for the longer one
errs.w: While he walked, he saw a dog.
errs.c: While he was walking, he saw a dog.

errs.d: Using past continuous for the short/interrupting action as well
errs.w: While he was walking, he was seeing a dog.
errs.c: While he was walking, he saw a dog.

errs.d: Using continuous with stative verbs
errs.w: I was knowing the answer.
errs.c: I knew the answer.

structures.desc: Past perfect (had + past participle) for an action completed before another past action, establishing temporal sequence in narratives.

rules.t: Form: Subject + had (not) + past participle. Had is the same for all persons. Use it when one past event happened BEFORE another past event.
rules.td: Bildung: Subjekt + had (not) + Past Participle. Had ist für alle Personen gleich. Verwende es, wenn eine vergangene Handlung VOR einer anderen vergangenen Handlung geschah.

ex.en: When Harry got up, he realised that he had put his tent in the wrong place.
ex.de: Als Harry aufstand, merkte er, dass er sein Zelt am falschen Platz aufgestellt hatte.

ex.en: Nobody was in the building. All the employees had left.
ex.de: Niemand war im Gebäude. Alle Angestellten waren gegangen.

rules.t: The past perfect marks the EARLIER event; the past simple marks the LATER event. Pattern: When X happened (simple), Y had already happened (perfect).
rules.td: Das Past Perfect markiert das FRÜHERE Ereignis; das Past Simple markiert das SPÄTERE. Muster: When X happened (simple), Y had already happened (perfect).

ex.en: When we arrived, the film had already started.
ex.de: Als wir ankamen, hatte der Film schon begonnen.

ex.en: She was tired because she hadn't slept well.
ex.de: Sie war müde, weil sie nicht gut geschlafen hatte.

rules.t: Common time markers with past perfect: already, just, by the time, before, after, when, never (before).
rules.td: Häufige Zeitausdrücke mit dem Past Perfect: already, just, by the time, before, after, when, never (before).

ex.en: I had never seen snow before I went to Austria.
ex.de: Ich hatte noch nie Schnee gesehen, bevor ich nach Österreich fuhr.

ex.en: By the time we got there, the shop had closed.
ex.de: Als wir dort ankamen, hatte der Laden schon geschlossen.

questions: Had you eaten before you came?

errs.d: Using past perfect for ALL past events, not just the earlier one
errs.w: I had eaten breakfast and then I had gone to school.
errs.c: I had eaten breakfast and then I went to school.

errs.d: Missing past perfect when temporal sequence matters
errs.w: When I arrived, everyone left.
errs.c: When I arrived, everyone had left.

errs.d: Using wrong past participle form after had
errs.w: I had went to the shop.
errs.c: I had gone to the shop.

structures.desc: Reported/indirect speech for statements with systematic tense backshift, pronoun shifts, and time/place expression shifts.

rules.t: When reporting what someone said, shift the tense one step back: present simple -> past simple, present continuous -> past continuous, past simple -> past perfect, present perfect -> past perfect, will -> would, can -> could, must -> had to.
rules.td: Wenn man wiedergibt, was jemand gesagt hat, verschiebt man die Zeitform um eine Stufe zurück: Present Simple -> Past Simple, Present Continuous -> Past Continuous, Past Simple -> Past Perfect, Present Perfect -> Past Perfect, will -> would, can -> could, must -> had to.

ex.en: "I like jazz." -> He said (that) he liked jazz.
ex.de: "Ich mag Jazz." -> Er sagte, dass er Jazz mochte.

ex.en: "I'll help you." -> He said he would help me.
ex.de: "Ich werde dir helfen." -> Er sagte, er würde mir helfen.

rules.t: Shift pronouns to match the new perspective: I -> he/she, my -> his/her, you -> I/me (or the person addressed), we -> they.
rules.td: Passe die Pronomen an die neue Perspektive an: I -> he/she, my -> his/her, you -> I/me (oder die angesprochene Person), we -> they.

ex.en: "I love my dog." -> She said she loved her dog.
ex.de: "Ich liebe meinen Hund." -> Sie sagte, sie liebte ihren Hund.

ex.en: "We are going to win." -> They said they were going to win.
ex.de: "Wir werden gewinnen." -> Sie sagten, sie würden gewinnen.

rules.t: Shift time and place expressions: here -> there, today -> that day, now -> then, yesterday -> the day before, tomorrow -> the next day, this week -> that week, ago -> before.
rules.td: Passe Zeit- und Ortsausdrücke an: here -> there, today -> that day, now -> then, yesterday -> the day before, tomorrow -> the next day, this week -> that week, ago -> before.

ex.en: "I'm leaving tomorrow." -> She said she was leaving the next day.
ex.de: "Ich fahre morgen ab." -> Sie sagte, sie würde am nauchsten Tag abfahren.

ex.en: "I was here yesterday." -> He said he had been there the day before.
ex.de: "Ich war gestern hier." -> Er sagte, er sei am Tag davor dort gewesen.

rules.t: Use 'said' without a personal object: He said (that)... Use 'told' WITH a personal object: He told me (that)... Never say 'He said me' or 'He told that'.
rules.td: Verwende 'said' OHNE Personalobjekt: He said (that)... Verwende 'told' MIT Personalobjekt: He told me (that)... Nie 'He said me' oder 'He told that'.

ex.en: He said that he was tired.
ex.de: Er sagte, dass er müde war.

ex.en: He told me that he was tired.
ex.de: Er sagte mir, dass er müde war.

affirmative: She told me (that) she was leaving.
They said they had seen the film.
negative: He said he didn't like jazz.
She told me she wasn't coming.
They said they hadn't seen the film.

errs.d: No tense backshift — keeping the original tense in reported speech
errs.w: He said he likes jazz.
errs.c: He said he liked jazz.

errs.d: Confusing said and told — using 'said me' or 'told that' without object
errs.w: He said me that he was tired.
errs.c: He told me that he was tired.

errs.d: Failing to shift pronouns when reporting
errs.w: He said I like jazz.
errs.c: He said he liked jazz.

structures.desc: Reported/indirect questions: yes/no questions with if/whether and wh-questions with statement word order, no inversion, and tense backshift.

rules.t: For yes/no questions, use 'if' or 'whether' + subject + verb (backshifted). No question mark. No do/does/did.
rules.td: Bei Ja/Nein-Fragen verwende 'if' oder 'whether' + Subjekt + Verb (zurückverschoben). Kein Fragezeichen. Kein do/does/did.

ex.en: "Do you like pizza?" -> She asked me if I liked pizza.
ex.de: "Magst du Pizza?" -> Sie fragte mich, ob ich Pizza möchte.

ex.en: "Can you start on Monday?" -> They asked me if I could start on Monday.
ex.de: "Kannst du am Montag anfangen?" -> Sie fragten mich, ob ich am Montag anfangen könnte.

rules.t: For wh-questions, keep the question word + subject + verb (backshifted). Statement word order — NO inversion. No question mark.
rules.td: Bei W-Fragen behalte das Fragewort + Subjekt + Verb (zurückverschoben). Aussagesatz-Wortstellung — KEINE Inversion. Kein Fragezeichen.

ex.en: "Where do you live?" -> She asked me where I lived.
ex.de: "Wo wohnst du?" -> Sie fragte mich, wo ich wohnte.

ex.en: "Where have you worked before?" -> He asked me where I had worked before.
ex.de: "Wo hast du vorher gearbeitet?" -> Er fragte mich, wo ich vorher gearbeitet hatte.

rules.t: In reported questions, use STATEMENT word order (subject before verb). Never keep question inversion or do/does/did.
rules.td: In indirekten Fragen verwende AUSSAGESATZ-Wortstellung (Subjekt vor Verb). Behalte nie die Fragestellung-Inversion oder do/does/did bei.

ex.en: "Why do you have a dog?" -> He asked me why I had a dog.
ex.de: "Warum hast du einen Hund?" -> Er fragte mich, warum ich einen Hund hatte.

ex.en: "What time does the train leave?" -> She asked what time the train left.
ex.de: "Wann fährt der Zug ab?" -> Sie fragte, wann der Zug abfuhr.

affirmative: He asked me where I lived.
She asked if I liked pizza.
They wanted to know whether I could start.
negative: He asked me why I hadn't called.
She asked if I didn't like pizza.
They wanted to know whether I couldn't come earlier.

errs.d: Keeping question word order (inversion) in reported questions
errs.w: He asked where did I work.
errs.c: He asked where I worked.

errs.d: Keeping do/does/did in reported questions
errs.w: She asked me did I like pizza.
errs.c: She asked me if I liked pizza.

errs.d: Adding 'that' before if/whether
errs.w: He asked that if I was ready.
errs.c: He asked if I was ready.

structures.nd: Past Perfect mit Zeitkonnektoren (after, before, just)
structures.desc: Extension of past perfect with time connectors after, before, just, and by the time, plus connecting ideas with because, although, however, in order to, so that.

rules.t: Pattern: After + subject + had + past participle, subject + past simple. The past perfect marks the first event; past simple marks the second.
rules.td: Muster: After + Subjekt + had + Past Participle, Subjekt + Past Simple. Das Past Perfect markiert das erste Ereignis; Past Simple das zweite.

ex.en: After James had eaten two pizzas, he felt very ill.
ex.de: Nachdem James zwei Pizzen gegessen hatte, fühlte er sich sehr schlecht.

ex.en: After they had finished dinner, they went for a walk.
ex.de: Nachdem sie das Abendessen beendet hatten, gingen sie spazieren.

rules.t: Use 'had just + past participle' for an event that happened very shortly before another past event. 'Just' goes between 'had' and the past participle.
rules.td: Verwende 'had just + Past Participle' für ein Ereignis, das kurz vor einem anderen vergangenen Ereignis stattfand. 'Just' steht zwischen 'had' und dem Past Participle.

ex.en: Sally had just finished her meal when Tom came home.
ex.de: Sally hatte gerade ihr Essen beendet, als Tom nach Hause kam.

ex.en: I had just left the house when it started raining.
ex.de: Ich hatte gerade das Haus verlassen, als es zu regnen begann.

rules.t: Pattern: By the time + past simple, subject + had + past participle. 'By the time' introduces the later event.
rules.td: Muster: By the time + Past Simple, Subjekt + had + Past Participle. 'By the time' leitet das spätere Ereignis ein.

ex.en: By the time the police arrived, the criminal had fled.
ex.de: Als die Polizei eintraf, war der Verbrecher bereits geflohen.

ex.en: By the time we got to the cinema, the film had started.
ex.de: Als wir im Kino ankamen, hatte der Film schon begonnen.

affirmative: After she had eaten, she left.
He had just arrived when it started raining.
By the time I got there, they had finished.
negative: After she hadn't eaten for hours, she was very hungry.
He hadn't finished before the bell rang.
questions: Had they already left by the time you arrived?
Had she just finished when you called?

errs.d: Using past simple instead of past perfect after 'after'
errs.w: After I ate dinner, I went to bed.
errs.c: After I had eaten dinner, I went to bed.

errs.d: Placing 'just' in the wrong position
errs.w: I had finished just my meal when she arrived.
errs.c: I had just finished my meal when she arrived.

errs.d: Using double connectors (although...but)
errs.w: Although it was raining, but we went out.
errs.c: Although it was raining, we went out.

structures.nd: Adverbien der Art und Weise (Wiederholung + Erweiterung)
structures.desc: Formation of adverbs from adjectives (adjective + -ly), irregular adverbs (well, fast, hard/hardly), and the linking verb rule (adjective after look, sound, feel, taste, smell).

rules.t: Regular adverbs are formed by adding -ly to the adjective: careful -> carefully, quick -> quickly. Spelling changes: -y -> -ily (happy -> happily), -le -> -ly (terrible -> terribly), -ic -> -ically (dramatic -> dramatically).
rules.td: Regelmäßige Adverbien werden gebildet, indem man -ly an das Adjektiv anhängt: careful -> carefully, quick -> quickly. Schreibänderungen: -y -> -ily (happy -> happily), -le -> -ly (terrible -> terribly), -ic -> -ically (dramatic -> dramatically).

ex.en: She spoke clearly and carefully.
ex.de: Sie sprach klar und sorgfältig.

ex.en: He drove slowly through the village.
ex.de: Er fuhr langsam durch das Dorf.

rules.t: Some adverbs are irregular: good -> well, fast -> fast, hard -> hard, late -> late, early -> early. Important: hard means 'with effort'; hardly means 'almost not at all'.
rules.td: Einige Adverbien sind unregelmaussig: good -> well, fast -> fast, hard -> hard, late -> late, early -> early. Wichtig: hard bedeutet 'mit Anstrengung'; hardly bedeutet 'kaum'.

ex.en: She plays the piano well.
ex.de: Sie spielt Klavier gut.

ex.en: He works hard. / He hardly works.
ex.de: Er arbeitet hart. / Er arbeitet kaum.

rules.t: After linking verbs (look, sound, feel, taste, smell, seem, appear, become), use an ADJECTIVE, not an adverb. These verbs describe a state, not an action.
rules.td: Nach Bindungsverben (look, sound, feel, taste, smell, seem, appear, become) verwende ein ADJEKTIV, kein Adverb. Diese Verben beschreiben einen Zustand, keine Handlung.

ex.en: That sounds good.
ex.de: Das klingt gut.

ex.en: This food tastes awful.
ex.de: Dieses Essen schmeckt schrecklich.

errs.d: Using adjective form where an adverb is needed
errs.w: She sings beautiful.
errs.c: She sings beautifully.

errs.d: Using adverb after linking verbs instead of adjective
errs.w: It tastes badly.
errs.c: It tastes bad.

errs.d: Confusing 'hard' (with effort) and 'hardly' (almost not)
errs.w: He hardly works. (meaning: He works a lot.)
errs.c: He works hard.

structures.desc: Question tags with opposite polarity: positive sentence -> negative tag, negative sentence -> positive tag. Matching the auxiliary/modal or using do/does/did.

rules.t: Positive sentence -> negative tag: 'She is nice, isn't she?' Negative sentence -> positive tag: 'You aren't coming, are you?' The tag always has OPPOSITE polarity to the main sentence.
rules.td: Positiver Satz -> negativer Anhang: 'She is nice, isn't she?' Negativer Satz -> positiver Anhang: 'You aren't coming, are you?' Der Anhang hat immer die GEGENTEILIGE Polarität zum Hauptsatz.

ex.en: That's easy, isn't it?
ex.de: Das ist einfach, oder?

ex.en: You aren't from here, are you?
ex.de: Du bist nicht von hier, oder?

rules.t: Repeat the auxiliary/modal from the main sentence in the tag: be -> isn't/aren't, have -> haven't/hasn't, can -> can't, will -> won't, should -> shouldn't. If there is NO auxiliary, use do/does/did.
rules.td: Wiederhole das Hilfsverb/Modalverb aus dem Hauptsatz im Anhang: be -> isn't/aren't, have -> haven't/hasn't, can -> can't, will -> won't, should -> shouldn't. Wenn es KEIN Hilfsverb gibt, verwende do/does/did.

ex.en: She can swim, can't she?
ex.de: Sie kann schwimmen, oder?

ex.en: She started her channel, didn't she?
ex.de: Sie hat ihren Kanal gestartet, oder?

rules.t: The tag always uses a PRONOUN (never a noun). Replace any noun subject with the correct pronoun. Special case: 'I am..., aren't I?' (NOT 'amn't I?').
rules.td: Der Anhang verwendet immer ein PRONOMEN (nie ein Nomen). Ersetze jedes Nomen-Subjekt durch das richtige Pronomen. Sonderfall: 'I am..., aren't I?' (NICHT 'amn't I?').

ex.en: Amber was only 11, wasn't she?
ex.de: Amber war erst 11, oder?

ex.en: I am late, aren't I?
ex.de: Ich bin spät dran, oder?

errs.d: Using the wrong auxiliary in the tag (e.g. is instead of does)
errs.w: She likes music, isn't she?
errs.c: She likes music, doesn't she?

errs.d: Using same polarity (positive + positive) instead of opposite
errs.w: She likes music, does she?
errs.c: She likes music, doesn't she?

errs.d: Using wrong pronoun or noun in the tag
errs.w: The children are coming, isn't it?
errs.c: The children are coming, aren't they?

structures.nd: Present Simple für die Zukunft (Fahrplaune und feste Termine)
structures.desc: Using present simple for timetabled or scheduled future events (transport, events, fixed schedules), contrasted with going to, will, and first conditional.

rules.t: Use present simple for fixed timetables, schedules, and official events in the future. Common verbs: leave, arrive, start, finish, begin, end, open, close, depart.
rules.td: Verwende das Present Simple für feste Fahrplaune, Zeitplaune und offizielle Veranstaltungen in der Zukunft. Häufige Verben: leave, arrive, start, finish, begin, end, open, close, depart.

ex.en: Our plane to Alice Springs leaves at 8.30 tomorrow.
ex.de: Unser Flugzeug nach Alice Springs fliegt morgen um 8.30 ab.

ex.en: The film starts at 7 p.m.
ex.de: Der Film beginnt um 19 Uhr.

rules.t: Distinguish from other future forms: Present simple = fixed schedule. Going to = personal plans/intentions. Will = predictions, offers, promises. First conditional = future possibility after 'if'.
rules.td: Unterscheide von anderen Zukunftsformen: Present Simple = fester Fahrplan. Going to = persönliche Plaune/Absichten. Will = Vorhersagen, Angebote, Versprechen. First Conditional = Zukunftsmöglichkeit nach 'if'.

ex.en: The train leaves at 6. (schedule)
ex.de: Der Zug fährt um 6 ab. (Fahrplan)

ex.en: I'm going to visit my grandma. (plan)
ex.de: Ich werde meine Oma besuchen. (Plan)

negative: The museum doesn't open on Mondays.
questions: What time does the plane leave?

errs.d: Using present simple for ALL future events, not just timetables
errs.w: I go to the cinema tomorrow with my friends.
errs.c: I'm going to go to the cinema tomorrow with my friends.

errs.d: Using will for timetabled events instead of present simple
errs.w: The train will leave at 6 tomorrow.
errs.c: The train leaves at 6 tomorrow.

structures.desc: The pattern want/tell/ask + object (person) + to + infinitive for expressing wishes, commands, and requests involving another person.

rules.t: Pattern: want + person + to + base verb. NOT: want + that + clause. This is the English way to express 'Ich will, dass jemand etwas tut'.
rules.td: Muster: want + Person + to + Grundform. NICHT: want + that + Satz. So drückt man im Englischen 'Ich will, dass jemand etwas tut' aus.

ex.en: Mum and Dad want us to see Ayers Rock.
ex.de: Mama und Papa wollen, dass wir den Ayers Rock sehen.

ex.en: I want you to help me.
ex.de: Ich will, dass du mir hilfst.

rules.t: The same pattern works with tell and ask: tell + person + to + base verb (command), ask + person + to + base verb (request). Negative: verb + person + not to + base verb.
rules.td: Das gleiche Muster funktioniert mit tell und ask: tell + Person + to + Grundform (Befehl), ask + Person + to + Grundform (Bitte). Verneinung: Verb + Person + not to + Grundform.

ex.en: She told him to sit down.
ex.de: Sie sagte ihm, er solle sich hinsetzen.

ex.en: He asked me to wait.
ex.de: Er bat mich zu warten.

rules.t: Do not confuse with 'let': let + person + BARE verb (no 'to'). 'My parents let me go' (NOT 'let me to go'). But: 'My parents want me to go.'
rules.td: Nicht verwechseln mit 'let': let + Person + Grundform OHNE 'to'. 'My parents let me go' (NICHT 'let me to go'). Aber: 'My parents want me to go.'

ex.en: My parents let me stay up late. (bare infinitive)
ex.de: Meine Eltern lassen mich lange aufbleiben.

ex.en: My parents want me to go to bed early. (to-infinitive)
ex.de: Meine Eltern wollen, dass ich früh ins Bett gehe.

affirmative: She told him to sit down.
negative: I want you not to worry.
She told him not to shout.
He asked me not to be late.
questions: Do you want me to help?
Did she tell you to leave?
Did he ask you to come?

errs.d: Using 'want that + clause' instead of 'want + person + to'
errs.w: I want that you come.
errs.c: I want you to come.

errs.d: Missing 'to' before the infinitive
errs.w: I want you come.
errs.c: I want you to come.

errs.d: Adding 'to' after let (mixing up the patterns)
errs.w: My parents let me to go out.
errs.c: My parents let me go out.

structures.nd: Present Perfect vs Past Simple — Systematische Wiederholung
structures.desc: Systematic review making explicit the connection between tenses and their time expressions: past simple with yesterday/ago/last, present perfect with ever/never/already/yet/just/for/since.

rules.t: Use past simple for finished actions with a finished time expression: yesterday, last week/month/year, in 2020, two months ago, when I was young.
rules.td: Verwende Past Simple für abgeschlossene Handlungen mit einem abgeschlossenen Zeitausdruck: yesterday, last week/month/year, in 2020, two months ago, when I was young.

ex.en: Last year I got a wonderful collection from a professor.
ex.de: Letztes Jahr bekam ich eine wunderbare Sammlung von einem Professor.

ex.en: A few hours later I started playing again.
ex.de: Ein paar Stunden später begann ich wieder zu spielen.

rules.t: Use present perfect for actions connected to the present: ever, never, already, yet, just, recently, so far, today, this week. Also with for (duration) and since (point in time).
rules.td: Verwende Present Perfect für Handlungen, die mit der Gegenwart verbunden sind: ever, never, already, yet, just, recently, so far, today, this week. Auch mit for (Dauer) und since (Zeitpunkt).

ex.en: I've collected between 18,000 and 19,000 kinds of sand since I started.
ex.de: Ich habe zwischen 18.000 und 19.000 Arten von Sand gesammelt, seit ich angefangen habe.

ex.en: It hasn't turned up on the market yet.
ex.de: Es ist noch nicht auf dem Markt aufgetaucht.

rules.t: Use 'for' with a period of time (for two years, for a long time). Use 'since' with a point in time (since 2015, since Monday, since I was a child). Both require present perfect.
rules.td: Verwende 'for' mit einer Zeitspanne (for two years, for a long time). Verwende 'since' mit einem Zeitpunkt (since 2015, since Monday, since I was a child). Beide erfordern Present Perfect.

ex.en: I've lived here for ten years.
ex.de: Ich lebe seit zehn Jahren hier.

ex.en: She's been a teacher since 2010.
ex.de: Sie ist seit 2010 Lehrerin.

negative: I didn't visit London last year.
questions: Have you ever been to London?
How long have you lived here?

errs.d: Using present perfect with a past time marker
errs.w: I have been there yesterday.
errs.c: I was there yesterday.

errs.d: Using past simple with a present perfect marker
errs.w: Did you ever eat sushi?
errs.c: Have you ever eaten sushi?

errs.d: Confusing for and since
errs.w: I live here since ten years.
errs.c: I've lived here for ten years.

structures.n: Modals of Possibility: might / may / could
structures.nd: Modalverben der Möglichkeit: might / may / could
structures.desc: Expanding the modal possibility system with might, may, and could for expressing different degrees of certainty. Includes the certainty scale: must > may/might/could > can't.

rules.t: might / may / could + base verb (bare infinitive, no 'to'). Same form for all persons. Negatives: might not, may not (NO contraction 'mayn't'), couldn't.
rules.td: might / may / could + Grundform (Infinitiv ohne 'to'). Gleiche Form für alle Personen. Verneinung: might not, may not (KEINE Kurzform 'mayn't'), couldn't.

ex.en: It might rain later.
ex.de: Es könnte später regnen.

ex.en: She may be right.
ex.de: Sie könnte recht haben.

rules.t: Certainty scale from most to least certain: must (almost certain, logical deduction) > may (possible, more formal) > might (possible, less sure) > could (possible in theory) > can't (logically impossible).
rules.td: Sicherheitsskala von am sichersten bis am unsichersten: must (fast sicher, logische Schlussfolgerung) > may (möglich, formeller) > might (möglich, weniger sicher) > could (theoretisch möglich) > can't (logisch unmöglich).

ex.en: He must be at home — his car is in the driveway.
ex.de: Er muss zu Hause sein — sein Auto steht in der Einfahrt.

ex.en: That can't be true — I saw him this morning.
ex.de: Das kann nicht stimmen — ich habe ihn heute Morgen gesehen.

rules.t: Alternative expressions for possibility: 'There is a chance that...', 'It's possible that...', 'X is likely to...', 'The likelihood of + -ing...'.
rules.td: Alternative Ausdrücke für Möglichkeit: 'There is a chance that...', 'It's possible that...', 'X is likely to...', 'The likelihood of + -ing...'.

ex.en: There is a chance that a smile could get you into trouble.
ex.de: Es besteht die Möglichkeit, dass ein Laucheln dich in Schwierigkeiten bringen könnte.

ex.en: You are likely to offend someone if you blow your nose loudly.
ex.de: Es ist wahrscheinlich, dass du jemanden beleidigst, wenn du dir laut die Nase putzt.

errs.d: Adding 'to' after the modal verb
errs.w: She might to come later.
errs.c: She might come later.

errs.d: Using 'can' for specific-situation possibility instead of might/may/could
errs.w: It can rain tomorrow.
errs.c: It might rain tomorrow.

errs.d: Confusing 'could' for present possibility with 'could' for past ability
errs.w: She could swim as a child. (intended: She might be able to swim.)
errs.c: She could be at the pool. (present possibility) / She could swim as a child. (past ability)

structures.desc: Third conditional (If + past perfect, would have + past participle) for hypothetical past situations, combined with systematic revision of all three conditional types.

rules.t: Third conditional: If + past perfect (had + pp), would (not) have + past participle. It talks about a past situation that did NOT happen — it is too late to change it.
rules.td: Third Conditional: If + Past Perfect (had + pp), would (not) have + Past Participle. Es geht um eine vergangene Situation, die NICHT passiert ist — es ist zu spät, sie zu ändern.

ex.en: If I had known, I would have helped.
ex.de: Wenn ich es gewusst hätte, hätte ich geholfen.

ex.en: If Fair Trade had existed, he wouldn't have sold the farm.
ex.de: Wenn es Fair Trade gegeben hätte, hätte er die Farm nicht verkauft.

rules.t: Three conditional types: 1st (If + present, will + base = real future possibility), 2nd (If + past, would + base = hypothetical present/future), 3rd (If + past perfect, would have + pp = hypothetical past). Key: 1st = might happen, 2nd = probably won't, 3rd = definitely didn't.
rules.td: Drei Conditional-Typen: 1. (If + Present, will + Grundform = reale Zukunftsmöglichkeit), 2. (If + Past, would + Grundform = hypothetische Gegenwart/Zukunft), 3. (If + Past Perfect, would have + pp = hypothetische Vergangenheit). 1. = könnte passieren, 2. = passiert wahrscheinlich nicht, 3. = ist definitiv nicht passiert.

ex.en: If you study, you'll pass. (1st)
ex.de: Wenn du lernst, wirst du bestehen. (1.)

ex.en: If I were rich, I'd travel. (2nd)
ex.de: Wenn ich reich wäre, würde ich reisen. (2.)

rules.t: Common contractions: would have -> would've / 'd have, would not have -> wouldn't have. These are very common in spoken English.
rules.td: Häufige Kurzformen: would have -> would've / 'd have, would not have -> wouldn't have. Diese sind im gesprochenen Englisch sehr gebräuchlich.

ex.en: If you had read the invitation, you would've known what to wear.
ex.de: Wenn du die Einladung gelesen hättest, hättest du gewusst, was du anziehen sollst.

ex.en: I wouldn't have gone if I'd known.
ex.de: Ich wäre nicht gegangen, wenn ich es gewusst hätte.

affirmative: If I had studied, I would have passed.
If she had known, she would have called.
negative: If I hadn't studied, I wouldn't have passed.
If she hadn't told me, I wouldn't have known.
questions: What would you have done if you had been there?
Would you have helped if you had known?

errs.d: Using 'would' in the if-clause instead of past perfect
errs.w: If I would have known, I would have helped.
errs.c: If I had known, I would have helped.

errs.d: Mixing second and third conditional forms
errs.w: If I had money, I would have bought it.
errs.c: If I had had money, I would have bought it. (3rd) / If I had money, I would buy it. (2nd)

errs.d: Missing 'have' in the result clause
errs.w: If I had known, I would helped.
errs.c: If I had known, I would have helped.

structures.desc: Reflexive pronouns (myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves) for reflexive and emphatic use, including verbs that do NOT take reflexives in English.

rules.t: Reflexive pronouns: myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves. Use when the subject and object are the same person.
rules.td: Reflexivpronomen: myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves. Verwende sie, wenn Subjekt und Objekt dieselbe Person sind.

ex.en: He hurt himself.
ex.de: Er hat sich verletzt.

ex.en: She asked herself a big question.
ex.de: Sie stellte sich eine grosse Frage.

rules.t: Emphatic use: The reflexive pronoun can mean 'without help / personally'. Also: by + reflexive = alone.
rules.td: Emphatische Verwendung: Das Reflexivpronomen kann 'ohne Hilfe / persönlich' bedeuten. Auch: by + Reflexivpronomen = allein.

ex.en: I wrote the book myself.
ex.de: Ich habe das Buch selbst geschrieben.

ex.en: She lives by herself.
ex.de: Sie lebt allein.

rules.t: Some verbs that are reflexive in German do NOT take reflexive pronouns in English: enjoy (NOT 'enjoy myself the party'), decide, feel, concentrate, remember, meet, hurry, complain.
rules.td: Einige Verben, die im Deutschen reflexiv sind, haben im Englischen KEIN Reflexivpronomen: enjoy (NICHT 'enjoy myself the party'), decide, feel, concentrate, remember, meet, hurry, complain.

ex.en: I enjoyed the party. (NOT: I enjoyed myself the party.)
ex.de: Ich habe die Party genossen. (Deutsch reflexiv: Ich habe mich über die Party gefreut.)

ex.en: She decided to leave. (NOT: She decided herself to leave.)
ex.de: Sie entschied sich zu gehen. (Deutsch reflexiv: sich entscheiden)

errs.d: Using reflexive pronouns where English does not require them (German reflexive verb transfer)
errs.w: I enjoyed myself the party.
errs.c: I enjoyed the party.

errs.d: Using wrong reflexive pronoun form
errs.w: He hurt hisself.
errs.c: He hurt himself.

errs.d: Missing reflexive pronoun where one is needed
errs.w: He hurt when he fell.
errs.c: He hurt himself when he fell.

structures.desc: Phrasal verbs (verb + particle combinations) with changed meanings, separable vs inseparable types, and the critical pronoun placement rule.

rules.t: Phrasal verbs are verb + particle combinations where the particle changes the verb's meaning. They must be learned as vocabulary items. Core M4 phrasal verbs: take off, set off, set up, come up with, get on (with), run out (of), pick up.
rules.td: Phrasal Verbs sind Verb + Partikel-Kombinationen, bei denen die Partikel die Bedeutung des Verbs ändert. Sie müssen als Vokabeln gelernt werden. M4 Phrasal Verbs: take off, set off, set up, come up with, get on (with), run out (of), pick up.

ex.en: The plane took off at 6 a.m. (= left the ground)
ex.de: Das Flugzeug hob um 6 Uhr ab.

ex.en: She came up with a great idea. (= thought of)
ex.de: Sie hatte eine grossartige Idee. (= sich ausdenken)

rules.t: Separable phrasal verbs: With noun objects, the particle can go before or after the object. With PRONOUN objects, the particle MUST go AFTER the pronoun: 'Pick it up' (NOT 'Pick up it').
rules.td: Trennbare Phrasal Verbs: Mit Nomen-Objekten kann die Partikel vor oder nach dem Objekt stehen. Mit PRONOMEN-Objekten MUSS die Partikel NACH dem Pronomen stehen: 'Pick it up' (NICHT 'Pick up it').

ex.en: Take off your coat. / Take your coat off. (both correct with noun)
ex.de: Zieh deinen Mantel aus.

ex.en: Take it off. (NOT: Take off it.)
ex.de: Zieh ihn aus.

rules.t: Inseparable phrasal verbs: The particle ALWAYS stays with the verb, even with pronoun objects: 'Look at this' (NOT 'Look this at'). 'Get on with her' (NOT 'Get her on with').
rules.td: Untrennbare Phrasal Verbs: Die Partikel bleibt IMMER beim Verb, auch mit Pronomen-Objekten: 'Look at this' (NICHT 'Look this at'). 'Get on with her' (NICHT 'Get her on with').

ex.en: She got on well with everyone.
ex.de: Sie kam gut mit allen aus.

ex.en: Look at this picture. / Look at it.
ex.de: Schau dir dieses Bild an. / Schau es dir an.

negative: He didn't set off on time.
They haven't run out of milk yet.
questions: Have they run out of milk?

errs.d: Placing the pronoun after the particle in separable phrasal verbs
errs.w: He picked up it.
errs.c: He picked it up.

errs.d: Using the wrong particle with a phrasal verb
errs.w: She set off a new business. (intended: established)
errs.c: She set up a new business.

errs.d: Trying to separate an inseparable phrasal verb
errs.w: She looked this at carefully.
errs.c: She looked at this carefully.

structures.desc: Word formation using negative prefixes (un-, in-, il-, im-, ir-, dis-, mis-) and suffixes (-ness, -ful, -less) to build new words and understand unfamiliar vocabulary.

rules.t: Negative prefixes for adjectives follow phonological rules: il- before l (illegal, illogical), im- before m/p (impossible, impatient), ir- before r (irregular, irresponsible), in- for most others (incorrect, invisible), un- (most common: unhappy, unfair, unusual).
rules.td: Negative Vorsilben für Adjektive folgen phonologischen Regeln: il- vor l (illegal, illogical), im- vor m/p (impossible, impatient), ir- vor r (irregular, irresponsible), in- für die meisten anderen (incorrect, invisible), un- (am häufigsten: unhappy, unfair, unusual).

ex.en: It's impossible to finish this today.
ex.de: Es ist unmöglich, das heute fertigzustellen.

ex.en: His behaviour was irresponsible.
ex.de: Sein Verhalten war unverantwortlich.

rules.t: Negative prefixes for verbs: dis- means 'not/opposite' (agree -> disagree, appear -> disappear). mis- means 'badly/wrongly' (understand -> misunderstand, behave -> misbehave, spell -> misspell).
rules.td: Negative Vorsilben für Verben: dis- bedeutet 'nicht/Gegenteil' (agree -> disagree, appear -> disappear). mis- bedeutet 'schlecht/falsch' (understand -> misunderstand, behave -> misbehave, spell -> misspell).

ex.en: I completely disagree with you.
ex.de: Ich bin völlig anderer Meinung als du.

ex.en: Don't misunderstand me.
ex.de: Missversteh mich nicht.

rules.t: Key suffixes: -ness turns adjectives into nouns (happy -> happiness, dark -> darkness). -ful turns nouns into adjectives meaning 'full of' (care -> careful, beauty -> beautiful). -less turns nouns into adjectives meaning 'without' (hope -> hopeless, home -> homeless). Note: -ful has only one 'l'.
rules.td: Wichtige Nachsilben: -ness macht aus Adjektiven Nomen (happy -> happiness, dark -> darkness). -ful macht aus Nomen Adjektive mit der Bedeutung 'voll von' (care -> careful, beauty -> beautiful). -less macht aus Nomen Adjektive mit der Bedeutung 'ohne' (hope -> hopeless, home -> homeless). Beachte: -ful hat nur ein 'l'.

ex.en: Her kindness surprised everyone.
ex.de: Ihre Freundlichkeit überraschte alle.

ex.en: The situation seemed hopeless, but she remained hopeful.
ex.de: Die Situation schien hoffnungslos, aber sie blieb hoffnungsvoll.

errs.d: Using the wrong negative prefix
errs.w: That's inpossible.
errs.c: That's impossible.

errs.d: Spelling -ful with double l
errs.w: She was beautifull.
errs.c: She was beautiful.

errs.d: Confusing -ful (full of) and -less (without)
errs.w: Be careless when you cross the road! (intended: Be careful)
errs.c: Be careful when you cross the road!

items.p: While I ___ (walk) to school, I met my friend Tom.
a: was walking
items.h: 'While' introduces the longer action. Which form of 'be' matches 'I' in the past?
items.hd: 'While' leitet die längere Handlung ein. Welche Form von 'be' passt zu 'I' in der Vergangenheit?
items.e: 'While' introduces the longer background action, which needs past continuous. 'I' takes 'was' + -ing: 'was walking'.
items.ed: 'While' leitet die längere Hintergrundhandlung ein, die das Past Continuous braucht. 'I' braucht 'was' + -ing: 'was walking'.

items.p: They ___ (play) football when it started to rain.
a: were playing
items.h: 'When it started to rain' is the short interrupting action. What was happening before that?
items.hd: 'When it started to rain' ist die kurze unterbrechende Handlung. Was passierte davor?
items.e: The longer action ('playing') needs past continuous. 'They' takes 'were' + -ing: 'were playing'.
items.ed: Die längere Handlung ('playing') braucht Past Continuous. 'They' braucht 'were' + -ing: 'were playing'.

items.p: At 8 o'clock last night, she ___ (read) a book.
a: was reading
items.h: 'At 8 o'clock last night' describes a specific moment. Was the action still in progress at that moment?
items.hd: 'At 8 o'clock last night' beschreibt einen bestimmten Moment. War die Handlung zu diesem Zeitpunkt noch im Gange?
items.e: 'At 8 o'clock last night' shows an action in progress at a specific past time. Use past continuous: 'was reading'.
items.ed: 'At 8 o'clock last night' zeigt eine Handlung, die zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt in der Vergangenheit im Gange war. Verwende Past Continuous: 'was reading'.

items.p: While Mum ___ (cook), Dad ___ (set) the table.
a: was cooking ... was setting
items.h: 'While' connects two actions happening at the same time. What form do BOTH verbs need?
items.hd: 'While' verbindet zwei gleichzeitige Handlungen. Welche Form brauchen BEIDE Verben?
items.e: When two longer actions happen at the same time, both use past continuous: 'was cooking ... was setting'.
items.ed: Wenn zwei längere Handlungen gleichzeitig stattfinden, verwenden beide das Past Continuous: 'was cooking ... was setting'.

items.p: The sun ___ (shine) and the birds ___ (sing) when we left the house.
a: was shining ... were singing
items.h: This describes the background scene. Check which form of 'be' matches each subject.
items.hd: Das beschreibt die Hintergrundszene. Prüfe, welche Form von 'be' zu jedem Subjekt passt.
items.e: Past continuous sets the scene: 'The sun' (singular) = 'was shining'; 'the birds' (plural) = 'were singing'.
items.ed: Das Past Continuous setzt die Szene: 'The sun' (Einzahl) = 'was shining'; 'the birds' (Mehrzahl) = 'were singing'.

items.p: While everyone ___ (dance), the lights suddenly ___ (go) out.
a: was dancing ... went
items.h: 'While' introduces the background action (continuous). 'Suddenly' introduces the interrupting action (simple).
items.hd: 'While' leitet die Hintergrundhandlung ein (Continuous). 'Suddenly' leitet die unterbrechende Handlung ein (Simple).
items.e: 'Everyone' is singular, so 'was dancing' (background). 'Went out' is past simple (sudden interruption).
items.ed: 'Everyone' ist Einzahl, also 'was dancing' (Hintergrund). 'Went out' ist Past Simple (plötzliche Unterbrechung).

items.p: You're describing what was happening at the park yesterday at 3pm. Which sentence correctly uses past continuous for the background action?
items.c: While she was sleeping, someone knocked on the door.
a: While she was sleeping, someone knocked on the door.
ds: While she slept, someone was knocking on the door.
While she was sleeping, someone was knocking on the door.
While she slept, someone knocked on the door.
items.h: The longer background action goes with 'while' and needs past continuous. The short action uses past simple.
items.hd: Die längere Hintergrundhandlung steht mit 'while' und braucht Past Continuous. Die kurze Handlung nutzt Past Simple.
items.e: 'While she was sleeping' (longer action = continuous) + 'someone knocked' (short interruption = simple).
items.ed: 'While she was sleeping' (längere Handlung = Continuous) + 'someone knocked' (kurze Unterbrechung = Simple).

items.p: Choose the correct sentence to describe the scene:
items.c: It was raining and the wind was blowing hard.
a: It was raining and the wind was blowing hard.
ds: It rained and the wind blew hard.
It was raining and the wind blew hard.
It rained and the wind was blowing hard.
items.h: When describing a scene or setting the background, which tense shows ongoing actions?
items.hd: Wenn man eine Szene beschreibt oder den Hintergrund setzt, welche Zeitform zeigt andauernde Handlungen?
items.e: Scene-setting uses past continuous for all background details: 'was raining' and 'was blowing'.
items.ed: Das Setzen der Szene verwendet Past Continuous für alle Hintergrunddetails: 'was raining' und 'was blowing'.

items.p: Which sentence is NOT correct?
items.c: I was knowing the answer when the teacher asked.
a: I was knowing the answer when the teacher asked.
ds: I was reading a book when the phone rang.
She was listening to music while he was cooking.
We were walking home when we saw a rainbow.
items.h: One of these sentences uses past continuous with a verb that does not normally take the continuous form.
items.hd: Einer dieser Sätze verwendet Past Continuous mit einem Verb, das normalerweise nicht in der Continuous-Form steht.
items.e: 'Know' is a stative verb and cannot be used in the continuous form. The correct form is 'I knew the answer'.
items.ed: 'Know' ist ein Zustandsverb und kann nicht in der Continuous-Form stehen. Die richtige Form ist 'I knew the answer'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: While he walked to the bus stop, he dropped his phone.
items.c: While he was walking to the bus stop, he dropped his phone.
a: While he was walking to the bus stop, he dropped his phone.
While he was walking to the bus stop, he dropped his phone
He was walking to the bus stop when he dropped his phone.
items.h: 'While' introduces the longer action. Should that action be in past simple or past continuous?
items.hd: 'While' leitet die längere Handlung ein. Sollte diese Handlung im Past Simple oder Past Continuous stehen?
items.e: After 'while', the longer background action needs past continuous: 'was walking', not 'walked'.
items.ed: Nach 'while' braucht die längere Hintergrundhandlung Past Continuous: 'was walking', nicht 'walked'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She were watching TV when her mum called.
items.c: She was watching TV when her mum called.
a: She was watching TV when her mum called.
She was watching TV when her mum called
She was watching television when her mum called.
items.h: 'She' is 3rd person singular. Which form of 'be' matches: 'was' or 'were'?
items.hd: 'She' ist 3. Person Einzahl. Welche Form von 'be' passt: 'was' oder 'were'?
items.e: 'She' is singular and takes 'was', not 'were'. The correct form is 'She was watching TV'.
items.ed: 'She' ist Einzahl und braucht 'was', nicht 'were'. Die richtige Form ist 'She was watching TV'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: When I was doing my homework, the doorbell was ringing.
items.c: When I was doing my homework, the doorbell rang.
a: When I was doing my homework, the doorbell rang.
When I was doing my homework, the doorbell rang
I was doing my homework when the doorbell rang.
items.h: The doorbell ringing is a short, sudden event. Should it be in past continuous?
items.hd: Das Klingeln ist ein kurzes, plötzliches Ereignis. Sollte es im Past Continuous stehen?
items.e: The doorbell ringing is a short interruption, so it needs past simple: 'rang', not 'was ringing'.
items.ed: Das Klingeln ist eine kurze Unterbrechung, also braucht es Past Simple: 'rang', nicht 'was ringing'.

items.p: You're telling your friend about yesterday evening. Complete: 'I ___ (have) a shower when the phone ___ (ring).'
a: was having ... rang
was having, rang
was having a shower when the phone rang
items.h: The shower was the longer action (past continuous). The phone ringing interrupted it (past simple).
items.hd: Das Duschen war die längere Handlung (past continuous). Das Telefonklingeln hat es unterbrochen (past simple).
items.e: Use past continuous for the longer background action and past simple for the shorter interrupting action.
items.ed: Verwende past continuous für die längere Hintergrundhandlung und past simple für die kürzere unterbrechende Handlung.

items.p: Describe what happened to Emma on her way to school: 'While she ___ (cycle) to school, she ___ (see) an accident.'
a: was cycling ... saw
was cycling, saw
was cycling to school, she saw an accident
items.h: 'While' introduces the longer action. Which tense shows the ongoing action?
items.hd: 'While' leitet die längere Handlung ein. Welche Zeitform zeigt die andauernde Handlung?
items.e: 'While' signals the ongoing background action (past continuous). The accident was a sudden event (past simple).
items.ed: 'While' signalisiert die andauernde Hintergrundhandlung (past continuous). Der Unfall war ein plötzliches Ereignis (past simple).

items.p: Set the scene for a story about last Saturday: 'It ___ (snow) heavily and the children ___ (build) a snowman when suddenly...'
a: was snowing ... were building
was snowing, were building
items.h: Both actions were happening at the same time in the background. What form shows two simultaneous ongoing actions?
items.hd: Beide Handlungen passierten gleichzeitig im Hintergrund. Welche Form zeigt zwei gleichzeitige andauernde Handlungen?
items.e: When two longer actions happen simultaneously in the past, both use past continuous.
items.ed: Wenn zwei längere Handlungen gleichzeitig in der Vergangenheit stattfinden, verwenden beide past continuous.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Waehrend ich lernte, rief mein Freund an.
items.c: While I was studying, my friend called.
a: While I was studying, my friend called.
While I was studying, my friend called
My friend called while I was studying.
While I was learning, my friend called.
items.h: 'Waehrend' = 'while'. The longer action after 'while' needs past continuous.
items.hd: 'Während' = 'while'. Die längere Handlung nach 'while' braucht Past Continuous.
items.e: 'Waehrend ich lernte' = 'While I was studying' (past continuous for the longer action). 'Rief an' = 'called' (past simple for the interruption).
items.ed: 'Während ich lernte' = 'While I was studying' (Past Continuous für die längere Handlung). 'Rief an' = 'called' (Past Simple für die Unterbrechung).

items.p: 🇩🇪 Es regnete und die Kinder spielten im Garten, als ploetzlich ein Hund ueber den Zaun sprang.
items.c: It was raining and the children were playing in the garden when suddenly a dog jumped over the fence.
a: It was raining and the children were playing in the garden when suddenly a dog jumped over the fence.
It was raining and the children were playing in the garden when a dog suddenly jumped over the fence.
It was raining and the kids were playing in the garden when suddenly a dog jumped over the fence.
It was raining and the children were playing in the garden, when suddenly a dog jumped over the fence.
items.h: The first part describes the scene (past continuous). The dog jumping is a sudden event (past simple).
items.hd: Der erste Teil beschreibt die Szene (Past Continuous). Der springende Hund ist ein plötzliches Ereignis (Past Simple).
items.e: Scene-setting: 'was raining' + 'were playing' (both past continuous). The sudden event: 'jumped' (past simple).
items.ed: Szenenbeschreibung: 'was raining' + 'were playing' (beide Past Continuous). Das plötzliche Ereignis: 'jumped' (Past Simple).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: was / she / when / sleeping / the / alarm / rang
items.c: She was sleeping when the alarm rang.
a: She was sleeping when the alarm rang.
She was sleeping when the alarm rang
items.h: Start with the subject and the background action, then add 'when' and the short event.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Subjekt und der Hintergrundhandlung, dann füge 'when' und das kurze Ereignis hinzu.
items.e: Subject + was/were + -ing (background) + when + subject + past simple (interruption): 'She was sleeping when the alarm rang.'
items.ed: Subjekt + was/were + -ing (Hintergrund) + when + Subjekt + Past Simple (Unterbrechung): 'She was sleeping when the alarm rang.'

items.p: Match each sentence beginning with the correct ending. 1: While I was reading, 2: When the bell rang, 3: She was cooking dinner 4: While they were watching TV,
ds: a: everyone was sitting in class.
c: the dog jumped on the sofa.
d: when her brother came home.
items.h: Think about which action is the background (continuous) and which is the interruption (simple).
items.hd: Überlege, welche Handlung der Hintergrund (Continuous) und welche die Unterbrechung (Simple) ist.
items.e: 1+c: background + interruption. 2+a: 'when' + scene. 3+d: background + 'when' interruption. 4+b: background + sudden event.
items.ed: 1+c: Hintergrund + Unterbrechung. 2+a: 'when' + Szene. 3+d: Hintergrund + 'when' Unterbrechung. 4+b: Hintergrund + plötzliches Ereignis.

items.p: It's midnight. The whole family is in the living room. Which sentence fits best?
items.c: The whole family was watching a film together.
a: The whole family was watching a film together.
ds: The whole family watched a film together.
The whole family watches a film together.
The whole family had watched a film together.
items.h: At midnight, the family is in the middle of doing something. Which tense shows an ongoing action in the past?
items.hd: Um Mitternacht ist die Familie mitten in einer Aktivität. Welche Zeitform zeigt eine andauernde Handlung in der Vergangenheit?
items.e: Past continuous describes an action in progress at a specific time in the past. At midnight, the watching was still happening.
items.ed: Past continuous beschreibt eine Handlung, die zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt in der Vergangenheit noch andauerte.

items.p: When we arrived at the cinema, the film ___ already started.
a: had
items.h: The film started BEFORE you arrived. Which auxiliary shows an earlier past action?
items.hd: Der Film begann VOR eurer Ankunft. Welches Hilfsverb zeigt eine frühere vergangene Handlung?
items.e: The film starting happened BEFORE arriving. Past perfect (had + past participle) marks the earlier event: 'had already started'.
items.ed: Der Filmbeginn war VOR der Ankunft. Das Past Perfect (had + Past Participle) markiert das frühere Ereignis: 'had already started'.

items.p: She was tired because she ___ (not / sleep) well the night before.
a: hadn't slept
had not slept
items.h: Not sleeping happened BEFORE she was tired. Which tense shows the earlier of two past events?
items.hd: Das schlechte Schlafen passierte VOR der Müdigkeit. Welche Zeitform zeigt das frühere von zwei vergangenen Ereignissen?
items.e: Not sleeping happened before being tired, so we use past perfect negative: 'hadn't slept'.
items.ed: Das schlechte Schlafen passierte vor der Müdigkeit, also verwenden wir Past Perfect in der Verneinung: 'hadn't slept'.

items.p: By the time we got to the station, the train ___ (leave).
a: had left
had already left
items.h: 'By the time' is a strong signal for past perfect. The train left BEFORE you arrived.
items.hd: 'By the time' ist ein starkes Signal für Past Perfect. Der Zug fuhr VOR eurer Ankunft ab.
items.e: 'By the time' + past simple triggers past perfect for the earlier event: 'had left'.
items.ed: 'By the time' + Past Simple löst Past Perfect für das frühere Ereignis aus: 'had left'.

items.p: I ___ never ___ (see) snow before I visited Austria.
a: had ... seen
had never seen
items.h: Seeing snow happened BEFORE visiting Austria. After 'had', use the past participle (3rd column).
items.hd: Schnee sehen passierte VOR dem Besuch in Österreich. Nach 'had' verwende das Past Participle (3. Spalte).
items.e: 'Had never seen' — past perfect with 'never'. After 'had', use the past participle 'seen' (not 'saw').
items.ed: 'Had never seen' — Past Perfect mit 'never'. Nach 'had' verwende das Past Participle 'seen' (nicht 'saw').

items.p: After she ___ (finish) her homework, she went out to play.
a: had finished
items.h: 'After' tells you which action came first. The first action in a past sequence needs past perfect.
items.hd: 'After' sagt dir, welche Handlung zuerst kam. Die erste Handlung in einer vergangenen Reihenfolge braucht Past Perfect.
items.e: 'After' + past perfect (had finished) shows the earlier event. Then past simple (went) for the later event.
items.ed: 'After' + Past Perfect (had finished) zeigt das frühere Ereignis. Dann Past Simple (went) für das spätere Ereignis.

items.p: He realised he ___ (leave) his keys at home, so he ___ (go) back.
a: had left ... went
items.h: Leaving the keys happened BEFORE realising. Going back happened AFTER realising. Which tense for each?
items.hd: Die Schlüssel vergessen passierte VOR dem Bemerken. Zurückgehen passierte NACH dem Bemerken. Welche Zeitform für jedes?
items.e: 'Had left' (earlier event, past perfect) + 'went' (later event, past simple). Only the first action needs past perfect.
items.ed: 'Had left' (früheres Ereignis, Past Perfect) + 'went' (späteres Ereignis, Past Simple). Nur die erste Handlung braucht Past Perfect.

items.p: Detective Brown is interviewing a witness about last night. Which sentence correctly uses past perfect to show what happened first?
items.c: When I got home, my sister had already eaten dinner.
a: When I got home, my sister had already eaten dinner.
ds: When I got home, my sister already ate dinner.
When I had got home, my sister already ate dinner.
When I got home, my sister has already eaten dinner.
items.h: Which event happened first? That event needs past perfect. The later event uses past simple.
items.hd: Welches Ereignis passierte zuerst? Dieses braucht Past Perfect. Das spätere Ereignis nutzt Past Simple.
items.e: Eating (earlier) = past perfect: 'had already eaten'. Getting home (later) = past simple: 'got'.
items.ed: Essen (früher) = Past Perfect: 'had already eaten'. Nachhausekommen (später) = Past Simple: 'got'.

items.p: Which sentence has the WRONG past participle?
items.c: I had went to the supermarket before it closed.
a: I had went to the supermarket before it closed.
ds: She had written the email before the meeting.
They had eaten breakfast before leaving.
We had taken the wrong bus.
items.h: Check the verb form after 'had'. One sentence uses the past simple form instead of the past participle.
items.hd: Prüfe die Verbform nach 'had'. Ein Satz verwendet die Past Simple-Form statt des Past Participle.
items.e: 'Went' is past simple. After 'had', use the past participle: 'gone'. Correct: 'I had gone to the supermarket'.
items.ed: 'Went' ist Past Simple. Nach 'had' verwende das Past Participle: 'gone'. Richtig: 'I had gone to the supermarket'.

items.p: Choose the correct option: Before I ___ to bed, I ___ my teeth.
a: went ... had brushed
items.h: 'Before I went to bed' = later event (past simple). What happened before that?
items.hd: 'Before I went to bed' = späteres Ereignis (Past Simple). Was passierte davor?
items.e: 'Before I went to bed' (later, past simple), 'I had brushed my teeth' (earlier, past perfect).
items.ed: 'Before I went to bed' (später, Past Simple), 'I had brushed my teeth' (früher, Past Perfect).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: When she arrived, everyone already left the party.
items.c: When she arrived, everyone had already left the party.
a: When she arrived, everyone had already left the party.
When she arrived, everyone had already left the party
Everyone had already left the party when she arrived.
items.h: Leaving happened BEFORE arriving. Which tense marks the earlier of two past events?
items.hd: Das Verlassen passierte VOR dem Ankommen. Welche Zeitform markiert das frühere von zwei vergangenen Ereignissen?
items.e: Leaving happened before arriving, so it needs past perfect: 'had already left', not just 'left'.
items.ed: Das Verlassen passierte vor dem Ankommen, also braucht es Past Perfect: 'had already left', nicht nur 'left'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He had went to the shop before it closed.
items.c: He had gone to the shop before it closed.
a: He had gone to the shop before it closed.
He had gone to the shop before it closed
He'd gone to the shop before it closed.
items.h: Look at the verb form after 'had'. Is 'went' the correct form to use after 'had'?
items.hd: Schau dir die Verbform nach 'had' an. Ist 'went' die richtige Form nach 'had'?
items.e: After 'had', use the past participle: 'gone' (not 'went'). 'Went' is past simple, 'gone' is the 3rd column form.
items.ed: Nach 'had' verwende das Past Participle: 'gone' (nicht 'went'). 'Went' ist Past Simple, 'gone' ist die 3. Spalte.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I had eaten breakfast and then I had walked to school.
items.c: I had eaten breakfast and then I walked to school.
a: I had eaten breakfast and then I walked to school.
I had eaten breakfast and then I walked to school
After I had eaten breakfast, I walked to school.
items.h: Only the EARLIER event needs past perfect. The later event should be in past simple.
items.hd: Nur das FRÜHERE Ereignis braucht Past Perfect. Das spätere Ereignis sollte im Past Simple stehen.
items.e: Don't use past perfect for both events. Only the earlier one (eating) needs it. Walking is the later event: 'walked' (past simple).
items.ed: Verwende nicht Past Perfect für beide Ereignisse. Nur das frühere (Essen) braucht es. Gehen ist das spätere Ereignis: 'walked' (Past Simple).

items.p: You're telling a detective what happened. Complete: 'When I ___ (arrive) at the station, the bus ___ already ___ (leave).'
a: arrived ... had already left
arrived, had already left
items.h: Which action happened first — your arrival or the bus leaving? The earlier action needs past perfect.
items.hd: Welche Handlung passierte zuerst — deine Ankunft oder die Abfahrt des Busses? Die frühere Handlung braucht past perfect.
items.e: The bus left before you arrived. Use past perfect (had + past participle) for the earlier action.
items.ed: Der Bus fuhr ab, bevor du ankamst. Verwende past perfect (had + past participle) für die frühere Handlung.

items.p: Describe Emma's evening routine: 'After she ___ (finish) her homework, she ___ (go) out to meet her friends.'
a: had finished ... went
had finished, went
items.h: 'After' tells you which action came first. The first action needs past perfect.
items.hd: 'After' sagt dir, welche Handlung zuerst kam. Die erste Handlung braucht past perfect.
items.e: After + past perfect (had finished) shows the first action. Past simple (went) shows the second action.
items.ed: After + past perfect (had finished) zeigt die erste Handlung. Past simple (went) zeigt die zweite Handlung.

items.p: You arrived late to the airport. Tell your friend: 'By the time we ___ (get) to the airport, the plane ___ already ___ (take off).'
a: got ... had already taken off
got, had already taken off
items.h: 'By the time' introduces the later event. What had already happened before you got there?
items.hd: 'By the time' leitet das spätere Ereignis ein. Was war schon passiert, bevor ihr dort ankamt?
items.e: 'By the time' + past simple introduces the later action. The earlier action (the plane taking off) uses past perfect.
items.ed: 'By the time' + past simple leitet die spätere Handlung ein. Die frühere Handlung (das Abheben) verwendet past perfect.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Als wir ankamen, hatte der Film schon angefangen.
items.c: When we arrived, the film had already started.
a: When we arrived, the film had already started.
When we arrived, the movie had already started.
The film had already started when we arrived.
When we arrived, the film had already begun.
items.h: 'Als wir ankamen' = 'When we arrived' (past simple). 'Hatte schon angefangen' = 'had already started' (past perfect).
items.hd: 'Als wir ankamen' = 'When we arrived' (Past Simple). 'Hatte schon angefangen' = 'had already started' (Past Perfect).
items.e: German 'hatte angefangen' (Plusquamperfekt) = English 'had started' (past perfect). 'Als' = 'when' + past simple.
items.ed: 'Hatte angefangen' (Plusquamperfekt) = 'had started' (Past Perfect). 'Als' = 'when' + Past Simple.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Er war muede, weil er nicht gut geschlafen hatte.
items.c: He was tired because he hadn't slept well.
a: He was tired because he hadn't slept well.
He was tired because he had not slept well.
He was tired because he hadn't slept well
He was tired, because he hadn't slept well.
items.h: 'War muede' = 'was tired' (past simple). 'Hatte nicht geschlafen' = 'hadn't slept' (past perfect).
items.hd: 'War müde' = 'was tired' (Past Simple). 'Hatte nicht geschlafen' = 'hadn't slept' (Past Perfect).
items.e: 'Was tired' (past simple, result) + 'hadn't slept' (past perfect, cause that happened earlier).
items.ed: 'Was tired' (Past Simple, Ergebnis) + 'hadn't slept' (Past Perfect, frühere Ursache).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: had / the / already / she / book / read / before / film / the / she / watched
items.c: She had already read the book before she watched the film.
a: She had already read the book before she watched the film.
She had already read the book before she watched the film
items.h: Start with the subject + 'had already' + past participle. Then 'before' + subject + past simple.
items.hd: Beginne mit Subjekt + 'had already' + Past Participle. Dann 'before' + Subjekt + Past Simple.
items.e: Past perfect (had already read) comes first, then 'before' introduces the later event (watched) in past simple.
items.ed: Past Perfect (had already read) kommt zuerst, dann leitet 'before' das spätere Ereignis (watched) im Past Simple ein.

items.p: Match each sentence beginning with the correct ending. 1: When the teacher came in, 2: By the time we arrived, 3: She was happy because 4: After they had finished eating, 5: He couldn't find his phone because
ds: a: the concert had already started.
b: he had left it on the bus.
c: she had passed all her exams.
d: the students had already sat down.
e: they went for a walk.
items.h: Look for past perfect (had + past participle) in the earlier event, and past simple in the later event.
items.hd: Suche nach Past Perfect (had + Past Participle) beim früheren Ereignis und Past Simple beim späteren.
items.e: Each pair shows temporal sequence: the past perfect event happened first, the past simple event happened second.
items.ed: Jedes Paar zeigt eine zeitliche Abfolge: das Past Perfect-Ereignis passierte zuerst, das Past Simple-Ereignis als zweites.

items.p: Sarah arrived at the cinema at 7:30. The film started at 7:15. Which sentence is correct?
items.c: When Sarah arrived, the film had already started.
a: When Sarah arrived, the film had already started.
ds: When Sarah arrived, the film already started.
When Sarah arrived, the film has already started.
When Sarah arrived, the film was already starting.
items.h: The film started BEFORE Sarah arrived. Which tense shows an action completed before another past action?
items.hd: Der Film begann VOR Sarahs Ankunft. Welche Zeitform zeigt eine Handlung, die vor einer anderen vergangenen Handlung abgeschlossen war?
items.e: Past perfect (had started) shows an action completed before another past action (arrived).
items.ed: Past perfect (had started) zeigt eine Handlung, die vor einer anderen vergangenen Handlung abgeschlossen war.

items.p: 'I like pizza but I don't eat it very often.' — He said that he ___ pizza but he ___ it very often.
a: liked ... didn't eat
liked, didn't eat
items.h: Both verbs need tense backshift: 'like' and 'don't eat' move one step into the past.
items.hd: Beide Verben brauchen Zeitverschiebung: 'like' und 'don't eat' verschieben sich einen Schritt in die Vergangenheit.
items.e: Tense backshift: 'like' (present simple) becomes 'liked' (past simple) in reported speech.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'like' (Present Simple) wird zu 'liked' (Past Simple) in der indirekten Rede.

items.p: "I will help you." ��� She told me she ___ help me.
a: would
items.h: In reported speech, 'will' shifts back to which modal?
items.hd: In der indirekten Rede wird 'will' zu welchem Modalverb?
items.e: Tense backshift: 'will' becomes 'would' in reported speech. Also note: 'you' (original) becomes 'me' (pronoun shift).
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'will' wird zu 'would' in der indirekten Rede. Beachte auch: 'you' (Original) wird zu 'me' (Pronomenverschiebung).

items.p: "I am reading a great book." — She said she ___ reading a great book.
a: was
items.h: Present continuous ('am reading') shifts to past continuous in reported speech. What replaces 'am' for 'she'?
items.hd: Present Continuous ('am reading') wird zu Past Continuous in der indirekten Rede. Was ersetzt 'am' für 'she'?
items.e: Backshift: 'am reading' (present continuous) becomes 'was reading' (past continuous). 'I' becomes 'she'.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'am reading' (Present Continuous) wird zu 'was reading' (Past Continuous). 'I' wird zu 'she'.

items.p: "I saw a good film yesterday." — He ___ me that he ___ a good film the day before.
a: told ... had seen
items.h: Which verb needs a personal object: 'said' or 'told'? And what does past simple become in reported speech?
items.hd: Welches Verb braucht ein Personalobjekt: 'said' oder 'told'? Und was wird aus Past Simple in der indirekten Rede?
items.e: 'Told me' (tell + person). Backshift: 'saw' (past simple) becomes 'had seen' (past perfect). 'Yesterday' becomes 'the day before'.
items.ed: 'Told me' (tell + Person). Zeitverschiebung: 'saw' (Past Simple) wird zu 'had seen' (Past Perfect). 'Yesterday' wird zu 'the day before'.

items.p: "We can come to your party tomorrow." — They said they ___ come to ___ party the next day.
a: could ... my
items.h: 'Can' shifts to 'could'. 'Your' changes to match who the speaker is talking to.
items.hd: 'Can' wird zu 'could'. 'Your' ändert sich je nachdem, zu wem der Sprecher spricht.
items.e: Backshift: 'can' becomes 'could'. Pronoun shift: 'your' becomes 'my'. Time shift: 'tomorrow' becomes 'the next day'.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'can' wird zu 'could'. Pronomenverschiebung: 'your' wird zu 'my'. Zeitverschiebung: 'tomorrow' wird zu 'the next day'.

items.p: "I have never been to London." — She said she ___ never ___ to London.
a: had ... been
had never been
items.h: Present perfect ('have been') shifts to past perfect ('had been') in reported speech.
items.hd: Present Perfect ('have been') wird zu Past Perfect ('had been') in der indirekten Rede.
items.e: Backshift: 'have never been' (present perfect) becomes 'had never been' (past perfect).
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'have never been' (Present Perfect) wird zu 'had never been' (Past Perfect).

items.p: "I'm tired." — He said that he ___ tired.
a: was
items.h: 'I'm tired' = 'am tired'. In reported speech, what does 'am' become?
items.hd: 'I'm tired' = 'am tired'. In der indirekten Rede, was wird aus 'am'?
items.e: Backshift: 'am' (present) becomes 'was' (past). 'I' becomes 'he'.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'am' (Gegenwart) wird zu 'was' (Vergangenheit). 'I' wird zu 'he'.

items.p: Which reported sentence is correct? Original: "I live in Vienna."
items.c: She said she lived in Vienna.
a: She said she lived in Vienna.
ds: She said she lives in Vienna.
She said me she lived in Vienna.
She told that she lived in Vienna.
items.h: Check three things: tense backshift, say/tell usage, and pronoun shifts.
items.hd: Prüfe drei Dinge: Zeitverschiebung, say/tell-Verwendung und Pronomenverschiebung.
items.e: 'Said' (no person object) + backshift 'live' to 'lived' + pronoun 'I' to 'she'. All correct in the first option.
items.ed: 'Said' (ohne Personalobjekt) + Zeitverschiebung 'live' zu 'lived' + Pronomen 'I' zu 'she'. Alles korrekt in der ersten Option.

items.p: "I must leave now." — Which is the correct reported form?
items.c: He said he had to leave then.
a: He said he had to leave then.
ds: He said he must leave now.
He said he musted leave then.
He said he had to leave now.
items.h: 'Must' becomes 'had to' in reported speech. 'Now' becomes 'then'.
items.hd: 'Must' wird zu 'had to' in der indirekten Rede. 'Now' wird zu 'then'.
items.e: Backshift: 'must' becomes 'had to'. Time shift: 'now' becomes 'then'. 'I' becomes 'he'.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'must' wird zu 'had to'. Zeitverschiebung: 'now' wird zu 'then'. 'I' wird zu 'he'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He said he likes jazz.
a: He said he liked jazz.
He said he liked jazz
He said that he liked jazz.
items.h: In reported speech after 'said', the tense must shift back. What is the past of 'likes'?
items.hd: In der indirekten Rede nach 'said' muss die Zeitform zurückverschoben werden. Was ist die Vergangenheit von 'likes'?
items.e: Tense backshift is required: 'likes' (present simple) must become 'liked' (past simple) in reported speech.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung ist erforderlich: 'likes' (Present Simple) muss zu 'liked' (Past Simple) in der indirekten Rede werden.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She said me that she was hungry.
items.c: She told me that she was hungry.
a: She told me that she was hungry.
She told me she was hungry.
She said that she was hungry.
items.h: 'Said me' is wrong. Which verb takes a personal object: 'say' or 'tell'?
items.hd: 'Said me' ist falsch. Welches Verb braucht ein Personalobjekt: 'say' oder 'tell'?
items.e: 'Say' cannot take a personal object. Either 'She said that...' (no person) or 'She told me that...' (tell + person).
items.ed: 'Say' kann kein Personalobjekt haben. Entweder 'She said that...' (ohne Person) oder 'She told me that...' (tell + Person).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Tom said that he will visit us tomorrow.
items.c: Tom said that he would visit us the next day.
a: Tom said that he would visit us the next day.
Tom said he would visit us the next day.
Tom said that he would visit us the following day.
items.h: Two things need to change: the modal verb and the time expression.
items.hd: Zwei Dinge müssen sich ändern: das Modalverb und der Zeitausdruck.
items.e: Two shifts needed: 'will' becomes 'would' (modal backshift) and 'tomorrow' becomes 'the next day' (time shift).
items.ed: Zwei Verschiebungen nötig: 'will' wird zu 'would' (Modalverschiebung) und 'tomorrow' wird zu 'the next day' (Zeitverschiebung).

items.p: Your friend Tom missed the party. You tell him what Sarah said: 'Sarah told me she ___ (learn) English at a new school.'
a: was learning
items.h: Sarah originally said 'I am learning...' What happens to present continuous in reported speech?
items.hd: Sarah sagte ursprünglich 'I am learning...' Was passiert mit present continuous in der indirekten Rede?
items.e: Present continuous (am learning) becomes past continuous (was learning) in reported speech.
items.ed: Present continuous (am learning) wird in der indirekten Rede zu past continuous (was learning).

items.p: The police are asking what your friend said. Complete: 'He told the police that he ___ (buy) a new phone the day ___.'
a: had bought ... before
had bought, before
items.h: He originally said 'I bought a new phone yesterday.' What happens to past simple and 'yesterday' in reported speech?
items.hd: Er sagte ursprünglich 'I bought a new phone yesterday.' Was passiert mit past simple und 'yesterday' in der indirekten Rede?
items.e: Past simple (bought) becomes past perfect (had bought). 'Yesterday' becomes 'the day before'.
items.ed: Past simple (bought) wird zu past perfect (had bought). 'Yesterday' wird zu 'the day before'.

items.p: You're telling your mum what your friends said on the phone: 'They said they ___ (can / not) come to my party that ___.'
a: couldn't ... day
could not ... day
items.h: They originally said 'We can't come to your party today.' What happens to 'can' and 'today'?
items.hd: Sie sagten ursprünglich 'We can't come to your party today.' Was passiert mit 'can' und 'today'?
items.e: 'Can't' becomes 'couldn't' and 'today' becomes 'that day' in reported speech.
items.ed: 'Can't' wird zu 'couldn't' und 'today' wird zu 'that day' in der indirekten Rede.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Er sagte, dass er muede war.
items.c: He said that he was tired.
a: He said that he was tired.
He said he was tired.
He said that he was tired
He said he was tired
items.h: 'Sagte, dass' = 'said that'. Remember: German doesn't require backshift, but English does.
items.hd: 'Sagte, dass' = 'said that'. Denk daran: Deutsch braucht keine Zeitverschiebung, aber Englisch schon.
items.e: 'Er sagte, dass er muede war' = 'He said that he was tired.' The backshift is the same as German here (war = was).
items.ed: 'Er sagte, dass er müde war' = 'He said that he was tired.' Die Zeitverschiebung ist hier gleich wie im Deutschen (war = was).

items.p: 🇩🇪 Sie erzaehlte mir, dass sie am naechsten Tag nach Wien fahren wuerde.
items.c: She told me that she would go to Vienna the next day.
a: She told me that she would go to Vienna the next day.
She told me she would go to Vienna the next day.
She told me that she would travel to Vienna the next day.
She told me she would go to Vienna the following day.
items.h: 'Erzaehlte mir' = 'told me'. 'Wuerde fahren' = 'would go'. 'Am naechsten Tag' = 'the next day'.
items.hd: 'Erzählte mir' = 'told me'. 'Würde fahren' = 'would go'. 'Am nauchsten Tag' = 'the next day'.
items.e: 'Told me' (tell + person), 'would go' (backshifted 'will go'), 'the next day' (time shift from 'tomorrow').
items.ed: 'Told me' (tell + Person), 'would go' (zurückverschobenes 'will go'), 'the next day' (Zeitverschiebung von 'tomorrow').

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: said / he / liked / that / she / chocolate
items.c: He said that she liked chocolate.
a: He said that she liked chocolate.
He said that she liked chocolate
He said she liked chocolate.
items.h: Start with the reporting verb: who said something? Then 'that' + reported clause.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Berichtsverb: wer hat etwas gesagt? Dann 'that' + berichteter Satz.
items.e: Structure: Subject + said + (that) + reported clause. 'He said that she liked chocolate.'
items.ed: Struktur: Subjekt + said + (that) + berichteter Satz. 'He said that she liked chocolate.'

items.p: Match each direct speech time expression with its reported speech equivalent. 1: today 2: yesterday 3: tomorrow 4: last week 5: next month
items.h: Each time expression shifts to a more 'distant' form in reported speech.
items.hd: Jeder Zeitausdruck verschiebt sich in der indirekten Rede zu einer 'entfernteren' Form.
items.e: In reported speech: today→that day, yesterday→the day before, tomorrow→the next day, last week→the week before, next month→the following month.
items.ed: In der indirekten Rede: today→that day, yesterday→the day before, tomorrow→the next day, last week→the week before, next month→the following month.

items.p: Lisa said: "I don't have any homework today." Report what Lisa said to your teacher, starting with: Lisa said that ...
items.c: Lisa said that she didn't have any homework that day.
a: Lisa said that she didn't have any homework that day.
Lisa said she didn't have any homework that day.
Lisa said that she did not have any homework that day.
items.h: Three shifts: 'I' -> 'she', 'don't have' -> 'didn't have', 'today' -> 'that day'.
items.hd: Drei Verschiebungen: 'I' -> 'she', 'don't have' -> 'didn't have', 'today' -> 'that day'.
items.e: Pronoun shift: 'I' -> 'she'. Backshift: 'don't have' -> 'didn't have'. Time shift: 'today' -> 'that day'.
items.ed: Pronomenverschiebung: 'I' -> 'she'. Zeitverschiebung: 'don't have' -> 'didn't have'. Zeitverschiebung: 'today' -> 'that day'.

items.p: She asked me ___ I liked pizza.
a: if
whether
items.h: The original question was 'Do you like pizza?' — a yes/no question. What word introduces reported yes/no questions?
items.hd: Die ursprüngliche Frage war 'Do you like pizza?' — eine Ja/Nein-Frage. Welches Wort leitet indirekte Ja/Nein-Fragen ein?
items.e: Reported yes/no questions use 'if' or 'whether': She asked me if/whether I liked pizza.
items.ed: Indirekte Ja/Nein-Fragen verwenden 'if' oder 'whether': She asked me if/whether I liked pizza.

items.p: He asked where I ___.
a: lived
items.h: Reported questions use statement word order (subject + verb). No auxiliaries like 'did' or 'do'.
items.hd: Indirekte Fragen verwenden Aussagesatzstellung (Subjekt + Verb). Keine Hilfsverben wie 'did' oder 'do'.
items.e: 'Where do you live?' becomes 'He asked where I lived.' — statement word order, no 'do/did'.
items.ed: 'Where do you live?' wird zu 'He asked where I lived.' — Aussagesatzstellung, kein 'do/did'.

items.p: The teacher asked us ___ we had finished our homework.
a: whether
if
items.h: 'Have you finished your homework?' is a yes/no question. What word introduces reported yes/no questions?
items.hd: 'Have you finished your homework?' ist eine Ja/Nein-Frage. Welches Wort leitet indirekte Ja/Nein-Fragen ein?
items.e: Reported yes/no questions use 'if' or 'whether': The teacher asked us whether/if we had finished.
items.ed: Indirekte Ja/Nein-Fragen brauchen 'if' oder 'whether': The teacher asked us whether/if we had finished.

items.p: She wanted to know what time the film ___.
a: started
items.h: In reported questions, use statement word order: subject + verb. No 'did' or 'does'.
items.hd: In indirekten Fragen verwende Aussagesatzstellung: Subjekt + Verb. Kein 'did' oder 'does'.
items.e: 'What time does the film start?' → She wanted to know what time the film started. Statement order, tense backshift.
items.ed: 'What time does the film start?' → She wanted to know what time the film started. Aussagesatzstellung, Zeitverschiebung.

items.p: He asked me how long I ___ English.
a: had been learning
had been studying
items.h: The original question used present perfect continuous. In reported speech, shift one step back.
items.hd: Die Originalfrage verwendete Present Perfect Continuous. In der indirekten Rede eine Zeitstufe zurückverschieben.
items.e: 'How long have you been learning English?' → He asked me how long I had been learning English. Present perfect continuous → past perfect continuous.
items.ed: 'How long have you been learning English?' → He asked me how long I had been learning English. Present Perfect Continuous → Past Perfect Continuous.

items.p: Mum asked me ___ I was going after school.
a: where
items.h: The original question was 'Where are you going after school?' — keep the wh-word.
items.hd: Die Originalfrage war 'Where are you going after school?' — behalte das Fragewort.
items.e: Reported wh-questions keep the wh-word: 'Where are you going?' → Mum asked me where I was going.
items.ed: Indirekte W-Fragen behalten das Fragewort: 'Where are you going?' → Mum asked me where I was going.

items.p: Choose the correct reported question: 'Do you have a pet?' she asked him.
items.c: She asked him if he had a pet.
a: She asked him if he had a pet.
ds: She asked him did he have a pet.
She asked him that he had a pet.
She asked him if did he have a pet.
items.h: Reported yes/no questions need 'if/whether' + statement word order (no 'did').
