items.hd: Indirekte Ja/Nein-Fragen brauchen 'if/whether' + Aussagesatzstellung (kein 'did').
items.e: 'Do you have a pet?' → She asked him if he had a pet. Use if/whether + subject + verb (statement order).
items.ed: 'Do you have a pet?' → She asked him if he had a pet. Verwende if/whether + Subjekt + Verb (Aussagesatzstellung).

items.p: Choose the correct reported question: 'Where did you buy that jacket?' Tom asked Lisa.
items.c: Tom asked Lisa where she had bought that jacket.
a: Tom asked Lisa where she had bought that jacket.
ds: Tom asked Lisa where did she buy that jacket.
Tom asked Lisa where she did buy that jacket.
Tom asked Lisa that where she had bought that jacket.
items.h: Remove 'did', backshift the tense, and use statement word order after the wh-word.
items.hd: Entferne 'did', verschiebe die Zeitform zurück und verwende Aussagesatzstellung nach dem Fragewort.
items.e: 'Where did you buy...?' → Tom asked Lisa where she had bought... Past simple → past perfect, statement word order.
items.ed: 'Where did you buy...?' → Tom asked Lisa where she had bought... Past Simple → Past Perfect, Aussagesatzstellung.

items.p: Choose the correct reported question: 'Will you come to my party?' she asked me.
items.c: She asked me if I would come to her party.
a: She asked me if I would come to her party.
ds: She asked me if will I come to her party.
She asked me that I would come to her party.
She asked me if I would come to my party.
items.h: Check three things: if/whether for yes/no, statement word order, and pronoun changes.
items.hd: Prüfe drei Dinge: if/whether für Ja/Nein, Aussagesatzstellung und Pronomen-Änderungen.
items.e: 'Will you come to my party?' → She asked me if I would come to her party. Will→would, you→I, my→her.
items.ed: 'Will you come to my party?' → She asked me if I would come to her party. Will→would, you→I, my→her.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He asked where did I live.
a: He asked where I lived.
He asked where I live.
He asked me where I lived.
items.h: Reported questions use statement word order — no inversion, no 'did'.
items.hd: Indirekte Fragen verwenden Aussagesatzstellung — keine Umstellung, kein 'did'.
items.e: In reported wh-questions, use statement word order: where + I + lived. Remove 'did' and put the verb in past tense.
items.ed: In indirekten W-Fragen verwende Aussagesatzstellung: where + I + lived. Entferne 'did' und setze das Verb ins Past Simple.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She asked me that I was hungry.
items.c: She asked me if I was hungry.
a: She asked me if I was hungry.
She asked me whether I was hungry.
She asked if I was hungry.
She asked whether I was hungry.
items.h: 'That' introduces reported statements. What word introduces reported yes/no questions?
items.hd: 'That' leitet indirekte Aussagesätze ein. Welches Wort leitet indirekte Ja/Nein-Fragen ein?
items.e: Reported yes/no questions need 'if' or 'whether', not 'that'. 'That' is only for reported statements.
items.ed: Indirekte Ja/Nein-Fragen brauchen 'if' oder 'whether', nicht 'that'. 'That' ist nur für indirekte Aussagesätze.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The doctor asked if was I feeling better.
items.c: The doctor asked if I was feeling better.
a: The doctor asked if I was feeling better.
The doctor asked whether I was feeling better.
The doctor asked if I was feeling better
items.h: After 'if', use statement word order: subject + verb, not verb + subject.
items.hd: Nach 'if' verwende Aussagesatzstellung: Subjekt + Verb, nicht Verb + Subjekt.
items.e: After 'if/whether' in reported questions, use statement word order: if + I + was, not 'if was I'.
items.ed: Nach 'if/whether' in indirekten Fragen verwende Aussagesatzstellung: if + I + was, nicht 'if was I'.

items.p: At the doctor's office, Dr Smith asked about your health. Tell your mum: 'She asked me ___ I ___ (be) feeling OK.'
a: if ... was
whether ... was
if/whether ... was
items.h: The original question was 'Are you feeling OK?' — a yes/no question. What word introduces reported yes/no questions?
items.hd: Die ursprüngliche Frage war 'Are you feeling OK?' — eine Ja/Nein-Frage. Welches Wort leitet indirekte Ja/Nein-Fragen ein?
items.e: Yes/no questions use 'if' or 'whether' in reported speech. The word order changes to statement order (subject before verb).
items.ed: Ja/Nein-Fragen verwenden 'if' oder 'whether' in der indirekten Rede. Die Wortstellung ändert sich zu Aussagesatzstellung.

items.p: Your teacher wants to know about your weekend. Later you tell a friend: 'He asked me what I ___ (do) at the weekend.'
a: had done
items.h: The original question was 'What did you do at the weekend?' What happens to past simple in reported speech?
items.hd: Die ursprüngliche Frage war 'What did you do at the weekend?' Was passiert mit past simple in der indirekten Rede?
items.e: Past simple (did) becomes past perfect (had done) in reported speech. Notice the statement word order: 'what I had done' not 'what had I done'.
items.ed: Past simple (did) wird zu past perfect (had done) in der indirekten Rede. Beachte die Aussagesatzstellung: 'what I had done', nicht 'what had I done'.

items.p: At a job interview, the interviewer asked about your languages. Tell your dad: 'She asked me how many languages I ___ (can) speak.'
a: could
items.h: The original question was 'How many languages can you speak?' What happens to 'can' in reported speech?
items.hd: Die ursprüngliche Frage war 'How many languages can you speak?' Was passiert mit 'can' in der indirekten Rede?
items.e: 'Can' becomes 'could' in reported speech. Statement word order: 'how many languages I could speak'.
items.ed: 'Can' wird zu 'could' in der indirekten Rede. Aussagesatzstellung: 'how many languages I could speak'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Er fragte mich, ob ich Geschwister habe.
items.c: He asked me if I had siblings.
a: He asked me if I had siblings.
He asked me whether I had siblings.
He asked me if I had brothers and sisters.
He asked me whether I had brothers and sisters.
He asked me if I had any siblings.
He asked whether I had siblings.
items.h: 'ob' = if/whether. Use statement word order after if/whether.
items.hd: 'ob' = if/whether. Verwende Aussagesatzstellung nach if/whether.
items.e: 'Er fragte mich, ob...' → He asked me if/whether... 'ob' signals a reported yes/no question.
items.ed: 'Er fragte mich, ob...' → He asked me if/whether... 'ob' zeigt eine indirekte Ja/Nein-Frage.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Sie wollte wissen, wo ich gestern war.
items.c: She wanted to know where I had been yesterday.
a: She wanted to know where I had been yesterday.
She wanted to know where I had been the day before.
She wanted to know where I was yesterday.
She wanted to know where I had been the previous day.
items.h: 'wollte wissen' = wanted to know. 'wo' = where. Statement word order after 'where'.
items.hd: 'wollte wissen' = wanted to know. 'wo' = where. Aussagesatzstellung nach 'where'.
items.e: 'Sie wollte wissen, wo...' → She wanted to know where... Statement word order: where + I + had been.
items.ed: 'Sie wollte wissen, wo...' → She wanted to know where... Aussagesatzstellung: where + I + had been.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: asked / she / was / if / me / I / tired
items.c: She asked me if I was tired.
a: She asked me if I was tired.
She asked me whether I was tired.
items.h: Start with the reporting verb structure: She asked me. Then if/whether + statement order.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Berichtssatz: She asked me. Dann if/whether + Aussagesatzstellung.
items.e: She asked me + if + I was tired. Statement word order after 'if'.
items.ed: She asked me + if + I was tired. Aussagesatzstellung nach 'if'.

items.p: Match the direct question with its correct reported form:
ds: 1: Do you like sport?|2: Where is the station?|3: Can you swim?|4: What time is it?|5: Are you happy?
a: He asked where the station was.|b: She asked what time it was.|c: He asked if I was happy.|d: She asked if I liked sport.|e: They asked if I could swim.
items.h: Match each direct question to its reported version. Check: if/whether for yes/no, wh-word for wh-questions, statement word order.
items.hd: Ordne jede direkte Frage ihrer indirekten Form zu. Prüfe: if/whether für Ja/Nein, Fragewort für W-Fragen, Aussagesatzstellung.
items.e: Yes/no questions → if/whether. Wh-questions keep their wh-word. All use statement word order.
items.ed: Ja/Nein-Fragen → if/whether. W-Fragen behalten ihr Fragewort. Alle verwenden Aussagesatzstellung.

items.p: Your friend said: 'I went to London.' You want to know why. Report your question starting with: I asked him...
items.c: I asked him why he had gone to London.
a: I asked him why he had gone to London.
I asked him why he went to London.
I asked him why he had gone to London
items.h: Use the wh-word 'why' + statement word order. Backshift: went → had gone.
items.hd: Verwende das Fragewort 'why' + Aussagesatzstellung. Zeitverschiebung: went → had gone.
items.e: I asked him why he had gone to London. Wh-word + subject + verb in statement order. Past simple → past perfect.
items.ed: I asked him why he had gone to London. Fragewort + Subjekt + Verb in Aussagesatzstellung. Past Simple → Past Perfect.

items.p: After she ___ (finish) her homework, she watched TV.
a: had finished
items.h: 'After' introduces the action that happened FIRST. Which tense shows the earlier past action?
items.hd: 'After' leitet die Handlung ein, die ZÜRST passiert ist. Welche Zeitform zeigt die frühere Handlung?
items.e: After + past perfect (had finished) for the earlier action, then past simple (watched) for the later one.
items.ed: After + Past Perfect (had finished) für die frühere Handlung, dann Past Simple (watched) für die spätere.

items.p: By the time we arrived, the concert ___ (start).
a: had started
had already started
items.h: 'By the time we arrived' means the concert started BEFORE we arrived. Which tense for the earlier action?
items.hd: 'By the time we arrived' bedeutet, das Konzert begann VOR unserer Ankunft. Welche Zeitform für die frühere Handlung?
items.e: 'By the time' + past simple (arrived), the earlier action uses past perfect: had started.
items.ed: 'By the time' + Past Simple (arrived), die frühere Handlung verwendet Past Perfect: had started.

items.p: Before I ___ (go) to bed, I had brushed my teeth.
a: went
items.h: 'Before I ___ to bed' is the LATER action. 'I had brushed my teeth' (past perfect) is the EARLIER action. What tense for the later action?
items.hd: 'Before I ___ to bed' ist die SPÄTERE Handlung. 'I had brushed my teeth' (Past Perfect) ist die FRÜHERE Handlung. Welche Zeitform für die spätere Handlung?
items.e: With 'before', the clause with 'before' is the later action (past simple). The other clause is the earlier action (past perfect).
items.ed: Bei 'before' ist der Nebensatz mit 'before' die spätere Handlung (Past Simple). Der andere Satz ist die frühere Handlung (Past Perfect).

items.p: As soon as the bell ___ (ring), the students ran outside.
a: had rung
items.h: 'As soon as' + the first action (past perfect) + the second action (past simple).
items.hd: 'As soon as' + die erste Handlung (Past Perfect) + die zweite Handlung (Past Simple).
items.e: As soon as + past perfect (had rung) shows the bell was the first event; then the students ran (past simple).
items.ed: As soon as + Past Perfect (had rung) zeigt, dass das Klingeln zuerst passierte; dann rannten die Schüler (Past Simple).

items.p: When I got to the bus stop, the bus ___ already ___ (leave).
a: had ... left
had already left
items.h: 'When I got' (past simple) = the later action. The bus leaving happened BEFORE. Which tense?
items.hd: 'When I got' (Past Simple) = die spätere Handlung. Das Abfahren des Busses passierte DAVOR. Welche Zeitform?
items.e: When + past simple (got) for the later event. The earlier event (bus leaving) uses past perfect: had already left.
items.ed: When + Past Simple (got) für das spätere Ereignis. Das frühere Ereignis (Bus abgefahren) verwendet Past Perfect: had already left.

items.p: ___ the time the ambulance arrived, the man had recovered.
a: By
items.h: Which connector means 'before or when something happened'? It goes with 'the time'.
items.hd: Welcher Konnektor bedeutet 'bevor oder als etwas passierte'? Er geht mit 'the time'.
items.e: 'By the time' = before or at the moment when. By the time the ambulance arrived, the man had recovered.
items.ed: 'By the time' = bevor oder in dem Moment als. By the time the ambulance arrived, the man had recovered.

items.p: You're reading a detective story. Which sentence correctly describes the sequence of events?
items.c: After they had eaten dinner, they went for a walk.
a: After they had eaten dinner, they went for a walk.
ds: After they ate dinner, they had gone for a walk.
After they had eaten dinner, they had gone for a walk.
After they have eaten dinner, they went for a walk.
items.h: 'After' + earlier action (past perfect) + later action (past simple).
items.hd: 'After' + frühere Handlung (Past Perfect) + spätere Handlung (Past Simple).
items.e: After + past perfect (had eaten) for what happened first, then past simple (went) for what happened next.
items.ed: After + Past Perfect (had eaten) für das, was zuerst passierte, dann Past Simple (went) für das, was danach passierte.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence:
items.c: By the time she got home, her family had already had dinner.
a: By the time she got home, her family had already had dinner.
ds: By the time she had got home, her family already had dinner.
By the time she got home, her family already has dinner.
By the time she gets home, her family had already had dinner.
items.h: 'By the time' + past simple (the later moment), + past perfect (what happened before that moment).
items.hd: 'By the time' + Past Simple (der spätere Moment), + Past Perfect (was davor passierte).
items.e: By the time + past simple (got home) = the later reference point. The earlier action uses past perfect: had already had dinner.
items.ed: By the time + Past Simple (got home) = der spätere Bezugspunkt. Die frühere Handlung verwendet Past Perfect: had already had dinner.

items.p: Which connector best completes the sentence? '___ she had read the book, she returned it to the library.'
a: After
items.h: She read the book FIRST, then returned it. Which connector means 'following the completion of'?
items.hd: Sie las das Buch ZÜRST, dann gab sie es zurück. Welcher Konnektor bedeutet 'nach dem Abschluss von'?
items.e: 'After' + past perfect (had read) shows this happened first. Then she returned it (past simple).
items.ed: 'After' + Past Perfect (had read) zeigt, dass dies zuerst passierte. Dann gab sie es zurück (Past Simple).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: After he finished the race, he had drunk some water.
items.c: After he had finished the race, he drank some water.
a: After he had finished the race, he drank some water.
After he had finished the race, he drank water.
After he had finished the race he drank some water.
items.h: Which action happened first — finishing the race or drinking water? The first action needs past perfect.
items.hd: Was passierte zuerst — das Rennen beenden oder Wasser trinken? Die erste Handlung braucht Past Perfect.
items.e: Finishing the race happened first → past perfect (had finished). Drinking water happened second → past simple (drank).
items.ed: Das Rennen beenden passierte zuerst → Past Perfect (had finished). Wasser trinken passierte danach → Past Simple (drank).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: By the time we got there, the shop already closed.
items.c: By the time we got there, the shop had already closed.
a: By the time we got there, the shop had already closed.
By the time we got there the shop had already closed.
By the time we arrived, the shop had already closed.
items.h: 'By the time' + past simple = the later point. The earlier event (shop closing) needs which tense?
items.hd: 'By the time' + Past Simple = der spätere Zeitpunkt. Das frühere Ereignis (Laden schloss) braucht welche Zeitform?
items.e: 'By the time we got there' (later event). The shop closing happened earlier, so use past perfect: had already closed.
items.ed: 'By the time we got there' (späteres Ereignis). Das Schliessen des Ladens passierte früher, also verwende Past Perfect: had already closed.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Before she had left the house, she checked all the windows.
items.c: Before she left the house, she had checked all the windows.
a: Before she left the house, she had checked all the windows.
Before she left the house she had checked all the windows.
She had checked all the windows before she left the house.
items.h: 'Before she left' = the later action. Checking the windows happened first. Which clause gets past perfect?
items.hd: 'Before she left' = die spätere Handlung. Das Überprüfen der Fenster passierte zuerst. Welcher Teilsatz bekommt Past Perfect?
items.e: Checking windows happened before leaving. 'Before she left' (later = past simple). 'She had checked' (earlier = past perfect).
items.ed: Fenster prüfen passierte vor dem Weggehen. 'Before she left' (später = Past Simple). 'She had checked' (früher = Past Perfect).

items.p: Tell your friend about your evening: 'After I ___ (do) my homework, I ___ (go) out to play football.'
a: had done ... went
had done, went
items.h: 'After' tells you which action came first. The first action needs past perfect.
items.hd: 'After' sagt dir, welche Handlung zuerst kam. Die erste Handlung braucht past perfect.
items.e: The earlier action (homework) gets past perfect after 'after': After I had done... Then past simple for the later action: I played...
items.ed: Die frühere Handlung (Hausaufgaben) bekommt Past Perfect nach 'after': After I had done... Dann Past Simple für die spätere Handlung: I played...

items.p: You arrived late to the station. Tell your parents: 'By the time I ___ (get) to the station, the train ___ already ___ (leave).'
a: got ... had already left
got, had already left
items.h: 'By the time' introduces the later event. What had already happened?
items.hd: 'By the time' leitet das spätere Ereignis ein. Was war schon passiert?
items.e: By the time + past simple (arrived) = later event. Past perfect (had left) = earlier event.
items.ed: By the time + Past Simple (arrived) = späteres Ereignis. Past Perfect (had left) = früheres Ereignis.

items.p: Describe what happened after the rain stopped: 'As soon as it ___ (stop) raining, the children ___ (run) outside to play.'
a: had stopped ... ran
had stopped, ran
stopped ... ran
items.h: 'As soon as' connects two actions. Which happened first?
items.hd: 'As soon as' verbindet zwei Handlungen. Welche passierte zuerst?
items.e: As soon as + past perfect (had stopped raining) for the trigger event, then past simple (went) for the reaction.
items.ed: As soon as + Past Perfect (had stopped raining) für das auslösende Ereignis, dann Past Simple (went) für die Reaktion.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Nachdem er gegessen hatte, ging er spazieren.
items.c: After he had eaten, he went for a walk.
a: After he had eaten, he went for a walk.
After he had eaten he went for a walk.
He went for a walk after he had eaten.
After he'd eaten, he went for a walk.
items.h: 'Nachdem' = After. 'hatte gegessen' = had eaten (past perfect). 'ging spazieren' = went for a walk.
items.hd: 'Nachdem' = After. 'hatte gegessen' = had eaten (Past Perfect). 'ging spazieren' = went for a walk.
items.e: 'Nachdem' + Plusquamperfekt in German maps to 'after' + past perfect in English. Then past simple for the later action.
items.ed: 'Nachdem' + Plusquamperfekt im Deutschen entspricht 'after' + Past Perfect im Englischen. Dann Past Simple für die spätere Handlung.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Als wir ankamen, hatte das Spiel schon begonnen.
items.c: When we arrived, the game had already started.
a: When we arrived, the game had already started.
By the time we arrived, the game had already started.
The game had already started when we arrived.
When we arrived the game had already started.
items.h: 'Als wir ankamen' = When we arrived. 'hatte schon begonnen' = had already started (past perfect).
items.hd: 'Als wir ankamen' = When we arrived. 'hatte schon begonnen' = had already started (Past Perfect).
items.e: 'Als' = when (past simple for the later event). 'hatte begonnen' = had started (past perfect for the earlier event).
items.ed: 'Als' = when (Past Simple für das spätere Ereignis). 'hatte begonnen' = had started (Past Perfect für das frühere Ereignis).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: left / had / he / after / he / the house / locked / the door
items.c: After he had locked the door, he left the house.
a: After he had locked the door, he left the house.
After he had locked the door he left the house.
He left the house after he had locked the door.
items.h: 'After' + past perfect (had locked) comes first, then the past simple (left).
items.hd: 'After' + Past Perfect (had locked) kommt zuerst, dann das Past Simple (left).
items.e: After he had locked (earlier action, past perfect) the door, he left (later action, past simple) the house.
items.ed: After he had locked (frühere Handlung, Past Perfect) the door, he left (spätere Handlung, Past Simple) the house.

items.p: Match each sentence beginning with its correct ending:
ds: 1: After she had packed her bag,|2: By the time the teacher arrived,|3: As soon as it had stopped snowing,|4: Before he went to sleep,
a: we went outside to build a snowman.|b: he had brushed his teeth.|c: she walked to school.|d: the students had already sat down.
items.h: Match each connector + past perfect clause with the correct past simple result (or vice versa).
items.hd: Ordne jeden Konnektor + Past Perfect Teilsatz dem richtigen Past Simple Ergebnis zu (oder umgekehrt).
items.e: After/as soon as + past perfect = earlier event. By the time + past simple = later reference. Before + past simple = later event, other clause = past perfect.
items.ed: After/as soon as + Past Perfect = früheres Ereignis. By the time + Past Simple = späterer Bezugspunkt. Before + Past Simple = späteres Ereignis, anderer Teilsatz = Past Perfect.

items.p: Tom did his homework at 4pm. He went to the park at 5pm. Which sentence is correct?
items.c: After he had done his homework, he went to the park.
a: After he had done his homework, he went to the park.
ds: After he did his homework, he had gone to the park.
After he has done his homework, he went to the park.
After he was doing his homework, he went to the park.
items.h: Which action happened first? The first action goes with 'after' and needs past perfect.
items.hd: Welche Handlung passierte zuerst? Die erste Handlung steht mit 'after' und braucht past perfect.
items.e: 'After' + past perfect (had done) for the first event, then past simple (went) for the next event.
items.ed: 'After' + past perfect (had done) für das erste Ereignis, dann past simple (went) für das nächste.

items.p: She sings ___. (beautiful)
a: beautifully
items.h: We need an adverb (how does she sing?). Add -ly to the adjective.
items.hd: Wir brauchen ein Adverb (wie singt sie?). Füge -ly an das Adjektiv an.
items.e: 'Sings' is a verb, so we need an adverb: beautiful → beautifully. Adverbs of manner tell us HOW someone does something.
items.ed: 'Sings' ist ein Verb, also brauchen wir ein Adverb: beautiful → beautifully. Adverbien der Art und Weise sagen uns, WIE jemand etwas tut.

items.p: He runs very ___. (fast)
a: fast
items.h: 'Fast' is an irregular adverb. It has the same form as the adjective — don't add -ly!
items.hd: 'Fast' ist ein unregelmäßiges Adverb. Es hat die gleiche Form wie das Adjektiv — kein -ly anfügen!
items.e: 'Fast' is both an adjective and an adverb. No -ly needed: He runs fast. ('fastly' does NOT exist!)
items.ed: 'Fast' ist sowohl Adjektiv als auch Adverb. Kein -ly nötig: He runs fast. ('fastly' gibt es NICHT!)

items.p: She plays the piano very ___. (good)
a: well
items.h: 'Good' is an adjective. What is the adverb form of 'good'?
items.hd: 'Good' ist ein Adjektiv. Was ist die Adverbform von 'good'?
items.e: The adverb of 'good' is 'well' (NOT 'goodly'!). She plays the piano well = She plays in a good way.
items.ed: Das Adverb von 'good' ist 'well' (NICHT 'goodly'!). She plays the piano well = Sie spielt Klavier gut.

items.p: I can ___ hear you — please speak louder!
a: hardly
items.h: 'Please speak louder' means the speaker ALMOST CAN'T hear. Which adverb means 'almost not'?
items.hd: 'Please speak louder' bedeutet, der Sprecher KANN KAUM hören. Welches Adverb bedeutet 'kaum/fast nicht'?
items.e: 'Hardly' = almost not. 'Hard' = with great effort. 'I can hardly hear you' = I can almost not hear you.
items.ed: 'Hardly' = kaum/fast nicht. 'Hard' = hart/mit grosser Anstrengung. 'I can hardly hear you' = Ich kann dich kaum hören.

items.p: I haven't seen Tom ___. Is he OK?
a: lately
recently
items.h: The speaker hasn't seen Tom RECENTLY. Which adverb means 'recently'? Be careful: 'late' has a different meaning!
items.hd: Der Sprecher hat Tom IN LETZTER ZEIT nicht gesehen. Welches Adverb bedeutet 'in letzter Zeit'? Achtung: 'late' hat eine andere Bedeutung!
items.e: 'Lately' = recently, in the recent past. 'Late' = not on time. I haven't seen Tom lately = I haven't seen Tom recently.
items.ed: 'Lately' = in letzter Zeit, kürzlich. 'Late' = zu spät. I haven't seen Tom lately = Ich habe Tom in letzter Zeit nicht gesehen.

items.p: The children were playing ___ in the garden. (happy)
a: happily
items.h: We need an adverb (how were they playing?). When the adjective ends in -y, change y to i before adding -ly.
items.hd: Wir brauchen ein Adverb (wie spielten sie?). Wenn das Adjektiv auf -y endet, ändere y zu i bevor du -ly anfügst.
items.e: happy → happily (change -y to -ily). The children were playing happily = they played in a happy way.
items.ed: happy → happily (ändere -y zu -ily). The children were playing happily = sie spielten auf fröhliche Weise.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence:
a: She speaks English fluently.
items.h: Adverbs of manner go AFTER the verb + object, not between them.
items.hd: Adverbien der Art und Weise stehen NACH Verb + Objekt, nicht dazwischen.
items.e: Adverb position: She speaks English (verb + object) + fluently (adverb after object). NOT between subject and verb!
items.ed: Adverb-Position: She speaks English (Verb + Objekt) + flüntly (Adverb nach Objekt). NICHT zwischen Subjekt und Verb!

items.p: Choose the correct sentence: 'He worked ___ for the exam but he could ___ pass it.'
items.c: He worked hard for the exam but he could hardly pass it.
a: He worked hard for the exam but he could hardly pass it.
ds: He worked hardly for the exam but he could hard pass it.
He worked hard for the exam but he could hard pass it.
He worked hardly for the exam but he could hardly pass it.
items.h: First blank: 'with great effort' = ___. Second blank: 'almost not' = ___. They look similar but mean very different things!
items.hd: Erste Lücke: 'mit grosser Anstrengung' = ___. Zweite Lücke: 'kaum/fast nicht' = ___. Sie sehen ähnlich aus, bedeuten aber sehr Verschiedenes!
items.e: 'Hard' = with great effort (He worked hard). 'Hardly' = almost not (He could hardly pass). Don't confuse them!
items.ed: 'Hard' = mit grosser Anstrengung (He worked hard). 'Hardly' = kaum (He could hardly pass). Nicht verwechseln!

items.p: Tom speaks three languages. He speaks French ___.
a: fluently
items.h: You need an adverb to describe HOW he speaks. How do you form the adverb from 'fluent'?
items.hd: Du brauchst ein Adverb, um zu beschreiben, WIE er spricht. Wie bildet man das Adverb von 'fluent'?
items.e: Add -ly to the adjective 'fluent' to make the adverb 'fluently'. Adverbs describe how an action is done.
items.ed: Füge -ly zum Adjektiv 'fluent' hinzu, um das Adverb 'fluently' zu bilden. Adverben beschreiben, wie eine Handlung ausgeführt wird.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She dances very good.
a: She dances very well.
She dances really well.
She dances well.
items.h: 'Dances' is a verb. We need an adverb, not an adjective. What is the adverb form of 'good'?
items.hd: 'Dances' ist ein Verb. Wir brauchen ein Adverb, kein Adjektiv. Was ist die Adverbform von 'good'?
items.e: 'Good' is an adjective. The adverb is 'well'. She dances very well (NOT good).
items.ed: 'Good' ist ein Adjektiv. Das Adverb ist 'well'. She dances very well (NICHT good).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He drove fastly to the hospital.
items.c: He drove fast to the hospital.
a: He drove fast to the hospital.
He drove fast to the hospital
He fast drove to the hospital.
items.h: 'Fast' is one of those adverbs that does NOT add -ly. The adverb is the same as the adjective.
items.hd: 'Fast' ist eines der Adverbien, die KEIN -ly anfügen. Das Adverb hat die gleiche Form wie das Adjektiv.
items.e: 'Fast' is an irregular adverb — it's the same as the adjective. 'Fastly' does NOT exist in English!
items.ed: 'Fast' ist ein unregelmäßiges Adverb — es hat die gleiche Form wie das Adjektiv. 'Fastly' gibt es im Englischen NICHT!

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She quietly closed the door and careful walked down the stairs.
items.c: She quietly closed the door and carefully walked down the stairs.
a: She quietly closed the door and carefully walked down the stairs.
She quietly closed the door and carefully walked down the stairs
She closed the door quietly and carefully walked down the stairs.
items.h: Both verbs ('closed' and 'walked') need adverbs, not adjectives. Is 'careful' an adverb?
items.hd: Beide Verben ('closed' und 'walked') brauchen Adverbien, keine Adjektive. Ist 'careful' ein Adverb?
items.e: 'Quietly' is correct (adverb). 'Careful' should be 'carefully' — it modifies the verb 'walked', so it needs the adverb form.
items.ed: 'Quietly' ist korrekt (Adverb). 'Careful' sollte 'carefully' sein — es beschreibt das Verb 'walked', also brauchen wir die Adverbform.

items.p: Rewrite using an adverb: He is a slow runner. → He runs ___.
a: He runs slowly.
He runs slowly
He runs very slowly.
items.h: Change the adjective 'slow' to an adverb. How do you usually form adverbs from adjectives?
items.hd: Ändere das Adjektiv 'slow' in ein Adverb. Wie bildet man normalerweise Adverbien aus Adjektiven?
items.e: 'Slow' (adjective) → 'slowly' (adverb). He is a slow runner = He runs slowly.
items.ed: 'Slow' (Adjektiv) → 'slowly' (Adverb). He is a slow runner = He runs slowly.

items.p: Your friend asks about your mum's cooking. You say: 'My mum is a great cook — she cooks really ___.'
a: well
items.h: 'Good' is the adjective. What's the irregular adverb form?
items.hd: 'Good' ist das Adjektiv. Wie lautet die unregelmäßige Adverb-Form?
items.e: 'Good' (adjective) → 'well' (adverb). NOT 'goodly'! She is a good cook = She cooks well.
items.ed: 'Good' (Adjektiv) → 'well' (Adverb). NICHT 'goodly'! She is a good cook = She cooks well.

items.p: It was very foggy this morning. Tell a friend: 'I could ___ see anything — it was so foggy!'
a: hardly
items.h: 'Hardly' means 'almost not'. Don't confuse it with 'hard' (= with a lot of effort).
items.hd: 'Hardly' bedeutet 'kaum'. Verwechsle es nicht mit 'hard' (= mit viel Anstrengung).
items.e: 'Hardly' = almost not. 'He almost couldn't see' = 'He could hardly see'. Don't confuse with 'hard' (= with great effort)!
items.ed: 'Hardly' = kaum/fast nicht. 'He almost couldn't see' = 'He could hardly see'. Nicht mit 'hard' (= hart/angestrengt) verwechseln!

items.p: 🇩🇪 Er spricht sehr leise.
a: He speaks very quietly.
He talks very quietly.
He speaks really quietly.
He speaks very softly.
items.h: 'leise' = quietly (adverb). Remember to use the adverb form, not the adjective 'quiet'.
items.hd: 'leise' = quietly (Adverb). Verwende die Adverbform, nicht das Adjektiv 'quiet'.
items.e: 'leise' → quietly (adverb modifying the verb 'speaks'). He speaks very quietly.
items.ed: 'leise' → quietly (Adverb, das das Verb 'speaks' beschreibt). He speaks very quietly.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Sie hat hart gearbeitet, aber sie konnte die Pruefung kaum bestehen.
items.c: She worked hard, but she could hardly pass the exam.
a: She worked hard, but she could hardly pass the exam.
She worked hard but she could hardly pass the exam.
She had worked hard, but she could hardly pass the exam.
She worked hard, but she hardly passed the exam.
items.h: 'hart' (effort) = hard. 'kaum' (almost not) = hardly. Two similar words but very different meanings!
items.hd: 'hart' (Anstrengung) = hard. 'kaum' (fast nicht) = hardly. Zwei ähnliche Wörter mit sehr unterschiedlicher Bedeutung!
items.e: 'hart gearbeitet' = worked hard (with effort). 'kaum bestehen' = hardly pass (almost not pass). Hard ≠ hardly!
items.ed: 'hart gearbeitet' = worked hard (mit Anstrengung). 'kaum bestehen' = hardly pass (fast nicht bestehen). Hard ≠ hardly!

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: carefully / she / letter / read / the
a: She read the letter carefully.
She carefully read the letter.
items.h: Adverbs of manner usually go AFTER the verb + object: subject + verb + object + adverb.
items.hd: Adverbien der Art und Weise stehen meistens NACH Verb + Objekt: Subjekt + Verb + Objekt + Adverb.
items.e: She (subject) + read (verb) + the letter (object) + carefully (adverb of manner after the object).
items.ed: She (Subjekt) + read (Verb) + the letter (Objekt) + carefully (Adverb der Art und Weise nach dem Objekt).

items.p: Match the adjective with its correct adverb form:
ds: 1: good|2: fast|3: careful|4: happy|5: hard|6: late
a: carefully|b: hard|c: happily|d: well|e: fast|f: late
items.h: Watch out for the irregular ones! Not all adverbs end in -ly. Some look the same as the adjective.
items.hd: Achte auf die unregelmäßigen! Nicht alle Adverbien enden auf -ly. Manche sehen gleich aus wie das Adjektiv.
items.e: good→well, fast→fast (no -ly!), careful→carefully, happy→happily, hard→hard (not hardly!), late→late (not lately!).
items.ed: good→well, fast→fast (kein -ly!), careful→carefully, happy→happily, hard→hard (nicht hardly!), late→late (nicht lately!).

items.p: She plays the guitar beautifully. Ask about HOW she plays the guitar.
items.c: How does she play the guitar?
a: How does she play the guitar?
How does she play guitar?
How well does she play the guitar?
items.h: To ask about manner (how someone does something), start with 'How' + auxiliary + subject + verb.
items.hd: Um nach der Art und Weise zu fragen (wie jemand etwas tut), beginne mit 'How' + Hilfsverb + Subjekt + Verb.
items.e: Questions about manner use 'How...?': How does she play the guitar? Answer: She plays the guitar beautifully.
items.ed: Fragen nach der Art und Weise verwenden 'How...?': How does she play the guitar? Antwort: She plays the guitar beautifully.

items.p: She is your sister, ___ ?
a: isn't she
items.h: The main sentence uses 'is'. What is the opposite (negative) form?
items.hd: Der Hauptsatz verwendet 'is'. Was ist die gegenteilige (negative) Form?
items.e: The sentence is positive ('She is...'), so the tag must be negative: 'isn't she'. We repeat the auxiliary 'is' in negative form.
items.ed: Der Satz ist positiv ('She is...'), also muss der Anhang negativ sein: 'isn't she'. Wir wiederholen das Hilfsverb 'is' in negativer Form.

items.p: They can't come to the party, ___ ?
a: can they
items.h: The main sentence is negative ('can't'). Should the tag be positive or negative?
items.hd: Der Hauptsatz ist negativ ('can't'). Soll der Anhang positiv oder negativ sein?
items.e: The sentence is negative ('can't'), so the tag must be positive: 'can they'. We match the modal 'can' from the main clause.
items.ed: Der Satz ist negativ ('can't'), also muss der Anhang positiv sein: 'can they'. Wir verwenden das Modalverb 'can' aus dem Hauptsatz.

items.p: Tom likes football, ___ ?
a: doesn't he
items.h: There is no auxiliary in the main sentence. Which auxiliary do you use for 3rd person singular present?
items.hd: Im Hauptsatz gibt es kein Hilfsverb. Welches Hilfsverb verwendest du für die 3. Person Einzahl im Präsens?
items.e: When there is no auxiliary in the main clause, we use do/does/did. 'Tom' = he (3rd person singular, present), so we need 'doesn't he'.
items.ed: Wenn es kein Hilfsverb im Hauptsatz gibt, verwenden wir do/does/did. 'Tom' = he (3. Person Einzahl, Präsens), also brauchen wir 'doesn't he'.

items.p: The children were playing outside, ___ ?
a: weren't they
items.h: 'The children' = they. Which auxiliary is in the main sentence?
items.hd: 'The children' = they. Welches Hilfsverb steht im Hauptsatz?
items.e: The auxiliary is 'were' and the subject 'the children' = 'they'. Positive statement needs a negative tag: 'weren't they'.
items.ed: Das Hilfsverb ist 'were' und das Subjekt 'the children' = 'they'. Ein positiver Satz braucht einen negativen Anhang: 'weren't they'.

items.p: You haven't finished your homework, ___ ?
a: have you
items.h: The sentence is negative ('haven't'). The tag must be the opposite.
items.hd: Der Satz ist negativ ('haven't'). Der Anhang muss das Gegenteil sein.
items.e: The sentence is negative ('haven't finished'), so the tag must be positive: 'have you'. We match the auxiliary 'have'.
items.ed: Der Satz ist negativ ('haven't finished'), also muss der Anhang positiv sein: 'have you'. Wir verwenden das Hilfsverb 'have'.

items.p: I am late, ___ ?
a: aren't I
items.h: This is a special case. 'I am...' uses a unique tag form. It's NOT 'amn't'.
items.hd: Das ist ein Sonderfall. 'I am...' verwendet eine besondere Form. Es ist NICHT 'amn't'.
items.e: 'I am' is a special case: the correct tag is 'aren't I?' (not 'amn't I', which doesn't exist in standard English).
items.ed: 'I am' ist ein Sonderfall: Der richtige Anhang ist 'aren't I?' ('amn't I' gibt es im Standardenglisch nicht).

items.p: Which sentence has the correct question tag?
items.c: She will come tomorrow, won't she?
a: She will come tomorrow, won't she?
ds: She will come tomorrow, will she?
She will come tomorrow, doesn't she?
She will come tomorrow, isn't she?
items.h: Look at the auxiliary verb in the main sentence. The tag must use the same auxiliary.
items.hd: Schau auf das Hilfsverb im Hauptsatz. Der Anhang muss das gleiche Hilfsverb verwenden.
items.e: The main clause uses 'will'. The tag must match: 'won't she?' (negative, matching 'will' and the pronoun 'she').
items.ed: Der Hauptsatz verwendet 'will'. Der Anhang muss passen: 'won't she?' (negativ, passend zu 'will' und dem Pronomen 'she').

items.p: Which sentence has the correct question tag?
items.c: Your parents went to Italy, didn't they?
a: Your parents went to Italy, didn't they?
ds: Your parents went to Italy, weren't they?
Your parents went to Italy, didn't he?
Your parents went to Italy, don't they?
items.h: 'Went' is past simple with no visible auxiliary. What auxiliary do you use in tags for past simple?
items.hd: 'Went' ist Past Simple ohne sichtbares Hilfsverb. Welches Hilfsverb verwendest du im Anhang für Past Simple?
items.e: 'Went' is past simple with no auxiliary, so we use 'did' in the tag. 'Your parents' = 'they'. Result: 'didn't they?'
items.ed: 'Went' ist Past Simple ohne Hilfsverb, also verwenden wir 'did' im Anhang. 'Your parents' = 'they'. Ergebnis: 'didn't they?'

items.p: Which sentence has the correct question tag?
a: Nobody called, did they?
items.h: 'Nobody' makes the sentence negative even though there's no 'not'. So the tag must be...?
items.hd: 'Nobody' macht den Satz negativ, auch wenn kein 'not' da ist. Also muss der Anhang...?
items.e: 'Nobody' is a negative word, so the sentence is negative. The tag must be positive: 'did they?' ('nobody' → 'they' in the tag).
items.ed: 'Nobody' ist ein negatives Wort, also ist der Satz negativ. Der Anhang muss positiv sein: 'did they?' ('nobody' → 'they' im Anhang).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She likes pizza, isn't she?
a: She likes pizza, doesn't she?
She likes pizza, doesn't she
She likes pizza doesn't she?
items.h: Look at the main verb 'likes'. Is there an auxiliary 'is' in the sentence?
items.hd: Schau dir das Hauptverb 'likes' an. Gibt es ein Hilfsverb 'is' im Satz?
items.e: There is no 'be' in the main clause — 'likes' is a main verb. We need do-support: 'doesn't she?' (3rd person singular present).
items.ed: Es gibt kein 'be' im Hauptsatz — 'likes' ist ein Vollverb. Wir brauchen do-Unterstützung: 'doesn't she?' (3. Person Einzahl Präsens).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The students have finished the test, haven't it?
items.c: The students have finished the test, haven't they?
a: The students have finished the test, haven't they?
The students have finished the test, haven't they
The students have finished the test haven't they?
items.h: What pronoun replaces 'the students'?
items.hd: Welches Pronomen ersetzt 'the students'?
items.e: 'The students' is plural, so the pronoun in the tag must be 'they', not 'it'. Correct: 'haven't they?'
items.ed: 'The students' ist Mehrzahl, also muss das Pronomen im Anhang 'they' sein, nicht 'it'. Richtig: 'haven't they?'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: You don't like maths, don't you?
items.c: You don't like maths, do you?
a: You don't like maths, do you?
You don't like maths, do you
You don't like maths do you?
items.h: The sentence is negative. Should the tag also be negative?
items.hd: Der Satz ist negativ. Soll der Anhang auch negativ sein?
items.e: The main clause is negative ('don't like'), so the tag must be positive: 'do you?' — not 'don't you?'. Tags always have opposite polarity.
items.ed: Der Hauptsatz ist negativ ('don't like'), also muss der Anhang positiv sein: 'do you?' — nicht 'don't you?'. Anhänge haben immer die gegenteilige Polarität.

items.p: You're checking something with your friend during a chat: 'He should study more, ___?'
items.c: He should study more, shouldn't he?
a: He should study more, shouldn't he?
He should study more, shouldn't he
He should study more shouldn't he?
items.h: What is the modal verb in the sentence? Make it negative for the tag.
items.hd: Was ist das Modalverb im Satz? Mache es negativ für den Anhang.
items.e: The modal is 'should'. Positive sentence → negative tag: 'shouldn't he?'
items.ed: Das Modalverb ist 'should'. Positiver Satz → negativer Anhang: 'shouldn't he?'

items.p: Add the correct question tag: Sarah doesn't eat meat.
items.c: Sarah doesn't eat meat, does she?
a: Sarah doesn't eat meat, does she?
Sarah doesn't eat meat, does she
Sarah doesn't eat meat does she?
items.h: The sentence is negative. What auxiliary and pronoun go in the positive tag?
items.hd: Der Satz ist negativ. Welches Hilfsverb und Pronomen kommen in den positiven Anhang?
items.e: The sentence is negative ('doesn't'), so the tag is positive: 'does she?' Sarah = she.
items.ed: Der Satz ist negativ ('doesn't'), also ist der Anhang positiv: 'does she?' Sarah = she.

items.p: Add the correct question tag: Let's go to the cinema.
items.c: Let's go to the cinema, shall we?
a: Let's go to the cinema, shall we?
Let's go to the cinema, shall we
Let's go to the cinema shall we?
items.h: After 'Let's...', there is a special question tag. Think: suggestion.
items.hd: Nach 'Let's...' gibt es einen besonderen Frageanhang. Denke: Vorschlag.
items.e: After 'Let's...', the correct tag is 'shall we?' This is a special case — 'Let's' is a suggestion, and 'shall we?' confirms it.
items.ed: Nach 'Let's...' lautet der richtige Anhang 'shall we?' Das ist ein Sonderfall — 'Let's' ist ein Vorschlag, und 'shall we?' bestätigt ihn.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Er ist nett, oder?
a: He is nice, isn't he?
He's nice, isn't he?
He is nice isn't he?
He's nice isn't he?
items.h: German uses 'oder?' for all question tags. In English you need to match the auxiliary.
items.hd: Im Deutschen verwendet man 'oder?' für alle Frageanhänge. Im Englischen muss man das Hilfsverb anpassen.
items.e: German uses 'oder?' for everything, but English needs the matching auxiliary: 'is' → 'isn't he?'
items.ed: Im Deutschen verwendet man 'oder?' für alles, aber im Englischen braucht man das passende Hilfsverb: 'is' → 'isn't he?'

items.p: 🇩🇪 Du spielst gern Tennis, oder?
items.c: You like playing tennis, don't you?
a: You like playing tennis, don't you?
You like to play tennis, don't you?
You enjoy playing tennis, don't you?
You like playing tennis don't you?
items.h: There is no auxiliary in the English main clause. Which auxiliary do you add?
items.hd: Es gibt kein Hilfsverb im englischen Hauptsatz. Welches Hilfsverb fügst du hinzu?
items.e: No auxiliary in the main clause → use 'do'. 'You' + present → 'don't you?'
items.ed: Kein Hilfsverb im Hauptsatz → verwende 'do'. 'You' + Präsens → 'don't you?'

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: she / can / swim / , / can't / she / ?
a: She can swim, can't she?
She can swim can't she?
items.h: The main sentence comes first, then a comma, then the tag.
items.hd: Der Hauptsatz kommt zuerst, dann ein Komma, dann der Anhang.
items.e: Structure: [subject + auxiliary + verb], [negative auxiliary + pronoun]? → 'She can swim, can't she?'
items.ed: Struktur: [Subjekt + Hilfsverb + Verb], [negatives Hilfsverb + Pronomen]? → 'She can swim, can't she?'

items.p: Match each sentence with the correct question tag. 1: She is tired 2: They went home 3: He can drive 4: You don't smoke 5: We should leave
items.h: Find the auxiliary (or use do/does/did if there is none) and flip the polarity.
items.hd: Finde das Hilfsverb (oder verwende do/does/did, wenn keines da ist) und drehe die Polarität um.
items.e: 'is' → 'isn't she?', no aux (past) → 'didn't they?', 'can' → 'can't he?', 'don't' → 'do you?', 'should' → 'shouldn't we?'
items.ed: 'is' → 'isn't she?', kein Hilfsverb (Verg.) → 'didn't they?', 'can' → 'can't he?', 'don't' → 'do you?', 'should' → 'shouldn't we?'

items.p: Form the complete sentence with the correct question tag: Your brother / has been / to London
items.c: Your brother has been to London, hasn't he?
a: Your brother has been to London, hasn't he?
Your brother has been to London hasn't he?
Your brother has been to London, hasn't he
items.h: First form the statement, then add the tag. What auxiliary and pronoun do you need?
items.hd: Bilde zuerst den Aussagesatz, dann füge den Anhang hinzu. Welches Hilfsverb und Pronomen brauchst du?
items.e: 'Has been' is the auxiliary. 'Your brother' = 'he'. Positive statement → negative tag: 'hasn't he?'
items.ed: 'Has been' ist das Hilfsverb. 'Your brother' = 'he'. Positiver Satz → negativer Anhang: 'hasn't he?'

items.p: The bus ___ (leave) at 7:30 every morning.
a: leaves
items.h: This is a regular timetable. Which tense do we use for fixed schedules?
items.hd: Das ist ein regulärer Fahrplan. Welche Zeitform verwenden wir für feste Zeitplaune?
items.e: For fixed timetables and schedules, we use present simple: 'The bus leaves at 7:30.'
items.ed: Für feste Fahrplaune und Zeitplaune verwenden wir das Present Simple: 'The bus leaves at 7:30.'

items.p: The film ___ (start) at 8 p.m. tonight.
a: starts
items.h: A film at the cinema has a fixed schedule. Which tense is used for that?
items.hd: Ein Film im Kino hat einen festen Zeitplan. Welche Zeitform wird dafür verwendet?
items.e: Cinema times are fixed schedules, so we use present simple: 'The film starts at 8 p.m.'
items.ed: Kinozeiten sind feste Zeitplaune, also verwenden wir das Present Simple: 'The film starts at 8 p.m.'

items.p: Our plane to London ___ (depart) at 6:15 tomorrow morning.
a: departs
items.h: Flight times are published schedules. What tense do we use?
items.hd: Flugzeiten sind veröffentlichte Fahrplaune. Welche Zeitform verwenden wir?
items.e: Flight departures are part of a fixed timetable, so we use present simple: 'departs'.
items.ed: Abflugzeiten sind Teil eines festen Fahrplans, also verwenden wir das Present Simple: 'departs'.

items.p: Choose the correct form: I ___ (visit) my grandparents this weekend. It's my plan.
a: am going to visit
'm going to visit
items.h: This is a personal plan, not a timetable. Which future form is for personal plans?
items.hd: Das ist ein persönlicher Plan, kein Fahrplan. Welche Zukunftsform ist für persönliche Plaune?
items.e: Personal plans use 'going to', not present simple. Present simple for future is ONLY for timetables and schedules.
items.ed: Persönliche Plaune verwenden 'going to', nicht Present Simple. Present Simple für die Zukunft ist NUR für Fahrplaune und Zeitplaune.

items.p: The museum ___ (not / open) on Mondays.
a: doesn't open
does not open
items.h: This is a permanent schedule rule. Which tense, and which form of 'do' for 'the museum'?
items.hd: Das ist eine feste Regel im Zeitplan. Welche Zeitform, und welche Form von 'do' für 'the museum'?
items.e: Fixed schedules use present simple. 'The museum' = 3rd person singular → 'doesn't open'.
items.ed: Feste Zeitplaune verwenden Present Simple. 'The museum' = 3. Person Einzahl → 'doesn't open'.

items.p: What time ___ the concert ___ (begin) on Saturday?
a: does ... begin
does...begin
does the concert begin
items.h: The concert has a fixed time. Form the present simple question for 3rd person singular.
items.hd: Das Konzert hat eine feste Uhrzeit. Bilde die Present-Simple-Frage für die 3. Person Einzahl.
items.e: Asking about a scheduled event → present simple question. 'The concert' = 3rd person singular → 'does ... begin'.
items.ed: Frage über ein geplantes Ereignis → Present-Simple-Frage. 'The concert' = 3. Person Einzahl → 'does ... begin'.

items.p: Which sentence correctly uses the present simple for a future event?
items.c: The shop closes at 9 p.m. today.
a: The shop closes at 9 p.m. today.
ds: I close the door when I leave.
The shop will close at 9 p.m. today.
I go shopping tomorrow with friends.
items.h: Present simple for the future is only for timetables and fixed schedules, not personal plans.
items.hd: Present Simple für die Zukunft gilt nur für Fahrplaune und feste Zeitplaune, nicht für persönliche Plaune.
items.e: 'The shop closes at 9 p.m.' is a fixed schedule → present simple is correct. Personal plans need 'going to'.
items.ed: 'The shop closes at 9 p.m.' ist ein fester Zeitplan → Present Simple ist richtig. Persönliche Plaune brauchen 'going to'.

items.p: Your friend says 'The concert is going to start at 7pm.' You check the printed schedule. What should you tell them?
items.c: The concert starts at 7pm — it's on the schedule.
a: The concert starts at 7pm — it's on the schedule.
ds: The concert will start at 7pm — I decided.
The concert is starting at 7pm — I arranged it.
The concert started at 7pm — it's in the past.
items.h: Think about the TYPE of future event. Is a train timetable a personal plan or a fixed schedule?
items.hd: Denke über die ART des zukünftigen Ereignisses nach. Ist ein Zugfahrplan ein persönlicher Plan oder ein fester Zeitplan?
items.e: Train arrivals are part of a fixed timetable. English uses present simple only for timetabled/scheduled future events, not for all future events.
items.ed: Zugankunfte sind Teil eines festen Fahrplans. Englisch verwendet Present Simple nur für fahrplanmäßige/geplante Zukunftsereignisse, nicht für alle.

items.p: Which sentence uses the WRONG future form?
items.c: The plane will depart at 14:30 tomorrow.
a: The plane will depart at 14:30 tomorrow.
ds: I'm going to study medicine at university.
items.h: One sentence uses 'will' for something that has a fixed timetable. Timetables don't use 'will'.
items.hd: Ein Satz verwendet 'will' für etwas mit festem Fahrplan. Fahrplaune verwenden kein 'will'.
items.e: Flight times are fixed timetables → present simple ('The plane departs at 14:30'), not 'will'. The other sentences use the correct future forms.
items.ed: Flugzeiten sind feste Fahrplaune → Present Simple ('The plane departs at 14:30'), nicht 'will'. Die anderen Sätze verwenden die richtigen Zukunftsformen.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The train will arrive at platform 3 at 10:15.
items.c: The train arrives at platform 3 at 10:15.
a: The train arrives at platform 3 at 10:15.
The train arrives at platform 3 at 10:15
The train arrives at platform three at 10:15.
items.h: Is a train arrival time a personal decision or a fixed timetable?
items.hd: Ist eine Zugankunftszeit eine persönliche Entscheidung oder ein fester Fahrplan?
items.e: Train schedules are fixed timetables. We use present simple, not 'will': 'The train arrives at 10:15.'
items.ed: Zugfahrplaune sind feste Zeitplaune. Wir verwenden Present Simple, nicht 'will': 'The train arrives at 10:15.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I play football with my friends tomorrow afternoon.
items.c: I am going to play football with my friends tomorrow afternoon.
a: I am going to play football with my friends tomorrow afternoon.
I'm going to play football with my friends tomorrow afternoon.
I'm playing football with my friends tomorrow afternoon.
I am playing football with my friends tomorrow afternoon.
items.h: Is playing football with friends a fixed schedule or a personal plan?
items.hd: Ist Fußballspielen mit Freunden ein fester Fahrplan oder ein persönlicher Plan?
items.e: Playing football with friends is a personal plan, not a timetable. Use 'going to' or present continuous, not present simple.
items.ed: Fußballspielen mit Freunden ist ein persönlicher Plan, kein Fahrplan. Verwende 'going to' oder Present Continuous, nicht Present Simple.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: School is going to start at 8 o'clock every day.
items.c: School starts at 8 o'clock every day.
a: School starts at 8 o'clock every day.
School starts at 8 o'clock every day
School starts at eight o'clock every day.
items.h: Is the school schedule a personal intention or a fixed timetable?
items.hd: Ist der Schulzeitplan eine persönliche Absicht oder ein fester Fahrplan?
items.e: School times are fixed schedules. We use present simple: 'School starts at 8 o'clock.' 'Going to' is for personal plans.
items.ed: Schulzeiten sind feste Zeitplaune. Wir verwenden Present Simple: 'School starts at 8 o'clock.' 'Going to' ist für persönliche Plaune.

items.p: You're at the airport checking your flight details. Tell your friend: 'Our plane ___ (depart) at 6:15 tomorrow morning.'
a: departs
items.h: Flights and timetables use present simple for scheduled future events.
items.hd: Flüge und Fahrpläne verwenden present simple für geplante zukünftige Ereignisse.
items.e: For timetables, replace 'will + base verb' with present simple: 'will leave' → 'leaves'.
items.ed: Für Fahrplaune ersetze 'will + Grundform' durch Present Simple: 'will leave' → 'leaves'.

items.p: Rewrite using the correct future form (personal plan): The party starts at 7. → I / go / to the party
items.c: I am going to go to the party.
a: I am going to go to the party.
I'm going to go to the party.
I am going to go to the party
I'm going to go to the party
items.h: This is YOUR plan, not a timetable. Which future form shows personal plans?
items.hd: Das ist DEIN Plan, kein Fahrplan. Welche Zukunftsform zeigt persönliche Plaune?
items.e: A personal plan uses 'going to': 'I am going to go to the party.' Present simple is only for timetables.
items.ed: Ein persönlicher Plan verwendet 'going to': 'I am going to go to the party.' Present Simple ist nur für Fahrplaune.

items.p: Ask the hotel receptionist about the bus schedule: 'What time ___ the bus ___ (leave) for the city centre?'
a: does ... leave
does the bus leave
items.h: Questions about timetables use present simple: do/does + subject + base form.
items.hd: Fragen über Fahrpläne verwenden present simple: do/does + Subjekt + Grundform.
items.e: For questions about schedules, use present simple: 'What time does the library close on Saturdays?'
items.ed: Für Fragen über Zeitplaune verwende Present Simple: 'What time does the library close on Saturdays?'

items.p: 🇩🇪 Der Zug fährt um 9 Uhr ab.
a: The train leaves at 9 o'clock.
The train leaves at 9.
The train departs at 9 o'clock.
The train departs at 9.
items.h: This is a timetable. Use present simple in English.
items.hd: Das ist ein Fahrplan. Verwende Present Simple im Englischen.
items.e: German uses present tense for future schedules too. In English, this is present simple: 'The train leaves at 9.'
items.ed: Deutsch verwendet auch das Präsens für zukünftige Fahrplaune. Im Englischen ist das Present Simple: 'The train leaves at 9.'

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wann beginnt die Vorstellung?
a: When does the show start?
When does the show begin?
What time does the show start?
What time does the show begin?
items.h: This is about a scheduled performance. Form a present simple question.
items.hd: Es geht um eine geplante Vorstellung. Bilde eine Present-Simple-Frage.
items.e: Asking about a scheduled event → present simple question: 'When does the show start?'
items.ed: Frage über ein geplantes Ereignis → Present-Simple-Frage: 'When does the show start?'

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: at / the / 3:15 / bus / leaves
a: The bus leaves at 3:15.
The bus leaves at 3:15
items.h: Start with the subject (the bus), then the verb, then the time.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Subjekt (the bus), dann das Verb, dann die Uhrzeit.
items.e: Standard English word order for timetable sentences: Subject + verb + time → 'The bus leaves at 3:15.'
items.ed: Standardreihenfolge im Englischen für Fahrplansätze: Subjekt + Verb + Zeit → 'The bus leaves at 3:15.'

items.p: Match each situation with the correct future form. 1: The bus / leave / at 8 a.m. 2: I / visit / my aunt next week 3: Look at those clouds! It / rain 4: The concert / start / at 7 p.m. 5: I think they / win / the match
items.h: Timetables → present simple. Personal plans → going to. Predictions → will.
items.hd: Fahrplaune → Present Simple. Persönliche Plaune → going to. Vorhersagen → will.
items.e: Bus/concert = timetables → present simple. Visiting aunt = plan → going to. Clouds/rain = evidence → going to. 'I think' = prediction → will.
items.ed: Bus/Konzert = Fahrplaune → Present Simple. Tante besuchen = Plan → going to. Wolken/Regen = Beweis → going to. 'I think' = Vorhersage → will.

items.p: You're looking at a cinema schedule. The 8:30 showing is the only evening option. Which sentence is correct?
a: The film starts at 8:30.
ds: The film is going to start at 8:30.
items.h: Cinema schedules are fixed timetables. Which future form is used for timetables?
items.hd: Kinoprogramme sind feste Fahrpläne. Welche Zukunftsform verwendet man für Fahrpläne?
items.e: Present simple is used for scheduled events and timetables: trains, flights, cinemas, school schedules.
items.ed: Present simple wird für geplante Ereignisse und Fahrpläne verwendet: Züge, Flüge, Kino, Schulpläne.

items.p: I want you ___ help me with my homework.
a: to
items.h: After 'want + person', which little word comes before the verb?
items.hd: Nach 'want + Person', welches kleine Wort kommt vor dem Verb?
items.e: The pattern is: want + person + TO + base verb. 'I want you to help me.' Not 'want that you help' (German pattern).
items.ed: Das Muster ist: want + Person + TO + Grundform. 'I want you to help me.' Nicht 'want that you help' (deutsches Muster).

items.p: She told ___ to sit down and be quiet.
a: him
items.h: 'Tell' needs an object pronoun (me, you, him, her, us, them) before 'to'.
items.hd: 'Tell' braucht ein Objektpronomen (me, you, him, her, us, them) vor 'to'.
items.e: The pattern is: tell + OBJECT PRONOUN + to + verb. 'She told him to sit down.' Use 'him', not 'he'.
items.ed: Das Muster ist: tell + OBJEKTPRONOMEN + to + Verb. 'She told him to sit down.' Verwende 'him', nicht 'he'.

items.p: My parents want me ___ (come) home before nine.
a: to come
items.h: After 'want + person', always use 'to' + base verb.
items.hd: Nach 'want + Person' verwende immer 'to' + Grundform.
items.e: 'Want + person + to + base verb': My parents want me to come home. The German 'dass'-clause doesn't work here.
items.ed: 'Want + Person + to + Grundform': My parents want me to come home. Die deutsche 'dass'-Konstruktion funktioniert hier nicht.

items.p: The teacher asked us ___ (not / talk) during the test.
a: not to talk
items.h: To make this pattern negative, put 'not' BEFORE 'to': ask + person + not to + verb.
items.hd: Um dieses Muster zu verneinen, stelle 'not' VOR 'to': ask + Person + not to + Verb.
items.e: Negative pattern: ask + person + NOT TO + verb. 'The teacher asked us not to talk.' The 'not' comes before 'to'.
items.ed: Verneinungsmuster: ask + Person + NOT TO + Verb. 'The teacher asked us not to talk.' Das 'not' kommt vor 'to'.

items.p: Do you want ___ (I / carry) your bag for you?
a: me to carry
items.h: Change 'I' to the object form, then add 'to' + base verb.
items.hd: Ändere 'I' zur Objektform, dann füge 'to' + Grundform hinzu.
items.e: 'Want + object pronoun + to + verb': Do you want me to carry your bag? 'I' becomes 'me' (object form).
items.ed: 'Want + Objektpronomen + to + Verb': Do you want me to carry your bag? 'I' wird zu 'me' (Objektform).

items.p: My mum doesn't want ___ (we / stay) out late, but my dad ___ (let / we / go) to the party.
items.c: us to stay ... lets us go
a: us to stay ... lets us go
ds: us to stay ... lets us to go
that we stay ... lets us go
items.h: 'Want + person + TO + verb' but 'let + person + verb' (no 'to'). Which pattern goes where?
items.hd: 'Want + Person + TO + Verb' aber 'let + Person + Verb' (ohne 'to'). Welches Muster passt wo?
items.e: 'Want' needs 'to': 'doesn't want us to stay'. 'Let' takes the bare infinitive: 'lets us go' (no 'to').
items.ed: 'Want' braucht 'to': 'doesn't want us to stay'. 'Let' nimmt den reinen Infinitiv: 'lets us go' (ohne 'to').

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: The coach told them to run faster.
a: The coach told them to run faster.
ds: The coach told that they run faster.
The coach told them run faster.
The coach told to them to run faster.
items.h: The pattern is: tell + person (object) + to + base verb.
items.hd: Das Muster ist: tell + Person (Objekt) + to + Grundform.
items.e: 'Tell + object + to + verb': The coach told them to run faster. No 'that', no extra 'to' before the object.
items.ed: 'Tell + Objekt + to + Verb': The coach told them to run faster. Kein 'that', kein extra 'to' vor dem Objekt.

items.p: Which sentence correctly uses the negative form?
items.c: She asked him not to be late.
a: She asked him not to be late.
ds: She asked him to not be late.
She asked him don't be late.
She asked that he not be late.
items.h: For the negative, put 'not' BEFORE 'to': ask + person + not to + verb.
items.hd: Für die Verneinung stelle 'not' VOR 'to': ask + Person + not to + Verb.
items.e: Negative pattern: ask + person + NOT TO + verb. 'She asked him not to be late.' The 'not' comes before 'to'.
items.ed: Verneinungsmuster: ask + Person + NOT TO + Verb. 'She asked him not to be late.' Das 'not' kommt vor 'to'.

items.p: Which sentence is NOT correct?
items.c: I want that she comes to my party.
a: I want that she comes to my party.
ds: I want her to come to my party.
I asked her to come to my party.
I told her to come to my party.
items.h: One sentence uses a German-influenced structure with 'that'. Is that correct in English?
items.hd: Ein Satz verwendet eine vom Deutschen beeinflusste Struktur mit 'that'. Ist das im Englischen korrekt?
items.e: 'I want that she comes' is wrong — it's a direct transfer from German 'Ich will, dass sie kommt'. The correct English pattern is 'I want her to come'.
items.ed: 'I want that she comes' ist falsch — es ist eine direkte Übertragung vom Deutschen 'Ich will, dass sie kommt'. Das korrekte englische Muster ist 'I want her to come'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I want that you help me with this project.
items.c: I want you to help me with this project.
a: I want you to help me with this project.
I want you to help me with this project
I'd like you to help me with this project.
items.h: In English, 'want' doesn't use a 'that'-clause. What pattern does it use instead?
items.hd: Im Englischen verwendet 'want' keinen 'dass'-Satz. Welches Muster verwendet es stattdessen?
items.e: 'Want that you help' is a German transfer from 'Ich will, dass du hilfst.' Correct English: 'want + person + to + verb' = 'I want you to help me.'
items.ed: 'Want that you help' ist eine Übertragung vom Deutschen 'Ich will, dass du hilfst.' Korrektes Englisch: 'want + Person + to + Verb' = 'I want you to help me.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: My parents let me to go out on Saturday evenings.
items.c: My parents let me go out on Saturday evenings.
a: My parents let me go out on Saturday evenings.
My parents let me go out on Saturday evenings
My parents let me go out on Saturdays.
items.h: 'Let' is different from 'want/tell/ask'. Does 'let' need 'to' before the verb?
items.hd: 'Let' unterscheidet sich von 'want/tell/ask'. Braucht 'let' ein 'to' vor dem Verb?
items.e: 'Let' takes the bare infinitive (no 'to'): 'let me go', not 'let me to go'. Don't confuse it with 'want me to go'.
items.ed: 'Let' nimmt den reinen Infinitiv (ohne 'to'): 'let me go', nicht 'let me to go'. Nicht verwechseln mit 'want me to go'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She told to him not leave early.
items.c: She told him not to leave early.
a: She told him not to leave early.
She told him not to leave early
She told him to not leave early.
items.h: 'Tell' takes a direct object (no 'to' before the person). The negative infinitive pattern is: not to + verb.
items.hd: 'Tell' nimmt ein direktes Objekt (kein 'to' vor der Person). Das negative Infinitivmuster ist: not to + Verb.
items.e: Two errors: 1) 'told to him' should be 'told him' (direct object, no preposition). 2) 'not leave' should be 'not to leave' (negative to-infinitive).
items.ed: Zwei Fehler: 1) 'told to him' sollte 'told him' sein (direktes Objekt, keine Präposition). 2) 'not leave' sollte 'not to leave' sein (negativer to-Infinitiv).

items.p: Your little brother is noisy. Tell your mum what you want: 'I want him ___ (be) quiet!'
a: to be
items.h: want + object (him) + to-infinitive
items.hd: want + Objekt (him) + to-Infinitiv
items.e: German 'Ich will, dass du leise bist' = English 'I want you to be quiet.' No 'that' in English — use the to-infinitive pattern.
items.ed: Deutsch 'Ich will, dass du leise bist' = Englisch 'I want you to be quiet.' Kein 'that' im Englischen — verwende das to-Infinitiv-Muster.

items.p: The teacher gave instructions. Tell your friend: 'She told us ___ (not / use) our phones in class.'
a: not to use
items.h: tell + object + not + to-infinitive for negative reported commands.
items.hd: tell + Objekt + not + to-Infinitiv für negative indirekte Aufforderungen.
items.e: Direct command 'Clean your room!' becomes 'Mum told Tim to clean his room.' Pattern: told + object + to + verb.
items.ed: Der direkte Befehl 'Clean your room!' wird zu 'Mum told Tim to clean his room.' Muster: told + Objekt + to + Verb.

items.p: You missed a message from your mum. Your friend says: 'Your mum asked you ___ (not / be) late for dinner.'
a: not to be
items.h: ask + object + not + to-infinitive
items.hd: ask + Objekt + not + to-Infinitiv
items.e: A polite negative request: 'asked us not to use our phones'. Negative infinitive = not to + verb.
items.ed: Eine höfliche negative Bitte: 'asked us not to use our phones'. Negativer Infinitiv = not to + Verb.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ich will, dass du mir zuhoerst.
items.c: I want you to listen to me.
a: I want you to listen to me.
I want you to listen to me
I'd like you to listen to me.
I want you to listen.
items.h: Don't translate 'dass' with 'that'. Use: want + person + to + verb.
items.hd: Übersetze 'dass' nicht mit 'that'. Verwende: want + Person + to + Verb.
items.e: 'Ich will, dass du mir zuhoerst' = 'I want you to listen to me.' The German 'dass' disappears — English uses the to-infinitive pattern.
items.ed: 'Ich will, dass du mir zuhörst' = 'I want you to listen to me.' Das deutsche 'dass' verschwindet — Englisch verwendet das to-Infinitiv-Muster.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Er bat mich, ihm bei den Hausaufgaben zu helfen.
items.c: He asked me to help him with his homework.
a: He asked me to help him with his homework.
He asked me to help him with his homework
He asked me to help him with the homework.
He asked me to help with his homework.
items.h: Use: asked + person + to + verb. 'Bat mich' = 'asked me'.
items.hd: Verwende: asked + Person + to + Verb. 'Bat mich' = 'asked me'.
items.e: 'Er bat mich, ihm zu helfen' = 'He asked me to help him.' Pattern: ask + object + to + verb.
items.ed: 'Er bat mich, ihm zu helfen' = 'He asked me to help him.' Muster: ask + Objekt + to + Verb.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: wants / me / to / mum / tidy / my / my / room
items.c: My mum wants me to tidy my room.
a: My mum wants me to tidy my room.
My mum wants me to tidy my room
items.h: Start with the subject ('My mum'), then the verb ('wants'), then object + to + verb.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Subjekt ('My mum'), dann das Verb ('wants'), dann Objekt + to + Verb.
items.e: Subject + wants + object + to + verb + rest: 'My mum wants me to tidy my room.'
items.ed: Subjekt + wants + Objekt + to + Verb + Rest: 'My mum wants me to tidy my room.'

items.p: Match the sentence beginnings with the correct endings. 1: I want you 2: She told him 3: They asked us 4: My parents let me 5: He wants her
ds: b: stay up late on Fridays.
items.h: Remember: want/tell/ask need 'to', but 'let' takes the bare infinitive (no 'to').
items.hd: Denk daran: want/tell/ask brauchen 'to', aber 'let' nimmt den reinen Infinitiv (ohne 'to').
items.e: 1+d, 2+a, 3+e, 5+c all use 'to + verb'. 4+b uses bare infinitive after 'let' (stay, not to stay).
items.ed: 1+d, 2+a, 3+e, 5+c verwenden alle 'to + Verb'. 4+b verwendet den reinen Infinitiv nach 'let' (stay, nicht to stay).

items.p: Your friend wants to know if you need help. Form the question starting with: Do you want...
items.c: Do you want me to help you?
a: Do you want me to help you?
Do you want me to help you
Do you want me to help?
items.h: Use: Do you want + object pronoun + to + base verb?
items.hd: Verwende: Do you want + Objektpronomen + to + Grundform?
items.e: Question with 'want + person + to': 'Do you want me to help you?' The pattern stays the same in questions.
items.ed: Frage mit 'want + Person + to': 'Do you want me to help you?' Das Muster bleibt in Fragen gleich.

items.p: I ___ (visit) my grandparents last weekend.
a: visited
items.h: 'Last weekend' is a finished past time expression. Which tense goes with finished past times?
items.hd: 'Last weekend' ist ein abgeschlossener Zeitausdruck der Vergangenheit. Welche Zeitform passt zu abgeschlossenen Vergangenheitszeiten?
items.e: 'Last weekend' = finished past time → past simple: 'visited'. Never use present perfect with 'last...'.
items.ed: 'Last weekend' = abgeschlossene Vergangenheit → Past Simple: 'visited'. Verwende nie Present Perfect mit 'last...'.

items.p: She has ___ finished her homework.
a: already
just
items.h: Which time word works with present perfect ('has finished')? Look for words that mean 'before now'.
items.hd: Welches Zeitwort funktioniert mit Present Perfect ('has finished')? Suche nach Wörtern, die 'vor jetzt' bedeuten.
items.e: 'Already' (or 'just') signals present perfect: 'She has already finished.' Words like 'yesterday' or 'ago' need past simple.
items.ed: 'Already' (oder 'just') signalisiert Present Perfect: 'She has already finished.' Wörter wie 'yesterday' oder 'ago' brauchen Past Simple.

items.p: We ___ (live) in Vienna for ten years.
a: have lived
've lived
items.h: 'For + time period' (and still true now) requires which tense?
items.hd: 'For + Zeitraum' (und immer noch wahr) braucht welche Zeitform?
items.e: 'For ten years' (still living there) → present perfect: 'have lived'. German uses present tense with 'seit', but English needs present perfect.
items.ed: 'For ten years' (wohnen immer noch dort) → Present Perfect: 'have lived'. Deutsch verwendet Präsens mit 'seit', aber Englisch braucht Present Perfect.

items.p: I ___ (not / see) Tom since Monday.
a: haven't seen
have not seen
items.h: 'Since + point in time' always needs present perfect.
items.hd: 'Since + Zeitpunkt' braucht immer Present Perfect.
items.e: 'Since Monday' = from that point until now → present perfect: 'haven't seen'. 'Since' always requires present perfect.
items.ed: 'Since Monday' = von diesem Zeitpunkt bis jetzt → Present Perfect: 'haven't seen'. 'Since' braucht immer Present Perfect.

items.p: She ___ (move) to London two years ago, and she ___ (live) there ever since.
a: moved ... has lived
moved ... 's lived
items.h: 'Two years ago' → which tense? 'Ever since' → which tense? Each time expression tells you which tense to use.
items.hd: 'Two years ago' → welche Zeitform? 'Ever since' → welche Zeitform? Jeder Zeitausdruck sagt dir, welche Zeitform du verwenden sollst.
items.e: 'Ago' → past simple: 'moved'. 'Ever since' → present perfect: 'has lived'. Each time expression demands its own tense.
items.ed: 'Ago' → Past Simple: 'moved'. 'Ever since' → Present Perfect: 'has lived'. Jeder Zeitausdruck verlangt seine eigene Zeitform.

items.p: ___ you ever ___ (try) bungee jumping? — Yes, I ___ (do) it in Australia in 2023.
a: Have ... tried ... did
items.h: 'Ever' (experience, no specific time) → present perfect. 'In 2023' (specific year) → past simple.
items.hd: 'Ever' (Erfahrung, keine bestimmte Zeit) → Present Perfect. 'In 2023' (bestimmtes Jahr) → Past Simple.
items.e: 'Have you ever tried' (experience = present perfect). 'I did it in 2023' (specific time = past simple).
items.ed: 'Have you ever tried' (Erfahrung = Present Perfect). 'I did it in 2023' (bestimmte Zeit = Past Simple).

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
a: I have never eaten sushi.
items.h: 'Never' describes a life experience (up to now). Which tense expresses experience?
items.hd: 'Never' beschreibt eine Lebenserfahrung (bis jetzt). Welche Zeitform drückt Erfahrung aus?
items.e: 'Never' for life experience → present perfect: 'have never eaten'. Use the past participle (eaten, not ate) after 'have'.
items.ed: 'Never' für Lebenserfahrung → Present Perfect: 'have never eaten'. Verwende das Past Participle (eaten, nicht ate) nach 'have'.

items.p: Which time expression can NOT be used with present perfect?
a: yesterday
items.h: Which of these words refers to a specific, finished day in the past?
items.hd: Welches dieser Wörter bezieht sich auf einen bestimmten, abgeschlossenen Tag in der Vergangenheit?
items.e: 'Yesterday' is a finished past time → it needs past simple. 'Already', 'just', and 'recently' all work with present perfect.
items.ed: 'Yesterday' ist eine abgeschlossene Vergangenheit → es braucht Past Simple. 'Already', 'just' und 'recently' funktionieren alle mit Present Perfect.

items.p: Choose the correct pair: I ___ here ___ 2019.
a: have lived ... since
items.h: '2019' is a point in time (for/since?). And which tense goes with that word?
items.hd: '2019' ist ein Zeitpunkt (for/since?). Und welche Zeitform passt zu diesem Wort?
items.e: 'Since' + point in time (2019) + present perfect: 'I have lived here since 2019.' German uses present tense with 'seit', but English needs present perfect.
items.ed: 'Since' + Zeitpunkt (2019) + Present Perfect: 'I have lived here since 2019.' Deutsch verwendet Präsens mit 'seit', aber Englisch braucht Present Perfect.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I have been to Paris last summer.
items.c: I went to Paris last summer.
a: I went to Paris last summer.
I went to Paris last summer
I was in Paris last summer.
items.h: 'Last summer' is a finished past time. Can you use present perfect with it?
items.hd: 'Last summer' ist eine abgeschlossene Vergangenheit. Kannst du Present Perfect damit verwenden?
items.e: 'Last summer' = finished past time → past simple required. 'I went to Paris last summer.' Present perfect doesn't work with 'last...'.
items.ed: 'Last summer' = abgeschlossene Vergangenheit → Past Simple nötig. 'I went to Paris last summer.' Present Perfect funktioniert nicht mit 'last...'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I live in this house since five years.
items.c: I have lived in this house for five years.
a: I have lived in this house for five years.
I have lived in this house for five years
I've lived in this house for five years.
items.h: Two problems: 1) German uses present tense with 'seit', but English doesn't. 2) Is 'five years' a point in time or a duration?
items.hd: Zwei Probleme: 1) Deutsch verwendet Präsens mit 'seit', aber Englisch nicht. 2) Ist 'five years' ein Zeitpunkt oder eine Zeitspanne?
items.e: Two errors: 1) English needs present perfect with 'since/for' (not present simple like German 'seit'). 2) 'Five years' is a duration → 'for', not 'since'.
items.ed: Zwei Fehler: 1) Englisch braucht Present Perfect mit 'since/for' (nicht Präsens wie deutsches 'seit'). 2) 'Five years' ist eine Zeitspanne → 'for', nicht 'since'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Did you ever eat insects?
a: Have you ever eaten insects?
Have you ever eaten insects
Have you ever tried insects?
items.h: 'Ever' asks about life experience (any time in your life). Which tense expresses experience?
items.hd: 'Ever' fragt nach Lebenserfahrung (irgendwann im Leben). Welche Zeitform drückt Erfahrung aus?
items.e: 'Ever' in experience questions → present perfect: 'Have you ever eaten?' Not past simple 'Did you ever eat?'.
items.ed: 'Ever' in Erfahrungsfragen → Present Perfect: 'Have you ever eaten?' Nicht Past Simple 'Did you ever eat?'.

items.p: Your friend asks how long you've lived here. Answer: 'I ___ (live) here ___ ten years.'
a: have lived ... for
have lived, for
've lived ... for
items.h: A duration (ten years) needs 'for'. Which tense goes with 'for' + duration?
items.hd: Eine Zeitdauer (ten years) braucht 'for'. Welche Zeitform passt zu 'for' + Dauer?
items.e: Started in 2020, still continuing → present perfect + 'for' + duration: 'She has learned English for four years.'
items.ed: Begann 2020, dauert noch an → Present Perfect + 'for' + Zeitspanne: 'She has learned English for four years.'

items.p: Tell your pen pal when you last saw Tom: 'I ___ (not / see) Tom ___ last Monday.'
a: haven't seen ... since
have not seen ... since
items.h: 'Last Monday' is a specific point in time. Which word connects present perfect to a starting point?
items.hd: 'Last Monday' ist ein bestimmter Zeitpunkt. Welches Wort verbindet present perfect mit einem Startpunkt?
items.e: 'I last ate' → 'I haven't eaten since Monday.' Present perfect negative + 'since' + point in time.
items.ed: 'I last ate' → 'I haven't eaten since Monday.' Present Perfect verneint + 'since' + Zeitpunkt.

items.p: Your mum asks about your project. Tell her: 'I ___ already ___ (finish) it, but Anna ___ (not / start) hers ___.'
items.c: have already finished ... hasn't started ... yet
a: have already finished ... hasn't started ... yet
've already finished ... hasn't started ... yet
items.h: 'Already' goes with completed actions. 'Yet' goes with negative sentences about expected actions.
items.hd: 'Already' steht bei abgeschlossenen Handlungen. 'Yet' steht bei verneinten Sätzen über erwartete Handlungen.
items.e: 'Already' in affirmative: 'has already finished'. 'Yet' in negative: 'hasn't finished ... yet'. Both need present perfect.
items.ed: 'Already' in bejahenden Sätzen: 'has already finished'. 'Yet' in verneinten Sätzen: 'hasn't finished ... yet'. Beide brauchen Present Perfect.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ich lebe seit drei Jahren in Graz.
items.c: I have lived in Graz for three years.
a: I have lived in Graz for three years.
I've lived in Graz for three years.
I have been living in Graz for three years.
I've been living in Graz for three years.
items.h: Careful! German uses present tense with 'seit', but English uses present perfect. 'Drei Jahren' = duration → for.
items.hd: Achtung! Deutsch verwendet Präsens mit 'seit', aber Englisch verwendet Present Perfect. 'Drei Jahren' = Zeitspanne → for.
