items.e: German 'Ich lebe seit...' = English 'I have lived for...' Two traps: 1) present tense → present perfect, 2) 'seit' + duration → 'for'.
items.ed: Deutsch 'Ich lebe seit...' = Englisch 'I have lived for...' Zwei Fallen: 1) Präsens → Present Perfect, 2) 'seit' + Zeitspanne → 'for'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Hast du schon einmal Sushi gegessen?
a: Have you ever eaten sushi?
Have you ever eaten sushi
Have you ever tried sushi?
Have you ever had sushi?
items.h: 'Schon einmal' = 'ever'. Which tense do we use with 'ever' for life experience?
items.hd: 'Schon einmal' = 'ever'. Welche Zeitform verwenden wir mit 'ever' für Lebenserfahrung?
items.e: 'Schon einmal' = 'ever' → present perfect: 'Have you ever eaten sushi?' Not past simple (Did you ever eat...).
items.ed: 'Schon einmal' = 'ever' → Present Perfect: 'Have you ever eaten sushi?' Nicht Past Simple (Did you ever eat...).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: hasn't / yet / she / replied / to / email / the
items.c: She hasn't replied to the email yet.
a: She hasn't replied to the email yet.
She hasn't replied to the email yet
items.h: Subject first, then 'hasn't', then the past participle, then the rest. Where does 'yet' go?
items.hd: Subjekt zuerst, dann 'hasn't', dann das Past Participle, dann der Rest. Wo steht 'yet'?
items.e: Present perfect negative + 'yet' at the end: 'She hasn't replied to the email yet.'
items.ed: Present Perfect verneint + 'yet' am Ende: 'She hasn't replied to the email yet.'

items.p: Match each time expression with the correct tense. 1: yesterday 2: already 3: two weeks ago 4: since Monday 5: just 6: last summer
items.h: Past simple goes with finished past times (yesterday, ago, last...). Present perfect goes with words connected to now (already, since, just).
items.hd: Past Simple passt zu abgeschlossenen Vergangenheitszeiten (yesterday, ago, last...). Present Perfect passt zu Wörtern, die mit der Gegenwart verbunden sind (already, since, just).
items.e: Past simple: yesterday, two weeks ago, last summer (finished past). Present perfect: already, since Monday, just (connected to now).
items.ed: Past Simple: yesterday, two weeks ago, last summer (abgeschlossene Vergangenheit). Present Perfect: already, since Monday, just (mit der Gegenwart verbunden).

items.p: Tom started learning English in 2020. He's still learning now. Which sentence is correct?
items.c: He has been learning English since 2020.
a: He has been learning English since 2020.
items.h: He started in 2020 and is STILL learning. Which tense shows an action that started in the past and continues now?
items.hd: Er begann 2020 und lernt NOCH. Welche Zeitform zeigt eine Handlung, die in der Vergangenheit begann und jetzt weitergeht?
items.e: Present perfect (continuous) with 'since' + point in time shows an action continuing from the past to now.
items.ed: Present perfect (continuous) mit 'since' + Zeitpunkt zeigt eine Handlung, die von der Vergangenheit bis jetzt andauert.

items.p: It's cloudy. It ___ rain later.
a: might
may
could
items.h: The speaker thinks rain is possible but not certain. Which modal expresses possibility?
items.hd: Der Sprecher denkt, Regen ist möglich, aber nicht sicher. Welches Modalverb drückt Möglichkeit aus?
items.e: 'Might/may/could' express possibility (it's possible but uncertain). 'Can' is too general, 'must' is too strong, 'will' is too certain.
items.ed: 'Might/may/could' drücken Möglichkeit aus (es ist möglich, aber unsicher). 'Can' ist zu allgemein, 'must' ist zu stark, 'will' ist zu sicher.

items.p: He's been studying all day. He ___ be very tired.
a: must
items.h: Studying all day makes tiredness almost certain. Which modal means 'I'm almost sure'?
items.hd: Den ganzen Tag lernen macht Müdigkeit fast sicher. Welches Modalverb bedeutet 'ich bin fast sicher'?
items.e: 'Must' for logical deduction (near-certainty based on evidence): He's been studying → he must be tired.
items.ed: 'Must' für logische Schlussfolgerung (fast sicher aufgrund von Beweisen): Er hat den ganzen Tag gelernt → he must be tired.

items.p: That ___ be Sarah — she's on holiday in Spain!
a: can't
cannot
items.h: Sarah is in Spain, so it's IMPOSSIBLE that she's here. Which modal means 'it's impossible'?
items.hd: Sarah ist in Spanien, also ist es UNMÖGLICH, dass sie hier ist. Welches Modalverb bedeutet 'es ist unmöglich'?
items.e: 'Can't' for logical impossibility: She's in Spain → That can't be Sarah. NOT 'mustn't' (= not allowed).
items.ed: 'Can't' für logische Unmöglichkeit: Sie ist in Spanien → That can't be Sarah. NICHT 'mustn't' (= nicht erlaubt).

items.p: She ___ be at the library — I'm not sure where she is.
a: might
may
could
items.h: 'I'm not sure' tells you the speaker is uncertain. Which modals express uncertainty?
items.hd: 'I'm not sure' zeigt, dass der Sprecher unsicher ist. Welche Modalverben drücken Unsicherheit aus?
items.e: 'Might/may/could' = possible but uncertain. 'I'm not sure' confirms the speaker is guessing, so use a possibility modal.
items.ed: 'Might/may/could' = möglich, aber unsicher. 'I'm not sure' bestätigt, dass der Sprecher ratet, also verwende ein Möglichkeits-Modalverb.

items.p: The restaurant ___ be good — it's always full of people.
a: must
items.h: 'Always full of people' gives strong evidence. Which modal means 'I'm almost certain based on evidence'?
items.hd: 'Immer voller Leute' liefert starke Beweise. Welches Modalverb bedeutet 'ich bin fast sicher aufgrund von Beweisen'?
items.e: Strong evidence (always full) → logical deduction → 'must': The restaurant must be good.
items.ed: Starke Beweise (immer voll) → logische Schlussfolgerung → 'must': The restaurant must be good.

items.p: A: Where's Tom? B: He ___ be at football practice, or he ___ be at his friend's house — I really don't know.
a: could ... could
might ... might
may ... may
could ... might
might ... could
items.h: The speaker gives two equal possibilities and admits 'I really don't know'. Use the same modal for both.
items.hd: Der Sprecher gibt zwei gleiche Möglichkeiten und gibt zu 'I really don't know'. Verwende dasselbe Modalverb für beide.
items.e: Two equal possibilities with 'I don't know' → could/might/may for both: 'He could be at practice or he could be at his friend's.'
items.ed: Zwei gleiche Möglichkeiten mit 'I don't know' → could/might/may für beide: 'He could be at practice or he could be at his friend's.'

items.p: Your friend hasn't eaten all day. Which sentence expresses logical deduction?
a: He must be really hungry.
items.h: Strong evidence (hasn't eaten all day) leads to near-certainty. Which modal is the strongest for deduction?
items.hd: Starke Beweise (hat den ganzen Tag nichts gegessen) führen zu fast Sicherheit. Welches Modalverb ist das stärkste für Schlussfolgerungen?
items.e: 'Must' for logical deduction: strong evidence → near-certain conclusion. 'He must be hungry' (= I'm almost sure).
items.ed: 'Must' für logische Schlussfolgerung: starke Beweise → fast sichere Schlussfolgerung. 'He must be hungry' (= ich bin fast sicher).

items.p: Tim says he ran 100 metres in 8 seconds. That ___!
a: can't be true
items.h: 8 seconds for 100m is impossible (the world record is about 9.5 seconds). Which modal says 'impossible'?
items.hd: 8 Sekunden für 100m ist unmöglich (der Weltrekord liegt bei etwa 9,5 Sekunden). Welches Modalverb sagt 'unmöglich'?
items.e: 'Can't' for impossibility: That can't be true (= it's logically impossible). 'Mustn't' means 'not allowed', which is different.
items.ed: 'Can't' für Unmöglichkeit: That can't be true (= es ist logisch unmöglich). 'Mustn't' bedeutet 'nicht erlaubt', was etwas anderes ist.

items.p: Put the modals in order from MOST certain to LEAST certain:
a: must → may → might → can't
items.h: Think of a scale: which means 'almost certain'? Which means 'possible'? Which means 'impossible'?
items.hd: Denk an eine Skala: welches bedeutet 'fast sicher'? Welches bedeutet 'möglich'? Welches bedeutet 'unmöglich'?
items.e: Certainty scale: must (almost certain) → may (possible, slightly more formal) → might (possible, less sure) → can't (impossible).
items.ed: Sicherheitsskala: must (fast sicher) → may (möglich, etwas formeller) → might (möglich, weniger sicher) → can't (unmöglich).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: It can rain tomorrow, so take an umbrella.
items.c: It might rain tomorrow, so take an umbrella.
a: It might rain tomorrow, so take an umbrella.
It may rain tomorrow, so take an umbrella.
It could rain tomorrow, so take an umbrella.
items.h: 'Can' is for ability or general possibility. For a specific future possibility, use a different modal.
items.hd: 'Can' ist für Fähigkeit oder allgemeine Möglichkeit. Für eine bestimmte zukünftige Möglichkeit verwende ein anderes Modalverb.
items.e: 'Can' is for general possibility ('Accidents can happen'). For specific-situation possibility, use 'might/may/could': 'It might rain tomorrow.'
items.ed: 'Can' ist für allgemeine Möglichkeit ('Accidents can happen'). Für spezifische Möglichkeit verwende 'might/may/could': 'It might rain tomorrow.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She must not be his sister — she looks completely different.
items.c: She can't be his sister — she looks completely different.
a: She can't be his sister — she looks completely different.
She can't be his sister - she looks completely different.
She cannot be his sister — she looks completely different.
items.h: The speaker thinks it's IMPOSSIBLE that she is his sister. 'Must not' means something different from 'can't'. What does 'must not' actually mean?
items.hd: Der Sprecher denkt, es ist UNMÖGLICH, dass sie seine Schwester ist. 'Must not' bedeutet etwas anderes als 'can't'. Was bedeutet 'must not' eigentlich?
items.e: 'Must not' = it is forbidden / not allowed. 'Can't' = it's impossible (logical deduction). Looking different → it's impossible → 'can't be'.
items.ed: 'Must not' = es ist verboten / nicht erlaubt. 'Can't' = es ist unmöglich (logische Schlussfolgerung). Sieht anders aus → es ist unmöglich → 'can't be'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She might to come to the party later.
items.c: She might come to the party later.
a: She might come to the party later.
She might come to the party later
She may come to the party later.
items.h: Modal verbs like might, may, could, must are always followed directly by the base verb. No extra word needed!
items.hd: Modalverben wie might, may, could, must werden immer direkt vom Grundverb gefolgt. Kein extra Wort nötig!
items.e: Modal + base verb (no 'to'): 'She might come', not 'might to come'. The 'to' is a common error from patterns like 'have to' or 'want to'.
items.ed: Modalverb + Grundform (ohne 'to'): 'She might come', nicht 'might to come'. Das 'to' ist ein häufiger Fehler von Mustern wie 'have to' oder 'want to'.

items.p: You're not sure where your keys are. Express the possibility: 'They ___ be in my bag — I'm not sure.'
a: might
may
could
items.h: For uncertain possibility, use might, may, or could + base form.
items.hd: Für unsichere Möglichkeit verwende might, may oder could + Grundform.
items.e: 'Perhaps she is' = 'She might/may/could be'. The modal replaces the word 'perhaps'.
items.ed: 'Perhaps she is' = 'She might/may/could be'. Das Modalverb ersetzt das Wort 'perhaps'.

items.p: You see your friend has been studying for 10 hours straight. You're almost certain: 'He ___ be exhausted!'
a: must
items.h: When you're almost certain something is true based on evidence, which modal do you use?
items.hd: Wenn du fast sicher bist, dass etwas aufgrund von Beweisen wahr ist, welches Modalverb verwendest du?
items.e: 'I'm almost sure' → 'must' (near-certainty): He must know the answer. 'Must' = logical deduction.
items.ed: 'I'm almost sure' → 'must' (fast sicher): He must know the answer. 'Must' = logische Schlussfolgerung.

items.p: Someone claims this student painting is by Picasso. You're sure it's impossible: 'That ___ be a real Picasso — it's impossible!'
a: can't
cannot
items.h: When you're certain something is NOT true, which modal do you use?
items.hd: Wenn du sicher bist, dass etwas NICHT wahr ist, welches Modalverb verwendest du?
items.e: 'It's impossible that X is Y' → 'X can't be Y'. 'Can't' replaces 'it's impossible that'.
items.ed: 'It's impossible that X is Y' → 'X can't be Y'. 'Can't' ersetzt 'it's impossible that'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Er koennte recht haben, aber ich bin mir nicht sicher.
items.c: He might be right, but I'm not sure.
a: He might be right, but I'm not sure.
He may be right, but I'm not sure.
He could be right, but I'm not sure.
He might be right but I'm not sure.
items.h: 'Koennte' for possibility = might/may/could (NOT 'can'). 'I'm not sure' confirms uncertainty.
items.hd: 'Könnte' für Möglichkeit = might/may/could (NICHT 'can'). 'I'm not sure' bestätigt Unsicherheit.
items.e: 'Koennte recht haben' (possibility) = 'might/may/could be right'. German 'koennte' maps to 'might/may/could' for possibility, not 'can'.
items.ed: 'Könnte recht haben' (Möglichkeit) = 'might/may/could be right'. Deutsches 'könnte' wird zu 'might/may/could' für Möglichkeit, nicht 'can'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Das kann nicht wahr sein — sie ist doch erst 14!
items.c: That can't be true — she's only 14!
a: That can't be true — she's only 14!
That can't be true - she's only 14!
That cannot be true — she's only 14!
That can't be true, she's only 14!
items.h: 'Kann nicht wahr sein' = impossibility. In this case, German and English use the same modal: can't.
items.hd: 'Kann nicht wahr sein' = Unmöglichkeit. In diesem Fall verwenden Deutsch und Englisch dasselbe Modalverb: can't.
items.e: 'Das kann nicht wahr sein' = 'That can't be true.' For impossibility, German 'kann nicht' = English 'can't' (one of the few direct matches).
items.ed: 'Das kann nicht wahr sein' = 'That can't be true.' Für Unmöglichkeit ist deutsches 'kann nicht' = englisches 'can't' (eine der wenigen direkten Übereinstimmungen).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: be / the / answer / that / right / might
items.c: That might be the right answer.
a: That might be the right answer.
That might be the right answer
items.h: Start with the subject, then the modal, then 'be', then the rest.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Subjekt, dann das Modalverb, dann 'be', dann der Rest.
items.e: Subject + modal + base verb: 'That might be the right answer.' The modal comes before the base verb.
items.ed: Subjekt + Modalverb + Grundform: 'That might be the right answer.' Das Modalverb steht vor der Grundform.

items.p: Match each situation with the correct modal response. 1: He hasn't slept in 24 hours. 2: I'm not sure where my keys are. 3: She says she's 200 years old. 4: It's a bit cloudy today.
ds: c: They might be in my bag.
items.h: Match the strength of the evidence to the strength of the modal: strong evidence → must, no evidence → might/could, impossible → can't.
items.hd: Ordne die Stärke der Beweise der Stärke des Modalverbs zu: starke Beweise → must, keine Beweise → might/could, unmöglich → can't.
items.e: 1+b: strong evidence → must. 2+c: uncertain → might. 3+a: impossible → can't. 4+d: weak evidence → could.
items.ed: 1+b: starke Beweise → must. 2+c: unsicher → might. 3+a: unmöglich → can't. 4+d: schwache Beweise → could.

items.p: You see a strange light in the sky. Form a question using 'could' to ask your friend what it might be. Start with: What...
a: What could that be?
What could that be
What could it be?
items.h: Use: What + could + subject + be? No extra words needed.
items.hd: Verwende: What + could + Subjekt + be? Keine extra Wörter nötig.
items.e: Question with 'could' for speculation: 'What could that be?' Modal questions follow: Wh-word + modal + subject + base verb?
items.ed: Frage mit 'could' für Spekulation: 'What could that be?' Modalfragen folgen: Fragewort + Modalverb + Subjekt + Grundform?

items.p: If I ___ (study) harder, I would have passed the exam.
a: had studied
items.h: In the third conditional, the if-clause uses: if + had + past participle.
items.hd: Im Third Conditional verwendet der if-Satz: if + had + Past Participle.
items.e: The third conditional if-clause requires 'had + past participle': 'If I had studied'. Never use 'would' in the if-clause.
items.ed: Der if-Satz im Third Conditional braucht 'had + Past Participle': 'If I had studied'. Verwende nie 'would' im if-Satz.

items.p: If she had left earlier, she ___ (miss / not) the train.
a: wouldn't have missed
would not have missed
items.h: The result clause of the third conditional uses: would (not) have + past participle.
items.hd: Der Ergebnissatz im Third Conditional verwendet: would (not) have + Past Participle.
items.e: The result clause needs 'would not have + past participle': 'wouldn't have missed'. The negative form uses 'wouldn't have'.
items.ed: Der Ergebnissatz braucht 'would not have + Past Participle': 'wouldn't have missed'. Die Verneinung verwendet 'wouldn't have'.

items.p: If we had known about the concert, we ___ (go).
a: would have gone
would've gone
items.h: This is about an unreal past situation. The result clause needs: would have + past participle.
items.hd: Es geht um eine irreale Vergangenheitssituation. Der Ergebnissatz braucht: would have + Past Participle.
items.e: 'Would have gone' is correct because we're talking about an unreal past result. 'Go' has the irregular past participle 'gone'.
items.ed: 'Would have gone' ist richtig, weil wir über ein irreales Ergebnis in der Vergangenheit sprechen. 'Go' hat das unregelmäßige Past Participle 'gone'.

items.p: If Tom ___ (not / break) his leg, he would have played in the final.
a: hadn't broken
had not broken
items.h: The if-clause of the third conditional uses: if + had (not) + past participle. What is the past participle of 'break'?
items.hd: Der if-Satz im Third Conditional verwendet: if + had (not) + Past Participle. Was ist das Past Participle von 'break'?
items.e: 'Break' has the irregular past participle 'broken'. In the negative if-clause: 'hadn't broken'.
items.ed: 'Break' hat das unregelmäßige Past Participle 'broken'. Im verneinten if-Satz: 'hadn't broken'.

items.p: If you ___ (read) the invitation, you would've known what to wear.
a: had read
items.h: Remember: 'would' never appears in the if-clause of a third conditional sentence.
items.hd: Merke: 'would' kommt nie im if-Satz eines Third Conditional Satzes vor.
items.e: 'Had read' is the past perfect form needed in the if-clause. Note: 'read' (past participle) is pronounced /red/, like the colour.
items.ed: 'Had read' ist die Past Perfect Form, die im if-Satz nötig ist. Achtung: 'read' (Past Participle) wird /red/ ausgesprochen, wie die Farbe.

items.p: We ___ (not / get) lost if we had taken a map with us.
a: wouldn't have got
would not have got
wouldn't have gotten
would not have gotten
items.h: This is the result clause (main clause) of a third conditional. It comes before the if-clause here.
items.hd: Das ist der Ergebnissatz (Hauptsatz) eines Third Conditional. Er steht hier vor dem if-Satz.
items.e: When the result clause comes first, it still uses 'would (not) have + pp'. Both 'got' (BrE) and 'gotten' (AmE) are accepted.
items.ed: Wenn der Ergebnissatz zuerst kommt, verwendet er trotzdem 'would (not) have + pp'. Sowohl 'got' (BrE) als auch 'gotten' (AmE) sind richtig.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: If I had known about the party, I would have come.
a: If I had known about the party, I would have come.
ds: If I would have known about the party, I would have come.
If I had known about the party, I would came.
If I knew about the party, I would have come.
items.h: Check both the if-clause AND the result clause. What tense goes in each?
items.hd: Prüfe sowohl den if-Satz ALS AUCH den Ergebnissatz. Welche Zeitform gehört wohin?
items.e: Third conditional: if + had + pp (had known), would have + pp (would have come). Never use 'would' in the if-clause.
items.ed: Third Conditional: if + had + pp (had known), would have + pp (would have come). Verwende nie 'would' im if-Satz.

items.p: Tom says: 'If I had more money, I'd buy a new phone.' Sarah says: 'If I had had more money last week, I would have bought that phone.' What's the difference?
items.c: Tom is imagining a present situation; Sarah is talking about a past regret.
a: Tom is imagining a present situation; Sarah is talking about a past regret.
ds: Both are talking about the past.
Tom is talking about the future; Sarah about the past.
There is no difference — both sentences mean the same.
items.h: Something that did NOT happen in the past = it's too late to change. Which conditional expresses regret?
items.hd: Etwas, das in der Vergangenheit NICHT passiert ist = es ist zu spät, es zu ändern. Welches Conditional drückt Bedauern aus?
items.e: The third conditional talks about unreal past situations — things that didn't happen. It uses: If + had + pp, would have + pp.
items.ed: Das Third Conditional spricht über irreale Vergangenheitssituationen — Dinge, die nicht passiert sind. Formel: If + had + pp, would have + pp.

items.p: Which sentence correctly uses the third conditional?
items.c: If they had arrived on time, they wouldn't have missed the beginning of the film.
a: If they had arrived on time, they wouldn't have missed the beginning of the film.
ds: If they arrived on time, they wouldn't have missed the beginning of the film.
If they had arrived on time, they wouldn't miss the beginning of the film.
If they would have arrived on time, they wouldn't have missed the beginning of the film.
items.h: All three parts must be correct: the if-clause (had + pp) AND the result clause (would have + pp) AND no 'would' in the if-clause.
items.hd: Alle drei Teile müssen stimmen: der if-Satz (had + pp) UND der Ergebnissatz (would have + pp) UND kein 'would' im if-Satz.
items.e: Only the first option has both clauses correct: 'had arrived' (if-clause) + 'wouldn't have missed' (result clause).
items.ed: Nur die erste Option hat beide Sätze richtig: 'had arrived' (if-Satz) + 'wouldn't have missed' (Ergebnissatz).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: If I would have studied, I would have passed the test.
items.c: If I had studied, I would have passed the test.
a: If I had studied, I would have passed the test.
If I had studied, I would have passed the test
If I'd studied, I would have passed the test.
items.h: Look at the if-clause. Should 'would' be there?
items.hd: Schau dir den if-Satz an. Sollte 'would' dort stehen?
items.e: Never use 'would' in the if-clause. The correct form is 'If I had studied' (past perfect), not 'If I would have studied'.
items.ed: Verwende nie 'would' im if-Satz. Die richtige Form ist 'If I had studied' (Past Perfect), nicht 'If I would have studied'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: If she had told me, I would helped her.
items.c: If she had told me, I would have helped her.
a: If she had told me, I would have helped her.
If she had told me, I would have helped her
If she had told me, I would've helped her.
items.h: Look at the result clause. What is missing between 'would' and 'helped'?
items.hd: Schau dir den Ergebnissatz an. Was fehlt zwischen 'would' und 'helped'?
items.e: 'Would' needs 'have' before the past participle in the third conditional: 'would have helped'.
items.ed: 'Would' braucht 'have' vor dem Past Participle im Third Conditional: 'would have helped'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: If we had money, we would have bought the tickets.
items.c: If we had had money, we would have bought the tickets.
a: If we had had money, we would have bought the tickets.
If we had had money, we would have bought the tickets
If we'd had money, we would have bought the tickets.
items.h: This is the third conditional (unreal PAST). The if-clause needs past perfect. What is the past perfect of 'have'?
items.hd: Das ist Third Conditional (irreale VERGANGENHEIT). Der if-Satz braucht Past Perfect. Wie lautet das Past Perfect von 'have'?
items.e: To make 'have' past perfect, you need 'had had' (had + past participle of 'have'). 'If we had money' is 2nd conditional (unreal present).
items.ed: Um 'have' ins Past Perfect zu setzen, braucht man 'had had' (had + Past Participle von 'have'). 'If we had money' ist 2nd Conditional (irreale Gegenwart).

items.p: It rained on the school trip and you got wet. Express your regret: 'If we ___ (bring) an umbrella, we ___ (not / get) so wet.'
a: had brought ... wouldn't have got
had brought ... wouldn't have gotten
had brought, wouldn't have got
items.h: Third conditional: if + past perfect ... would (not) have + past participle.
items.hd: Third conditional: if + past perfect ... would (not) have + past participle.
items.e: The fact was: I didn't bring an umbrella (so I got wet). The third conditional imagines the opposite: If I had brought -> I wouldn't have got wet.
items.ed: Die Tatsache war: Ich hatte keinen Regenschirm mit (also wurde ich nass). Das Third Conditional stellt sich das Gegenteil vor: If I had brought -> I wouldn't have got wet.

items.p: Your friend missed the bus this morning. She says: 'If I ___ (leave) home earlier, I ___ (catch) the bus.'
a: had left ... would have caught
had left, would have caught
items.h: She didn't leave early — that's the past hypothetical. The result is also unreal past.
items.hd: Sie ist nicht früh losgegangen — das ist die hypothetische Vergangenheit. Das Ergebnis ist ebenfalls irreal.
items.e: The negative fact 'didn't see' becomes positive in the if-clause: 'had seen'. The result becomes negative: 'wouldn't have missed'.
items.ed: Die negative Tatsache 'didn't see' wird im if-Satz positiv: 'had seen'. Das Ergebnis wird negativ: 'wouldn't have missed'.

items.p: Think about a regret: You ate too much cake and felt sick. 'If I ___ (not / eat) so much cake, I ___ (not / feel) sick.'
a: hadn't eaten ... wouldn't have felt
had not eaten ... would not have felt
items.h: Both parts are negative. if + had not + past participle ... would not have + past participle.
items.hd: Beide Teile sind negativ. if + had not + past participle ... would not have + past participle.
items.e: A positive fact reverses to a negative if-clause: 'ate too much' -> 'hadn't eaten so much'. 'Eat' -> 'eaten' (irregular past participle).
items.ed: Eine positive Tatsache wird zum negativen if-Satz: 'ate too much' -> 'hadn't eaten so much'. 'Eat' -> 'eaten' (unregelmäßiges Past Participle).

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wenn ich das gewusst haette, haette ich dir geholfen.
items.c: If I had known that, I would have helped you.
a: If I had known that, I would have helped you.
If I had known that, I would have helped you
If I'd known that, I would've helped you.
I would have helped you if I had known that.
items.h: 'Wenn ich gewusst haette' = 'If I had known'. Don't use 'would' in the if-clause!
items.hd: 'Wenn ich gewusst hätte' = 'If I had known'. Kein 'would' im if-Satz!
items.e: German 'haette gewusst' might tempt you to write 'would have known' in the if-clause. But English uses 'had known' (past perfect).
items.ed: Deutsches 'hätte gewusst' verleitet zu 'would have known' im if-Satz. Aber Englisch verwendet 'had known' (Past Perfect).

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wenn sie frueher losgefahren waeren, haetten sie den Zug nicht verpasst.
items.c: If they had left earlier, they wouldn't have missed the train.
a: If they had left earlier, they wouldn't have missed the train.
If they had left earlier, they would not have missed the train.
They wouldn't have missed the train if they had left earlier.
If they had set off earlier, they wouldn't have missed the train.
items.h: 'losgefahren waeren' = 'had left/had set off'. 'haetten nicht verpasst' = 'wouldn't have missed'.
items.hd: 'losgefahren wären' = 'had left/had set off'. 'hätten nicht verpasst' = 'wouldn't have missed'.
items.e: German subjunctive II 'waeren losgefahren' = English past perfect 'had left'. German 'haetten verpasst' = English 'would have missed'.
items.ed: Deutscher Konjunktiv II 'wären losgefahren' = englisches Past Perfect 'had left'. Deutsches 'hätten verpasst' = englisches 'would have missed'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: had / if / I / known / , / have / I / helped / would / you
items.c: If I had known, I would have helped you.
a: If I had known, I would have helped you.
If I had known, I would have helped you
items.h: Start with 'If' + subject + 'had' + past participle, then comma, then subject + 'would have' + past participle.
items.hd: Beginne mit 'If' + Subjekt + 'had' + Past Participle, dann Komma, dann Subjekt + 'would have' + Past Participle.
items.e: Third conditional word order: If + S + had + pp, S + would have + pp + (object): 'If I had known, I would have helped you.'
items.ed: Third Conditional Wortstellung: If + S + had + pp, S + would have + pp + (Objekt): 'If I had known, I would have helped you.'

items.p: Match each if-clause with the correct result clause. 1: If it rains tomorrow, 2: If I were you, 3: If she had studied harder, 4: If you heat water to 100°C,
ds: a: she would have passed the exam.
d: I would talk to him.
items.h: Identify which conditional type each if-clause is (0th, 1st, 2nd, or 3rd) and match the result clause accordingly.
items.hd: Bestimme, welcher Conditional-Typ jeder if-Satz ist (0., 1., 2. oder 3.) und ordne den passenden Ergebnissatz zu.
items.e: 1+b: 1st conditional (real future). 2+d: 2nd conditional (unreal present). 3+a: 3rd conditional (unreal past). 4+c: Zero conditional (fact).
items.ed: 1+b: 1st Conditional (reale Zukunft). 2+d: 2nd Conditional (irreale Gegenwart). 3+a: 3rd Conditional (irreale Vergangenheit). 4+c: Zero Conditional (Fakt).

items.p: Tom didn't study and he failed the exam. He's talking about it now. Which sentence fits?
items.c: If I had studied harder, I would have passed.
a: If I had studied harder, I would have passed.
ds: If I studied harder, I would pass.
If I study harder, I will pass.
If I would have studied, I passed.
items.h: Tom is talking about something that didn't happen in the past. Which conditional expresses past regret?
items.hd: Tom spricht über etwas, das in der Vergangenheit nicht passiert ist. Welches Conditional drückt vergangenes Bedauern aus?
items.e: Third conditional (if + past perfect, would have + past participle) expresses unreal past situations and regret.
items.ed: Third conditional (if + past perfect, would have + past participle) drückt irreale vergangene Situationen und Bedauern aus.

items.p: Be careful with the knife! You might cut ___.
a: yourself
items.h: The subject is 'you' and the object is also 'you'. When subject = object, use a reflexive pronoun.
items.hd: Das Subjekt ist 'you' und das Objekt ist auch 'you'. Wenn Subjekt = Objekt, verwende ein Reflexivpronomen.
items.e: When the subject and object are the same person, use a reflexive pronoun. 'You' -> 'yourself'.
items.ed: Wenn Subjekt und Objekt dieselbe Person sind, verwende ein Reflexivpronomen. 'You' -> 'yourself'.

items.p: The cat licked ___ after dinner.
a: itself
items.h: 'The cat' is the subject and also the object. What reflexive pronoun matches 'it'?
items.hd: 'The cat' ist Subjekt und auch Objekt. Welches Reflexivpronomen passt zu 'it'?
items.e: 'The cat' takes 'it' as a pronoun, so the reflexive is 'itself'. Subject = object -> reflexive pronoun.
items.ed: 'The cat' verwendet 'it' als Pronomen, daher ist das Reflexivpronomen 'itself'. Subjekt = Objekt -> Reflexivpronomen.

items.p: My little brother is only three, but he can already dress ___.
a: himself
items.h: 'My little brother' = 'he'. The reflexive pronoun for 'he' is...
items.hd: 'Mein kleiner Bruder' = 'he'. Das Reflexivpronomen für 'he' ist...
items.e: 'He' -> 'himself' (NOT 'hisself'). Remember: himself (not hisself), themselves (not theirselves).
items.ed: 'He' -> 'himself' (NICHT 'hisself'). Merke: himself (nicht hisself), themselves (nicht theirselves).

items.p: We really ___ the party last night.
a: enjoyed
items.h: In English, 'enjoy' takes a direct object (the party). It does NOT need a reflexive pronoun here. German 'sich freuen' is reflexive, but English 'enjoy' is not.
items.hd: 'Enjoy' braucht im Englischen ein direktes Objekt (the party). Es braucht hier KEIN Reflexivpronomen. Deutsch 'sich freuen' ist reflexiv, aber English 'enjoy' ist es nicht.
items.e: 'Enjoy' + noun object does NOT need a reflexive: 'We enjoyed the party' (NOT 'We enjoyed ourselves the party'). Note: 'enjoy yourself' means 'have a good time' (no other object).
items.ed: 'Enjoy' + Substantiv-Objekt braucht KEIN Reflexivpronomen: 'We enjoyed the party' (NICHT 'We enjoyed ourselves the party'). Achtung: 'enjoy yourself' bedeutet 'Spass haben' (ohne anderes Objekt).

items.p: She looked at ___ in the mirror and smiled.
a: herself
items.h: 'She' looked at ... 'she'. Subject = object. Which reflexive pronoun matches 'she'?
items.hd: 'She' schaute ... 'she' an. Subjekt = Objekt. Welches Reflexivpronomen passt zu 'she'?
items.e: 'She' is looking at 'she' (same person) -> 'herself'. 'Her' would mean a different person.
items.ed: 'She' schaut 'she' an (dieselbe Person) -> 'herself'. 'Her' würde eine andere Person bedeuten.

items.p: We need to concentrate ___ on the task. No distractions!
items.h: In English, 'concentrate' does NOT use a reflexive pronoun. German 'sich konzentrieren' is reflexive, but the English equivalent is not. The gap should be left empty.
items.hd: Im Englischen verwendet 'concentrate' KEIN Reflexivpronomen. Deutsch 'sich konzentrieren' ist reflexiv, aber das englische Äquivalent nicht. Die Lücke bleibt leer.
items.e: English 'concentrate' is NOT reflexive, even though German 'sich konzentrieren' is. The correct sentence is: 'We need to concentrate on the task.'
items.ed: Englisch 'concentrate' ist NICHT reflexiv, obwohl Deutsch 'sich konzentrieren' reflexiv ist. Der richtige Satz lautet: 'We need to concentrate on the task.'

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: The children enjoyed the trip to the zoo.
a: The children enjoyed the trip to the zoo.
ds: The children enjoyed themselves the trip to the zoo.
The children enjoyed them the trip to the zoo.
The children enjoyed themself the trip to the zoo.
items.h: 'Enjoy' + a noun object (the trip) does NOT need a reflexive pronoun in English.
items.hd: 'Enjoy' + Substantiv-Objekt (the trip) braucht im Englischen KEIN Reflexivpronomen.
items.e: 'Enjoy' directly takes the object: 'enjoyed the trip'. No reflexive needed. German 'sich freuen ueber' is reflexive, but English 'enjoy' is not.
items.ed: 'Enjoy' nimmt direkt das Objekt: 'enjoyed the trip'. Kein Reflexivpronomen nötig. Deutsch 'sich freuen über' ist reflexiv, aber English 'enjoy' nicht.

items.p: Choose the correct option: 'She painted the picture all by ___.'
a: herself
items.h: 'By ___' means 'alone / without help'. Which reflexive pronoun matches 'she'?
items.hd: 'By ___' bedeutet 'allein / ohne Hilfe'. Welches Reflexivpronomen passt zu 'she'?
items.e: 'By herself' means 'alone / without help'. 'She' -> 'herself'. The pattern is: by + reflexive pronoun.
items.ed: 'By herself' bedeutet 'allein / ohne Hilfe'. 'She' -> 'herself'. Das Muster ist: by + Reflexivpronomen.

items.p: Which sentence uses the reflexive pronoun correctly?
items.c: He hurt himself while playing football.
a: He hurt himself while playing football.
ds: He hurt hisself while playing football.
She decided herself to stay home.
We met ourselves at the cinema.
items.h: Check: (1) Is the reflexive form correct? (2) Does the verb actually NEED a reflexive in English?
items.hd: Prüfe: (1) Ist die Reflexivform richtig? (2) Braucht das Verb im Englischen wirklich ein Reflexivpronomen?
items.e: 'He hurt himself' is correct (subject = object, correct form). 'Decided herself' is wrong (English 'decide' is not reflexive). 'Met ourselves' is wrong (English 'meet' is not reflexive).
items.ed: 'He hurt himself' ist richtig (Subjekt = Objekt, richtige Form). 'Decided herself' ist falsch (English 'decide' ist nicht reflexiv). 'Met ourselves' ist falsch (English 'meet' ist nicht reflexiv).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He hurt hisself when he fell off the bike.
items.c: He hurt himself when he fell off the bike.
a: He hurt himself when he fell off the bike.
He hurt himself when he fell off the bike
He hurt himself when he fell off his bike.
items.h: Look at the reflexive pronoun. Is 'hisself' the correct form?
items.hd: Schau dir das Reflexivpronomen an. Ist 'hisself' die richtige Form?
items.e: 'Hisself' is NOT a word. The correct reflexive for 'he' is 'himself' (him + self).
items.ed: 'Hisself' ist KEIN Wort. Das richtige Reflexivpronomen für 'he' ist 'himself' (him + self).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I can't remember myself where I put my keys.
items.c: I can't remember where I put my keys.
a: I can't remember where I put my keys.
I can't remember where I put my keys
I cannot remember where I put my keys.
items.h: Does 'remember' need a reflexive pronoun in English? Think about German 'sich erinnern'.
items.hd: Braucht 'remember' im Englischen ein Reflexivpronomen? Denke an Deutsch 'sich erinnern'.
items.e: English 'remember' is NOT reflexive, even though German 'sich erinnern' is. Remove 'myself'.
items.ed: Englisch 'remember' ist NICHT reflexiv, obwohl Deutsch 'sich erinnern' reflexiv ist. Entferne 'myself'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The children enjoyed themselves the new playground.
items.c: The children enjoyed the new playground.
a: The children enjoyed the new playground.
The children enjoyed the new playground
The kids enjoyed the new playground.
items.h: 'Enjoy' + noun object does NOT need a reflexive. Remove the reflexive pronoun.
items.hd: 'Enjoy' + Nomen-Objekt braucht KEIN Reflexivpronomen. Entferne das Reflexivpronomen.
items.e: 'Enjoy' with a noun object (the playground) does not take a reflexive: 'enjoyed the playground'. German 'sich freuen ueber' misleads learners here.
items.ed: 'Enjoy' mit einem Nomen-Objekt (the playground) braucht kein Reflexivpronomen: 'enjoyed the playground'. Deutsch 'sich freuen über' führt hier in die Irre.

items.p: Your friend asks if someone helped you paint. Tell them: 'No, I painted this picture all by ___.'
a: myself
items.h: 'By + reflexive pronoun' means 'alone, without help'.
items.hd: 'By + Reflexivpronomen' bedeutet 'allein, ohne Hilfe'.
items.e: 'Alone' = 'by herself'. The reflexive for 'she' is 'herself'.
items.ed: 'Allein' = 'by herself'. Das Reflexivpronomen für 'she' ist 'herself'.

items.p: Your parents weren't home last night. Tell them what you did: 'Don't worry — I made dinner ___!'
a: myself
items.h: Here the reflexive pronoun adds emphasis: YOU did it (without anyone's help).
items.hd: Hier fügt das Reflexivpronomen Betonung hinzu: DU hast es gemacht (ohne Hilfe).
items.e: Emphatic reflexive: 'I made this cake myself' means 'I did it without help'. 'Myself' adds emphasis.
items.ed: Emphatisches Reflexivpronomen: 'I made this cake myself' bedeutet 'Ich habe es ohne Hilfe gemacht'. 'Myself' verstärkt die Aussage.

items.p: Your friend wrote 'they taught theyself.' Fix the reflexive pronoun: 'They taught ___.'
a: themselves
items.h: 'They' needs the reflexive pronoun 'themselves' — not 'theyself'.
items.hd: 'They' braucht das Reflexivpronomen 'themselves' — nicht 'theyself'.
items.e: When 'they' is both subject and object, use 'themselves': 'They taught themselves' (NOT 'theirselves').
items.ed: Wenn 'they' Subjekt und Objekt ist, verwende 'themselves': 'They taught themselves' (NICHT 'theirselves').

items.p: 🇩🇪 Er hat sich beim Fussballspielen verletzt.
items.c: He hurt himself while playing football.
a: He hurt himself while playing football.
He hurt himself while playing football
He hurt himself playing football.
He injured himself while playing football.
items.h: 'Sich verletzen' = 'hurt oneself'. What reflexive matches 'he'?
items.hd: 'Sich verletzen' = 'hurt oneself'. Welches Reflexivpronomen passt zu 'he'?
items.e: German 'sich verletzen' correctly translates to 'hurt oneself' — this is one of the verbs that IS reflexive in both languages.
items.ed: Deutsch 'sich verletzen' übersetzt sich korrekt als 'hurt oneself' — das ist eines der Verben, die in BEIDEN Sprachen reflexiv sind.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ich kann mich nicht erinnern, wo ich meinen Schluessel hingelegt habe.
items.c: I can't remember where I put my key.
a: I can't remember where I put my key.
I cannot remember where I put my key.
I can't remember where I put my key
I can't remember where I left my key.
items.h: Careful! German 'sich erinnern' is reflexive, but English 'remember' is NOT. Do NOT add a reflexive pronoun.
items.hd: Vorsicht! Deutsch 'sich erinnern' ist reflexiv, aber Englisch 'remember' ist es NICHT. Füge KEIN Reflexivpronomen hinzu.
items.e: German 'sich erinnern' = English 'remember' (NO reflexive). A common mistake is writing 'I can't remember myself' — but this is wrong in English.
items.ed: Deutsch 'sich erinnern' = Englisch 'remember' (OHNE Reflexivpronomen). Ein häufiger Fehler ist 'I can't remember myself' — aber das ist im Englischen falsch.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: herself / she / in / the / looked / at / mirror
items.c: She looked at herself in the mirror.
a: She looked at herself in the mirror.
She looked at herself in the mirror
items.h: Subject first, then verb + preposition + reflexive pronoun + location.
items.hd: Subjekt zuerst, dann Verb + Präposition + Reflexivpronomen + Ort.
items.e: Word order: She + looked at + herself + in the mirror. The reflexive comes right after the preposition.
items.ed: Wortstellung: She + looked at + herself + in the mirror. Das Reflexivpronomen kommt direkt nach der Präposition.

items.p: Match each subject with the correct reflexive pronoun. 1: I 2: he 3: they 4: we 5: she
items.h: Match each subject pronoun (I, he, they, we, she) with the reflexive form that belongs to it.
items.hd: Ordne jedes Subjektpronomen (I, he, they, we, she) dem passenden Reflexivpronomen zu.
items.e: I -> myself, he -> himself, they -> themselves, we -> ourselves, she -> herself.
items.ed: I -> myself, he -> himself, they -> themselves, we -> ourselves, she -> herself.

items.p: Tom taught himself to play the guitar. Ask about: who taught Tom
items.c: Who taught Tom to play the guitar?
a: Who taught Tom to play the guitar?
Who taught Tom to play guitar?
Who taught him to play the guitar?
items.h: The answer is 'he taught himself'. The question asks 'who taught Tom?' — the answer should reveal it was Tom himself.
items.hd: Die Antwort ist 'he taught himself'. Die Frage fragt 'wer hat Tom beigebracht?' — die Antwort sollte zeigen, dass es Tom selbst war.
items.e: The question 'Who taught Tom to play the guitar?' can be answered with 'He taught himself.' The reflexive shows the subject did the action to/for himself.
items.ed: Die Frage 'Who taught Tom to play the guitar?' kann mit 'He taught himself.' beantwortet werden. Das Reflexivpronomen zeigt, dass das Subjekt die Handlung an/für sich selbst ausgeführt hat.

items.p: It's cold outside. Please put ___ your jacket.
a: on
items.h: 'Put ___' your jacket means to wear it. The opposite of 'take off' is...
items.hd: 'Put ___' your jacket bedeutet, sie anzuziehen. Das Gegenteil von 'take off' ist...
items.e: 'Put on' means to wear a piece of clothing. 'Put on your jacket' = 'Zieh deine Jacke an.'
items.ed: 'Put on' bedeutet ein Kleidungsstück anziehen. 'Put on your jacket' = 'Zieh deine Jacke an.'

items.p: We've ___ of milk. Can you buy some?
a: run out
items.h: 'Run ___ of' means there is nothing left. We have no more milk.
items.hd: 'Run ___ of' bedeutet, dass nichts mehr da ist. Wir haben keine Milch mehr.
items.e: 'Run out of' means to have no more of something. 'We've run out of milk' = 'Uns ist die Milch ausgegangen.'
items.ed: 'Run out of' bedeutet, dass etwas aufgebraucht ist. 'We've run out of milk' = 'Uns ist die Milch ausgegangen.'

items.p: She ___ a great idea for the school project.
a: came up with
items.h: 'Come ___ ___' means to think of or invent an idea.
items.hd: 'Come ___ ___' bedeutet, sich eine Idee auszudenken.
items.e: 'Come up with' means to think of or suggest an idea. It is inseparable: 'came up with a great idea'.
items.ed: 'Come up with' bedeutet, sich etwas auszudenken oder vorzuschlagen. Es ist untrennbar: 'came up with a great idea'.

items.p: Your shoes are dirty. Please take ___ off before coming in.
a: them
items.h: With separable phrasal verbs + pronoun objects, the pronoun goes BETWEEN the verb and particle: take ___ off.
items.hd: Bei trennbaren Phrasal Verbs + Pronomen-Objekten steht das Pronomen ZWISCHEN Verb und Partikel: take ___ off.
items.e: 'Shoes' = 'them' (plural). With a pronoun object, the pronoun MUST go between verb and particle: 'take them off' (NOT 'take off them').
items.ed: 'Shoes' = 'them' (Plural). Mit einem Pronomen-Objekt MUSS das Pronomen zwischen Verb und Partikel stehen: 'take them off' (NICHT 'take off them').

items.p: I found this old photo while I was ___ my room.
a: tidying up
cleaning up
items.h: Which phrasal verb means 'to make a room clean and organised'?
items.hd: Welches Phrasal Verb bedeutet 'ein Zimmer sauber und ordentlich machen'?
items.e: 'Tidy up' or 'clean up' means to make a place neat and ordered. 'Tidying up my room' = 'mein Zimmer aufraeumen'.
items.ed: 'Tidy up' oder 'clean up' bedeutet, einen Ort ordentlich zu machen. 'Tidying up my room' = 'mein Zimmer aufräumen'.

items.p: Don't give ___! You can do it if you keep trying.
a: up
items.h: 'Give ___' means to stop trying. It's the opposite of 'keep trying'.
items.hd: 'Give ___' bedeutet, aufzuhören es zu versuchen. Es ist das Gegenteil von 'keep trying'.
items.e: 'Give up' means to stop trying or quit. 'Don't give up!' = 'Gib nicht auf!'
items.ed: 'Give up' bedeutet aufzuhören oder aufzugeben. 'Don't give up!' = 'Gib nicht auf!'

items.p: Which sentence uses the correct word order?
items.c: She picked it up from the floor.
a: She picked it up from the floor.
ds: She picked up it from the floor.
She it picked up from the floor.
She picked from the floor it up.
items.h: With a pronoun object (it, them, him, etc.), the pronoun must go BETWEEN the verb and particle.
items.hd: Mit einem Pronomen-Objekt (it, them, him, etc.) muss das Pronomen ZWISCHEN Verb und Partikel stehen.
items.e: Separable phrasal verb + pronoun: verb + pronoun + particle. 'Pick it up' (NOT 'pick up it').
items.ed: Trennbares Phrasal Verb + Pronomen: Verb + Pronomen + Partikel. 'Pick it up' (NICHT 'pick up it').

items.p: Choose the correct option: 'The plane ___ at 7 a.m.'
a: took off
items.h: Which phrasal verb with 'take' means 'to leave the ground' (for a plane)?
items.hd: Welches Phrasal Verb mit 'take' bedeutet 'abheben' (für ein Flugzeug)?
items.e: 'Take off' means to leave the ground (planes) or to remove (clothing). Here: 'The plane took off' = 'Das Flugzeug hob ab.'
items.ed: 'Take off' bedeutet abheben (Flugzeuge) oder ausziehen (Kleidung). Hier: 'The plane took off' = 'Das Flugzeug hob ab.'

items.p: Which sentence is INCORRECT?
items.c: She looks after it well. -> She looks it after well.
a: She looks after it well. -> She looks it after well.
ds: She turned on the TV. -> She turned it on.
He picked up the ball. -> He picked it up.
I put on my coat. -> I put it on.
items.h: 'Look after' is INSEPARABLE. Can you put a pronoun between 'look' and 'after'?
items.hd: 'Look after' ist UNTRENNBAR. Kann man ein Pronomen zwischen 'look' und 'after' setzen?
items.e: 'Look after' is inseparable: 'look after it' (NOT 'look it after'). The other three (turn on, pick up, put on) are separable.
items.ed: 'Look after' ist untrennbar: 'look after it' (NICHT 'look it after'). Die anderen drei (turn on, pick up, put on) sind trennbar.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He picked up it from the floor.
items.c: He picked it up from the floor.
a: He picked it up from the floor.
He picked it up from the floor
He picked it up off the floor.
items.h: 'Pick up' is separable. Where does the pronoun 'it' go?
items.hd: 'Pick up' ist trennbar. Wohin gehört das Pronomen 'it'?
items.e: With separable phrasal verbs and pronoun objects, the pronoun goes between verb and particle: 'picked it up' (NOT 'picked up it').
items.ed: Bei trennbaren Phrasal Verbs mit Pronomen-Objekten steht das Pronomen zwischen Verb und Partikel: 'picked it up' (NICHT 'picked up it').

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She set off a new club at school.
items.c: She set up a new club at school.
a: She set up a new club at school.
She set up a new club at school
She started a new club at school.
items.h: 'Set off' means to start a journey. Which particle means 'to establish or create'?
items.hd: 'Set off' bedeutet, eine Reise anzutreten. Welche Partikel bedeutet 'gründen oder erstellen'?
items.e: 'Set off' = start a journey. 'Set up' = establish, create. To start a club, use 'set up': 'She set up a new club.'
items.ed: 'Set off' = eine Reise antreten. 'Set up' = gründen, einrichten. Um einen Club zu gründen, verwende 'set up': 'She set up a new club.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She looked the children after while their mum was out.
items.c: She looked after the children while their mum was out.
a: She looked after the children while their mum was out.
She looked after the children while their mum was out
She looked after the kids while their mum was out.
items.h: 'Look after' is INSEPARABLE. The particle must stay with the verb.
items.hd: 'Look after' ist UNTRENNBAR. Die Partikel muss beim Verb bleiben.
items.e: 'Look after' is inseparable — the particle always stays with the verb: 'looked after the children' (NOT 'looked the children after').
items.ed: 'Look after' ist untrennbar — die Partikel bleibt immer beim Verb: 'looked after the children' (NICHT 'looked the children after').

items.p: Your mum says: 'Turn on the TV.' You already did. Tell your brother: 'I've already turned ___ ___.'
a: it on
items.h: When you replace the noun with a pronoun, the pronoun goes BETWEEN the verb and particle.
items.hd: Wenn du das Nomen durch ein Pronomen ersetzt, steht das Pronomen ZWISCHEN Verb und Partikel.
items.e: 'Turn on' is separable. With a pronoun, the pronoun goes between verb and particle: 'turned it on'.
items.ed: 'Turn on' ist trennbar. Mit einem Pronomen steht das Pronomen zwischen Verb und Partikel: 'turned it on'.

items.p: Your friend dropped a pen. Tell them: 'Here, I ___ it ___ for you.'
a: picked ... up
picked it up
items.h: Pick up → with a pronoun, the pronoun goes in the middle: 'pick it up'.
items.hd: Pick up → mit einem Pronomen steht das Pronomen in der Mitte: 'pick it up'.
items.e: 'The books' -> 'them'. Separable phrasal verb + pronoun: 'picked them up' (NOT 'picked up them').
items.ed: 'The books' -> 'them'. Trennbares Phrasal Verb + Pronomen: 'picked them up' (NICHT 'picked up them').

items.p: Replace the underlined word with a phrasal verb. Your friend says she invented a great story. You say: 'She ___ ___ ___ a great story!'
a: came up with
items.h: Which phrasal verb means 'to invent or think of'?
items.hd: Welches Phrasal Verb bedeutet 'erfinden oder sich ausdenken'?
items.e: 'Come up with' = invent/think of. It is inseparable: 'came up with a great story' (the object follows the full phrasal verb).
items.ed: 'Come up with' = erfinden/sich ausdenken. Es ist untrennbar: 'came up with a great story' (das Objekt folgt nach dem gesamten Phrasal Verb).

items.p: 🇩🇪 Zieh deine Schuhe aus, bevor du reinkommst.
items.c: Take off your shoes before you come in.
a: Take off your shoes before you come in.
Take your shoes off before you come in.
Take off your shoes before coming in.
Take your shoes off before coming in.
items.h: 'Ausziehen' (Kleidung) = 'take off'. With noun objects, both word orders work: 'take off your shoes' or 'take your shoes off'.
items.hd: 'Ausziehen' (Kleidung) = 'take off'. Mit Nomen-Objekten funktionieren beide Wortstellungen: 'take off your shoes' oder 'take your shoes off'.
items.e: 'Take off' = remove (clothing/shoes). With a noun object, both orders work: 'Take off your shoes' / 'Take your shoes off'.
items.ed: 'Take off' = ausziehen (Kleidung/Schuhe). Mit einem Nomen-Objekt funktionieren beide Reihenfolgen: 'Take off your shoes' / 'Take your shoes off'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wer passt auf die Kinder auf, wenn du nicht da bist?
items.c: Who looks after the children when you're not there?
a: Who looks after the children when you're not there?
Who looks after the children when you are not there?
Who looks after the kids when you're not there?
Who looks after the kids when you are not there?
items.h: 'Aufpassen auf' = 'look after'. Remember: 'look after' is inseparable — keep the particle next to the verb.
items.hd: 'Aufpassen auf' = 'look after'. Merke: 'look after' ist untrennbar — die Partikel bleibt beim Verb.
items.e: 'Look after' = take care of. It is inseparable: 'looks after the children' (NOT 'looks the children after').
items.ed: 'Look after' = aufpassen auf. Es ist untrennbar: 'looks after the children' (NICHT 'looks the children after').

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: it / turn / please / off / can / you / ?
items.c: Can you turn it off, please?
a: Can you turn it off, please?
Can you turn it off please?
Please can you turn it off?
items.h: The pronoun 'it' goes between the verb 'turn' and the particle 'off'.
items.hd: Das Pronomen 'it' steht zwischen dem Verb 'turn' und der Partikel 'off'.
items.e: Separable phrasal verb + pronoun: 'turn it off'. Full question: 'Can you turn it off, please?'
items.ed: Trennbares Phrasal Verb + Pronomen: 'turn it off'. Vollständige Frage: 'Can you turn it off, please?'

items.p: Match each phrasal verb with its meaning. 1: give up 2: take off 3: set up 4: run out of 5: come up with
items.h: Think about what each phrasal verb means in everyday English.
items.hd: Überlege, was jedes Phrasal Verb im alltäglichen Englisch bedeutet.
items.e: give up = stop trying, take off = remove/leave ground, set up = establish, run out of = have none left, come up with = think of.
items.ed: give up = aufgeben, take off = ausziehen/abheben, set up = gründen, run out of = ausgehen/aufbrauchen, come up with = sich ausdenken.

items.p: Tom picked up the phone. Ask about: what Tom picked up
a: What did Tom pick up?
What did Tom pick up
items.h: The particle 'up' stays at the end of the question when the object is a question word.
items.hd: Die Partikel 'up' bleibt am Ende der Frage, wenn das Objekt ein Fragewort ist.
items.e: In questions with separable phrasal verbs, the particle goes to the end: 'What did Tom pick up?' (NOT 'What did Tom pick?').
items.ed: In Fragen mit trennbaren Phrasal Verbs geht die Partikel ans Ende: 'What did Tom pick up?' (NICHT 'What did Tom pick?').

items.p: The opposite of 'happy' is ___.
a: unhappy
items.h: The most common negative prefix in English is 'un-'. Does it work with 'happy'?
items.hd: Die häufigste negative Vorsilbe im Englischen ist 'un-'. Funktioniert sie mit 'happy'?
items.e: 'Un-' is the correct negative prefix for 'happy': 'unhappy'. Not all adjectives take the same prefix.
items.ed: 'Un-' ist die richtige negative Vorsilbe für 'happy': 'unhappy'. Nicht alle Adjektive verwenden dieselbe Vorsilbe.

items.p: It's ___ (possible) to finish all this homework in one hour.
a: impossible
items.h: Before 'p' and 'm', the prefix 'in-' changes to 'im-'.
items.hd: Vor 'p' und 'm' ändert sich die Vorsilbe 'in-' zu 'im-'.
items.e: The negative prefix for 'possible' is 'im-' (not 'in-' or 'un-'). Before 'p' and 'm', use 'im-': impossible, impatient, immature.
items.ed: Die negative Vorsilbe für 'possible' ist 'im-' (nicht 'in-' oder 'un-'). Vor 'p' und 'm' verwende 'im-': impossible, impatient, immature.

items.p: Her ___ (kind) really surprised everyone. She helped all the new students.
a: kindness
items.h: The sentence needs a NOUN (subject of 'surprised'). Which suffix turns an adjective into a noun?
items.hd: Der Satz braucht ein NOMEN (Subjekt von 'surprised'). Welche Nachsilbe macht aus einem Adjektiv ein Nomen?
items.e: '-ness' turns adjectives into nouns: kind -> kindness, happy -> happiness, dark -> darkness.
items.ed: '-ness' macht aus Adjektiven Nomen: kind -> kindness, happy -> happiness, dark -> darkness.

items.p: Be ___ (care) when you cross the street!
a: careful
items.h: 'Full of care' = 'care' + which suffix? The opposite of 'careless'.
items.hd: 'Voller Vorsicht' = 'care' + welche Nachsilbe? Das Gegenteil von 'careless'.
items.e: '-ful' means 'full of': careful = full of care. '-less' means 'without': careless = without care.
items.ed: '-ful' bedeutet 'voll von': careful = voller Vorsicht. '-less' bedeutet 'ohne': careless = ohne Vorsicht.

items.p: His behaviour was completely ___ (responsible).
a: irresponsible
items.h: Before 'r', the prefix 'in-' changes form. Think: irregular, irresponsible...
items.hd: Vor 'r' ändert sich die Vorsilbe 'in-'. Denke: irregular, irresponsible...
items.e: Before 'r', 'in-' becomes 'ir-': irresponsible, irregular, irrelevant. This is a phonological rule.
items.ed: Vor 'r' wird 'in-' zu 'ir-': irresponsible, irregular, irrelevant. Das ist eine phonologische Regel.

items.p: I think you ___ (understand) me. Let me explain again.
a: misunderstood
items.h: 'Mis-' means 'wrongly'. 'Understand wrongly' = ? Also check the tense.
items.hd: 'Mis-' bedeutet 'falsch'. 'Falsch verstehen' = ? Prüfe auch die Zeitform.
items.e: 'Mis-' + 'understand' = 'misunderstand' (understand wrongly). Past tense: 'misunderstood'.
items.ed: 'Mis-' + 'understand' = 'misunderstand' (falsch verstehen). Vergangenheit: 'misunderstood'.

items.p: Choose the correct option: 'The children behaved very badly. Their ___ was terrible.'
a: behaviour
items.h: The noun form of 'behave' is a common English word. It ends with '-iour' (British English).
items.hd: Die Nomenform von 'behave' ist ein gebräuchliches englisches Wort. Es endet mit '-iour' (britisches Englisch).
items.e: 'Behave' -> 'behaviour' (BrE) / 'behavior' (AmE). This is an irregular noun formation that must be learned.
items.ed: 'Behave' -> 'behaviour' (BrE) / 'behavior' (AmE). Das ist eine unregelmäßige Nomenbildung, die man lernen muss.

items.p: Which word is formed correctly?
a: illegal
items.h: Think about which prefix changes before this letter.
items.hd: Überlege, welches Präfix sich vor diesem Buchstaben ändert.
items.e: Before 'l', 'in-' becomes 'il-': illegal, illogical, illiterate. This follows a phonological assimilation rule.
items.ed: Vor 'l' wird 'in-' zu 'il-': illegal, illogical, illiterate. Das folgt einer phonologischen Assimilationsregel.

items.p: Which word is formed correctly?
a: unhappiness
items.h: Check which prefix is correct for each word. Not every word uses 'un-'.
items.hd: Überprüfe, welche Vorsilbe für jedes Wort richtig ist. Nicht jedes Wort verwendet 'un-'.
items.e: '-ly' turns adjectives into adverbs: quiet -> quietly, slow -> slowly, careful -> carefully.
items.ed: '-ly' macht aus Adjektiven Adverbien: quiet -> quietly, slow -> slowly, careful -> carefully.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The homework was unpossible to finish in one hour.
items.c: The homework was impossible to finish in one hour.
a: The homework was impossible to finish in one hour.
The homework was impossible to finish in one hour
It was impossible to finish the homework in one hour.
items.h: 'Possible' does NOT take the prefix 'un-'. Which prefix goes before 'p'?
items.hd: 'Possible' verwendet NICHT die Vorsilbe 'un-'. Welche Vorsilbe kommt vor 'p'?
items.e: 'Un-' is the most common negative prefix, but 'possible' takes 'im-' (not 'un-'): impossible. Before 'p', use 'im-'.
items.ed: 'Un-' ist die häufigste negative Vorsilbe, aber 'possible' nimmt 'im-' (nicht 'un-'): impossible. Vor 'p' verwende 'im-'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She drove very careless and had an accident.
items.c: She drove very carelessly and had an accident.
a: She drove very carelessly and had an accident.
She drove very carelessly and had an accident
She drove carelessly and had an accident.
items.h: 'Drove' is a verb. You need an adverb, not an adjective, to describe HOW she drove.
items.hd: 'Drove' ist ein Verb. Du brauchst ein Adverb, nicht ein Adjektiv, um zu beschreiben, WIE sie fuhr.
items.e: Verbs need adverbs, not adjectives. 'Careless' (adj) -> 'carelessly' (adv). 'She drove carelessly' (NOT 'careless').
items.ed: Verben brauchen Adverbien, nicht Adjektive. 'Careless' (Adj.) -> 'carelessly' (Adv.). 'She drove carelessly' (NICHT 'careless').

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The teacher was very unpatient with the noisy students.
items.c: The teacher was very impatient with the noisy students.
a: The teacher was very impatient with the noisy students.
The teacher was very impatient with the noisy students
The teacher was impatient with the noisy students.
items.h: 'Patient' starts with 'p'. Before 'p', which variant of the negative prefix do we use?
items.hd: 'Patient' beginnt mit 'p'. Vor 'p' — welche Variante der negativen Vorsilbe verwenden wir?
items.e: Before 'p' and 'm', 'in-' becomes 'im-': impatient, impossible, immature. NOT 'un-patient'.
items.ed: Vor 'p' und 'm' wird 'in-' zu 'im-': impatient, impossible, immature. NICHT 'un-patient'.

items.p: Your friend asks what the opposite of 'kind' is. Answer in a sentence: 'Her ___ (kind) really upset everyone.'
a: unkindness
items.h: First add un- for the opposite, then add -ness to make it a noun.
items.hd: Füge zuerst un- für das Gegenteil hinzu, dann -ness um ein Nomen zu bilden.
items.e: Adjective + '-ness' = noun: dark -> darkness, happy -> happiness, sad -> sadness.
items.ed: Adjektiv + '-ness' = Nomen: dark -> darkness, happy -> happiness, sad -> sadness.

items.p: Describe the road: 'Driving on this road is very ___ (danger). It's really ___ (safe)!'
a: dangerous ... unsafe
dangerous, unsafe
items.h: 'Danger' + suffix = adjective. 'Safe' + prefix = opposite adjective.
items.hd: 'Danger' + Nachsilbe = Adjektiv. 'Safe' + Vorsilbe = gegenteiliges Adjektiv.
items.e: Before 'r', 'in-' becomes 'ir-': irregular. Other examples: irresponsible, irrelevant.
items.ed: Vor 'r' wird 'in-' zu 'ir-': irregular. Weitere Beispiele: irresponsible, irrelevant.

items.p: Your teacher says your essay needs work: 'Your conclusion is a bit ___ (hope). Try to make it more ___ (hope).'
a: hopeless ... hopeful
hopeless, hopeful
items.h: '-less' means 'without'. '-ful' means 'full of'.
items.hd: '-less' bedeutet 'ohne'. '-ful' bedeutet 'voll von'.
items.e: Adjective -> adverb: add '-ly'. Slow -> slowly. 'She runs slowly' describes HOW she runs.
items.ed: Adjektiv -> Adverb: füge '-ly' hinzu. Slow -> slowly. 'She runs slowly' beschreibt, WIE sie läuft.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Sein Verhalten war unverantwortlich.
a: His behaviour was irresponsible.
His behavior was irresponsible.
His behaviour was irresponsible
His behavior was irresponsible
items.h: 'Unverantwortlich' = 'irresponsible' (ir- before 'r'). 'Verhalten' = 'behaviour'.
items.hd: 'Unverantwortlich' = 'irresponsible' (ir- vor 'r'). 'Verhalten' = 'behaviour'.
items.e: German 'un-' often becomes different English prefixes: 'unverantwortlich' = 'irresponsible' (ir- before r), not 'unresponsible'.
items.ed: Deutsches 'un-' wird im Englischen oft zu verschiedenen Vorsilben: 'unverantwortlich' = 'irresponsible' (ir- vor r), nicht 'unresponsible'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ihre Freundlichkeit hat mich sehr ueberrascht.
items.c: Her friendliness surprised me a lot.
a: Her friendliness surprised me a lot.
Her friendliness really surprised me.
Her kindness surprised me a lot.
Her friendliness surprised me very much.
items.h: 'Freundlichkeit' = noun from 'friendly'. 'Friendly' + '-ness' = ? (Note the spelling change!)
items.hd: 'Freundlichkeit' = Nomen von 'friendly'. 'Friendly' + '-ness' = ? (Achte auf die Rechtschreibänderung!)
items.e: 'Friendly' ends in '-ly', so the '-y' changes to '-i' before '-ness': friendliness (NOT *friendlyness).
items.ed: 'Friendly' endet auf '-ly', daher ändert sich das '-y' zu '-i' vor '-ness': friendliness (NICHT *friendlyness).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: is / behaviour / completely / his / unacceptable
a: His behaviour is completely unacceptable.
His behaviour is completely unacceptable
His behavior is completely unacceptable.
items.h: Start with the possessive + noun (subject), then the verb, then the adverb + adjective.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Possessivpronomen + Nomen (Subjekt), dann das Verb, dann das Adverb + Adjektiv.
items.e: Word order: His behaviour (subject) + is (verb) + completely (adverb) + unacceptable (adjective with un- prefix).
items.ed: Wortstellung: His behaviour (Subjekt) + is (Verb) + completely (Adverb) + unacceptable (Adjektiv mit un- Vorsilbe).

items.p: Match each base word with the correct negative prefix. 1: possible 2: legal 3: regular 4: agree 5: happy 6: spell
items.h: Remember the phonological rules: im- before p/m, il- before l, ir- before r. Also: dis- for verbs, mis- for 'wrongly'.
items.hd: Merke die phonologischen Regeln: im- vor p/m, il- vor l, ir- vor r. Auch: dis- für Verben, mis- für 'falsch'.
items.e: possible -> impossible (im- before p), legal -> illegal (il- before l), regular -> irregular (ir- before r), agree -> disagree (dis-), happy -> unhappy (un-), spell -> misspell (mis-).
items.ed: possible -> impossible (im- vor p), legal -> illegal (il- vor l), regular -> irregular (ir- vor r), agree -> disagree (dis-), happy -> unhappy (un-), spell -> misspell (mis-).

items.p: Tom's painting was beautiful. Ask about the beauty: 'Did you see the ___ (beautiful) of his painting?'
a: beauty
items.h: You need the noun form of 'beautiful'.
items.hd: Du brauchst die Nomenform von 'beautiful'.
items.e: 'Lonely' -> change -y to -i -> 'loneli-' + '-ness' = 'loneliness'. Same as: happy -> happiness, lazy -> laziness.
items.ed: 'Lonely' -> ändere -y zu -i -> 'loneli-' + '-ness' = 'loneliness'. Genau wie: happy -> happiness, lazy -> laziness.

items.p: At 10 o'clock last night, we ___ (watch) a documentary about sharks.
a: were watching
items.h: 'At 10 o'clock last night' describes a specific moment in the past. Was the action still going on?
items.hd: 'At 10 o'clock last night' beschreibt einen bestimmten Moment in der Vergangenheit. War die Handlung noch im Gange?
items.e: At a specific past time, an action in progress uses past continuous. 'We' takes 'were' + -ing: 'were watching'.
items.ed: Zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt in der Vergangenheit verwendet man Past Continuous für eine andauernde Handlung. 'We' braucht 'were' + -ing: 'were watching'.

items.p: My brother ___ (listen) to music when I came into his room.
a: was listening
items.h: The listening was happening when you came in. Which tense shows an ongoing action interrupted by a shorter one?
items.hd: Das Musikhören war im Gange, als du hereinkamst. Welche Zeitform zeigt eine andauernde Handlung, die unterbrochen wird?
items.e: 'Was listening' (past continuous) for the longer action in progress. 'Came' (past simple) is the interruption.
items.ed: 'Was listening' (Past Continuous) für die längere andauernde Handlung. 'Came' (Past Simple) ist die Unterbrechung.

items.p: While the teacher ___ (explain) the exercise, some students ___ (talk) to each other.
a: was explaining ... were talking
items.h: Both actions were happening at the same time. Check which form of 'be' matches each subject.
items.hd: Beide Handlungen passierten gleichzeitig. Prüfe, welche Form von 'be' zu jedem Subjekt passt.
items.e: 'The teacher' (singular) = 'was explaining'. 'Some students' (plural) = 'were talking'. Both past continuous for simultaneous actions.
items.ed: 'The teacher' (Einzahl) = 'was explaining'. 'Some students' (Mehrzahl) = 'were talking'. Beide Past Continuous für gleichzeitige Handlungen.

items.p: I ___ (not / sleep) when the earthquake happened.
a: wasn't sleeping
was not sleeping
items.h: The earthquake happened at a specific moment. What were you doing (or not doing) at that moment?
items.hd: Das Erdbeben passierte zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt. Was hast du (oder nicht) zu diesem Zeitpunkt gemacht?
items.e: Past continuous negative: 'I wasn't sleeping' — the state at the time of the sudden event.
items.ed: Past Continuous verneint: 'I wasn't sleeping' — der Zustand zum Zeitpunkt des plötzlichen Ereignisses.

items.p: The dog ___ (bark) loudly while the cat ___ (sit) on the fence.
a: was barking ... was sitting
items.h: 'While' tells you two things were happening at the same time. Both need past continuous.
items.hd: 'While' zeigt, dass zwei Dinge gleichzeitig passierten. Beide brauchen Past Continuous.
items.e: Both actions are simultaneous backgrounds: 'was barking' and 'was sitting' (both singular subjects take 'was').
items.ed: Beide Handlungen sind gleichzeitige Hintergrundhandlungen: 'was barking' und 'was sitting' (beide Einzahl-Subjekte brauchen 'was').

items.p: ___ you ___ (wait) for the bus when it ___ (start) snowing?
a: Were ... waiting ... started
Were you waiting ... started
items.h: The waiting was the longer action (past continuous question). Snowing starting was the interruption (past simple).
items.hd: Das Warten war die längere Handlung (Past Continuous Frage). Der Schneebeginn war die Unterbrechung (Past Simple).
items.e: 'Were you waiting' (past continuous question) for the ongoing action + 'started' (past simple) for the sudden event.
items.ed: 'Were you waiting' (Past Continuous Frage) für die andauernde Handlung + 'started' (Past Simple) für das plötzliche Ereignis.

items.p: Yesterday at 5pm, my sister ___.
a: was doing her homework
items.h: 'Yesterday at 5pm' is a specific point in the past. Was the action still happening at that moment?
items.hd: 'Yesterday at 5pm' ist ein bestimmter Zeitpunkt in der Vergangenheit. War die Handlung zu diesem Zeitpunkt noch im Gange?
items.e: An action in progress at a specific past time uses past continuous: 'was doing'.
items.ed: Eine Handlung, die zu einem bestimmten vergangenen Zeitpunkt im Gange war, verwendet Past Continuous: 'was doing'.

items.p: Which sentence correctly describes two actions happening at the same time?
items.c: While Dad was washing the car, Mum was cleaning the kitchen.
a: While Dad was washing the car, Mum was cleaning the kitchen.
ds: While Dad washed the car, Mum cleaned the kitchen.
While Dad was washing the car, Mum cleaned the kitchen.
While Dad washed the car, Mum was cleaning the kitchen.
items.h: When two longer actions happen at the same time, both need the same tense.
items.hd: Wenn zwei längere Handlungen gleichzeitig stattfinden, brauchen beide die gleiche Zeitform.
items.e: Two simultaneous ongoing past actions both use past continuous: 'was washing' and 'was cleaning'.
items.ed: Zwei gleichzeitige andauernde vergangene Handlungen verwenden beide Past Continuous: 'was washing' und 'was cleaning'.

items.p: Which sentence is NOT correct?
items.c: I was wanting a new bike for my birthday.
a: I was wanting a new bike for my birthday.
ds: She was running in the park when it started to rain.
They were having dinner when the lights went out.
We were waiting for the bus when our teacher drove past.
items.h: One sentence uses past continuous with a verb that should not normally be in the continuous form.
items.hd: Ein Satz verwendet Past Continuous mit einem Verb, das normalerweise nicht in der Continuous-Form stehen sollte.
items.e: 'Want' is a stative verb and cannot be used in the continuous form. Say 'I wanted a new bike', not 'was wanting'.
items.ed: 'Want' ist ein Zustandsverb und kann nicht in der Continuous-Form stehen. Sage 'I wanted a new bike', nicht 'was wanting'.

items.p: Choose the correct option: While we ___ in the garden, a bird ___ into the window.
a: were sitting ... flew
items.h: Sitting was the longer action, the bird flying into the window was the sudden event.
items.hd: Das Sitzen war die längere Handlung, der Vogel, der ins Fenster flog, war das plötzliche Ereignis.
items.e: 'Were sitting' (past continuous, longer action with 'we') + 'flew' (past simple, sudden event).
items.ed: 'Were sitting' (Past Continuous, längere Handlung mit 'we') + 'flew' (Past Simple, plötzliches Ereignis).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The children was playing in the garden.
items.c: The children were playing in the garden.
a: The children were playing in the garden.
The children were playing in the garden
items.h: 'The children' is plural. Which form of 'be' matches a plural subject?
items.hd: 'The children' ist Mehrzahl. Welche Form von 'be' passt zu einem Plural-Subjekt?
items.e: 'Children' is plural, so it takes 'were', not 'was': 'The children were playing'.
items.ed: 'Children' ist Mehrzahl und braucht 'were', nicht 'was': 'The children were playing'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: While she was cooking dinner, the phone was ringing.
items.c: While she was cooking dinner, the phone rang.
a: While she was cooking dinner, the phone rang.
While she was cooking dinner, the phone rang
She was cooking dinner when the phone rang.
items.h: The phone ringing is a short, sudden event. It shouldn't be in the continuous form.
items.hd: Das Telefonklingeln ist ein kurzes, plötzliches Ereignis. Es sollte nicht in der Continuous-Form stehen.
items.e: The phone ringing is a short interruption (past simple: 'rang'), not an ongoing action.
items.ed: Das Telefonklingeln ist eine kurze Unterbrechung (Past Simple: 'rang'), keine andauernde Handlung.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I was believing that the test was easy.
items.c: I believed that the test was easy.
a: I believed that the test was easy.
I believed that the test was easy
I believed the test was easy.
items.h: 'Believe' is a stative verb. Can stative verbs be used in the continuous form?
items.hd: 'Believe' ist ein Zustandsverb. Können Zustandsverben in der Continuous-Form stehen?
items.e: 'Believe' is a stative verb and cannot be used in the continuous form. Use past simple: 'I believed'.
items.ed: 'Believe' ist ein Zustandsverb und kann nicht in der Continuous-Form stehen. Verwende Past Simple: 'I believed'.

items.p: You're writing in your diary about yesterday. Two things happened at the same time: your dad was driving and you were listening to music. Write one sentence using 'while'.
items.c: While my dad was driving, I was listening to music.
a: While my dad was driving, I was listening to music.
While Dad was driving, I was listening to music.
I was listening to music while my dad was driving.
I was listening to music while Dad was driving.
items.h: Use 'while' to connect two past continuous actions that happened at the same time.
items.hd: Verwende 'while', um zwei gleichzeitige Past Continuous-Handlungen zu verbinden.
items.e: Two simultaneous ongoing actions both use past continuous, connected by 'while'.
items.ed: Zwei gleichzeitige andauernde Handlungen verwenden beide Past Continuous, verbunden durch 'while'.

items.p: You're telling a friend about a funny moment. You were eating lunch in the school canteen. A bird flew through the window. Write one sentence using 'when'.
items.c: I was eating lunch in the school canteen when a bird flew through the window.
a: I was eating lunch in the school canteen when a bird flew through the window.
I was eating lunch in the canteen when a bird flew through the window.
When a bird flew through the window, I was eating lunch in the school canteen.
items.h: The eating is the background action (past continuous). The bird flying in is the sudden event (past simple).
items.hd: Das Essen ist die Hintergrundhandlung (Past Continuous). Der einfliegende Vogel ist das plötzliche Ereignis (Past Simple).
items.e: Past continuous for the longer action + 'when' + past simple for the interrupting event.
items.ed: Past Continuous für die längere Handlung + 'when' + Past Simple für das unterbrechende Ereignis.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Um 9 Uhr gestern Abend schlief sie schon.
items.c: At 9 o'clock last night, she was already sleeping.
a: At 9 o'clock last night, she was already sleeping.
She was already sleeping at 9 o'clock last night.
At 9 o'clock yesterday evening, she was already sleeping.
At 9 last night she was already sleeping.
items.h: 'Um 9 Uhr gestern Abend' = 'at 9 o'clock last night'. An action in progress at a specific past time uses past continuous.
items.hd: 'Um 9 Uhr gestern Abend' = 'at 9 o'clock last night'. Eine andauernde Handlung zu einem bestimmten vergangenen Zeitpunkt braucht Past Continuous.
items.e: German uses simple past ('schlief') for ongoing past actions. English needs past continuous: 'was sleeping'.
items.ed: Deutsch verwendet einfache Vergangenheit ('schlief') für andauernde vergangene Handlungen. Englisch braucht Past Continuous: 'was sleeping'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Die Sonne schien und die Voegel sangen, als wir aufwachten.
items.c: The sun was shining and the birds were singing when we woke up.
a: The sun was shining and the birds were singing when we woke up.
When we woke up, the sun was shining and the birds were singing.
The sun was shining and the birds were singing when we woke up
items.h: The sun shining and birds singing describe the background scene (past continuous). Waking up is the event (past simple).
items.hd: Sonnenschein und Vogelgesang beschreiben die Hintergrundszene (Past Continuous). Aufwachen ist das Ereignis (Past Simple).
items.e: Scene-setting: 'was shining' + 'were singing' (past continuous). Single event: 'woke up' (past simple).
items.ed: Szenenbeschreibung: 'was shining' + 'were singing' (Past Continuous). Einzelereignis: 'woke up' (Past Simple).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: while / were / dinner / having / they / the / doorbell / rang
items.c: While they were having dinner, the doorbell rang.
a: While they were having dinner, the doorbell rang.
While they were having dinner, the doorbell rang
The doorbell rang while they were having dinner.
items.h: Start with 'while' + subject + was/were + -ing for the background action. Then add the interrupting event.
items.hd: Beginne mit 'while' + Subjekt + was/were + -ing für die Hintergrundhandlung. Dann füge das unterbrechende Ereignis hinzu.
items.e: 'While' + past continuous (were having) for the background action, + past simple (rang) for the interruption.
items.ed: 'While' + Past Continuous (were having) für die Hintergrundhandlung, + Past Simple (rang) für die Unterbrechung.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: at / 7pm / I / was / my / reading / favourite / book / last / night
items.c: At 7pm last night, I was reading my favourite book.
a: At 7pm last night, I was reading my favourite book.
At 7pm last night I was reading my favourite book.
I was reading my favourite book at 7pm last night.
Last night at 7pm, I was reading my favourite book.
items.h: The time expression ('at 7pm last night') can go at the beginning or end. Then subject + was/were + -ing.
items.hd: Der Zeitausdruck ('at 7pm last night') kann am Anfang oder Ende stehen. Dann Subjekt + was/were + -ing.
items.e: Time expression + subject + was + -ing: 'At 7pm last night, I was reading my favourite book.'
items.ed: Zeitausdruck + Subjekt + was + -ing: 'At 7pm last night, I was reading my favourite book.'

items.p: You looked out of the window at 6am. The street was covered in snow. A man was outside. Which sentence best describes what you saw?
items.c: A man was clearing the snow from the pavement.
a: A man was clearing the snow from the pavement.
ds: A man cleared the snow from the pavement.
A man is clearing the snow from the pavement.
A man had cleared the snow from the pavement.
items.h: You looked out and saw someone in the middle of doing something. Which tense shows an action in progress?
items.hd: Du hast hinausgeschaut und jemanden gesehen, der gerade etwas machte. Welche Zeitform zeigt eine andauernde Handlung?
items.e: You saw the man in the middle of clearing snow — an action in progress at a specific past moment needs past continuous.
