items.ed: Du hast den Mann mitten beim Schneeschaufeln gesehen — eine andauernde Handlung zu einem bestimmten vergangenen Zeitpunkt braucht Past Continuous.

items.p: When we got to the restaurant, our friends ___ already ___ (order).
a: had ... ordered
had already ordered
items.h: Ordering happened BEFORE you got there. Which auxiliary verb + past participle shows the earlier of two past events?
items.hd: Das Bestellen passierte VOR eurer Ankunft. Welches Hilfsverb + Past Participle zeigt das frühere von zwei vergangenen Ereignissen?
items.e: Past perfect: 'had already ordered' — the ordering was completed before the arrival.
items.ed: Past Perfect: 'had already ordered' — das Bestellen war vor der Ankunft abgeschlossen.

items.p: She couldn't pay because she ___ (forget) her wallet at home.
a: had forgotten
items.h: Forgetting the wallet happened BEFORE she couldn't pay. Which tense marks the earlier past event?
items.hd: Das Vergessen der Geldbörse passierte VOR dem Nicht-bezahlen-Können. Welche Zeitform markiert das frühere Ereignis?
items.e: Past perfect 'had forgotten' shows the cause (forgetting) happened before the result (couldn't pay).
items.ed: Past Perfect 'had forgotten' zeigt, dass die Ursache (Vergessen) vor dem Ergebnis (nicht bezahlen können) passierte.

items.p: The match ___ already ___ (begin) when we turned on the TV.
a: had ... begun
had already begun
items.h: The match beginning happened BEFORE you turned on the TV. After 'had', use the past participle (3rd column).
items.hd: Der Spielbeginn war VOR dem Einschalten des TVs. Nach 'had' verwende das Past Participle (3. Spalte).
items.e: Past perfect with irregular verb: 'begin — began — begun'. After 'had', use 'begun'.
items.ed: Past Perfect mit unregelmäßigem Verb: 'begin — began — begun'. Nach 'had' verwende 'begun'.

items.p: I was really nervous because I ___ never ___ (fly) in a plane before.
a: had ... flown
had never flown
items.h: Not having flown before caused the nervousness. After 'had', use the 3rd column form of 'fly'.
items.hd: Noch nie geflogen zu sein verursachte die Nervosität. Nach 'had' verwende die 3. Spalte von 'fly'.
items.e: 'Had never flown' — past perfect with 'never' and irregular participle 'flown' (fly — flew — flown).
items.ed: 'Had never flown' — Past Perfect mit 'never' und unregelmäßigem Partizip 'flown' (fly — flew — flown).

items.p: By the time the teacher arrived, the students ___ (sit) down and ___ (open) their books.
a: had sat ... had opened
had sat ... opened
items.h: Both actions were completed BEFORE the teacher arrived. 'By the time' signals past perfect.
items.hd: Beide Handlungen waren VOR der Ankunft des Lehrers abgeschlossen. 'By the time' signalisiert Past Perfect.
items.e: Both earlier actions use past perfect: 'had sat down' and 'had opened'. The teacher arriving is the later event.
items.ed: Beide früheren Handlungen verwenden Past Perfect: 'had sat down' und 'had opened'. Die Ankunft des Lehrers ist das spätere Ereignis.

items.p: ___ she ___ (finish) the book before the library closed?
a: Had ... finished
Had she finished
items.h: The question asks whether the finishing happened BEFORE the library closed. Past perfect question: Had + subject + past participle?
items.hd: Die Frage ist, ob das Fertiglesen VOR der Schließung der Bibliothek passierte. Past Perfect Frage: Had + Subjekt + Past Participle?
items.e: Past perfect question form: 'Had she finished the book before the library closed?'
items.ed: Past Perfect Frageform: 'Had she finished the book before the library closed?'

items.p: We were late. The bus ___.
a: had already left
items.h: The bus left BEFORE you got there. Which tense shows a completed action before another past event?
items.hd: Der Bus fuhr VOR eurer Ankunft ab. Welche Zeitform zeigt eine abgeschlossene Handlung vor einem anderen vergangenen Ereignis?
items.e: Past perfect: 'had already left' — the bus departure was completed before the arrival.
items.ed: Past Perfect: 'had already left' — die Busabfahrt war vor der Ankunft abgeschlossen.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence about Tom's bad day:
items.c: Tom was upset because he had lost his phone.
a: Tom was upset because he had lost his phone.
ds: Tom was upset because he lost his phone.
Tom was upset because he has lost his phone.
Tom had been upset because he lost his phone.
items.h: Losing the phone (cause) happened BEFORE being upset (result). Which event gets past perfect?
items.hd: Das Verlieren des Handys (Ursache) passierte VOR der Verärgertheit (Ergebnis). Welches Ereignis bekommt Past Perfect?
items.e: The cause (losing the phone) happened before the result (being upset), so the cause gets past perfect: 'had lost'.
items.ed: Die Ursache (Handy verlieren) passierte vor dem Ergebnis (verärgert sein), also bekommt die Ursache Past Perfect: 'had lost'.

items.p: Which sentence has the WRONG past participle form?
items.c: She had drinked all the juice before breakfast.
a: She had drinked all the juice before breakfast.
ds: They had spoken to the teacher before the lesson.
He had driven to the airport before sunrise.
I had chosen my outfit the night before.
items.h: Look at the verb form after 'had' in each sentence. One uses a made-up participle instead of the correct irregular form.
items.hd: Schau dir die Verbform nach 'had' in jedem Satz an. Einer verwendet ein erfundenes Partizip statt der richtigen unregelmäßigen Form.
items.e: 'Drinked' is not a word. The correct past participle of 'drink' is 'drunk': 'She had drunk all the juice.'
items.ed: 'Drinked' existiert nicht. Das richtige Past Participle von 'drink' ist 'drunk': 'She had drunk all the juice.'

items.p: Choose the correct option: After the rain ___, the sun came out.
a: had stopped
items.h: 'After' introduces the FIRST action. The first action in a past sequence needs past perfect.
items.hd: 'After' leitet die ERSTE Handlung ein. Die erste Handlung in einer vergangenen Reihenfolge braucht Past Perfect.
items.e: 'After' + past perfect (had stopped) for the earlier event. 'Came out' (past simple) for the later event.
items.ed: 'After' + Past Perfect (had stopped) für das frühere Ereignis. 'Came out' (Past Simple) für das spätere Ereignis.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: After she has finished the test, she left the classroom.
items.c: After she had finished the test, she left the classroom.
a: After she had finished the test, she left the classroom.
After she had finished the test, she left the classroom
items.h: 'Has finished' is present perfect. But both events are in the past. Which tense marks the earlier past event?
items.hd: 'Has finished' ist Present Perfect. Aber beide Ereignisse liegen in der Vergangenheit. Welche Zeitform markiert das frühere Ereignis?
items.e: Use past perfect 'had finished', not present perfect 'has finished', when both events are in the past.
items.ed: Verwende Past Perfect 'had finished', nicht Present Perfect 'has finished', wenn beide Ereignisse in der Vergangenheit liegen.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She had wrote a long email to her friend.
items.c: She had written a long email to her friend.
a: She had written a long email to her friend.
She had written a long email to her friend
items.h: Look at the form after 'had'. Is 'wrote' the correct past participle of 'write'?
items.hd: Schau dir die Form nach 'had' an. Ist 'wrote' das richtige Past Participle von 'write'?
items.e: 'Wrote' is past simple. After 'had', use the past participle 'written' (write — wrote — written).
items.ed: 'Wrote' ist Past Simple. Nach 'had' verwende das Past Participle 'written' (write — wrote — written).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: When the concert had started, we arrived at the hall.
items.c: When we arrived at the hall, the concert had already started.
a: When we arrived at the hall, the concert had already started.
The concert had already started when we arrived at the hall.
When we arrived at the hall, the concert had started.
items.h: Past perfect should be on the EARLIER event. Which happened first — arriving or the concert starting?
items.hd: Past Perfect sollte beim FRÜHEREN Ereignis stehen. Was passierte zuerst — das Ankommen oder der Konzertbeginn?
items.e: The concert starting is the earlier event (past perfect). Arriving is the later event (past simple with 'when').
items.ed: Der Konzertbeginn ist das frühere Ereignis (Past Perfect). Das Ankommen ist das spätere Ereignis (Past Simple mit 'when').

items.p: Two things happened yesterday. First: your sister did the shopping. Then: you came home. Combine using 'when': 'When I ___ (come) home, my sister ___ already ___ (do) the shopping.'
a: came ... had already done
came, had already done
items.h: The shopping happened first (past perfect). Your arrival was later (past simple).
items.hd: Das Einkaufen passierte zuerst (Past Perfect). Deine Ankunft war später (Past Simple).
items.e: 'Came home' (later event, past simple) + 'had already done' (earlier event, past perfect).
items.ed: 'Came home' (späteres Ereignis, Past Simple) + 'had already done' (früheres Ereignis, Past Perfect).

items.p: You're writing a story. First: it stopped raining. Then: the children went outside. Combine using 'as soon as': 'As soon as it ___ (stop) raining, the children ___ (go) outside.'
a: had stopped ... went
had stopped, went
items.h: 'As soon as' introduces the first completed action (past perfect). The reaction is the second action (past simple).
items.hd: 'As soon as' leitet die erste abgeschlossene Handlung ein (Past Perfect). Die Reaktion ist die zweite Handlung (Past Simple).
items.e: 'As soon as' + past perfect (had stopped) for the trigger event. Past simple (went) for the resulting action.
items.ed: 'As soon as' + Past Perfect (had stopped) für das auslösende Ereignis. Past Simple (went) für die resultierende Handlung.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Nachdem er seine Hausaufgaben gemacht hatte, ging er in den Park.
items.c: After he had done his homework, he went to the park.
a: After he had done his homework, he went to the park.
After he had finished his homework, he went to the park.
He went to the park after he had done his homework.
He went to the park after he had finished his homework.
items.h: 'Nachdem' = 'after'. 'Gemacht hatte' = 'had done' (Plusquamperfekt = past perfect). 'Ging' = 'went' (past simple).
items.hd: 'Nachdem' = 'after'. 'Gemacht hatte' = 'had done' (Plusquamperfekt = Past Perfect). 'Ging' = 'went' (Past Simple).
items.e: 'Nachdem + Plusquamperfekt' = 'After + past perfect'. The later event uses past simple.
items.ed: 'Nachdem + Plusquamperfekt' = 'After + Past Perfect'. Das spätere Ereignis verwendet Past Simple.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Sie hatte noch nie Sushi probiert, bevor sie nach Japan reiste.
items.c: She had never tried sushi before she travelled to Japan.
a: She had never tried sushi before she travelled to Japan.
She had never tried sushi before she traveled to Japan.
Before she travelled to Japan, she had never tried sushi.
She'd never tried sushi before she travelled to Japan.
items.h: 'Hatte noch nie probiert' = 'had never tried' (past perfect). 'Bevor sie reiste' = 'before she travelled' (past simple).
items.hd: 'Hatte noch nie probiert' = 'had never tried'. 'Bevor sie reiste' = 'before she travelled'.
items.e: 'Had never tried' (past perfect, earlier experience) + 'before she travelled' (past simple, later event).
items.ed: 'Had never tried' (Past Perfect, frühere Erfahrung) + 'before she travelled' (Past Simple, späteres Ereignis).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: the / train / had / when / left / we / arrived / at / the / station / already
items.c: The train had already left when we arrived at the station.
a: The train had already left when we arrived at the station.
When we arrived at the station, the train had already left.
The train had already left when we arrived at the station
items.h: The earlier event (train leaving) goes in past perfect. The later event (arriving) uses past simple.
items.hd: Das frühere Ereignis (Zug abfahren) steht im Past Perfect. Das spätere Ereignis (ankommen) verwendet Past Simple.
items.e: Past perfect (had already left) for the earlier event + 'when' + past simple (arrived) for the later event.
items.ed: Past Perfect (had already left) für das frühere Ereignis + 'when' + Past Simple (arrived) für das spätere Ereignis.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: by / time / the / the / had / party / we / got / to / ended / already
items.c: By the time we got to the party, it had already ended.
a: By the time we got to the party, it had already ended.
By the time we got to the party it had already ended.
The party had already ended by the time we got there.
items.h: 'By the time' introduces the later event. The party ending is the earlier event (past perfect).
items.hd: 'By the time' leitet das spätere Ereignis ein. Das Ende der Party ist das frühere Ereignis (Past Perfect).
items.e: 'By the time' + past simple (got) introduces the later event. Past perfect (had already ended) for the earlier event.
items.ed: 'By the time' + Past Simple (got) leitet das spätere Ereignis ein. Past Perfect (had already ended) für das frühere Ereignis.

items.p: Tom went to the fridge to get some milk. The milk was gone. His sister was in the kitchen. Which sentence explains the situation?
items.c: His sister had drunk all the milk.
a: His sister had drunk all the milk.
ds: His sister drank all the milk.
His sister has drunk all the milk.
His sister was drinking all the milk.
items.h: The milk was already gone when Tom looked. The drinking happened BEFORE he checked. Which tense shows this?
items.hd: Die Milch war schon weg, als Tom nachschaute. Das Trinken passierte VOR dem Nachschauen. Welche Zeitform zeigt das?
items.e: Past perfect 'had drunk' shows the action was completed before Tom went to the fridge.
items.ed: Past Perfect 'had drunk' zeigt, dass die Handlung abgeschlossen war, bevor Tom zum Kühlschrank ging.

items.p: "I am happy." — She said she ___ happy.
a: was
items.h: 'Am' in the original becomes which form in reported speech? Remember: 'she' is singular.
items.hd: 'Am' im Original wird zu welcher Form in der indirekten Rede? Denk daran: 'she' ist Einzahl.
items.e: Backshift: 'am' becomes 'was' in reported speech. Pronoun shift: 'I' becomes 'she'.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'am' wird zu 'was' in der indirekten Rede. Pronomenverschiebung: 'I' wird zu 'she'.

items.p: "We don't want to go." — They said they ___ want to go.
a: didn't
did not
items.h: 'Don't want' is present simple negative. What is its past simple form?
items.hd: 'Don't want' ist Present Simple verneint. Was ist die Past Simple-Form davon?
items.e: Backshift: 'don't want' becomes 'didn't want' in reported speech. 'We' becomes 'they'.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'don't want' wird zu 'didn't want' in der indirekten Rede. 'We' wird zu 'they'.

items.p: "I have already eaten lunch." — He told me he ___ already ___ lunch.
a: had ... eaten
had already eaten
items.h: Present perfect ('have eaten') shifts to past perfect ('had eaten') in reported speech.
items.hd: Present Perfect ('have eaten') wird zu Past Perfect ('had eaten') in der indirekten Rede.
items.e: Backshift: 'have already eaten' becomes 'had already eaten'. 'Told me' = tell + person.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'have already eaten' wird zu 'had already eaten'. 'Told me' = tell + Person.

items.p: "I'm going to visit my grandparents tomorrow." — She said she ___ going to visit ___ grandparents the next day.
a: was ... her
items.h: 'Am going to' shifts to 'was going to'. 'My' changes based on who is reporting.
items.hd: 'Am going to' wird zu 'was going to'. 'My' ändert sich je nachdem, wer berichtet.
items.e: Backshift: 'am going to' → 'was going to'. Pronoun shift: 'my' → 'her'. Time shift: 'tomorrow' → 'the next day'.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'am going to' → 'was going to'. Pronomen: 'my' → 'her'. Zeit: 'tomorrow' → 'the next day'.

items.p: "We were waiting for the bus yesterday." — They said they ___ been waiting for the bus the day ___.
a: had ... before
items.h: Past continuous ('were waiting') shifts to past perfect continuous ('had been waiting'). 'Yesterday' becomes 'the day before'.
items.hd: Past Continuous ('were waiting') wird zu Past Perfect Continuous ('had been waiting'). 'Yesterday' wird zu 'the day before'.
items.e: Backshift: 'were waiting' → 'had been waiting'. Time shift: 'yesterday' → 'the day before'.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'were waiting' → 'had been waiting'. Zeitverschiebung: 'yesterday' → 'the day before'.

items.p: "I should study more for the test." — He ___ that he ___ study more for the test.
a: said ... should
items.h: 'Should' is special — it does NOT change in reported speech. Also, check: 'said' or 'told'?
items.hd: 'Should' ist besonders — es ändert sich NICHT in der indirekten Rede. Prüfe auch: 'said' oder 'told'?
items.e: 'Should' remains 'should' in reported speech (no backshift). 'Said' (no person object) is correct here.
items.ed: 'Should' bleibt 'should' in der indirekten Rede (keine Zeitverschiebung). 'Said' (ohne Personalobjekt) ist hier richtig.

items.p: "I can swim very well." — She said she ___ swim very well.
a: could
items.h: In reported speech, 'can' shifts to which modal verb?
items.hd: In der indirekten Rede wird 'can' zu welchem Modalverb?
items.e: Backshift: 'can' becomes 'could' in reported speech.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'can' wird zu 'could' in der indirekten Rede.

items.p: Which is the correct reported version of: "We are leaving now."?
items.c: They said they were leaving then.
a: They said they were leaving then.
ds: They said they are leaving now.
They said they were leaving now.
They told they were leaving then.
items.h: Check three things: tense backshift, time word shift, and say/tell usage.
items.hd: Prüfe drei Dinge: Zeitverschiebung, Zeitwort-Verschiebung und say/tell-Verwendung.
items.e: Backshift: 'are leaving' → 'were leaving'. Time: 'now' → 'then'. 'Said' (no person) is correct.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'are leaving' → 'were leaving'. Zeit: 'now' → 'then'. 'Said' (ohne Person) ist richtig.

items.p: "I bought this jacket here last week." — She said she had bought ___ jacket ___ the week before.
a: that ... there
items.h: In reported speech, 'this' shifts to 'that' and 'here' shifts to 'there'.
items.hd: In der indirekten Rede wird 'this' zu 'that' und 'here' zu 'there'.
items.e: Reported speech shifts: 'this' → 'that', 'here' → 'there', 'last week' → 'the week before'.
items.ed: Verschiebungen in der indirekten Rede: 'this' → 'that', 'here' → 'there', 'last week' → 'the week before'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She told that she was going home.
items.c: She said that she was going home.
a: She said that she was going home.
She told me that she was going home.
She said she was going home.
She told us that she was going home.
items.h: 'Told' always needs a personal object (told me, told him, told us). Or use 'said' without an object.
items.hd: 'Told' braucht immer ein Personalobjekt (told me, told him, told us). Oder verwende 'said' ohne Objekt.
items.e: 'Told that...' is wrong. Either 'She said that...' (no person) or 'She told me/him/us that...' (person required).
items.ed: 'Told that...' ist falsch. Entweder 'She said that...' (ohne Person) oder 'She told me/him/us that...' (Person nötig).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He said he can't come to the party.
items.c: He said he couldn't come to the party.
a: He said he couldn't come to the party.
He said he could not come to the party.
He said he couldn't come to the party
items.h: 'Can't' needs to shift back in reported speech. What is the past form of 'can't'?
items.hd: 'Can't' muss in der indirekten Rede zurückverschoben werden. Was ist die Vergangenheitsform von 'can't'?
items.e: Backshift: 'can't' becomes 'couldn't' in reported speech after 'said'.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'can't' wird zu 'couldn't' in der indirekten Rede nach 'said'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She said me that she would come tomorrow.
items.c: She told me that she would come the next day.
a: She told me that she would come the next day.
She told me she would come the next day.
She said that she would come the next day.
items.h: Two mistakes: 'said me' is wrong (say doesn't take a person), and 'tomorrow' needs a time shift.
items.hd: Zwei Fehler: 'said me' ist falsch (say braucht keine Person), und 'tomorrow' braucht eine Zeitverschiebung.
items.e: Fix 1: 'said me' → 'told me' (or 'said that'). Fix 2: 'tomorrow' → 'the next day'.
items.ed: Fix 1: 'said me' → 'told me' (oder 'said that'). Fix 2: 'tomorrow' → 'the next day'.

items.p: Your teacher said: "You must hand in your homework tomorrow." Report what the teacher said. Start with: The teacher told us that...
items.c: The teacher told us that we had to hand in our homework the next day.
a: The teacher told us that we had to hand in our homework the next day.
The teacher told us we had to hand in our homework the next day.
The teacher told us that we had to hand in our homework the following day.
items.h: 'Must' becomes 'had to'. 'You' becomes 'we'. 'Your' becomes 'our'. 'Tomorrow' becomes 'the next day'.
items.hd: 'Must' wird zu 'had to'. 'You' wird zu 'we'. 'Your' wird zu 'our'. 'Tomorrow' wird zu 'the next day'.
items.e: Backshift: 'must' → 'had to'. Pronouns: 'you/your' → 'we/our'. Time: 'tomorrow' → 'the next day'.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'must' → 'had to'. Pronomen: 'you/your' → 'we/our'. Zeit: 'tomorrow' → 'the next day'.

items.p: Your friend Max said: "I saw a great film at the cinema yesterday." Report what Max said. Start with: Max told me that...
items.c: Max told me that he had seen a great film at the cinema the day before.
a: Max told me that he had seen a great film at the cinema the day before.
Max told me he had seen a great film at the cinema the day before.
Max told me that he had seen a great film at the cinema the previous day.
items.h: 'Saw' (past simple) shifts to 'had seen' (past perfect). 'I' becomes 'he'. 'Yesterday' becomes 'the day before'.
items.hd: 'Saw' (Past Simple) wird zu 'had seen' (Past Perfect). 'I' wird zu 'he'. 'Yesterday' wird zu 'the day before'.
items.e: Backshift: 'saw' → 'had seen'. Pronoun: 'I' → 'he'. Time: 'yesterday' → 'the day before'.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'saw' → 'had seen'. Pronomen: 'I' → 'he'. Zeit: 'yesterday' → 'the day before'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Sie sagte, dass sie Kopfschmerzen habe.
items.c: She said that she had a headache.
a: She said that she had a headache.
She said she had a headache.
She said that she had a headache
items.h: 'Sagte, dass' = 'said that'. German Konjunktiv I ('habe') = English past simple ('had') in reported speech.
items.hd: 'Sagte, dass' = 'said that'. Konjunktiv I ('habe') wird im Englischen zu Past Simple ('had') in der indirekten Rede.
items.e: German uses Konjunktiv for reported speech. English uses tense backshift: 'has' → 'had'.
items.ed: Deutsch verwendet den Konjunktiv für indirekte Rede. Englisch verwendet Zeitverschiebung: 'has' → 'had'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Er erzaehlte uns, dass er am Tag zuvor sein Fahrrad verloren hatte.
items.c: He told us that he had lost his bike the day before.
a: He told us that he had lost his bike the day before.
He told us he had lost his bike the day before.
He told us that he had lost his bicycle the day before.
He told us that he had lost his bike the previous day.
items.h: 'Erzaehlte uns' = 'told us'. 'Hatte verloren' = 'had lost'. 'Am Tag zuvor' = 'the day before'.
items.hd: 'Erzählte uns' = 'told us'. 'Hatte verloren' = 'had lost'. 'Am Tag zuvor' = 'the day before'.
items.e: 'Told us' (tell + person). 'Had lost' (past perfect backshift). 'The day before' (time shift).
items.ed: 'Told us' (tell + Person). 'Had lost' (Past Perfect Zeitverschiebung). 'The day before' (Zeitverschiebung).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: told / me / would / she / help / that / she / me
items.c: She told me that she would help me.
a: She told me that she would help me.
She told me that she would help me
She told me she would help me.
items.h: Start with subject + 'told me' + 'that' + reported clause with 'would'.
items.hd: Beginne mit Subjekt + 'told me' + 'that' + berichteter Satz mit 'would'.
items.e: Structure: She told me (that) she would help me. 'Told' + person + that-clause.
items.ed: Struktur: She told me (that) she would help me. 'Told' + Person + that-Satz.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: the / teacher / said / that / had / we / to / study / harder
items.c: The teacher said that we had to study harder.
a: The teacher said that we had to study harder.
The teacher said that we had to study harder
The teacher said we had to study harder.
items.h: Subject + 'said that' + reported clause. 'Had to' replaces 'must' in reported speech.
items.hd: Subjekt + 'said that' + berichteter Satz. 'Had to' ersetzt 'must' in der indirekten Rede.
items.e: 'The teacher said that we had to study harder.' — 'must' backshifts to 'had to'.
items.ed: 'The teacher said that we had to study harder.' — 'must' wird zu 'had to' zurückverschoben.

items.p: Match each direct speech sentence with its correct reported version. 1: "I like football." 2: "I will call you." 3: "I can drive." 4: "I am studying."
ds: a: He said he could drive.
b: He said he liked football.
c: He said he was studying.
d: He said he would call me.
items.h: Match each tense backshift: like→liked, will→would, can→could, am studying→was studying.
items.hd: Ordne jede Zeitverschiebung zu: like→liked, will→would, can→could, am studying→was studying.
items.e: Backshift rules: present simple→past simple, will→would, can→could, present continuous→past continuous.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebungsregeln: Present Simple→Past Simple, will→would, can→could, Present Continuous→Past Continuous.

items.p: Anna spoke to you yesterday and said: "I love my new school." Today you tell your mum what Anna said. Which sentence is correct?
items.c: Anna said she loved her new school.
a: Anna said she loved her new school.
ds: Anna said she loves her new school.
Anna said she loved my new school.
Anna told that she loved her new school.
items.h: Check three things: is the tense backshifted? Are the pronouns correct? Is 'said/told' used correctly?
items.hd: Prüfe drei Dinge: Ist die Zeitform zurückverschoben? Sind die Pronomen richtig? Ist 'said/told' korrekt verwendet?
items.e: 'Said' + backshift ('love' → 'loved') + pronoun shift ('I' → 'she', 'my' → 'her').
items.ed: 'Said' + Zeitverschiebung ('love' → 'loved') + Pronomenverschiebung ('I' → 'she', 'my' → 'her').

items.p: "Do you play football?" — He asked me ___ I played football.
a: if
whether
items.h: 'Do you play football?' is a yes/no question. What word introduces reported yes/no questions?
items.hd: 'Do you play football?' ist eine Ja/Nein-Frage. Welches Wort leitet indirekte Ja/Nein-Fragen ein?
items.e: Reported yes/no questions use 'if' or 'whether': He asked me if/whether I played football.
items.ed: Indirekte Ja/Nein-Fragen verwenden 'if' oder 'whether': He asked me if/whether I played football.

items.p: "Where is the library?" — She asked me where the library ___.
a: was
items.h: Reported questions use statement word order. 'Is' backshifts to which form?
items.hd: Indirekte Fragen verwenden Aussagesatzstellung. 'Is' wird zurückverschoben zu welcher Form?
items.e: 'Where is the library?' → 'She asked where the library was.' Statement order + backshift.
items.ed: 'Where is the library?' → 'She asked where the library was.' Aussagesatzstellung + Zeitverschiebung.

items.p: "What are you doing?" — My mum asked me what I ___ doing.
a: was
items.h: 'Are doing' (present continuous) shifts to past continuous. What form of 'be' matches 'I'?
items.hd: 'Are doing' (Present Continuous) wird zu Past Continuous zurückverschoben. Welche Form von 'be' passt zu 'I'?
items.e: Backshift: 'are doing' → 'was doing'. Pronoun: 'you' → 'I'. Statement word order.
items.ed: Zeitverschiebung: 'are doing' → 'was doing'. Pronomen: 'you' → 'I'. Aussagesatzstellung.

items.p: "Have you ever been to Paris?" — She wanted to know ___ I ___ ever been to Paris.
a: if ... had
whether ... had
items.h: Yes/no question = 'if/whether'. Present perfect ('have been') shifts to past perfect ('had been').
items.hd: Ja/Nein-Frage = 'if/whether'. Present Perfect ('have been') wird zu Past Perfect ('had been') zurückverschoben.
items.e: 'If/whether' for the yes/no question. 'Have been' → 'had been' (backshift).
items.ed: 'If/whether' für die Ja/Nein-Frage. 'Have been' → 'had been' (Zeitverschiebung).

items.p: "How much does this cost?" — I asked the shop assistant how much it ___.
a: cost
items.h: Reported questions: no 'does/did'. Statement order: subject + verb. 'Cost' has the same past form.
items.hd: Indirekte Fragen: kein 'does/did'. Aussagesatzstellung: Subjekt + Verb. 'Cost' hat dieselbe Vergangenheitsform.
items.e: 'How much does this cost?' → 'how much it cost'. No 'did', statement order, and 'cost' stays the same.
items.ed: 'How much does this cost?' → 'how much it cost'. Kein 'did', Aussagesatzstellung, und 'cost' bleibt gleich.

items.p: "Will you come to my party on Saturday?" — He asked me ___ I ___ come to ___ party on Saturday.
a: if ... would ... his
whether ... would ... his
items.h: Yes/no question = 'if/whether'. 'Will' → 'would'. 'My' (his party) → 'his'.
items.hd: Ja/Nein-Frage = 'if/whether'. 'Will' → 'would'. 'My' (seine Party) → 'his'.
items.e: 'If/whether' + 'would' (backshift from 'will') + 'his' (pronoun shift from 'my').
items.ed: 'If/whether' + 'would' (Zeitverschiebung von 'will') + 'his' (Pronomenverschiebung von 'my').

items.p: Which is the correct reported form of: "Where do you live?"
items.c: She asked me where I lived.
a: She asked me where I lived.
ds: She asked me where did I live.
She asked me where do I live.
She asked me where I did live.
items.h: Reported questions use STATEMENT word order: wh-word + subject + verb. No 'do/did'.
items.hd: Indirekte Fragen verwenden AUSSAGESATZSTELLUNG: Fragewort + Subjekt + Verb. Kein 'do/did'.
items.e: Statement order: 'where I lived' (not 'where did I live'). Backshift: 'live' → 'lived'.
items.ed: Aussagesatzstellung: 'where I lived' (nicht 'where did I live'). Zeitverschiebung: 'live' → 'lived'.

items.p: Original: "Can I borrow your pen?" — Which reported question is correct?
items.c: He asked if he could borrow my pen.
a: He asked if he could borrow my pen.
ds: He asked if could he borrow my pen.
He asked that he could borrow my pen.
He asked if he can borrow my pen.
items.h: Yes/no question = 'if'. Statement order after 'if'. 'Can' → 'could'. 'Your' → 'my'.
items.hd: Ja/Nein-Frage = 'if'. Aussagesatzstellung nach 'if'. 'Can' → 'could'. 'Your' → 'my'.
items.e: 'If' + statement order (he could) + backshift (can→could) + pronoun shift (your→my).
items.ed: 'If' + Aussagesatzstellung (he could) + Zeitverschiebung (can→could) + Pronomenverschiebung (your→my).

items.p: Original: "Why didn't you come to the party?" — Which reported form is correct?
items.c: She asked me why I hadn't come to the party.
a: She asked me why I hadn't come to the party.
ds: She asked me why didn't I come to the party.
She asked me why I didn't come to the party.
She asked me why hadn't I come to the party.
items.h: Statement order (why I + verb). Backshift: 'didn't come' → 'hadn't come'.
items.hd: Aussagesatzstellung (why I + Verb). Zeitverschiebung: 'didn't come' → 'hadn't come'.
items.e: Statement order: 'why I hadn't come'. Backshift: past simple negative → past perfect negative.
items.ed: Aussagesatzstellung: 'why I hadn't come'. Zeitverschiebung: Past Simple verneint → Past Perfect verneint.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He asked me where did I buy my shoes.
items.c: He asked me where I had bought my shoes.
a: He asked me where I had bought my shoes.
He asked me where I bought my shoes.
He asked me where I had bought my shoes
items.h: Reported questions use STATEMENT word order. Remove 'did' and put the verb in the right tense.
items.hd: Indirekte Fragen verwenden AUSSAGESATZSTELLUNG. Entferne 'did' und setze das Verb in die richtige Zeitform.
items.e: Statement order: 'where I bought/had bought' (not 'where did I buy'). No auxiliary 'did' in reported questions.
items.ed: Aussagesatzstellung: 'where I bought/had bought' (nicht 'where did I buy'). Kein Hilfsverb 'did' in indirekten Fragen.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She asked me that I liked chocolate.
items.c: She asked me if I liked chocolate.
a: She asked me if I liked chocolate.
She asked me whether I liked chocolate.
She asked me if I liked chocolate
items.h: 'Asked' + 'that' is wrong for questions. What word introduces reported yes/no questions?
items.hd: 'Asked' + 'that' ist falsch für Fragen. Welches Wort leitet indirekte Ja/Nein-Fragen ein?
items.e: Reported questions use 'if' or 'whether', not 'that'. 'That' is for reported statements.
items.ed: Indirekte Fragen verwenden 'if' oder 'whether', nicht 'that'. 'That' ist für indirekte Aussagen.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The teacher asked us what were we doing.
items.c: The teacher asked us what we were doing.
a: The teacher asked us what we were doing.
The teacher asked us what we were doing
items.h: After 'what', should the subject come before or after 'were' in a reported question?
items.hd: Nach 'what', sollte das Subjekt vor oder nach 'were' in einer indirekten Frage stehen?
items.e: Statement order in reported questions: 'what we were doing' (not 'what were we doing').
items.ed: Aussagesatzstellung in indirekten Fragen: 'what we were doing' (nicht 'what were we doing').

items.p: A tourist asked you: "Is there a supermarket near here?" Report the question. Start with: The tourist asked me...
items.c: The tourist asked me if there was a supermarket near there.
a: The tourist asked me if there was a supermarket near there.
The tourist asked me whether there was a supermarket near there.
The tourist asked me if there was a supermarket nearby.
items.h: Yes/no question = 'if/whether'. 'Is there' → 'there was' (backshift + statement order). 'Here' → 'there'.
items.hd: Ja/Nein-Frage = 'if/whether'. 'Is there' → 'there was' (Zeitverschiebung + Aussagesatzstellung). 'Here' → 'there'.
items.e: 'If there was' (yes/no + backshift + statement order). 'Here' → 'there' (place shift).
items.ed: 'If there was' (Ja/Nein + Zeitverschiebung + Aussagesatzstellung). 'Here' → 'there' (Ortsverschiebung).

items.p: Your friend asked you yesterday: "How many languages can you speak?" Report the question. Start with: My friend asked me...
items.c: My friend asked me how many languages I could speak.
a: My friend asked me how many languages I could speak.
My friend asked me how many languages I could speak
items.h: Wh-question: keep 'how many'. Statement order: 'I could speak' (not 'could I speak'). 'Can' → 'could'.
items.hd: Wh-Frage: behalte 'how many'. Aussagesatzstellung: 'I could speak' (nicht 'could I speak'). 'Can' → 'could'.
items.e: Wh-word + statement order + backshift: 'how many languages I could speak'.
items.ed: Fragewort + Aussagesatzstellung + Zeitverschiebung: 'how many languages I could speak'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Sie fragte mich, ob ich Geschwister habe.
items.c: She asked me if I had brothers and sisters.
a: She asked me if I had brothers and sisters.
She asked me whether I had brothers and sisters.
She asked me if I had siblings.
She asked me whether I had any siblings.
items.h: 'Fragte mich, ob' = 'asked me if/whether'. 'Habe' becomes 'had' (backshift).
items.hd: 'Fragte mich, ob' = 'asked me if/whether'. 'Habe' wird zu 'had' (Zeitverschiebung).
items.e: 'Ob' in German = 'if/whether' in English for reported yes/no questions. Backshift: 'have' → 'had'.
items.ed: 'Ob' auf Deutsch = 'if/whether' auf Englisch für indirekte Ja/Nein-Fragen. Zeitverschiebung: 'have' → 'had'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Er wollte wissen, wann der Zug abfaehrt.
items.c: He wanted to know when the train left.
a: He wanted to know when the train left.
He wanted to know when the train departed.
He wanted to know when the train was leaving.
items.h: 'Wollte wissen' = 'wanted to know'. 'Wann' = 'when'. Statement order after 'when'.
items.hd: 'Wollte wissen' = 'wanted to know'. 'Wann' = 'when'. Aussagesatzstellung nach 'when'.
items.e: 'Wanted to know when the train left' — statement word order, backshift from 'leaves' to 'left'.
items.ed: 'Wanted to know when the train left' — Aussagesatzstellung, Zeitverschiebung von 'leaves' zu 'left'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: asked / she / me / if / liked / I / music
items.c: She asked me if I liked music.
a: She asked me if I liked music.
She asked me if I liked music
items.h: Subject + asked + person + if + subject + verb. Statement word order after 'if'.
items.hd: Subjekt + asked + Person + if + Subjekt + Verb. Aussagesatzstellung nach 'if'.
items.e: Reported yes/no question: She asked me if I liked music. Statement order after 'if'.
items.ed: Indirekte Ja/Nein-Frage: She asked me if I liked music. Aussagesatzstellung nach 'if'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: teacher / asked / the / what / us / had / we / the / done / weekend / at
items.c: The teacher asked us what we had done at the weekend.
a: The teacher asked us what we had done at the weekend.
The teacher asked us what we had done at the weekend
items.h: Subject + asked + person + wh-word + subject + had + past participle. Statement order.
items.hd: Subjekt + asked + Person + Fragewort + Subjekt + had + Past Participle. Aussagesatzstellung.
items.e: Reported wh-question: asked us what we had done (statement order, past perfect backshift).
items.ed: Indirekte Wh-Frage: asked us what we had done (Aussagesatzstellung, Past Perfect Zeitverschiebung).

items.p: Match each direct question with its correct reported form. 1: "Do you like cats?" 2: "Where is the station?" 3: "Can you help me?" 4: "What did you eat?"
ds: a: He asked where the station was.
b: He asked what I had eaten.
c: He asked if I liked cats.
d: He asked if I could help him.
items.h: Yes/no questions use 'if'. Wh-questions keep the wh-word. All use statement word order.
items.hd: Ja/Nein-Fragen verwenden 'if'. Wh-Fragen behalten das Fragewort. Alle verwenden Aussagesatzstellung.
items.e: 1→c: if + liked (backshift). 2→a: where + was (backshift). 3→d: if + could (backshift). 4→b: what + had eaten (backshift).
items.ed: 1→c: if + liked. 2→a: where + was. 3→d: if + could. 4→b: what + had eaten. Alle mit Zeitverschiebung.

items.p: Your new neighbour said: "I have a dog." You want to know the dog's name. Report your question starting with: I asked her...
items.c: I asked her what her dog's name was.
a: I asked her what her dog's name was.
I asked her what the name of her dog was.
I asked her what her dog was called.
items.h: Wh-question ('what') + statement word order + backshift. 'Her' (not 'your').
items.hd: Wh-Frage ('what') + Aussagesatzstellung + Zeitverschiebung. 'Her' (nicht 'your').
items.e: Reported wh-question with statement order: 'what her dog's name was'. Backshift: 'is' → 'was'.
items.ed: Indirekte Wh-Frage mit Aussagesatzstellung: 'what her dog's name was'. Zeitverschiebung: 'is' → 'was'.

items.p: After I ___ (eat) breakfast, I left for school.
a: had eaten
items.h: 'After' introduces the FIRST action. The first action in a past sequence needs past perfect.
items.hd: 'After' leitet die ERSTE Handlung ein. Die erste Handlung in einer vergangenen Reihenfolge braucht Past Perfect.
items.e: 'After' + past perfect (had eaten) shows the completed earlier action. 'Left' (past simple) is the later action.
items.ed: 'After' + Past Perfect (had eaten) zeigt die abgeschlossene frühere Handlung. 'Left' (Past Simple) ist die spätere Handlung.

items.p: She didn't recognise him ___ he had changed so much.
a: because
as
items.h: Why didn't she recognise him? Because he had changed. Which connector shows a reason?
items.hd: Warum erkannte sie ihn nicht? Weil er sich verändert hatte. Welcher Konnektor zeigt einen Grund?
items.e: 'Because' connects the reason (he had changed) to the result (she didn't recognise him).
items.ed: 'Because' verbindet den Grund (er hatte sich verändert) mit dem Ergebnis (sie erkannte ihn nicht).

items.p: ___ she had locked the door, she realised her keys were inside.
a: After
items.h: She locked the door first. Then she realised the keys were inside. Which time connector fits?
items.hd: Sie schloss zuerst die Tür ab. Dann bemerkte sie, dass die Schlüssel drinnen waren. Welcher Zeitkonnektor passt?
items.e: 'After' + past perfect (had locked) shows the first completed action, then the realisation (past simple).
items.ed: 'After' + Past Perfect (had locked) zeigt die erste abgeschlossene Handlung, dann die Erkenntnis (Past Simple).

items.p: He didn't go to the party ___ he hadn't been invited.
a: because
as
since
items.h: Why didn't he go? He hadn't been invited. Which connector shows the reason?
items.hd: Warum ging er nicht? Er war nicht eingeladen worden. Welcher Konnektor zeigt den Grund?
items.e: 'Because' introduces the reason (not being invited) for the result (not going to the party).
items.ed: 'Because' leitet den Grund (nicht eingeladen) für das Ergebnis (nicht zur Party gehen) ein.

items.p: ___ he had saved enough money, he bought a new guitar.
a: As soon as
Once
When
items.h: He saved enough money first. Then immediately after, he bought a guitar. Which connector shows 'immediately after'?
items.hd: Er sparte zuerst genug Geld. Dann kaufte er sofort danach eine Gitarre. Welcher Konnektor zeigt 'sofort danach'?
items.e: 'As soon as' + past perfect shows the trigger event. The buying (past simple) followed immediately.
items.ed: 'As soon as' + Past Perfect zeigt das auslösende Ereignis. Der Kauf (Past Simple) folgte sofort.

items.p: ___ they had checked in at the hotel, they ___ (go) to the beach. They had a great time ___ the weather was beautiful.
a: After ... went ... because
Once ... went ... because
items.h: Three connectors: one for time sequence, one for the later action's tense, one for the reason.
items.hd: Drei Konnektoren: einer für die Zeitfolge, einer für die Zeitform der späteren Handlung, einer für den Grund.
items.e: 'After' + past perfect (checking in), past simple (went) for the later action, 'because' for the reason.
items.ed: 'After' + Past Perfect (Einchecken), Past Simple (went) für die spätere Handlung, 'because' für den Grund.

items.p: Choose the correct connector: ___ he had done his homework, he played video games.
a: After
items.h: He finished homework first. Then he played games. Which connector shows the order?
items.hd: Er machte zuerst die Hausaufgaben fertig. Dann spielte er Spiele. Welcher Konnektor zeigt die Reihenfolge?
items.e: 'After' + past perfect shows the earlier completed action, then past simple for the later one.
items.ed: 'After' + Past Perfect zeigt die frühere abgeschlossene Handlung, dann Past Simple für die spätere.

items.p: Which sentence correctly uses 'although' with past perfect?
items.c: Although he had studied hard, he didn't pass the test.
a: Although he had studied hard, he didn't pass the test.
ds: Although he had studied hard, he passed the test.
Although he studied hard, he didn't pass the test.
Although he was studying hard, he hadn't passed the test.
items.h: 'Although' shows contrast (unexpected result). Past perfect for the earlier action.
items.hd: 'Although' zeigt Gegensatz (unerwartetes Ergebnis). Past Perfect für die frühere Handlung.
items.e: 'Although' + past perfect (had studied) for the earlier action. Past simple (didn't pass) for the unexpected result.
items.ed: 'Although' + Past Perfect (had studied) für die frühere Handlung. Past Simple (didn't pass) für das unerwartete Ergebnis.

items.p: Choose the correct option: ___ I had never travelled abroad, I was nervous about the flight.
a: Since
Because
items.h: Never travelling is the REASON for the nervousness. Which connector shows reason?
items.hd: Nie gereist zu sein ist der GRUND für die Nervosität. Welcher Konnektor zeigt den Grund?
items.e: 'Since/Because' + past perfect gives the reason. 'Since' here means 'because', not 'since (time)'.
items.ed: 'Since/Because' + Past Perfect gibt den Grund an. 'Since' bedeutet hier 'weil', nicht 'seit (Zeit)'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: After we arrived at the cinema, the film started.
items.c: After we had arrived at the cinema, the film started.
a: After we had arrived at the cinema, the film started.
The film started after we had arrived at the cinema.
After we had arrived, the film started.
items.h: 'After' introduces the FIRST action. The first action needs past perfect to show the sequence clearly.
items.hd: 'After' leitet die ERSTE Handlung ein. Die erste Handlung braucht Past Perfect, um die Reihenfolge klar zu zeigen.
items.e: With 'after', the earlier action uses past perfect: 'had arrived' (not just 'arrived').
items.ed: Mit 'after' verwendet die frühere Handlung Past Perfect: 'had arrived' (nicht nur 'arrived').

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She was happy because she has got a good mark.
items.c: She was happy because she had got a good mark.
a: She was happy because she had got a good mark.
She was happy because she had gotten a good mark.
She was happy because she had got a good mark
items.h: 'Was happy' is past simple. The reason happened BEFORE she was happy. Use past perfect, not present perfect.
items.hd: 'Was happy' ist Past Simple. Der Grund passierte VOR der Freude. Verwende Past Perfect, nicht Present Perfect.
items.e: Both events are in the past. The cause (getting the mark) was earlier, so use 'had got', not 'has got'.
items.ed: Beide Ereignisse liegen in der Vergangenheit. Die Ursache (die Note bekommen) war früher, also verwende 'had got', nicht 'has got'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Although he had practised every day, but he didn't win the competition.
items.c: Although he had practised every day, he didn't win the competition.
a: Although he had practised every day, he didn't win the competition.
Although he had practised every day, he didn't win the competition
He had practised every day, but he didn't win the competition.
items.h: 'Although' and 'but' cannot be used together. Remove one of them.
items.hd: 'Although' und 'but' können nicht zusammen verwendet werden. Entferne eines davon.
items.e: 'Although' already shows contrast, so 'but' is redundant. Use 'Although..., [result]' OR '[clause], but [result]'.
items.ed: 'Although' zeigt bereits den Gegensatz, 'but' ist überflüssig. Verwende 'Although..., [Ergebnis]' ODER '[Satz], but [Ergebnis]'.

items.p: Two things happened: 1) The rain stopped. 2) We went for a walk. Write one sentence using 'as soon as'.
items.c: As soon as the rain had stopped, we went for a walk.
a: As soon as the rain had stopped, we went for a walk.
We went for a walk as soon as the rain had stopped.
As soon as it had stopped raining, we went for a walk.
items.h: 'As soon as' + the first action (past perfect) + the second action (past simple).
items.hd: 'As soon as' + die erste Handlung (Past Perfect) + die zweite Handlung (Past Simple).
items.e: 'As soon as' + past perfect (had stopped) for the trigger event. Past simple (went) for the resulting action.
items.ed: 'As soon as' + Past Perfect (had stopped) für das auslösende Ereignis. Past Simple (went) für die resultierende Handlung.

items.p: Tom studied very hard. He still failed the exam. This was surprising. Write one sentence using 'although'.
items.c: Although Tom had studied very hard, he still failed the exam.
a: Although Tom had studied very hard, he still failed the exam.
Tom still failed the exam although he had studied very hard.
Although Tom had studied very hard, he failed the exam.
items.h: 'Although' introduces the unexpected background. The studying came first (past perfect), failing was the surprising result.
items.hd: 'Although' leitet den unerwarteten Hintergrund ein. Das Lernen kam zuerst (Past Perfect), das Durchfallen war das überraschende Ergebnis.
items.e: 'Although' + past perfect (had studied) for the background, past simple (failed) for the unexpected result.
items.ed: 'Although' + Past Perfect (had studied) für den Hintergrund, Past Simple (failed) für das unerwartete Ergebnis.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Bevor sie ins Bett ging, hatte sie ihre Zaehne geputzt.
items.c: Before she went to bed, she had brushed her teeth.
a: Before she went to bed, she had brushed her teeth.
She had brushed her teeth before she went to bed.
Before she went to bed she had brushed her teeth.
items.h: 'Bevor' = 'before'. 'Ging' = 'went' (later action, past simple). 'Hatte geputzt' = 'had brushed' (earlier action, past perfect).
items.hd: 'Bevor' = 'before'. 'Ging' = 'went' (spätere Handlung, Past Simple). 'Hatte geputzt' = 'had brushed' (frühere Handlung, Past Perfect).
items.e: 'Before' + past simple (went) for the later action. Past perfect (had brushed) for the earlier action.
items.ed: 'Before' + Past Simple (went) für die spätere Handlung. Past Perfect (had brushed) für die frühere Handlung.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Obwohl er das Buch schon gelesen hatte, schaute er sich trotzdem den Film an.
items.c: Although he had already read the book, he still watched the film.
a: Although he had already read the book, he still watched the film.
Although he had already read the book, he watched the film anyway.
He still watched the film although he had already read the book.
Even though he had already read the book, he still watched the film.
items.h: 'Obwohl' = 'although'. 'Hatte gelesen' = 'had read' (past perfect). 'Trotzdem' = 'still/anyway'.
items.hd: 'Obwohl' = 'although'. 'Hatte gelesen' = 'had read' (Past Perfect). 'Trotzdem' = 'still/anyway'.
items.e: 'Although' + past perfect (had read). Past simple (watched) for the result. 'Trotzdem' = 'still/anyway'.
items.ed: 'Although' + Past Perfect (had read). Past Simple (watched) für das Ergebnis. 'Trotzdem' = 'still/anyway'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: as / as / the / had / soon / bell / rung / everyone / left / the / classroom
items.c: As soon as the bell had rung, everyone left the classroom.
a: As soon as the bell had rung, everyone left the classroom.
Everyone left the classroom as soon as the bell had rung.
As soon as the bell had rung everyone left the classroom.
items.h: 'As soon as' comes first, then the past perfect event (bell ringing), then the reaction (past simple).
items.hd: 'As soon as' kommt zuerst, dann das Past Perfect-Ereignis (Klingeln), dann die Reaktion (Past Simple).
items.e: 'As soon as' + past perfect (had rung) for the trigger. Past simple (left) for the reaction.
items.ed: 'As soon as' + Past Perfect (had rung) für den Auslöser. Past Simple (left) für die Reaktion.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: although / had / they / early / arrived / were / no / there / seats / left
items.c: Although they had arrived early, there were no seats left.
a: Although they had arrived early, there were no seats left.
Although they had arrived early there were no seats left.
There were no seats left although they had arrived early.
items.h: 'Although' starts the unexpected background (past perfect). The surprising result follows (past simple).
items.hd: 'Although' leitet den unerwarteten Hintergrund ein (Past Perfect). Das überraschende Ergebnis folgt (Past Simple).
items.e: 'Although' + past perfect (had arrived) + surprising result: 'there were no seats left'.
items.ed: 'Although' + Past Perfect (had arrived) + überraschendes Ergebnis: 'there were no seats left'.

items.p: Match each sentence beginning with the correct ending. 1: After the teacher had explained the exercise, 2: Although they had practised all week, 3: Because she hadn't eaten all day, 4: As soon as the match had finished,
ds: d: they still lost the game.
items.h: Match connectors: 'after' = next action, 'although' = unexpected result, 'because' = reason, 'as soon as' = immediate reaction.
items.hd: Ordne Konnektoren zu: 'after' = nächste Handlung, 'although' = unerwartetes Ergebnis, 'because' = Grund, 'as soon as' = sofortige Reaktion.
items.e: 1+c: after explaining → started working. 2+d: although practised → lost (unexpected). 3+a: because no food → hungry. 4+b: as soon as finished → shook hands.
items.ed: 1+c: nach Erklärung → begannen zu arbeiten. 2+d: obwohl geübt → verloren (unerwartet). 3+a: weil nichts gegessen → hungrig. 4+b: sobald beendet → gaben sich die Hand.

items.p: Lisa came to school very late. She missed the first two lessons. She was upset. What happened?
items.c: She was upset because she had missed the first two lessons.
a: She was upset because she had missed the first two lessons.
ds: She was upset although she had missed the first two lessons.
She was upset before she had missed the first two lessons.
She was upset so that she had missed the first two lessons.
items.h: Missing lessons is the REASON for being upset. Which connector shows a cause?
items.hd: Das Verpassen der Stunden ist der GRUND für die Verärgerung. Welcher Konnektor zeigt einen Grund?
items.e: 'Because' + past perfect (had missed) gives the reason for being upset. Past simple (was) for the result.
items.ed: 'Because' + Past Perfect (had missed) gibt den Grund für die Verärgerung. Past Simple (was) für das Ergebnis.

items.p: Please speak ___ — the baby is sleeping. (quiet)
a: quietly
items.h: We need to say HOW someone should speak. Add -ly to the adjective 'quiet'.
items.hd: Wir müssen sagen, WIE jemand sprechen soll. Füge -ly an das Adjektiv 'quiet' an.
items.e: 'Speak' is a verb, so we need the adverb 'quietly' (not the adjective 'quiet'). quiet → quietly.
items.ed: 'Speak' ist ein Verb, also brauchen wir das Adverb 'quietly' (nicht das Adjektiv 'quiet'). quiet → quietly.

items.p: The dog was barking ___ at the stranger. (angry)
a: angrily
items.h: For adjectives ending in -y, change the -y to -ily: angry → ?
items.hd: Bei Adjektiven, die auf -y enden, wird -y zu -ily: angry → ?
items.e: Adjectives ending in -y change to -ily: angry → angrily, happy → happily, easy → easily.
items.ed: Adjektive, die auf -y enden, werden zu -ily: angry → angrily, happy → happily, easy → easily.

items.p: She worked ___ on her project and still had problems. (hard)
a: hard
items.h: 'Hard' as an adverb means 'with great effort'. Be careful: 'hardly' means something completely different!
items.hd: 'Hard' als Adverb bedeutet 'mit grosser Anstrengung'. Achtung: 'hardly' bedeutet etwas ganz anderes!
items.e: 'Hard' is an irregular adverb (same form as the adjective). She worked hard = She worked with great effort. 'Hardly' = almost not!
items.ed: 'Hard' ist ein unregelmäßiges Adverb (gleiche Form wie das Adjektiv). She worked hard = Sie arbeitete hart. 'Hardly' = kaum!

items.p: He answered all the questions ___ and got the best mark. (correct)
a: correctly
items.h: How did he answer? We need an adverb. Add -ly to 'correct'.
items.hd: Wie hat er geantwortet? Wir brauchen ein Adverb. Füge -ly an 'correct' an.
items.e: 'Correctly' is the adverb of 'correct'. He answered correctly = He answered in a correct way.
items.ed: 'Correctly' ist das Adverb von 'correct'. He answered correctly = Er antwortete richtig.

items.p: The team played really ___ in the first half but ___ in the second. (good / terrible)
a: well ... terribly
items.h: Both words modify 'played' (a verb), so both need to be adverbs. Remember: good → well (irregular!).
items.hd: Beide Wörter beschreiben 'played' (ein Verb), also müssen beide Adverbien sein. Merke: good → well (unregelmäßig!).
items.e: good → well (irregular). terrible → terribly (regular -ly). Both modify the verb 'played'.
items.ed: good → well (unregelmäßig). terrible → terribly (regelmäßig -ly). Beide beschreiben das Verb 'played'.

items.p: Don't arrive ___ for the interview — it makes a bad impression. (late)
a: late
items.h: 'Late' as an adverb means 'not on time'. It does NOT change to 'lately' (which means 'recently')!
items.hd: 'Late' als Adverb bedeutet 'zu spät / nicht pünktlich'. Es wird NICHT zu 'lately' (das bedeutet 'in letzter Zeit')!
items.e: 'Late' is an irregular adverb — same form as the adjective when it means 'not on time'. 'Lately' = recently (different meaning!).
items.ed: 'Late' ist ein unregelmäßiges Adverb — gleiche Form wie das Adjektiv, wenn es 'zu spät' bedeutet. 'Lately' = in letzter Zeit (andere Bedeutung!).

items.p: Choose the correct sentence: The teacher explained the rules ___.
a: clearly
items.h: Which word is the correct adverb form of 'clear'?
items.hd: Welches Wort ist die richtige Adverbform von 'clear'?
items.e: 'Clearly' is the adverb of 'clear'. It tells us HOW the teacher explained.
items.ed: 'Clearly' ist das Adverb von 'clear'. Es sagt uns, WIE der Lehrer erklärt hat.

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
a: She dances really well.
items.h: What is the adverb form of 'good'? It's irregular!
items.hd: Was ist die Adverbform von 'good'? Sie ist unregelmäßig!
items.e: good → well (NOT goodly!). 'Well' is the adverb; 'good' is the adjective.
items.ed: good → well (NICHT goodly!). 'Well' ist das Adverb; 'good' ist das Adjektiv.

items.p: He studied ___ for the test, but he ___ passed.
a: hard ... hardly
items.h: 'With effort' = hard. 'Almost not' = hardly. Which goes where?
items.hd: 'Mit Anstrengung' = hard. 'Kaum' = hardly. Was passt wohin?
items.e: He studied hard (= with great effort) but he hardly passed (= almost didn't pass). Two different meanings!
items.ed: He studied hard (= mit grosser Anstrengung), aber he hardly passed (= bestand kaum). Zwei verschiedene Bedeutungen!

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He speaks French fluent.
a: He speaks French fluently.
He speaks French fluently
He fluently speaks French.
items.h: 'Speaks' is a verb. It needs an adverb, not an adjective.
items.hd: 'Speaks' ist ein Verb. Es braucht ein Adverb, kein Adjektiv.
items.e: 'Fluent' is an adjective. We need the adverb 'fluently' because it describes HOW he speaks.
items.ed: 'Fluent' ist ein Adjektiv. Wir brauchen das Adverb 'fluently', weil es beschreibt, WIE er spricht.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: We haven't seen Jake lately. He has been working hardly.
items.c: We haven't seen Jake lately. He has been working hard.
a: We haven't seen Jake lately. He has been working hard.
We haven't seen Jake lately. He has been working hard
items.h: 'Hardly' means 'almost not'. If Jake is busy, he must be working with effort, not 'almost not working'.
items.hd: 'Hardly' bedeutet 'kaum'. Wenn Jake beschäftigt ist, muss er hart arbeiten, nicht 'kaum arbeiten'.
items.e: 'Working hardly' = almost not working. 'Working hard' = with great effort. The context shows Jake is very busy.
items.ed: 'Working hardly' = kaum arbeiten. 'Working hard' = hart arbeiten. Der Kontext zeigt, dass Jake sehr beschäftigt ist.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She sings beautiful but dances bad.
items.c: She sings beautifully but dances badly.
a: She sings beautifully but dances badly.
She sings beautifully but dances badly
items.h: Both 'sings' and 'dances' are verbs. They both need adverbs, not adjectives.
items.hd: Sowohl 'sings' als auch 'dances' sind Verben. Beide brauchen Adverbien, keine Adjektive.
items.e: beautiful → beautifully (regular -ly). bad → badly (regular -ly). Both modify verbs, so both need adverb forms.
items.ed: beautiful → beautifully (regelmäßig -ly). bad → badly (regelmäßig -ly). Beide beschreiben Verben, also brauchen beide die Adverbform.

items.p: Rewrite using an adverb: She is a careful driver. → She drives ___.
a: She drives carefully.
She drives carefully
She drives very carefully.
items.h: Change the adjective 'careful' to an adverb. Just add -ly.
items.hd: Ändere das Adjektiv 'careful' in ein Adverb. Füge einfach -ly an.
items.e: 'Careful' (adjective) → 'carefully' (adverb). She is a careful driver = She drives carefully.
items.ed: 'Careful' (Adjektiv) → 'carefully' (Adverb). She is a careful driver = She drives carefully.

items.p: Rewrite using an adverb: He is a lazy worker. → He works ___.
a: He works lazily.
He works lazily
items.h: 'Lazy' ends in -y. Remember the rule: change -y to -ily.
items.hd: 'Lazy' endet auf -y. Erinnere dich an die Regel: ändere -y zu -ily.
items.e: lazy → lazily. Adjectives ending in -y change to -ily to form the adverb.
items.ed: lazy → lazily. Adjektive, die auf -y enden, werden zu -ily für die Adverbform.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Sie hat die Frage richtig beantwortet.
a: She answered the question correctly.
She correctly answered the question.
She answered the question right.
items.h: 'richtig' as an adverb = correctly. Use the adverb form, not the adjective 'correct'.
items.hd: 'richtig' als Adverb = correctly. Verwende die Adverbform, nicht das Adjektiv 'correct'.
items.e: 'richtig' → correctly (adverb, modifying the verb 'answered'). She answered the question correctly.
items.ed: 'richtig' → correctly (Adverb, beschreibt das Verb 'answered'). She answered the question correctly.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Er ist immer spät dran, aber in letzter Zeit kommt er noch später.
items.c: He is always late, but lately he has been coming even later.
a: He is always late, but lately he has been coming even later.
He's always late, but lately he's been coming even later.
He is always late but lately he has been arriving even later.
items.h: 'spät dran' = late. 'in letzter Zeit' = lately (NOT late!). Two different meanings!
items.hd: 'spät dran' = late. 'in letzter Zeit' = lately (NICHT late!). Zwei verschiedene Bedeutungen!
items.e: 'Late' = not on time. 'Lately' = recently. Don't confuse them! He is late (not on time), but lately (recently) he's been even later.
items.ed: 'Late' = zu spät. 'Lately' = in letzter Zeit. Nicht verwechseln! He is late (zu spät), but lately (in letzter Zeit) kommt er noch später.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: nervously / she / the / answered / phone
a: She answered the phone nervously.
She nervously answered the phone.
Nervously, she answered the phone.
items.h: Adverbs of manner usually go AFTER the verb and its object.
items.hd: Adverbien der Art und Weise stehen meistens NACH dem Verb und seinem Objekt.
items.e: She (subject) + answered (verb) + the phone (object) + nervously (adverb of manner, usually after the object).
items.ed: She (Subjekt) + answered (Verb) + the phone (Objekt) + nervously (Adverb der Art und Weise, meistens nach dem Objekt).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: well / surprisingly / the / team / played
a: The team played surprisingly well.
Surprisingly, the team played well.
items.h: 'Surprisingly' modifies 'well'. Both come after the verb 'played'.
items.hd: 'Surprisingly' beschreibt 'well'. Beide kommen nach dem Verb 'played'.
items.e: Subject + verb + adverb: The team played surprisingly well. ('Surprisingly' intensifies 'well'.)
items.ed: Subjekt + Verb + Adverb: The team played surprisingly well. ('Surprisingly' verstärkt 'well'.)

items.p: Match each sentence with the correct adverb:
ds: 1: He trains ___ every day.|2: She could ___ lift the box.|3: The bus arrived ___.|4: He spoke ___ about the problem.|5: She finished the race ___.|6: He has been sleeping ___ all week.
a: late|b: seriously|c: fast|d: hard|e: hardly|f: badly
items.h: Watch out for 'hard' (= with effort) vs 'hardly' (= almost not). And 'late' (= not on time) vs 'lately' (= recently).
items.hd: Achte auf 'hard' (= mit Anstrengung) vs 'hardly' (= kaum). Und 'late' (= zu spät) vs 'lately' (= in letzter Zeit).
items.e: trains hard (effort), hardly lift (almost couldn't), arrived late (not on time), spoke seriously, finished fast, sleeping badly.
items.ed: trains hard (Anstrengung), hardly lift (konnte kaum), arrived late (zu spät), spoke seriously (ernst), finished fast (schnell), sleeping badly (schlecht).

items.p: Tom speaks Spanish fluently. Ask about HOW Tom speaks Spanish.
a: How does Tom speak Spanish?
How well does Tom speak Spanish?
items.h: Use 'How' to ask about manner. Remember: question word + auxiliary + subject + base form.
items.hd: Verwende 'How', um nach der Art und Weise zu fragen. Merke: Fragewort + Hilfsverb + Subjekt + Grundform.
items.e: How + does + Tom + speak Spanish? The answer is: 'He speaks Spanish fluently.'
items.ed: How + does + Tom + speak Spanish? Die Antwort ist: 'He speaks Spanish fluently.'

items.p: You are from Vienna, ___ ?
a: aren't you
items.h: The sentence uses 'are'. Make it negative for the tag. What pronoun matches 'you'?
items.hd: Der Satz verwendet 'are'. Mache es negativ für den Anhang. Welches Pronomen passt zu 'you'?
items.e: Positive 'are' → negative 'aren't'. The pronoun stays 'you': aren't you.
items.ed: Positives 'are' → negatives 'aren't'. Das Pronomen bleibt 'you': aren't you.

items.p: She won't be at the meeting, ___ ?
a: will she
items.h: The sentence is negative ('won't'). The tag must be positive. Which auxiliary is used?
items.hd: Der Satz ist negativ ('won't'). Der Anhang muss positiv sein. Welches Hilfsverb wird verwendet?
items.e: Negative 'won't' → positive 'will'. Subject 'she' stays: 'will she'.
items.ed: Negatives 'won't' → positives 'will'. Subjekt 'she' bleibt: 'will she'.

items.p: Nobody told you about the party, ___ ?
a: did they
items.h: 'Nobody' is negative. The tag must be positive. 'Nobody' takes the pronoun 'they'.
items.hd: 'Nobody' ist negativ. Der Anhang muss positiv sein. 'Nobody' wird durch das Pronomen 'they' ersetzt.
items.e: 'Nobody' is a negative word, so the sentence is negative → positive tag. 'Nobody' → 'they'. Past tense 'told' → 'did they'.
items.ed: 'Nobody' ist ein negatives Wort, also ist der Satz negativ → positiver Anhang. 'Nobody' → 'they'. Vergangenheit 'told' → 'did they'.

items.p: Let's go to the cinema, ___ ?
a: shall we
items.h: 'Let's' is a special case. The question tag for 'Let's...' is always 'shall we'.
items.hd: 'Let's' ist ein Sonderfall. Der Anhang bei 'Let's...' ist immer 'shall we'.
items.e: 'Let's' (= Let us) always takes the tag 'shall we'. Let's go to the cinema, shall we?
items.ed: 'Let's' (= Let us) bekommt immer den Anhang 'shall we'. Let's go to the cinema, shall we?

items.p: Nothing happened at the party, ___ ?
a: did it
items.h: 'Nothing' is a negative word (like nobody/no one). The tag must be positive. 'Nothing' → 'it'.
items.hd: 'Nothing' ist ein negatives Wort (wie nobody/no one). Der Anhang muss positiv sein. 'Nothing' → 'it'.
items.e: 'Nothing' is negative → positive tag. 'Nothing' takes the pronoun 'it'. Past tense 'happened' → 'did it'.
items.ed: 'Nothing' ist negativ → positiver Anhang. 'Nothing' wird durch 'it' ersetzt. Vergangenheit 'happened' → 'did it'.

items.p: I am the oldest in the class, ___ ?
a: aren't I
items.h: This is a special case! The tag for 'I am...' is NOT 'am not I'. What irregular form is used?
items.hd: Das ist ein Sonderfall! Der Anhang bei 'I am...' ist NICHT 'am not I'. Welche unregelmäßige Form wird verwendet?
items.e: 'I am' takes the irregular tag 'aren't I' (NOT 'amn't I'!). This is the only case where 'aren't' goes with 'I'.
items.ed: 'I am' bekommt den unregelmäßigen Anhang 'aren't I' (NICHT 'amn't I'!). Dies ist der einzige Fall, in dem 'aren't' mit 'I' steht.

items.p: Your mum has already cooked dinner, ___ ?
a: hasn't she
items.h: The sentence uses 'has'. Positive sentence → negative tag. Which pronoun replaces 'your mum'?
items.hd: Der Satz verwendet 'has'. Positiver Satz → negativer Anhang. Welches Pronomen ersetzt 'your mum'?
items.e: 'Has' (present perfect) + positive → 'hasn't she'. Match the auxiliary and flip the polarity.
items.ed: 'Has' (Present Perfect) + positiv → 'hasn't she'. Gleiche das Hilfsverb an und drehe die Polarität um.

items.p: They didn't enjoy the film, ___ ?
a: did they
items.h: The sentence is negative ('didn't'). The tag must be positive and use the same auxiliary.
items.hd: Der Satz ist negativ ('didn't'). Der Anhang muss positiv sein und das gleiche Hilfsverb verwenden.
items.e: Negative 'didn't' → positive 'did'. Subject stays 'they': 'did they'.
items.ed: Negatives 'didn't' → positives 'did'. Subjekt bleibt 'they': 'did they'.

items.p: Everybody knows the answer, ___ ?
a: don't they
items.h: 'Everybody' is positive and takes the pronoun 'they'. The verb 'knows' is present simple → use 'do/don't'.
items.hd: 'Everybody' ist positiv und wird durch 'they' ersetzt. Das Verb 'knows' ist Present Simple → verwende 'do/don't'.
items.e: 'Everybody' = positive, pronoun 'they'. Present simple 'knows' → 'don't they' (positive → negative tag).
items.ed: 'Everybody' = positiv, Pronomen 'they'. Present Simple 'knows' → 'don't they' (positiv → negativer Anhang).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She likes pizza, doesn't it?
a: She likes pizza, doesn't she?
She likes pizza, doesn't she
items.h: The pronoun in the tag must match the subject. 'She' → which pronoun in the tag?
items.hd: Das Pronomen im Anhang muss zum Subjekt passen. 'She' → welches Pronomen im Anhang?
items.e: The subject is 'she', so the tag pronoun must be 'she' too: 'doesn't she', not 'doesn't it'.
items.ed: Das Subjekt ist 'she', also muss das Pronomen im Anhang auch 'she' sein: 'doesn't she', nicht 'doesn't it'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: They have been to London, didn't they?
items.c: They have been to London, haven't they?
a: They have been to London, haven't they?
They have been to London, haven't they
items.h: The auxiliary in the main sentence is 'have' (present perfect). The tag must use the same auxiliary.
items.hd: Das Hilfsverb im Hauptsatz ist 'have' (Present Perfect). Der Anhang muss das gleiche Hilfsverb verwenden.
items.e: The sentence uses 'have been' (present perfect), so the tag must use 'have': 'haven't they', NOT 'didn't they'.
items.ed: Der Satz verwendet 'have been' (Present Perfect), also muss der Anhang 'have' verwenden: 'haven't they', NICHT 'didn't they'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Nobody likes tests, do he?
a: Nobody likes tests, do they?
Nobody likes tests, do they
items.h: 'Nobody' is replaced by the pronoun 'they' in the tag, not 'he'.
items.hd: 'Nobody' wird im Anhang durch das Pronomen 'they' ersetzt, nicht 'he'.
items.e: 'Nobody' is negative → positive tag. The pronoun for 'nobody' is 'they' (not 'he'): 'do they'.
items.ed: 'Nobody' ist negativ → positiver Anhang. Das Pronomen für 'nobody' ist 'they' (nicht 'he'): 'do they'.

items.p: Add a question tag: We should leave early.
items.c: We should leave early, shouldn't we?
a: We should leave early, shouldn't we?
We should leave early, shouldn't we
items.h: The auxiliary is 'should'. Positive sentence → negative tag.
items.hd: Das Hilfsverb ist 'should'. Positiver Satz → negativer Anhang.
items.e: 'Should' (positive) → 'shouldn't we' (negative tag). The modal verb stays the same.
items.ed: 'Should' (positiv) → 'shouldn't we' (negativer Anhang). Das Modalverb bleibt gleich.

items.p: Add a question tag: Someone called you last night.
items.c: Someone called you last night, didn't they?
a: Someone called you last night, didn't they?
Someone called you last night, didn't they
items.h: 'Someone' is positive (unlike 'nobody'). It takes the pronoun 'they'. Past tense 'called' → 'did'.
items.hd: 'Someone' ist positiv (anders als 'nobody'). Es wird durch 'they' ersetzt. Vergangenheit 'called' → 'did'.
items.e: 'Someone' is positive → negative tag. Pronoun = 'they'. 'Called' (past simple) → 'didn't they'.
items.ed: 'Someone' ist positiv → negativer Anhang. Pronomen = 'they'. 'Called' (Past Simple) → 'didn't they'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Du magst Schokolade, oder? (present simple)
a: You like chocolate, don't you?
You like chocolate, don't you
items.h: 'oder?' at the end of a statement = question tag. 'Like' is present simple → 'do'.
items.hd: 'oder?' am Ende einer Aussage = Question Tag. 'Like' ist Present Simple → 'do'.
items.e: German 'oder?' / 'nicht wahr?' = English question tag. You like (positive, present simple) → don't you.
items.ed: Deutsches 'oder?' / 'nicht wahr?' = englischer Question Tag. You like (positiv, Present Simple) → don't you.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Niemand war dort, oder?
a: Nobody was there, were they?
Nobody was there, were they
No one was there, were they?
items.h: 'Niemand' = nobody (negative). The tag must be positive. 'Nobody' takes the pronoun 'they'.
items.hd: 'Niemand' = nobody (negativ). Der Anhang muss positiv sein. 'Nobody' wird durch 'they' ersetzt.
items.e: 'Nobody' is negative → positive tag. 'was' → 'were they'. We use 'they' as the pronoun for 'nobody'.
items.ed: 'Nobody' ist negativ → positiver Anhang. 'was' → 'were they'. 'They' ist das Pronomen für 'nobody'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order to form a question tag sentence: lovely / isn't / today / it / it / day / is / a / ,
items.c: It is a lovely day, isn't it?
a: It is a lovely day, isn't it?
It's a lovely day, isn't it?
items.h: First make the statement, then add the question tag with a comma.
items.hd: Bilde zuerst die Aussage, dann füge den Anhang mit einem Komma hinzu.
items.e: Statement: 'It is a lovely day' + question tag: 'isn't it?' Positive statement → negative tag.
items.ed: Aussage: 'It is a lovely day' + Anhang: 'isn't it?' Positiver Satz → negativer Anhang.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: we / go / swimming / , / shall / let's
a: Let's go swimming, shall we?
Let's go swimming, shall we
items.h: 'Let's' always takes the question tag 'shall we'.
items.hd: 'Let's' bekommt immer den Question Tag 'shall we'.
items.e: Let's + activity + , shall we? This is a fixed pattern for suggestions.
items.ed: Let's + Aktivität + , shall we? Das ist ein festes Muster für Vorschläge.

items.p: Match each statement with the correct question tag:
ds: 1: She can swim, ...|2: They won't come, ...|3: He doesn't like maths, ...|4: You were late, ...|5: We should go, ...|6: I am right, ...
a: shouldn't we?|b: weren't you?|c: aren't I?|d: will they?|e: can't she?|f: does he?
items.h: Match the auxiliary verb in the statement to the tag. Flip the polarity (positive → negative, or negative → positive).
items.hd: Ordne das Hilfsverb im Satz dem Anhang zu. Drehe die Polarität um (positiv → negativ oder negativ → positiv).
items.e: can→can't she, won't→will they, doesn't→does he, were→weren't you, should→shouldn't we, I am→aren't I (special!).
items.ed: can→can't she, won't→will they, doesn't→does he, were→weren't you, should→shouldn't we, I am→aren't I (Sonderfall!).

items.p: You think your friend has already been to Paris. Confirm with a question tag: You've been to Paris, ___?
a: haven't you
items.h: You've = You have (present perfect, positive). The tag needs to be negative with the same auxiliary.
items.hd: You've = You have (Present Perfect, positiv). Der Anhang muss negativ mit dem gleichen Hilfsverb sein.
items.e: 'You've been' = present perfect positive → 'haven't you' (negative tag with 'have').
items.ed: 'You've been' = Present Perfect positiv → 'haven't you' (negativer Anhang mit 'have').

items.p: The shop ___ (open) at 9 a.m. tomorrow.
a: opens
items.h: Shop opening times are fixed schedules. Which tense do we use for timetables and schedules?
items.hd: Öffnungszeiten sind feste Zeitpläne. Welche Zeitform verwenden wir für Fahrpläne und Zeitpläne?
items.e: Fixed schedules (shops, trains, schools) use present simple for future events: 'The shop opens at 9.'
items.ed: Feste Zeitpläne (Geschäfte, Züge, Schulen) verwenden Present Simple für zukünftige Ereignisse: 'The shop opens at 9.'

items.p: School ___ (finish) at 1:30 on Fridays.
a: finishes
items.h: This is a regular school schedule. Use present simple. Remember 3rd person singular!
items.hd: Das ist ein regelmäßiger Schulplan. Verwende Present Simple. Denke an die 3. Person Einzahl!
items.e: 'School' = it (3rd person singular), so we need 'finishes' (-es ending after -sh). Fixed schedule → present simple.
items.ed: 'School' = it (3. Person Einzahl), also brauchen wir 'finishes' (-es Endung nach -sh). Fester Zeitplan → Present Simple.

items.p: What time ___ the concert ___ (start) tonight?
a: does ... start
does the concert start
items.h: The concert has a fixed time. Questions about schedules use: does + subject + base verb.
items.hd: Das Konzert hat eine feste Uhrzeit. Fragen über Zeitpläne verwenden: does + Subjekt + Grundform.
items.e: Scheduled events → present simple. Question form: 'What time does the concert start?'
items.ed: Geplante Veranstaltungen → Present Simple. Frageform: 'What time does the concert start?'

items.p: The last bus ___ (leave) at 11:45 p.m. Don't miss it!
a: leaves
items.h: Bus schedules are fixed timetables. Which tense is used for future timetables?
items.hd: Busfahrpläne sind feste Zeitpläne. Welche Zeitform wird für zukünftige Fahrpläne verwendet?
items.e: Timetables and fixed schedules use present simple: 'The last bus leaves at 11:45 p.m.'
items.ed: Fahrpläne und feste Zeitpläne verwenden Present Simple: 'The last bus leaves at 11:45 p.m.'

items.p: Our exams ___ (begin) on the 15th of June and ___ (end) on the 22nd.
a: begin ... end
items.h: Exam dates are fixed schedules. 'Our exams' is PLURAL — no -s on the verb!
items.hd: Prüfungstermine sind feste Zeitpläne. 'Our exams' ist MEHRZAHL — kein -s am Verb!
items.e: Fixed schedule → present simple. 'Our exams' = they (plural) → 'begin' and 'end' (no -s).
items.ed: Fester Zeitplan → Present Simple. 'Our exams' = they (Mehrzahl) → 'begin' und 'end' (kein -s).

items.p: Which sentence correctly talks about a train timetable?
items.c: The train to Salzburg departs at 7:15.
a: The train to Salzburg departs at 7:15.
ds: The train to Salzburg will depart at 7:15.
The train to Salzburg is departing at 7:15.
The train to Salzburg is going to depart at 7:15.
items.h: Train timetables are fixed schedules. Which future form is used for schedules?
items.hd: Zugfahrpläne sind feste Zeitpläne. Welche Zukunftsform wird für Zeitpläne verwendet?
items.e: Official timetables (trains, buses, flights) → present simple: 'departs at 7:15'.
items.ed: Offizielle Fahrpläne (Züge, Busse, Flüge) → Present Simple: 'departs at 7:15'.

items.p: Which situation uses present simple for the future?
items.c: The flight to London takes off at 6:30 a.m.
a: The flight to London takes off at 6:30 a.m.
ds: I am visiting my grandma next weekend.
I think it will rain tomorrow.
She is going to study medicine.
items.h: Present simple for future is ONLY for fixed timetables and schedules.
items.hd: Present Simple für Zukunft ist NUR für feste Fahrpläne und Zeitpläne.
items.e: Only timetables/schedules use present simple for future. Personal plans → going to. Predictions → will. Arrangements → present continuous.
items.ed: Nur Fahrpläne/Zeitpläne verwenden Present Simple für die Zukunft. Persönliche Pläne → going to. Vorhersagen → will. Verabredungen → Present Continuous.

items.p: The library ___ at 5 p.m. on Saturdays.
a: closes
items.h: Regular opening hours are a fixed schedule. 'The library' = it (3rd person singular).
items.hd: Reguläre Öffnungszeiten sind ein fester Zeitplan. 'The library' = it (3. Person Einzahl).
items.e: Regular schedule → present simple. 'The library' = it → 'closes' (3rd person -es after -se).
items.ed: Regulärer Zeitplan → Present Simple. 'The library' = it → 'closes' (3. Person -es nach -se).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The train will arrive at platform 3 at 10:15. (timetable)
items.c: The train arrives at platform 3 at 10:15.
a: The train arrives at platform 3 at 10:15.
The train arrives at platform 3 at 10:15
items.h: This is a timetable. We don't use 'will' for scheduled arrivals. Which tense is correct?
items.hd: Das ist ein Fahrplan. Wir verwenden 'will' nicht für geplante Ankünfte. Welche Zeitform ist richtig?
items.e: Timetables use present simple, not 'will': 'The train arrives at 10:15.' (NOT 'will arrive').
items.ed: Fahrpläne verwenden Present Simple, nicht 'will': 'The train arrives at 10:15.' (NICHT 'will arrive').
