items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The film is starting at 8 p.m. tonight. (cinema schedule)
items.c: The film starts at 8 p.m. tonight.
a: The film starts at 8 p.m. tonight.
The film starts at 8 p.m. tonight
items.h: Cinema schedules are fixed timetables. Present continuous is for personal arrangements, not official schedules.
items.hd: Kinoprogramme sind feste Zeitpläne. Present Continuous ist für persönliche Verabredungen, nicht offizielle Zeitpläne.
items.e: Cinema times are fixed schedules → present simple: 'The film starts at 8 p.m.' (NOT 'is starting').
items.ed: Kinozeiten sind feste Zeitpläne → Present Simple: 'The film starts at 8 p.m.' (NICHT 'is starting').

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The lesson start at 8:00 a.m. every day.
items.c: The lesson starts at 8:00 a.m. every day.
a: The lesson starts at 8:00 a.m. every day.
The lesson starts at 8:00 a.m. every day
items.h: 'The lesson' is 3rd person singular. What ending does the verb need?
items.hd: 'The lesson' ist 3. Person Einzahl. Welche Endung braucht das Verb?
items.e: 'The lesson' = it (3rd person singular). Present simple needs -s: 'starts', not 'start'.
items.ed: 'The lesson' = it (3. Person Einzahl). Present Simple braucht -s: 'starts', nicht 'start'.

items.p: Rewrite using present simple (timetable): The ferry will leave at 3:30 p.m. → The ferry ___.
a: The ferry leaves at 3:30 p.m.
The ferry leaves at 3:30 p.m
items.h: Replace 'will leave' with present simple. Remember the 3rd person -s!
items.hd: Ersetze 'will leave' durch Present Simple. Denke an das -s der 3. Person!
items.e: Timetable → present simple: 'will leave' → 'leaves'. 'The ferry' = it, so add -s.
items.ed: Fahrplan → Present Simple: 'will leave' → 'leaves'. 'The ferry' = it, also füge -s hinzu.

items.p: Form a present simple question: You want to know what time the swimming pool closes. → What time ___?
items.c: What time does the swimming pool close?
a: What time does the swimming pool close?
What time does the pool close?
items.h: Questions about schedules use present simple: what time + does + subject + base verb.
items.hd: Fragen über Zeitpläne verwenden Present Simple: what time + does + Subjekt + Grundform.
items.e: Question about a schedule → present simple: 'What time does the swimming pool close?'
items.ed: Frage über einen Zeitplan → Present Simple: 'What time does the swimming pool close?'

items.p: 🇩🇪 Das Flugzeug landet um 14:30 Uhr.
a: The plane lands at 2:30 p.m.
The plane lands at 14:30.
The plane arrives at 2:30 p.m.
The plane lands at half past two.
items.h: This is a flight schedule. Use present simple in English. 'landen' = land/arrive.
items.hd: Das ist ein Flugplan. Verwende Present Simple im Englischen. 'landen' = land/arrive.
items.e: Flight schedules → present simple: 'The plane lands at 2:30 p.m.' German and English both use present tense for schedules.
items.ed: Flugpläne → Present Simple: 'The plane lands at 2:30 p.m.' Deutsch und Englisch verwenden beide die Gegenwartsform für Fahrpläne.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wann macht das Schwimmbad morgen auf?
items.c: When does the swimming pool open tomorrow?
a: When does the swimming pool open tomorrow?
What time does the swimming pool open tomorrow?
When does the pool open tomorrow?
items.h: This is about a schedule. Form a present simple question with 'does'.
items.hd: Es geht um einen Zeitplan. Bilde eine Present-Simple-Frage mit 'does'.
items.e: Questions about fixed schedules → present simple: 'When does the swimming pool open tomorrow?'
items.ed: Fragen über feste Zeitpläne → Present Simple: 'When does the swimming pool open tomorrow?'

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: at / starts / the / match / 3 p.m.
a: The match starts at 3 p.m.
The match starts at 3 p.m
items.h: Subject + verb + time: The match + starts + at 3 p.m.
items.hd: Subjekt + Verb + Zeit: The match + starts + at 3 p.m.
items.e: Standard word order: subject + present simple verb + time expression. 'The match starts at 3 p.m.'
items.ed: Standardreihenfolge: Subjekt + Present-Simple-Verb + Zeitangabe. 'The match starts at 3 p.m.'

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: does / time / the / what / museum / close / ?
items.c: What time does the museum close?
a: What time does the museum close?
items.h: Question word + does + subject + base verb: What time + does + the museum + close?
items.hd: Fragewort + does + Subjekt + Grundform: What time + does + the museum + close?
items.e: Present simple question about a schedule: 'What time does the museum close?'
items.ed: Present-Simple-Frage über einen Zeitplan: 'What time does the museum close?'

items.p: Match each situation with the correct verb form:
ds: 1: The shop ___ at 9 a.m.|2: I ___ my grandma tomorrow.|3: The plane ___ at 6:15.|4: Look! It ___ rain!|5: I think he ___ the test.
a: am visiting (arrangement)|b: is going to (evidence)|c: will pass (prediction)|d: opens (timetable)|e: takes off (timetable)
items.h: Timetables → present simple. Arrangements → present continuous. Evidence → going to. Predictions → will.
items.hd: Fahrpläne → Present Simple. Verabredungen → Present Continuous. Beweise → going to. Vorhersagen → will.
items.e: Shop opens/plane takes off = timetable → present simple. Visiting grandma = arrangement. Rain evidence = going to. 'I think' = will.
items.ed: Shop opens/plane takes off = Fahrplan → Present Simple. Oma besuchen = Verabredung. Regenbeweise = going to. 'I think' = will.

items.p: You're checking the bus schedule at the stop. The next bus is at 15:45. Which sentence is correct?
a: The next bus arrives at 15:45.
ds: The next bus will arrive at 15:45.
The next bus is arriving at 15:45.
The next bus is going to arrive at 15:45.
items.h: You're reading a bus schedule (timetable). Which future form is for timetables?
items.hd: Du liest einen Busfahrplan. Welche Zukunftsform ist für Fahrpläne?
items.e: Bus schedules are official timetables → present simple: 'The next bus arrives at 15:45.'
items.ed: Busfahrpläne sind offizielle Fahrpläne → Present Simple: 'The next bus arrives at 15:45.'

items.p: She isn't answering her phone. She ___ be in a meeting.
a: might
may
could
items.h: We don't know for sure — it's just a possibility. Which modal expresses 'maybe'?
items.hd: Wir wissen es nicht sicher — es ist nur eine Möglichkeit. Welches Modalverb drückt 'vielleicht' aus?
items.e: 'Might/may/could' = it's possible. We don't have strong evidence, just a guess.
items.ed: 'Might/may/could' = es ist möglich. Wir haben keine starken Beweise, nur eine Vermutung.

items.p: He's eaten three pizzas! He ___ be very hungry.
a: must
items.h: Three pizzas = strong evidence! Which modal means 'I'm almost sure / it's logical'?
items.hd: Drei Pizzen = starker Beweis! Welches Modalverb bedeutet 'ich bin fast sicher / es ist logisch'?
items.e: 'Must' for logical deduction: strong evidence (3 pizzas) → near-certainty (he must be hungry).
items.ed: 'Must' für logische Schlussfolgerung: starker Beweis (3 Pizzen) → fast sicher (he must be hungry).

items.p: He ___ be a teacher — he's only 15 years old!
a: can't
cannot
items.h: He's only 15 — it's IMPOSSIBLE that he's a teacher. Which modal means 'it's impossible'?
items.hd: Er ist erst 15 — es ist UNMÖGLICH, dass er Lehrer ist. Welches Modalverb bedeutet 'es ist unmöglich'?
items.e: 'Can't' for impossibility based on evidence: He's 15 → it's impossible he's a teacher. 'Mustn't' means 'not allowed' — different!
items.ed: 'Can't' für Unmöglichkeit aufgrund von Beweisen: Er ist 15 → es ist unmöglich, dass er Lehrer ist. 'Mustn't' bedeutet 'nicht erlaubt' — anders!

items.p: I'm not sure, but this ___ be the right answer.
a: could
might
may
items.h: 'I'm not sure' tells you the speaker is uncertain. Use a modal that expresses uncertainty.
items.hd: 'I'm not sure' sagt dir, dass der Sprecher unsicher ist. Verwende ein Modalverb, das Unsicherheit ausdrückt.
items.e: 'Could/might/may' express uncertainty. 'I'm not sure' signals the speaker is guessing, not making a deduction.
items.ed: 'Could/might/may' drücken Unsicherheit aus. 'I'm not sure' signalisiert, dass der Sprecher rät, keine Schlussfolgerung zieht.

items.p: She's been working for 12 hours. She ___ be exhausted!
a: must
items.h: 12 hours of work = strong evidence. Use the modal for near-certain deductions.
items.hd: 12 Stunden Arbeit = starker Beweis. Verwende das Modalverb für fast sichere Schlussfolgerungen.
items.e: 'Must' for strong deduction based on evidence. 12 hours of work → she must be exhausted (near-certain).
items.ed: 'Must' für starke Schlussfolgerung aufgrund von Beweisen. 12 Stunden Arbeit → she must be exhausted (fast sicher).

items.p: Where's Tom? — He ___ be at the library, or he ___ be at home. I'm not sure.
a: might ... might
could ... could
may ... may
might ... could
could ... might
items.h: The speaker doesn't know ('I'm not sure'), so both options are uncertain. Use the same uncertainty modal for both.
items.hd: Der Sprecher weiß es nicht ('I'm not sure'), also sind beide Möglichkeiten unsicher. Verwende das gleiche Unsicherheits-Modalverb für beide.
items.e: Two equally uncertain possibilities → might/could/may for both. 'I'm not sure' rules out 'must' (near-certainty).
items.ed: Zwei gleich unsichere Möglichkeiten → might/could/may für beide. 'I'm not sure' schließt 'must' (fast sicher) aus.

items.p: There's someone at the door. It ___ be the postman — he usually comes at this time.
a: could
might
may
items.h: The postman 'usually' comes now, but we're not 100% sure. Use a modal for possibility.
items.hd: Der Briefträger kommt 'normalerweise' jetzt, aber wir sind nicht 100% sicher. Verwende ein Modalverb für Möglichkeit.
items.e: 'Could/might/may' for a reasonable possibility. 'Usually' suggests it's likely but not certain enough for 'must'.
items.ed: 'Could/might/may' für eine vernünftige Möglichkeit. 'Usually' deutet an, dass es wahrscheinlich, aber nicht sicher genug für 'must' ist.

items.p: She's got the highest mark in the class. She ___ have studied a lot.
a: must
items.h: Highest mark = very strong evidence. Which modal expresses near-certainty?
items.hd: Beste Note = sehr starker Beweis. Welches Modalverb drückt fast sichere Schlussfolgerung aus?
items.e: 'Must have + past participle' for logical deduction about the past. Strong evidence → near-certainty.
items.ed: 'Must have + Past Participle' für logische Schlussfolgerung über die Vergangenheit. Starker Beweis → fast sicher.

items.p: That ___ be Maria's phone — Maria has an iPhone and this is a Samsung!
a: can't
cannot
items.h: Maria has an iPhone, this is a Samsung. It's IMPOSSIBLE that this is her phone. 'Mustn't' = not allowed (different!).
items.hd: Maria hat ein iPhone, das hier ist ein Samsung. Es ist UNMÖGLICH, dass das ihr Handy ist. 'Mustn't' = nicht erlaubt (anders!).
items.e: 'Can't' = impossible (based on evidence). 'Mustn't' = not allowed. Common confusion for German speakers: 'muss nicht' ≠ 'mustn't'!
items.ed: 'Can't' = unmöglich (aufgrund von Beweisen). 'Mustn't' = nicht erlaubt. Häufige Verwechslung für deutschsprachige: 'muss nicht' ≠ 'mustn't'!

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She can be at school — I'm not sure.
items.c: She might be at school — I'm not sure.
a: She might be at school — I'm not sure.
She could be at school — I'm not sure.
She may be at school — I'm not sure.
items.h: 'I'm not sure' means this is a guess. 'Can' is for ability, not for guessing in specific situations.
items.hd: 'I'm not sure' bedeutet, das ist eine Vermutung. 'Can' ist für Fähigkeit, nicht für Vermutungen in bestimmten Situationen.
items.e: 'Can' = ability (She can swim). For uncertain possibility, use 'might/could/may': She might be at school.
items.ed: 'Can' = Fähigkeit (She can swim). Für unsichere Möglichkeit verwende 'might/could/may': She might be at school.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: That mustn't be true — I don't believe it!
items.c: That can't be true — I don't believe it!
a: That can't be true — I don't believe it!
That cannot be true — I don't believe it!
items.h: The speaker thinks it's IMPOSSIBLE. 'Mustn't' means 'not allowed'. Which modal means 'impossible'?
items.hd: Der Sprecher denkt, es ist UNMÖGLICH. 'Mustn't' bedeutet 'nicht erlaubt'. Welches Modalverb bedeutet 'unmöglich'?
items.e: 'Can't' = impossible (deduction). 'Mustn't' = not allowed (prohibition). German 'Das muss nicht stimmen' ≠ 'That mustn't be true'!
items.ed: 'Can't' = unmöglich (Schlussfolgerung). 'Mustn't' = nicht erlaubt (Verbot). Deutsch 'Das muss nicht stimmen' ≠ 'That mustn't be true'!

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: He's running a fever. He can be sick.
items.c: He's running a fever. He must be sick.
a: He's running a fever. He must be sick.
items.h: A fever is strong evidence. 'Can' doesn't express logical deduction. Which modal is used for strong deductions?
items.hd: Fieber ist ein starker Beweis. 'Can' drückt keine logische Schlussfolgerung aus. Welches Modalverb wird für starke Schlussfolgerungen verwendet?
items.e: Strong evidence (fever) → 'must' (near-certainty). 'Can' is for ability/general possibility, not specific deductions.
items.ed: Starker Beweis (Fieber) → 'must' (fast sicher). 'Can' ist für Fähigkeit/allgemeine Möglichkeit, nicht für spezifische Schlussfolgerungen.

items.p: Rewrite using a modal of possibility: Maybe it will snow tomorrow. → It ___.
a: It might snow tomorrow.
It may snow tomorrow.
It could snow tomorrow.
items.h: 'Maybe' = it's possible. Replace 'maybe + will' with a modal of possibility.
items.hd: 'Maybe' = es ist möglich. Ersetze 'maybe + will' durch ein Modalverb der Möglichkeit.
items.e: 'Maybe it will snow' = 'It might/may/could snow'. The modal replaces both 'maybe' and 'will'.
items.ed: 'Maybe it will snow' = 'It might/may/could snow'. Das Modalverb ersetzt sowohl 'maybe' als auch 'will'.

items.p: Express near-certainty: I'm almost sure he is lying. → He ___.
a: He must be lying.
items.h: 'Almost sure' = near-certainty. Which modal expresses this? + continuous form for an ongoing action.
items.hd: 'Fast sicher' = nahezu sicher. Welches Modalverb drückt das aus? + Verlaufsform für eine laufende Handlung.
items.e: 'Almost sure' → 'must'. He is lying (ongoing) → 'He must be lying' (must + be + -ing).
items.ed: 'Fast sicher' → 'must'. He is lying (im Gange) → 'He must be lying' (must + be + -ing).

items.p: 🇩🇪 Er kann vielleicht morgen kommen.
a: He might come tomorrow.
He may come tomorrow.
He could come tomorrow.
items.h: 'vielleicht' = maybe/perhaps. Use might/may/could (NOT 'can' — that's for ability).
items.hd: 'vielleicht' = maybe/perhaps. Verwende might/may/could (NICHT 'can' — das ist für Fähigkeit).
items.e: German 'kann vielleicht' → English 'might/may/could'. NOT 'can' — 'can' expresses ability, not speculative possibility.
items.ed: Deutsch 'kann vielleicht' → Englisch 'might/may/could'. NICHT 'can' — 'can' drückt Fähigkeit aus, nicht spekulative Möglichkeit.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Das kann nicht stimmen! Er muss sich irren.
items.c: That can't be true! He must be wrong.
a: That can't be true! He must be wrong.
That cannot be true! He must be wrong.
That can't be right! He must be wrong.
items.h: 'kann nicht stimmen' = it's impossible → can't. 'muss sich irren' = near-certainty → must.
items.hd: 'kann nicht stimmen' = es ist unmöglich → can't. 'muss sich irren' = fast sicher → must.
items.e: 'Can't' for impossibility, 'must' for near-certainty. Both are deductions based on the speaker's belief.
items.ed: 'Can't' für Unmöglichkeit, 'must' für fast sichere Schlussfolgerung. Beides sind Schlussfolgerungen aufgrund der Überzeugung des Sprechers.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: be / she / at / might / home
a: She might be at home.
She might be at home
items.h: Subject + modal + base form: She + might + be + at home.
items.hd: Subjekt + Modalverb + Grundform: She + might + be + at home.
items.e: Word order: Subject + modal + base form + rest: 'She might be at home.'
items.ed: Wortstellung: Subjekt + Modalverb + Grundform + Rest: 'She might be at home.'

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: John's / be / that / car / can't
a: That can't be John's car.
That can't be John's car
items.h: Subject + modal + base form: That + can't + be + John's car.
items.hd: Subjekt + Modalverb + Grundform: That + can't + be + John's car.
items.e: Negative deduction: That can't be John's car = It's impossible that it's John's car.
items.ed: Negative Schlussfolgerung: That can't be John's car = Es ist unmöglich, dass das Johns Auto ist.

items.p: Match each situation with the correct modal:
ds: 1: He's trained every day. He ___ win.|2: Maybe she'll call later.|3: He's only 12 — he ___ drive!|4: I don't know, but he ___ be right.|5: She's eaten nothing all day. She ___ be starving.
a: might (possibility)|b: could (uncertain)|c: should (expectation)|d: must (deduction)|e: can't (impossibility)
items.h: Match the strength of certainty: must (almost sure) > might/could (maybe) > can't (impossible).
items.hd: Ordne die Stärke der Sicherheit zu: must (fast sicher) > might/could (vielleicht) > can't (unmöglich).
items.e: should win (trained=expectation), might call (maybe), can't drive (12=impossible), could be right (uncertain), must be starving (no food=deduction).
items.ed: should win (trainiert=Erwartung), might call (vielleicht), can't drive (12=unmöglich), could be right (unsicher), must be starving (kein Essen=Schlussfolgerung).

items.p: You see a light on in your neighbour's house. You're not sure if they're home. Ask your friend: 'Do you think they ___ be home?'
a: might
could
items.h: You're not sure — it's a possibility. Which modal fits in a question about possibility?
items.hd: Du bist nicht sicher — es ist eine Möglichkeit. Welches Modalverb passt in eine Frage über Möglichkeit?
items.e: 'Might/could' in a question: 'Do you think they might/could be home?' Uncertain possibility.
items.ed: 'Might/could' in einer Frage: 'Do you think they might/could be home?' Unsichere Möglichkeit.

items.p: If they ___ (arrive) on time, they would have caught the bus.
a: had arrived
items.h: Third conditional if-clause: if + had + past participle. Never use 'would' in the if-clause!
items.hd: Third Conditional if-Satz: if + had + Past Participle. Verwende nie 'would' im if-Satz!
items.e: If-clause: 'if + had + past participle' → 'if they had arrived'. Do NOT put 'would' in the if-clause.
items.ed: If-Satz: 'if + had + Past Participle' → 'if they had arrived'. Verwende NICHT 'would' im if-Satz.

items.p: If I hadn't set my alarm, I ___ (oversleep).
a: would have overslept
would've overslept
items.h: The result clause of the 3rd conditional: would (not) have + past participle.
items.hd: Der Ergebnissatz des 3rd Conditional: would (not) have + Past Participle.
items.e: Result clause: 'would have + past participle' → 'would have overslept'. Contraction: 'would've overslept'.
items.ed: Ergebnissatz: 'would have + Past Participle' → 'would have overslept'. Kurzform: 'would've overslept'.

items.p: If she ___ (not / eat) so much cake, she wouldn't have felt sick.
a: hadn't eaten
had not eaten
items.h: Negative if-clause: if + hadn't + past participle. What is the past participle of 'eat'?
items.hd: Negativer if-Satz: if + hadn't + Past Participle. Was ist das Past Participle von 'eat'?
items.e: eat → eaten (irregular). Negative if-clause: 'if she hadn't eaten'. Never put 'would' in the if-clause!
items.ed: eat → eaten (unregelmäßig). Negativer if-Satz: 'if she hadn't eaten'. Verwende nie 'would' im if-Satz!

items.p: If we ___ (take) a taxi, we would've arrived much earlier.
a: had taken
items.h: If + had + past participle. 'take' → took → ??? (past participle).
items.hd: If + had + Past Participle. 'take' → took → ??? (Past Participle).
items.e: take → taken (irregular past participle). 'If we had taken a taxi' + result with 'would've'.
items.ed: take → taken (unregelmäßiges Past Participle). 'If we had taken a taxi' + Ergebnis mit 'would've'.

items.p: She would've called you if she ___ (know) your number.
a: had known
items.h: The if-clause is at the end, but the rule is the same: if + had + past participle.
items.hd: Der if-Satz steht am Ende, aber die Regel ist die gleiche: if + had + Past Participle.
items.e: The if-clause can come first or second. Either way: if + had + past participle. know → known.
items.ed: Der if-Satz kann am Anfang oder Ende stehen. In beiden Fällen: if + had + Past Participle. know → known.

items.p: If I ___ (not / forget) my passport, I ___ (not / miss) the flight.
a: hadn't forgotten ... wouldn't have missed
had not forgotten ... would not have missed
hadn't forgotten ... wouldn't've missed
items.h: Double gap! if-clause: if + hadn't + past participle. Result: wouldn't have + past participle. 'forget' → forgot → forgotten.
items.hd: Doppellücke! if-Satz: if + hadn't + Past Participle. Ergebnis: wouldn't have + Past Participle. 'forget' → forgot → forgotten.
items.e: If I hadn't forgotten (if-clause, past perfect negative) ... wouldn't have missed (result, negative). Both parts of the 3rd conditional.
items.ed: If I hadn't forgotten (if-Satz, Past Perfect negativ) ... wouldn't have missed (Ergebnis, negativ). Beide Teile des 3rd Conditional.

items.p: If we had left earlier, we ___ on time.
a: would have arrived
items.h: The result clause of the 3rd conditional: would have + past participle.
items.hd: Der Ergebnissatz des 3rd Conditional: would have + Past Participle.
items.e: Result clause: 'would have arrived'. 'Would arrive' is 2nd conditional; 'had arrived' is for the if-clause.
items.ed: Ergebnissatz: 'would have arrived'. 'Would arrive' ist 2nd Conditional; 'had arrived' ist für den if-Satz.

items.p: Which sentence is a correct third conditional?
items.c: If I had known, I would have helped.
a: If I had known, I would have helped.
ds: If I would have known, I had helped.
If I knew, I would have helped.
If I had known, I would help.
items.h: 3rd conditional: if + had + pp, would have + pp. Only one option follows this pattern correctly.
items.hd: 3rd Conditional: if + had + pp, would have + pp. Nur eine Option folgt diesem Muster korrekt.
items.e: 'If I had known, I would have helped' = correct 3rd conditional. Both clauses follow the right pattern.
items.ed: 'If I had known, I would have helped' = korrektes 3rd Conditional. Beide Satzteile folgen dem richtigen Muster.

items.p: If she hadn't missed the bus, she ___.
a: wouldn't have been late
items.h: The if-clause uses 'hadn't missed' (past perfect) → result must use 'would (not) have + pp'.
items.hd: Der if-Satz verwendet 'hadn't missed' (Past Perfect) → Ergebnis muss 'would (not) have + pp' verwenden.
items.e: 'Hadn't missed' (3rd conditional if-clause) → 'wouldn't have been late' (3rd conditional result).
items.ed: 'Hadn't missed' (3rd Conditional if-Satz) → 'wouldn't have been late' (3rd Conditional Ergebnis).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: If I would have studied harder, I would have passed.
items.c: If I had studied harder, I would have passed.
a: If I had studied harder, I would have passed.
If I had studied harder, I would have passed
items.h: Never use 'would' in the if-clause! The if-clause needs: if + had + past participle.
items.hd: Verwende nie 'would' im if-Satz! Der if-Satz braucht: if + had + Past Participle.
items.e: 'Would' NEVER appears in the if-clause. Replace 'would have studied' with 'had studied'.
items.ed: 'Would' kommt NIE im if-Satz vor. Ersetze 'would have studied' durch 'had studied'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: If they had won the match, they would celebrate.
items.c: If they had won the match, they would have celebrated.
a: If they had won the match, they would have celebrated.
If they had won the match, they would have celebrated
items.h: The if-clause has 'had won' (3rd conditional). The result clause must also be 3rd conditional: would HAVE + past participle.
items.hd: Der if-Satz hat 'had won' (3rd Conditional). Der Ergebnissatz muss auch 3rd Conditional sein: would HAVE + Past Participle.
items.e: If-clause is 3rd conditional → result must also be 3rd: 'would have celebrated' (not 2nd conditional 'would celebrate').
items.ed: If-Satz ist 3rd Conditional → Ergebnis muss auch 3rd sein: 'would have celebrated' (nicht 2nd Conditional 'would celebrate').

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: If he didn't break his arm, he would have played in the final.
items.c: If he hadn't broken his arm, he would have played in the final.
a: If he hadn't broken his arm, he would have played in the final.
If he had not broken his arm, he would have played in the final.
items.h: Two errors! 1) Past simple 'didn't break' should be past perfect. 2) 'break' has an irregular past participle.
items.hd: Zwei Fehler! 1) Past Simple 'didn't break' sollte Past Perfect sein. 2) 'break' hat ein unregelmäßiges Past Participle.
items.e: 'didn't break' → 'hadn't broken'. The if-clause needs past perfect, and 'break' → 'broken' (not 'break').
items.ed: 'didn't break' → 'hadn't broken'. Der if-Satz braucht Past Perfect, und 'break' → 'broken' (nicht 'break').

items.p: She didn't bring an umbrella. She got wet. → If she ___, she ___.
items.c: If she had brought an umbrella, she wouldn't have got wet.
a: If she had brought an umbrella, she wouldn't have got wet.
If she had brought an umbrella, she wouldn't have gotten wet.
If she'd brought an umbrella, she wouldn't have got wet.
items.h: Change the facts to unreal past: 'didn't bring' → 'had brought'. 'got wet' → 'wouldn't have got wet'.
items.hd: Ändere die Fakten in irreale Vergangenheit: 'didn't bring' → 'had brought'. 'got wet' → 'wouldn't have got wet'.
items.e: Fact: she didn't bring → 3rd conditional: if she had brought. Fact: she got wet → wouldn't have got wet.
items.ed: Fakt: she didn't bring → 3rd Conditional: if she had brought. Fakt: she got wet → wouldn't have got wet.

items.p: He spent all his money. He couldn't buy the present. → If he ___.
items.c: If he hadn't spent all his money, he would have been able to buy the present.
a: If he hadn't spent all his money, he would have been able to buy the present.
If he hadn't spent all his money, he could have bought the present.
If he had not spent all his money, he would have been able to buy the present.
items.h: Change 'spent all his money' to the negative hypothetical: 'hadn't spent'. 'couldn't buy' → 'could have bought'.
items.hd: Ändere 'spent all his money' zur negativen Hypothese: 'hadn't spent'. 'couldn't buy' → 'could have bought'.
items.e: Fact: he spent → if he hadn't spent. Fact: he couldn't buy → he could have bought / would have been able to buy.
items.ed: Fakt: he spent → if he hadn't spent. Fakt: he couldn't buy → he could have bought / would have been able to buy.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wenn ich das gewusst hätte, hätte ich dir geholfen.
items.c: If I had known, I would have helped you.
a: If I had known, I would have helped you.
If I'd known, I would have helped you.
If I had known, I would've helped you.
If I had known that, I would have helped you.
items.h: 'Wenn ich gewusst hätte' = if I had known. 'hätte ich geholfen' = I would have helped. Don't put 'would' in the if-clause!
items.hd: 'Wenn ich gewusst hätte' = if I had known. 'hätte ich geholfen' = I would have helped. Kein 'would' im if-Satz!
items.e: German 'hätte gewusst' → 'had known' (NOT 'would have known'!). German 'hätte geholfen' → 'would have helped'.
items.ed: Deutsch 'hätte gewusst' → 'had known' (NICHT 'would have known'!). Deutsch 'hätte geholfen' → 'would have helped'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Wenn es nicht geregnet hätte, wären wir wandern gegangen.
items.c: If it hadn't rained, we would have gone hiking.
a: If it hadn't rained, we would have gone hiking.
If it had not rained, we would have gone hiking.
If it hadn't rained, we would've gone hiking.
items.h: 'nicht geregnet hätte' = hadn't rained. 'wären gegangen' = would have gone. Remember: no 'would' in the if-clause!
items.hd: 'nicht geregnet hätte' = hadn't rained. 'wären gegangen' = would have gone. Merke: kein 'would' im if-Satz!
items.e: German 'wären gegangen' can trick you into using 'would' in the if-clause. English: if + hadn't rained → would have gone.
items.ed: Deutsch 'wären gegangen' kann dazu verleiten, 'would' im if-Satz zu verwenden. Englisch: if + hadn't rained → would have gone.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: studied / had / she / if / harder / , / have / would / she / passed
items.c: If she had studied harder, she would have passed.
a: If she had studied harder, she would have passed.
She would have passed if she had studied harder.
items.h: If-clause first: If + subject + had + past participle. Then result: subject + would have + past participle.
items.hd: If-Satz zuerst: If + Subjekt + had + Past Participle. Dann Ergebnis: Subjekt + would have + Past Participle.
items.e: If she had studied harder (if-clause) + she would have passed (result clause). Comma after the if-clause when it comes first.
items.ed: If she had studied harder (if-Satz) + she would have passed (Ergebnissatz). Komma nach dem if-Satz, wenn er vorne steht.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: have / wouldn't / they / if / missed / hadn't / the / they / train / been / late / ,
items.c: If they hadn't missed the train, they wouldn't have been late.
a: If they hadn't missed the train, they wouldn't have been late.
They wouldn't have been late if they hadn't missed the train.
items.h: If-clause (negative): If + they + hadn't + missed. Result (negative): they + wouldn't have + been late.
items.hd: If-Satz (negativ): If + they + hadn't + missed. Ergebnis (negativ): they + wouldn't have + been late.
items.e: Both clauses negative: 'hadn't missed' (if-clause) + 'wouldn't have been late' (result).
items.ed: Beide Satzteile negativ: 'hadn't missed' (if-Satz) + 'wouldn't have been late' (Ergebnis).

items.p: Match each if-clause with the correct result clause:
ds: 1: If I had woken up earlier, ...|2: If she hadn't forgotten the tickets, ...|3: If we had studied together, ...|4: If it hadn't snowed, ...|5: If he had listened to the teacher, ...
a: we would have passed the test.|b: he would have understood the lesson.|c: we would have gone to the park.|d: I wouldn't have been late for school.|e: they would have got into the concert.
items.h: Match the if-clause situation with the logical result. All are 3rd conditional (unreal past).
items.hd: Ordne die if-Satz-Situation dem logischen Ergebnis zu. Alle sind 3rd Conditional (irreale Vergangenheit).
items.e: Woken up earlier→not late. Not forgotten tickets→got in. Studied together→passed. Not snowed→park. Listened→understood.
items.ed: Früher aufgewacht→nicht zu spät. Tickets nicht vergessen→reingekommen. Zusammen gelernt→bestanden. Nicht geschneit→Park. Zugehört→verstanden.

items.p: Your friend missed the school trip yesterday because she was ill. You feel sorry. Which sentence is correct?
items.c: If she hadn't been ill, she would have come on the trip.
a: If she hadn't been ill, she would have come on the trip.
ds: If she wasn't ill, she would come on the trip.
If she wouldn't have been ill, she had come on the trip.
If she isn't ill, she will come on the trip.
items.h: The trip was YESTERDAY (past). We're imagining a different past. Which conditional is for unreal past situations?
items.hd: Der Ausflug war GESTERN (Vergangenheit). Wir stellen uns eine andere Vergangenheit vor. Welcher Konditional ist für irreale Vergangenheitssituationen?
items.e: Past unreal situation → 3rd conditional. If she hadn't been ill (past perfect) + would have come (result).
items.ed: Irreale Vergangenheitssituation → 3rd Conditional. If she hadn't been ill (Past Perfect) + would have come (Ergebnis).

items.p: My parents ___ me to clean my room every Saturday. (want)
a: want
items.h: The structure is: want + person + to + infinitive. What form of 'want' fits with 'my parents'?
items.hd: Die Struktur ist: want + Person + to + Infinitiv. Welche Form von 'want' passt zu 'my parents'?
items.e: 'My parents' is plural, so we use 'want' (not 'wants'). The pattern is: want + object + to-infinitive.
items.ed: 'My parents' ist Mehrzahl, also verwenden wir 'want' (nicht 'wants'). Das Muster ist: want + Objekt + to-Infinitiv.

items.p: The teacher wants us ___ quietly during the test.
a: to work
items.h: After 'want + person', what word comes before the verb?
items.hd: Nach 'want + Person', welches Wort kommt vor dem Verb?
items.e: The structure is: want + object (us) + to + infinitive (work). You need 'to work'.
items.ed: Die Struktur ist: want + Objekt (us) + to + Infinitiv (work). Man braucht 'to work'.

items.p: She doesn't want ___ to go to the party without her.
a: them
items.h: After 'want', we need an object pronoun (me, you, him, her, us, them). Which one fits?
items.hd: Nach 'want' brauchen wir ein Objektpronomen (me, you, him, her, us, them). Welches passt?
items.e: After 'want' we use the object pronoun: 'them' (not 'they'). Pattern: want + object pronoun + to + infinitive.
items.ed: Nach 'want' verwenden wir das Objektpronomen: 'them' (nicht 'they'). Muster: want + Objektpronomen + to + Infinitiv.

items.p: I wanted my brother ___ me with my homework, but he was busy.
a: to help
items.h: Even in the past tense, 'want' follows the same pattern: want + person + ___ + verb.
items.hd: Auch in der Vergangenheit folgt 'want' dem gleichen Muster: want + Person + ___ + Verb.
items.e: In the past, the pattern stays the same: wanted + object + to + infinitive → 'wanted my brother to help'.
items.ed: In der Vergangenheit bleibt das Muster gleich: wanted + Objekt + to + Infinitiv → 'wanted my brother to help'.

items.p: Our coach doesn't want ___ to eat junk food before the match.
a: us
items.h: After 'doesn't want', you need an object pronoun. Which one refers to 'our team'?
items.hd: Nach 'doesn't want' braucht man ein Objektpronomen. Welches bezieht sich auf 'unser Team'?
items.e: After 'want' in negative form, we still use object pronoun + to: 'doesn't want us to eat'.
items.ed: Nach 'want' in der Verneinung verwenden wir immer noch Objektpronomen + to: 'doesn't want us to eat'.

items.p: Do you want ___ to pick you up from the station, or would you rather take a taxi?
a: me
items.h: In questions too, the pattern is: want + object pronoun + to + verb.
items.hd: Auch in Fragen ist das Muster: want + Objektpronomen + to + Verb.
items.e: Even in questions, the pattern is: want + object pronoun + to + infinitive. 'Do you want me to pick you up?'
items.ed: Auch in Fragen gilt: want + Objektpronomen + to + Infinitiv. 'Do you want me to pick you up?'

items.p: Which sentence correctly uses 'want someone to do something'?
items.c: Mum wants me to tidy up my room.
a: Mum wants me to tidy up my room.
ds: Mum wants that I tidy up my room.
Mum wants I to tidy up my room.
Mum wants me tidy up my room.
items.h: The correct pattern is: want + object pronoun + to + infinitive.
items.hd: Das richtige Muster ist: want + Objektpronomen + to + Infinitiv.
items.e: 'Mum wants me to tidy up' follows the pattern: want + object pronoun (me) + to + infinitive (tidy up).
items.ed: 'Mum wants me to tidy up' folgt dem Muster: want + Objektpronomen (me) + to + Infinitiv (tidy up).

items.p: Which sentence is correct?
items.c: I don't want you to be late for dinner.
a: I don't want you to be late for dinner.
ds: I don't want that you are late for dinner.
I don't want you be late for dinner.
I don't want you to being late for dinner.
items.h: Negative pattern: don't want + object pronoun + to + base form of verb.
items.hd: Verneinungsmuster: don't want + Objektpronomen + to + Grundform des Verbs.
items.e: In the negative: don't want + object (you) + to + infinitive (be). 'I don't want you to be late.'
items.ed: In der Verneinung: don't want + Objekt (you) + to + Infinitiv (be). 'I don't want you to be late.'

items.p: Choose the correct question form:
items.c: Does your dad want you to study medicine?
a: Does your dad want you to study medicine?
ds: Does your dad want that you study medicine?
Does your dad wants you to study medicine?
Does your dad want you study medicine?
items.h: In questions with 'does', the main verb stays in the base form. Then: want + object + to + verb.
items.hd: In Fragen mit 'does' bleibt das Hauptverb in der Grundform. Dann: want + Objekt + to + Verb.
items.e: With 'does', the main verb stays in base form: 'Does ... want you to study'. No 'that', no extra -s.
items.ed: Mit 'does' bleibt das Hauptverb in der Grundform: 'Does ... want you to study'. Kein 'dass', kein zusaetzliches -s.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: My mum wants that I come home before 9 o'clock.
items.c: My mum wants me to come home before 9 o'clock.
a: My mum wants me to come home before 9 o'clock.
My mum wants me to come home before 9 o'clock
items.h: In English, we don't use 'want that + clause'. What pattern do we use instead?
items.hd: Im Englischen verwenden wir nicht 'want that + Satz'. Welches Muster verwenden wir stattdessen?
items.e: English doesn't use 'want that...'. Replace with: want + object pronoun (me) + to + infinitive (come).
items.ed: Englisch verwendet nicht 'want that...'. Ersetze durch: want + Objektpronomen (me) + to + Infinitiv (come).

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The doctor wants he to take the medicine three times a day.
items.c: The doctor wants him to take the medicine three times a day.
a: The doctor wants him to take the medicine three times a day.
The doctor wants him to take the medicine three times a day
items.h: After 'want', do we use a subject pronoun (he) or an object pronoun (him)?
items.hd: Nach 'want', verwenden wir ein Subjektpronomen (he) oder ein Objektpronomen (him)?
items.e: After 'want', we need the object pronoun 'him', not the subject pronoun 'he'.
items.ed: Nach 'want' brauchen wir das Objektpronomen 'him', nicht das Subjektpronomen 'he'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Do you want me helping you with the project?
items.c: Do you want me to help you with the project?
a: Do you want me to help you with the project?
Do you want me to help you with the project
items.h: After 'want + object', we use 'to + infinitive', not '-ing'.
items.hd: Nach 'want + Objekt' verwenden wir 'to + Infinitiv', nicht '-ing'.
items.e: The pattern is want + object + to + infinitive: 'want me to help', not 'want me helping'.
items.ed: Das Muster ist want + Objekt + to + Infinitiv: 'want me to help', nicht 'want me helping'.

items.p: Rewrite using 'want someone to': My dad says to my sister: 'Please wash the dishes.' → My dad wants ___.
items.c: my sister to wash the dishes
a: my sister to wash the dishes
her to wash the dishes
My dad wants my sister to wash the dishes.
My dad wants her to wash the dishes.
items.h: Turn the request into the pattern: wants + person + to + verb.
items.hd: Wandle die Bitte in das Muster um: wants + Person + to + Verb.
items.e: The direct speech request becomes: 'My dad wants my sister / her to wash the dishes.'
items.ed: Die direkte Rede wird zu: 'My dad wants my sister / her to wash the dishes.'

items.p: Rewrite using 'not want someone to': The sign says: 'Students: Don't use your phones in class.' → The school doesn't want ___.
items.c: students to use their phones in class
a: students to use their phones in class
the students to use their phones in class
The school doesn't want students to use their phones in class.
The school doesn't want the students to use their phones in class.
items.h: Negative pattern: doesn't want + person + to + infinitive.
items.hd: Verneinungsmuster: doesn't want + Person + to + Infinitiv.
items.e: The negative pattern: 'The school doesn't want students to use their phones in class.'
items.ed: Das Verneinungsmuster: 'The school doesn't want students to use their phones in class.'

items.p: 🇩🇪 Meine Eltern wollen, dass ich mehr lese.
items.c: My parents want me to read more.
a: My parents want me to read more.
My parents want me to read more
items.h: German uses 'wollen, dass...' but English uses: want + object pronoun + to + infinitive.
items.hd: Deutsch verwendet 'wollen, dass...' aber Englisch verwendet: want + Objektpronomen + to + Infinitiv.
items.e: 'wollen, dass ich lese' = 'want me to read'. No 'that' clause in English!
items.ed: 'wollen, dass ich lese' = 'want me to read'. Kein 'dass'-Satz im Englischen!

items.p: 🇩🇪 Willst du, dass ich dir bei den Hausaufgaben helfe?
items.c: Do you want me to help you with your homework?
a: Do you want me to help you with your homework?
Do you want me to help you with your homework
Do you want me to help you with the homework?
items.h: German 'Willst du, dass ich...' becomes 'Do you want me to...' in English.
items.hd: 'Willst du, dass ich...' wird zu 'Do you want me to...' im Englischen.
items.e: 'Willst du, dass ich dir helfe' = 'Do you want me to help you'. Question + want + object + to + infinitive.
items.ed: 'Willst du, dass ich dir helfe' = 'Do you want me to help you'. Frage + want + Objekt + to + Infinitiv.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: wants / the coach / to / train / us / harder
items.c: The coach wants us to train harder.
a: The coach wants us to train harder.
The coach wants us to train harder
items.h: Start with the subject, then: wants + object pronoun + to + verb + adverb.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Subjekt, dann: wants + Objektpronomen + to + Verb + Adverb.
items.e: Subject (The coach) + wants + object (us) + to + verb (train) + adverb (harder).
items.ed: Subjekt (The coach) + wants + Objekt (us) + to + Verb (train) + Adverb (harder).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: doesn't / her / to / want / go / she / alone / him
items.c: She doesn't want him to go alone.
a: She doesn't want him to go alone.
She doesn't want him to go alone
items.h: Start with the subject + doesn't + want + object pronoun + to + verb + adverb.
items.hd: Beginne mit Subjekt + doesn't + want + Objektpronomen + to + Verb + Adverb.
items.e: She + doesn't want + him + to go + alone. Note: 'her' is a distractor word not needed in this sentence.
items.ed: She + doesn't want + him + to go + alone. Beachte: 'her' ist ein Ablenker-Wort, das in diesem Satz nicht gebraucht wird.

items.p: Match each sentence beginning with the correct ending. 1: My mum wants me 2: The teacher doesn't want us 3: Do you want her 4: Dad wanted them 5: She wants you
items.h: Match each subject + object combination with a logical to-infinitive ending.
items.hd: Ordne jede Subjekt + Objekt-Kombination einer logischen to-Infinitiv-Endung zu.
items.e: 1+d: wants me to do my homework. 2+a: doesn't want us to talk in class. 3+e: want her to come to the party? 4+b: wanted them to be quiet. 5+c: wants you to call her back. a: to talk in class. b: to be quiet. c: to call her back. d: to do my homework. e: to come to the party?
items.ed: 1+d: wants me to do my homework. 2+a: doesn't want us to talk in class. 3+e: want her to come to the party? 4+b: wanted them to be quiet. 5+c: wants you to call her back.

items.p: Your friend's parents want her to become a doctor. Ask about this: What / parents / want / do?
items.c: What do her parents want her to do?
a: What do her parents want her to do?
What do her parents want her to do
What do your parents want you to do?
items.h: Form a question with 'what' + do + subject + want + object + to + verb.
items.hd: Bilde eine Frage mit 'what' + do + Subjekt + want + Objekt + to + Verb.
items.e: Wh-question pattern: What + do + subject (her parents) + want + object (her) + to do?
items.ed: W-Fragen-Muster: What + do + Subjekt (her parents) + want + Objekt (her) + to do?

items.p: I ___ (play) football every weekend. It's my favourite hobby.
a: play
items.h: 'Every weekend' tells us this is a regular habit. Which tense describes routines?
items.hd: 'Every weekend' sagt uns, dass das eine Gewohnheit ist. Welche Zeitform beschreibt Routinen?
items.e: 'Every weekend' is a frequency expression signalling a habitual action → present simple: 'play'.
items.ed: 'Every weekend' ist ein Haeufigkeitsausdruck, der eine gewohnte Handlung signalisiert → Present Simple: 'play'.

items.p: Look! The cat ___ (climb) up the tree!
a: is climbing
's climbing
items.h: 'Look!' tells us something is happening RIGHT NOW. Which tense describes actions in progress?
items.hd: 'Look!' sagt uns, dass gerade etwas passiert. Welche Zeitform beschreibt Handlungen, die gerade stattfinden?
items.e: 'Look!' signals an action happening at this moment → present continuous: 'is climbing'.
items.ed: 'Look!' signalisiert eine Handlung, die in diesem Moment passiert → Present Continuous: 'is climbing'.

items.p: We ___ (visit) our grandparents last Sunday.
a: visited
items.h: 'Last Sunday' is a finished past time. Which tense do we use for completed actions at a specific past time?
items.hd: 'Last Sunday' ist eine abgeschlossene Vergangenheit. Welche Zeitform verwenden wir fuer abgeschlossene Handlungen?
items.e: 'Last Sunday' is a specific finished past time → past simple: 'visited'.
items.ed: 'Last Sunday' ist ein bestimmter, abgeschlossener Zeitpunkt in der Vergangenheit → Past Simple: 'visited'.

items.p: She ___ (live) in Vienna since 2019.
a: has lived
's lived
has been living
items.h: 'Since 2019' connects the past to the present. Which tense links past actions to the present?
items.hd: 'Since 2019' verbindet die Vergangenheit mit der Gegenwart. Welche Zeitform verbindet vergangene Handlungen mit der Gegenwart?
items.e: 'Since 2019' signals a duration from past to present → present perfect: 'has lived'.
items.ed: 'Since 2019' signalisiert eine Dauer von der Vergangenheit bis zur Gegenwart → Present Perfect: 'has lived'.

items.p: By the time we arrived, the concert ___ already ___ (start).
a: had ... started
had already started
items.h: 'By the time we arrived' = before we arrived. Which tense shows an action completed before another past action?
items.hd: 'By the time we arrived' = bevor wir ankamen. Welche Zeitform zeigt eine Handlung, die vor einer anderen vergangenen Handlung abgeschlossen war?
items.e: 'By the time we arrived' signals the concert started BEFORE arrival → past perfect: 'had started'.
items.ed: 'By the time we arrived' signalisiert, dass das Konzert VOR der Ankunft begonnen hatte → Past Perfect: 'had started'.

items.p: I ___ (read) six books so far this year, and I ___ (read) another one right now.
a: have read ... am reading
've read ... 'm reading
items.h: 'So far this year' connects past to present. 'Right now' describes what is happening at this moment.
items.hd: 'So far this year' verbindet Vergangenheit mit Gegenwart. 'Right now' beschreibt, was gerade passiert.
items.e: 'So far' → present perfect (have read). 'Right now' → present continuous (am reading). Two different time expressions, two different tenses.
items.ed: 'So far' → Present Perfect (have read). 'Right now' → Present Continuous (am reading). Zwei verschiedene Zeitausdruecke, zwei verschiedene Zeitformen.

items.p: Which sentence uses the correct tense for the time expression?
items.c: I have never been to Australia.
a: I have never been to Australia.
ds: I am never going to Australia.
items.h: 'Never' for life experience (up to now) uses which tense?
items.hd: 'Never' fuer Lebenserfahrung (bis jetzt) verwendet welche Zeitform?
items.e: 'Never' for life experience uses present perfect: 'I have never been'. It connects past experience to now.
items.ed: 'Never' fuer Lebenserfahrung verwendet Present Perfect: 'I have never been'. Es verbindet vergangene Erfahrung mit der Gegenwart.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence:
items.c: I was doing my homework when my friend called.
a: I was doing my homework when my friend called.
ds: I did my homework when my friend was calling.
I have done my homework when my friend called.
I was doing my homework when my friend has called.
items.h: The longer background action uses past continuous. The short interrupting action uses past simple.
items.hd: Die laengere Hintergrundhandlung verwendet Past Continuous. Die kurze unterbrechende Handlung verwendet Past Simple.
items.e: Past continuous (was doing) for the longer action + past simple (called) for the short interruption.
items.ed: Past Continuous (was doing) fuer die laengere Handlung + Past Simple (called) fuer die kurze Unterbrechung.

items.p: Which sentence is NOT correct?
items.c: I have seen that film yesterday.
a: I have seen that film yesterday.
ds: I have already seen that film.
I had seen that film before she recommended it.
items.h: Can you use present perfect with 'yesterday'? 'Yesterday' is a specific finished past time.
items.hd: Kann man Present Perfect mit 'yesterday' verwenden? 'Yesterday' ist ein bestimmter, abgeschlossener Zeitpunkt.
items.e: 'Yesterday' is a specific past time and cannot be used with present perfect. Use past simple: 'I saw that film yesterday.'
items.ed: 'Yesterday' ist ein bestimmter Zeitpunkt in der Vergangenheit und kann nicht mit Present Perfect verwendet werden. Verwende Past Simple: 'I saw that film yesterday.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She has visited London last summer.
a: She visited London last summer.
She visited London last summer
items.h: 'Last summer' is a specific finished past time. Can we use present perfect with it?
items.hd: 'Last summer' ist eine bestimmte, abgeschlossene Vergangenheit. Kann man Present Perfect damit verwenden?
items.e: 'Last summer' requires past simple, not present perfect. 'She visited London last summer.'
items.ed: 'Last summer' erfordert Past Simple, nicht Present Perfect. 'She visited London last summer.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I am living in Graz since I was born.
items.c: I have lived in Graz since I was born.
a: I have lived in Graz since I was born.
I have lived in Graz since I was born
I've lived in Graz since I was born.
I have been living in Graz since I was born.
items.h: 'Since' connects past to present. Which tense do we use for actions that started in the past and continue now?
items.hd: 'Since' verbindet Vergangenheit mit Gegenwart. Welche Zeitform verwenden wir fuer Handlungen, die in der Vergangenheit begannen und jetzt andauern?
items.e: 'Since' requires present perfect (or present perfect continuous), not present continuous. 'I have lived in Graz since I was born.'
items.ed: 'Since' erfordert Present Perfect (oder Present Perfect Continuous), nicht Present Continuous. 'I have lived in Graz since I was born.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: When I came home, my sister already ate all the pizza.
items.c: When I came home, my sister had already eaten all the pizza.
a: When I came home, my sister had already eaten all the pizza.
When I came home, my sister had already eaten all the pizza
items.h: The pizza eating happened BEFORE you came home. Which tense shows an action completed before another past action?
items.hd: Das Pizzaessen passierte VOR deiner Ankunft. Welche Zeitform zeigt eine Handlung, die vor einer anderen vergangenen Handlung abgeschlossen war?
items.e: The eating happened before the arrival. Use past perfect for the earlier action: 'had already eaten'.
items.ed: Das Essen geschah vor der Ankunft. Verwende Past Perfect fuer die fruehere Handlung: 'had already eaten'.

items.p: Change the time expression and adjust the tense: 'I go swimming every Friday.' → Change to 'last Friday': I ___.
a: went swimming last Friday
I went swimming last Friday.
I went swimming last Friday
items.h: 'Last Friday' is a past time expression. Change present simple to past simple.
items.hd: 'Last Friday' ist ein Vergangenheitsausdruck. Aendere Present Simple zu Past Simple.
items.e: 'Every Friday' (present simple) → 'last Friday' (past simple): 'go' becomes 'went'.
items.ed: 'Every Friday' (Present Simple) → 'last Friday' (Past Simple): 'go' wird zu 'went'.

items.p: Change the time expression and adjust the tense: 'She bought a new phone yesterday.' → Change to 'just': She ___.
items.c: has just bought a new phone
a: has just bought a new phone
She has just bought a new phone.
She has just bought a new phone
She's just bought a new phone.
items.h: 'Just' signals a very recent action and uses present perfect.
items.hd: 'Just' signalisiert eine sehr kuerzliche Handlung und verwendet Present Perfect.
items.e: 'Yesterday' (past simple) → 'just' (present perfect): 'bought' → 'has just bought'.
items.ed: 'Yesterday' (Past Simple) → 'just' (Present Perfect): 'bought' → 'has just bought'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ich lerne seit drei Jahren Englisch.
items.c: I have been learning English for three years.
a: I have been learning English for three years.
I have been learning English for three years
I've been learning English for three years.
I have learned English for three years.
I have learnt English for three years.
items.h: German uses present tense with 'seit', but English uses present perfect (continuous) with 'for'.
items.hd: Deutsch verwendet Praesens mit 'seit', aber Englisch verwendet Present Perfect (Continuous) mit 'for'.
items.e: German 'seit drei Jahren' (present tense) = English 'for three years' (present perfect/continuous). 'Seit' ≠ 'since' here — use 'for' with durations.
items.ed: Deutsch 'seit drei Jahren' (Praesens) = Englisch 'for three years' (Present Perfect/Continuous). Achtung: 'seit' mit Zeitraum = 'for', nicht 'since'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Als wir im Restaurant ankamen, hatte unser Essen schon begonnen kalt zu werden.
items.c: When we arrived at the restaurant, our food had already started to get cold.
a: When we arrived at the restaurant, our food had already started to get cold.
When we arrived at the restaurant, our food had already started to get cold
When we arrived at the restaurant, our food had already begun to get cold.
When we got to the restaurant, our food had already started to get cold.
items.h: Two actions in the past: the earlier one (food getting cold) needs past perfect. The later one (arriving) is past simple.
items.hd: Zwei Handlungen in der Vergangenheit: die fruehere (Essen wird kalt) braucht Past Perfect. Die spaetere (Ankunft) ist Past Simple.
items.e: 'Als wir ankamen' = 'When we arrived' (past simple). 'hatte schon begonnen' = 'had already started' (past perfect for the earlier action).
items.ed: 'Als wir ankamen' = 'When we arrived' (Past Simple). 'hatte schon begonnen' = 'had already started' (Past Perfect fuer die fruehere Handlung).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: for / she / lived / has / ten / here / years
items.c: She has lived here for ten years.
a: She has lived here for ten years.
She has lived here for ten years
items.h: Start with the subject, then has/have + past participle. 'For ten years' goes at the end.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Subjekt, dann has/have + Partizip. 'For ten years' kommt am Ende.
items.e: Subject + has + past participle + place + for + duration: 'She has lived here for ten years.'
items.ed: Subjekt + has + Partizip + Ort + for + Dauer: 'She has lived here for ten years.'

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: the / had / train / we / already / arrived / left / when
items.c: The train had already left when we arrived.
a: The train had already left when we arrived.
The train had already left when we arrived
When we arrived, the train had already left.
items.h: The earlier action (train leaving) uses past perfect. The later action (arriving) uses past simple.
items.hd: Die fruehere Handlung (Zug faehrt ab) verwendet Past Perfect. Die spaetere Handlung (Ankunft) verwendet Past Simple.
items.e: Earlier action (past perfect): 'The train had already left'. Later action (past simple): 'when we arrived'.
items.ed: Fruehere Handlung (Past Perfect): 'The train had already left'. Spaetere Handlung (Past Simple): 'when we arrived'.

items.p: Match each time expression with the correct tense. 1: every day 2: right now 3: yesterday 4: since 2020 5: before we arrived 6: just
items.h: Think about which time expression belongs to which tense: present simple, present continuous, past simple, present perfect, or past perfect.
items.hd: Ueberlege, welcher Zeitausdruck zu welcher Zeitform gehoert: Present Simple, Present Continuous, Past Simple, Present Perfect oder Past Perfect.
items.e: 1+c: every day → present simple. 2+d: right now → present continuous. 3+a: yesterday → past simple. 4+e: since 2020 → present perfect continuous. 5+b: before we arrived → past perfect. 6+f: just → present perfect. a: past simple. b: past perfect. c: present simple. d: present continuous. e: present perfect continuous. f: present perfect.
items.ed: 1+c: every day → Present Simple. 2+d: right now → Present Continuous. 3+a: yesterday → Past Simple. 4+e: since 2020 → Present Perfect Continuous. 5+b: before we arrived → Past Perfect. 6+f: just → Present Perfect.

items.p: Your friend tells you about her trip to London. She went there last week and is now back home. Which sentence fits?
items.c: I visited the Tower of London last week.
a: I visited the Tower of London last week.
ds: I have visited the Tower of London last week.
I was visiting the Tower of London last week.
I had visited the Tower of London last week.
items.h: 'Last week' is a specific finished past time. Which tense describes completed actions at a specific time?
items.hd: 'Last week' ist eine bestimmte, abgeschlossene Vergangenheit. Welche Zeitform beschreibt abgeschlossene Handlungen zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt?
items.e: 'Last week' requires past simple because it refers to a specific finished time: 'I visited'.
items.ed: 'Last week' erfordert Past Simple, weil es sich auf eine bestimmte, abgeschlossene Zeit bezieht: 'I visited'.

items.p: Be careful with the knife! Don't cut ___!
a: yourself
items.h: You're talking to one person. The subject (you) and the object are the same. Which reflexive pronoun matches 'you' singular?
items.hd: Du sprichst eine Person an. Subjekt (you) und Objekt sind gleich. Welches Reflexivpronomen passt zu 'you' Einzahl?
items.e: When talking to one person, use 'yourself' (not 'yourselves' for plural). Subject and object = same person.
items.ed: Wenn man eine Person anspricht, verwendet man 'yourself' (nicht 'yourselves' fuer Mehrzahl). Subjekt und Objekt = gleiche Person.

items.p: The children made the birthday cake all by ___.
a: themselves
items.h: 'The children' = they. 'By ___' means they did it alone. What is the reflexive pronoun for 'they'?
items.hd: 'The children' = they. 'By ___' bedeutet, sie haben es allein gemacht. Wie lautet das Reflexivpronomen fuer 'they'?
items.e: 'By themselves' = alone, without help. 'The children' = they → 'themselves'. Note: 'theirselves' is NOT correct.
items.ed: 'By themselves' = allein, ohne Hilfe. 'The children' = they → 'themselves'. Beachte: 'theirselves' ist NICHT korrekt.

items.p: He looked at ___ in the mirror and smiled.
a: himself
items.h: He looked at WHO in the mirror? He looked at... himself! Subject = object = same person.
items.hd: Er schaute WEN im Spiegel an? Er schaute... sich selbst an! Subjekt = Objekt = gleiche Person.
items.e: When the subject (he) and object are the same person, use the reflexive pronoun 'himself'.
items.ed: Wenn Subjekt (he) und Objekt die gleiche Person sind, verwende das Reflexivpronomen 'himself'.

items.p: I taught ___ to play the guitar using YouTube videos.
a: myself
items.h: Who taught you? You taught YOURSELF! Subject (I) and object are the same person.
items.hd: Wer hat dir beigebracht? Du hast es DIR SELBST beigebracht! Subjekt (I) und Objekt sind die gleiche Person.
items.e: When you do something to/for yourself, use the reflexive: 'I taught myself'. Subject = object.
items.ed: Wenn man etwas fuer sich selbst tut, verwende das Reflexivpronomen: 'I taught myself'. Subjekt = Objekt.

items.p: She introduced ___ to the new neighbours when she moved in.
a: herself
items.h: She introduced WHO? She introduced... herself! The person doing and receiving the action is the same.
items.hd: Sie stellte WEN vor? Sie stellte... sich selbst vor! Die Person, die die Handlung ausfuehrt und empfaengt, ist die gleiche.
items.e: 'She introduced herself' — the subject (she) is doing the action TO herself → reflexive pronoun 'herself'.
items.ed: 'She introduced herself' — das Subjekt (she) fuehrt die Handlung AN sich selbst aus → Reflexivpronomen 'herself'.

items.p: In English, we say 'I can't remember' — NOT 'I can't remember ___'.
a: myself
items.h: In German, 'sich erinnern' is reflexive, but in English, 'remember' does NOT need a reflexive pronoun. What's the word you should NOT add?
items.hd: Im Deutschen ist 'sich erinnern' reflexiv, aber im Englischen braucht 'remember' KEIN Reflexivpronomen. Welches Wort sollte man NICHT hinzufuegen?
items.e: German false friend: 'sich erinnern' uses a reflexive pronoun, but English 'remember' does NOT. Say 'I remember', NOT 'I remember myself'.
items.ed: Falscher Freund: 'sich erinnern' ist reflexiv, aber Englisch 'remember' ist es NICHT. Sage 'I remember', NICHT 'I remember myself'.

items.p: Which sentence correctly uses a reflexive pronoun?
items.c: She burnt herself while cooking dinner.
a: She burnt herself while cooking dinner.
ds: She burnt her while cooking dinner.
She burnt himself while cooking dinner.
She burnt themselves while cooking dinner.
items.h: The subject is 'she'. She did something to herself. Which reflexive pronoun matches 'she'?
items.hd: Das Subjekt ist 'she'. Sie hat sich etwas angetan. Welches Reflexivpronomen passt zu 'she'?
items.e: 'She' = female singular → 'herself'. 'She burnt herself' = she was the one who got burnt.
items.ed: 'She' = weiblich Einzahl → 'herself'. 'She burnt herself' = sie hat sich selbst verbrannt.

items.p: Which sentence does NOT need a reflexive pronoun?
a: I feel great today!
ds: She hurt herself during the match.
We enjoyed ourselves at the party.
items.h: Some English verbs that are reflexive in German (sich fuehlen) are NOT reflexive in English.
items.hd: Manche englische Verben, die im Deutschen reflexiv sind (sich fuehlen), sind im Englischen NICHT reflexiv.
items.e: 'Feel' is NOT reflexive in English (unlike German 'sich fuehlen'). We say 'I feel great', not 'I feel myself great'.
items.ed: 'Feel' ist im Englischen NICHT reflexiv (anders als Deutsch 'sich fuehlen'). Man sagt 'I feel great', nicht 'I feel myself great'.

items.p: Choose the correct sentence with 'by + reflexive pronoun':
items.c: She prefers to travel by herself.
a: She prefers to travel by herself.
ds: She prefers to travel by her.
She prefers to travel by sheself.
She prefers to travel by herselves.
items.h: 'By + reflexive pronoun' means 'alone'. What is the reflexive pronoun for 'she'?
items.hd: 'By + Reflexivpronomen' bedeutet 'allein'. Wie lautet das Reflexivpronomen fuer 'she'?
items.e: 'By herself' = alone. The reflexive for 'she' is 'herself'. 'By herself' emphasises she did it without anyone else.
items.ed: 'By herself' = allein. Das Reflexivpronomen fuer 'she' ist 'herself'. 'By herself' betont, dass sie es ohne andere getan hat.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: We really enjoyed us at the beach yesterday.
items.c: We really enjoyed ourselves at the beach yesterday.
a: We really enjoyed ourselves at the beach yesterday.
We really enjoyed ourselves at the beach yesterday
items.h: 'Enjoy' in English always needs a reflexive pronoun. 'We' → which reflexive form?
items.hd: 'Enjoy' im Englischen braucht immer ein Reflexivpronomen. 'We' → welche reflexive Form?
items.e: 'Enjoy' requires a reflexive pronoun in English: 'ourselves' (not 'us'). L1 trap: German 'Wir haben uns amuesiert.'
items.ed: 'Enjoy' erfordert im Englischen ein Reflexivpronomen: 'ourselves' (nicht 'us'). Deutsch: 'Wir haben uns amuesiert.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I can't concentrate myself when there's loud music.
items.c: I can't concentrate when there's loud music.
a: I can't concentrate when there's loud music.
I can't concentrate when there's loud music
items.h: 'Concentrate' is NOT reflexive in English, even though German 'sich konzentrieren' is.
items.hd: 'Concentrate' ist im Englischen NICHT reflexiv, obwohl Deutsch 'sich konzentrieren' reflexiv ist.
items.e: German 'sich konzentrieren' is reflexive, but English 'concentrate' is NOT. Remove 'myself'.
items.ed: Deutsch 'sich konzentrieren' ist reflexiv, aber Englisch 'concentrate' ist es NICHT. Entferne 'myself'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: The children dressed theirselves quickly because they were late.
items.c: The children dressed themselves quickly because they were late.
a: The children dressed themselves quickly because they were late.
The children dressed themselves quickly because they were late
items.h: Is 'theirselves' a real word? What's the correct reflexive form for 'they'?
items.hd: Ist 'theirselves' ein echtes Wort? Wie lautet die richtige reflexive Form fuer 'they'?
items.e: 'Theirselves' is NOT a word. The correct reflexive for 'they' is 'themselves'.
items.ed: 'Theirselves' ist KEIN Wort. Das richtige Reflexivpronomen fuer 'they' ist 'themselves'.

items.p: Rewrite with 'by + reflexive pronoun' to mean 'alone': My little sister cleaned her room without any help. → My little sister cleaned her room ___.
a: by herself
My little sister cleaned her room by herself.
all by herself
items.h: 'Without any help' = alone = by + reflexive pronoun. 'My sister' = she → ___?
items.hd: 'Without any help' = allein = by + Reflexivpronomen. 'My sister' = she → ___?
items.e: 'Without any help' means 'alone' = 'by herself'. 'My sister' = she → herself.
items.ed: 'Without any help' bedeutet 'allein' = 'by herself'. 'My sister' = she → herself.

items.p: Add the correct reflexive pronoun where needed. If no reflexive is needed, write 'no change': I need to relax ___ after this stressful week.
a: no change
No change
I need to relax after this stressful week.
items.h: In German, 'sich entspannen' is reflexive. But is 'relax' reflexive in English?
items.hd: Im Deutschen ist 'sich entspannen' reflexiv. Aber ist 'relax' reflexiv im Englischen?
items.e: 'Relax' is NOT reflexive in English (unlike German 'sich entspannen'). No reflexive pronoun needed.
items.ed: 'Relax' ist im Englischen NICHT reflexiv (anders als Deutsch 'sich entspannen'). Kein Reflexivpronomen noetig.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ich habe mich verletzt, als ich Fussball gespielt habe.
items.c: I hurt myself when I was playing football.
a: I hurt myself when I was playing football.
I hurt myself when I was playing football
I hurt myself playing football.
I hurt myself while playing football.
I injured myself when I was playing football.
items.h: 'Sich verletzen' = 'hurt oneself' — this IS reflexive in both languages.
items.hd: 'Sich verletzen' = 'hurt oneself' — das IST in beiden Sprachen reflexiv.
items.e: 'Sich verletzen' = 'hurt myself/yourself/etc.' This verb IS reflexive in both German and English.
items.ed: 'Sich verletzen' = 'hurt myself/yourself/etc.' Dieses Verb IST in beiden Sprachen reflexiv.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ich erinnere mich nicht an seinen Namen.
a: I don't remember his name.
I don't remember his name
I can't remember his name.
I do not remember his name.
items.h: 'Sich erinnern' is reflexive in German, but 'remember' in English is NOT reflexive!
items.hd: 'Sich erinnern' ist reflexiv im Deutschen, aber 'remember' im Englischen ist NICHT reflexiv!
items.e: German 'sich erinnern' is reflexive, but English 'remember' is NOT. Say 'I don't remember', NOT 'I don't remember myself'.
items.ed: Deutsch 'sich erinnern' ist reflexiv, aber Englisch 'remember' ist NICHT reflexiv. Sage 'I don't remember', NICHT 'I don't remember myself'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: himself / he / cut / shaving / while
a: He cut himself while shaving.
He cut himself while shaving
items.h: Start with the subject, then verb + reflexive pronoun + while + -ing.
items.hd: Beginne mit dem Subjekt, dann Verb + Reflexivpronomen + while + -ing.
items.e: Subject (He) + verb (cut) + reflexive (himself) + while + -ing (shaving).
items.ed: Subjekt (He) + Verb (cut) + Reflexivpronomen (himself) + while + -ing (shaving).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: by / the / project / ourselves / we / did / whole
items.c: We did the whole project by ourselves.
a: We did the whole project by ourselves.
We did the whole project by ourselves
items.h: Subject + verb + object + 'by ourselves' (= alone, without help).
items.hd: Subjekt + Verb + Objekt + 'by ourselves' (= allein, ohne Hilfe).
items.e: We + did + the whole project + by ourselves. 'By ourselves' means without any help.
items.ed: We + did + the whole project + by ourselves. 'By ourselves' bedeutet ohne jede Hilfe.

items.p: Match each subject with the correct reflexive pronoun. 1: I 2: you (singular) 3: he 4: she 5: we 6: they
items.h: Match each subject pronoun with its reflexive form: myself, yourself, himself, herself, ourselves, themselves.
items.hd: Ordne jedem Subjektpronomen seine reflexive Form zu: myself, yourself, himself, herself, ourselves, themselves.
items.e: 1+b: I → myself. 2+f: you (singular) → yourself. 3+d: he → himself. 4+a: she → herself. 5+c: we → ourselves. 6+e: they → themselves. a: herself. b: myself. c: ourselves. d: himself. e: themselves. f: yourself.
items.ed: 1+b: I → myself. 2+f: you (Einzahl) → yourself. 3+d: he → himself. 4+a: she → herself. 5+c: we → ourselves. 6+e: they → themselves.

items.p: Your friend fell off her bike yesterday. Ask if she hurt herself. Start with 'Did...'
a: Did you hurt yourself?
Did you hurt yourself
Did she hurt herself?
items.h: In questions, the reflexive pronoun still matches the subject. 'You' → ___?
items.hd: In Fragen stimmt das Reflexivpronomen immer noch mit dem Subjekt ueberein. 'You' → ___?
items.e: Question form: Did + subject (you) + verb (hurt) + reflexive (yourself)? The reflexive matches the subject.
items.ed: Frageform: Did + Subjekt (you) + Verb (hurt) + Reflexivpronomen (yourself)? Das Reflexivpronomen stimmt mit dem Subjekt ueberein.

items.p: Can you turn ___ the lights? It's too dark in here.
a: on
items.h: It's too dark — you need more light. Which particle means 'start/activate'?
items.hd: Es ist zu dunkel — man braucht mehr Licht. Welche Partikel bedeutet 'anschalten/aktivieren'?
items.e: 'Turn on' = switch on, activate. The context 'too dark' tells us the lights need to be switched on.
items.ed: 'Turn on' = anschalten, aktivieren. Der Kontext 'zu dunkel' sagt uns, dass das Licht angeschaltet werden muss.

items.p: My car broke ___ on the motorway and I had to call for help.
a: down
items.h: When a machine stops working, we say it 'breaks ___'.
items.hd: Wenn eine Maschine aufhoert zu funktionieren, sagen wir, sie 'breaks ___'.
items.e: 'Break down' = stop working (machines/vehicles). 'My car broke down' = my car stopped working.
items.ed: 'Break down' = aufhoeren zu funktionieren (Maschinen/Fahrzeuge). 'My car broke down' = mein Auto hat den Geist aufgegeben.

items.p: She gave ___ smoking last year and feels much healthier now.
a: up
items.h: When you stop doing something (especially a bad habit), you 'give ___'.
items.hd: Wenn man aufhoert, etwas zu tun (besonders eine schlechte Gewohnheit), sagt man 'give ___'.
items.e: 'Give up' = stop doing something, quit a habit. 'She gave up smoking' = she stopped smoking.
items.ed: 'Give up' = aufhoeren, etwas zu tun. 'She gave up smoking' = sie hat mit dem Rauchen aufgehoert.

items.p: Could you look ___ my dog while I'm on holiday?
a: after
items.h: When you take care of someone or something, you 'look ___' them.
items.hd: Wenn man sich um jemanden oder etwas kuemmert, sagt man 'look ___'.
items.e: 'Look after' = take care of. 'Look after my dog' = take care of my dog while I'm away.
items.ed: 'Look after' = sich kuemmern um. 'Look after my dog' = kuemmere dich um meinen Hund, waehrend ich weg bin.

items.p: The music is too loud. Please turn ___ ___! (pronoun: it)
a: it down
items.h: With separable phrasal verbs + a pronoun, the pronoun goes BETWEEN the verb and the particle: turn IT down.
items.hd: Bei trennbaren Phrasal Verbs + Pronomen steht das Pronomen ZWISCHEN Verb und Partikel: turn IT down.
items.e: 'Turn down' is separable. With pronouns, the pronoun MUST go in the middle: 'turn it down' (NOT 'turn down it').
items.ed: 'Turn down' ist trennbar. Bei Pronomen MUSS das Pronomen in die Mitte: 'turn it down' (NICHT 'turn down it').

items.p: We've run ___ ___ milk. Can you go to the shop?
a: out of
items.h: When you have used all of something and have none left, you 'run ___ ___ it'.
items.hd: Wenn man alles von etwas aufgebraucht hat und nichts mehr uebrig ist, sagt man 'run ___ ___ it'.
items.e: 'Run out of' = have none left, use up the supply. 'We've run out of milk' = there's no milk left.
items.ed: 'Run out of' = nichts mehr haben, den Vorrat aufbrauchen. 'We've run out of milk' = es ist keine Milch mehr da.

items.p: Which sentence correctly places the pronoun with the phrasal verb?
items.c: Please pick it up from the floor.
a: Please pick it up from the floor.
ds: Please pick up it from the floor.
Please pick from the floor it up.
Please it pick up from the floor.
items.h: With separable phrasal verbs, a pronoun MUST go between the verb and the particle.
items.hd: Bei trennbaren Phrasal Verbs MUSS ein Pronomen zwischen Verb und Partikel stehen.
items.e: 'Pick up' is separable. With pronouns: verb + pronoun + particle = 'pick it up' (NOT 'pick up it').
items.ed: 'Pick up' ist trennbar. Mit Pronomen: Verb + Pronomen + Partikel = 'pick it up' (NICHT 'pick up it').

items.p: What does 'take off' mean in this sentence? 'The business was slow at first, but then it really took off.'
a: became very successful suddenly
items.h: A business that 'takes off' starts doing very well. Think of a rocket taking off — it goes up quickly!
items.hd: Ein Geschaeft, das 'takes off', laeuft ploetzlich sehr gut. Denke an eine Rakete — sie geht schnell nach oben!
items.e: 'Take off' here means 'become suddenly successful'. Many phrasal verbs have multiple meanings depending on context.
items.ed: 'Take off' bedeutet hier 'ploetzlich erfolgreich werden'. Viele Phrasal Verbs haben je nach Kontext mehrere Bedeutungen.

items.p: Which phrasal verb is INSEPARABLE (the pronoun cannot go in the middle)?
a: look after
items.h: With inseparable phrasal verbs, you CANNOT put a pronoun between the verb and particle. Which one can't you split?
items.hd: Bei untrennbaren Phrasal Verbs KANN man kein Pronomen zwischen Verb und Partikel setzen. Welches kann man nicht trennen?
items.e: 'Look after' is inseparable: 'look after him' (correct), NOT 'look him after' (wrong). Turn off, pick up, and put away are all separable.
items.ed: 'Look after' ist untrennbar: 'look after him' (richtig), NICHT 'look him after' (falsch). Turn off, pick up und put away sind alle trennbar.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Can you turn off it? The TV is too loud.
items.c: Can you turn it off? The TV is too loud.
a: Can you turn it off? The TV is too loud.
Can you turn it off? The TV is too loud
items.h: With separable phrasal verbs, pronouns go BETWEEN the verb and the particle, not after.
items.hd: Bei trennbaren Phrasal Verbs stehen Pronomen ZWISCHEN Verb und Partikel, nicht danach.
items.e: 'Turn off' is separable. With pronouns: 'turn it off' (NOT 'turn off it'). Pronouns must go in the middle.
items.ed: 'Turn off' ist trennbar. Mit Pronomen: 'turn it off' (NICHT 'turn off it'). Pronomen muessen in die Mitte.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: She looks after very well her little brother.
items.c: She looks after her little brother very well.
a: She looks after her little brother very well.
She looks after her little brother very well
items.h: 'Look after' is inseparable. The object goes directly after the phrasal verb, then the adverb.
items.hd: 'Look after' ist untrennbar. Das Objekt kommt direkt nach dem Phrasal Verb, dann das Adverb.
items.e: 'Look after' is inseparable — nothing goes between 'after' and the object. 'Looks after her brother very well.'
items.ed: 'Look after' ist untrennbar — nichts kommt zwischen 'after' und das Objekt. 'Looks after her brother very well.'

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I need to come up an idea for the school project.
items.c: I need to come up with an idea for the school project.
a: I need to come up with an idea for the school project.
I need to come up with an idea for the school project
items.h: The complete phrasal verb is three words: come up ___. What's missing?
items.hd: Das vollstaendige Phrasal Verb besteht aus drei Woertern: come up ___. Was fehlt?
items.e: 'Come up with' is a three-word phrasal verb meaning 'think of / invent'. The 'with' is essential and cannot be dropped.
items.ed: 'Come up with' ist ein dreiteiliges Phrasal Verb, das 'sich ausdenken / erfinden' bedeutet. Das 'with' ist wesentlich und darf nicht weggelassen werden.

items.p: Replace the underlined word with a phrasal verb: She STOPPED eating chocolate for health reasons. → She ___.
items.c: gave up eating chocolate for health reasons
a: gave up eating chocolate for health reasons
She gave up eating chocolate for health reasons.
She gave up eating chocolate for health reasons
items.h: Which phrasal verb means 'to stop doing something' (especially a habit)?
items.hd: Welches Phrasal Verb bedeutet 'aufhoeren, etwas zu tun' (besonders eine Gewohnheit)?
items.e: 'Give up' = stop doing something. 'She gave up eating chocolate' = she stopped eating chocolate.
items.ed: 'Give up' = aufhoeren, etwas zu tun. 'She gave up eating chocolate' = sie hat aufgehoert, Schokolade zu essen.

items.p: Replace the noun with 'it' and rewrite: Please put away your phone. → Please ___.
a: put it away
Please put it away.
Please put it away
items.h: With separable phrasal verbs, when you replace the noun with a pronoun, where does the pronoun go?
items.hd: Bei trennbaren Phrasal Verbs, wenn man das Nomen durch ein Pronomen ersetzt, wo steht dann das Pronomen?
items.e: 'Put away' is separable. With noun: 'put away your phone' OR 'put your phone away'. With pronoun: ONLY 'put it away' (never 'put away it').
items.ed: 'Put away' ist trennbar. Mit Nomen: 'put away your phone' ODER 'put your phone away'. Mit Pronomen: NUR 'put it away' (nie 'put away it').

items.p: 🇩🇪 Kannst du auf meinen Hund aufpassen, waehrend ich weg bin?
items.c: Can you look after my dog while I'm away?
a: Can you look after my dog while I'm away?
Can you look after my dog while I'm away
Can you look after my dog while I am away?
Could you look after my dog while I'm away?
items.h: 'Aufpassen auf' = 'look after'. Remember: 'look after' is inseparable!
items.hd: 'Aufpassen auf' = 'look after'. Merke: 'look after' ist untrennbar!
items.e: 'Aufpassen auf' = 'look after'. 'Look after' is inseparable, so the object follows directly: 'look after my dog'.
items.ed: 'Aufpassen auf' = 'look after'. 'Look after' ist untrennbar, das Objekt folgt direkt: 'look after my dog'.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Ich habe die Heizung eingeschaltet, weil mir kalt war, aber mein Bruder hat sie wieder ausgeschaltet.
items.c: I turned on the heating because I was cold, but my brother turned it off again.
a: I turned on the heating because I was cold, but my brother turned it off again.
I turned on the heating because I was cold, but my brother turned it off again
I turned the heating on because I was cold, but my brother turned it off again.
items.h: 'Einschalten' = 'turn on'. 'Ausschalten' = 'turn off'. Watch out for pronoun placement with 'it'!
items.hd: 'Einschalten' = 'turn on'. 'Ausschalten' = 'turn off'. Achte auf die Pronomen-Stellung mit 'it'!
items.e: 'Einschalten' = 'turn on'. 'Ausschalten' = 'turn off'. With pronoun 'it': 'turned it off' (pronoun between verb and particle).
items.ed: 'Einschalten' = 'turn on'. 'Ausschalten' = 'turn off'. Mit Pronomen 'it': 'turned it off' (Pronomen zwischen Verb und Partikel).

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: up / picked / I / the / from / book / floor / the
items.c: I picked up the book from the floor.
