a: I picked up the book from the floor.
I picked up the book from the floor
I picked the book up from the floor.
items.h: With a noun object, you can say 'picked up the book' OR 'picked the book up'.
items.hd: Mit einem Nomen-Objekt kann man sagen 'picked up the book' ODER 'picked the book up'.
items.e: With nouns, both positions work: 'picked up the book' or 'picked the book up'. Both are correct.
items.ed: Mit Nomen funktionieren beide Positionen: 'picked up the book' oder 'picked the book up'. Beides ist richtig.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: with / a / come / we / up / solution / need / to
items.c: We need to come up with a solution.
a: We need to come up with a solution.
We need to come up with a solution
items.h: 'Come up with' is a three-word inseparable phrasal verb meaning 'think of'.
items.hd: 'Come up with' ist ein dreiteiliges, untrennbares Phrasal Verb, das 'sich ausdenken' bedeutet.
items.e: We + need to + come up with + a solution. 'Come up with' stays together as a unit.
items.ed: We + need to + come up with + a solution. 'Come up with' bleibt als Einheit zusammen.

items.p: Match each phrasal verb with its meaning. 1: pick up 2: give up 3: look after 4: break down 5: come up with 6: set off
items.h: Think about the meaning of each phrasal verb in context.
items.hd: Denke ueber die Bedeutung jedes Phrasal Verbs im Kontext nach.
items.e: 1+d: pick up = lift from the ground. 2+a: give up = stop trying/quit. 3+f: look after = take care of. 4+c: break down = stop working. 5+b: come up with = think of an idea. 6+e: set off = start a journey. a: stop trying/quit. b: think of an idea. c: stop working. d: lift from the ground. e: start a journey. f: take care of.
items.ed: 1+d: pick up = aufheben. 2+a: give up = aufgeben. 3+f: look after = sich kuemmern um. 4+c: break down = kaputtgehen. 5+b: come up with = sich etwas einfallen lassen. 6+e: set off = aufbrechen.

items.p: Your friend is looking for her keys. Ask what time she picked them up last. Start with 'When...'
items.c: When did you last pick them up?
a: When did you last pick them up?
When did you last pick them up
When did you pick them up last?
items.h: Use past simple question form with the phrasal verb. Remember: pronouns go between the verb and the particle.
items.hd: Verwende die Past Simple Frageform mit dem Phrasal Verb. Merke: Pronomen stehen zwischen Verb und Partikel.
items.e: When + did + you + pick them up? With pronoun 'them': 'pick them up' (pronoun between verb and particle).
items.ed: When + did + you + pick them up? Mit Pronomen 'them': 'pick them up' (Pronomen zwischen Verb und Partikel).

items.p: This exercise is very ___. I can't do it. (possible → NOT possible)
a: impossible
items.h: To make 'possible' negative, we use the prefix 'im-' (not 'un-' or 'in-').
items.hd: Um 'possible' zu verneinen, verwenden wir das Praefix 'im-' (nicht 'un-' oder 'in-').
items.e: Before 'p' and 'b', the prefix 'in-' changes to 'im-': impossible, impolite, impatient.
items.ed: Vor 'p' und 'b' aendert sich das Praefix 'in-' zu 'im-': impossible, impolite, impatient.

items.p: She's very ___. She always thinks about other people's feelings. (care + suffix)
a: careful
items.h: Which suffix means 'full of'? If she's full of care for others, she's ___.
items.hd: Welches Suffix bedeutet 'voll von'? Wenn sie voller Fuersorge fuer andere ist, ist sie ___.
items.e: The suffix '-ful' means 'full of': care + -ful = careful (full of care for others).
items.ed: Das Suffix '-ful' bedeutet 'voll von': care + -ful = careful (voll von Fuersorge fuer andere).

items.p: The ___ of the team surprised everyone. They won every game! (successful → noun)
a: success
items.h: What is the noun form of 'successful'? Think about the base word.
items.hd: Was ist die Nomenform von 'successful'? Denke an das Grundwort.
items.e: The adjective 'successful' comes from the noun 'success'. Remove the suffix to get back to the noun form.
items.ed: Das Adjektiv 'successful' kommt vom Nomen 'success'. Entferne das Suffix, um zur Nomenform zurueckzukommen.

items.p: Don't be so ___! Everything will be fine. (patience → opposite adjective)
a: impatient
items.h: First make the adjective from the noun: patience → patient. Then add the negative prefix for words starting with 'p'.
items.hd: Mache zuerst das Adjektiv aus dem Nomen: patience → patient. Dann fuege das Verneinungspraefix fuer Woerter mit 'p' hinzu.
items.e: Patience (noun) → patient (adjective) → impatient (negative adjective). Before 'p', use 'im-' not 'in-'.
items.ed: Patience (Nomen) → patient (Adjektiv) → impatient (verneintes Adjektiv). Vor 'p' verwende 'im-' nicht 'in-'.

items.p: The homework was completely ___. The teacher didn't understand what I wrote. (meaning + suffix → adjective meaning 'without meaning')
a: meaningless
items.h: Which suffix means 'without'? If something has no meaning, it's meaning___.
items.hd: Welches Suffix bedeutet 'ohne'? Wenn etwas keine Bedeutung hat, ist es meaning___.
items.e: The suffix '-less' means 'without': meaning + -less = meaningless (without meaning). Opposite of 'meaningful'.
items.ed: Das Suffix '-less' bedeutet 'ohne': meaning + -less = meaningless (ohne Bedeutung). Gegenteil von 'meaningful'.

items.p: I'm sorry, I completely ___ your instructions. That's why I did it wrong. (understood → prefix for 'wrongly')
a: misunderstood
items.h: The prefix 'mis-' means 'wrongly' or 'badly'. What's the past tense of 'misunderstand'?
items.hd: Das Praefix 'mis-' bedeutet 'falsch' oder 'schlecht'. Was ist die Vergangenheitsform von 'misunderstand'?
items.e: The prefix 'mis-' means 'wrongly': mis + understood = misunderstood (understood wrongly). Past tense because of 'did it wrong'.
items.ed: Das Praefix 'mis-' bedeutet 'falsch': mis + understood = misunderstood (falsch verstanden). Vergangenheit wegen 'did it wrong'.

items.p: Which word is correctly formed with a prefix?
a: unhappy
items.h: The most common negative prefix for adjectives is 'un-'. Which combination is a real word?
items.hd: Das haeufigste Verneinungspraefix fuer Adjektive ist 'un-'. Welche Kombination ist ein echtes Wort?
items.e: 'Happy' takes the prefix 'un-': unhappy. There's no fixed rule — you need to learn which prefix goes with which word.
items.ed: 'Happy' nimmt das Praefix 'un-': unhappy. Es gibt keine feste Regel — man muss lernen, welches Praefix zu welchem Wort passt.

items.p: Choose the correct word to complete: 'The ___ of his new book was a big surprise.' (publish)
a: publication
items.h: The sentence needs a noun (after 'The'). Which suffix turns 'publish' into a noun?
items.hd: Der Satz braucht ein Nomen (nach 'The'). Welches Suffix macht aus 'publish' ein Nomen?
items.e: 'Publish' → 'publication' (noun). The suffix '-ation' turns verbs into nouns: publish → publication.
items.ed: 'Publish' → 'publication' (Nomen). Das Suffix '-ation' verwandelt Verben in Nomen: publish → publication.

items.p: Which of these words uses the prefix 'il-' correctly?
a: illegal
items.h: The prefix 'il-' is only used before words starting with 'l'. Which word starts with 'l'?
items.hd: Das Praefix 'il-' wird nur vor Woertern verwendet, die mit 'l' beginnen. Welches Wort beginnt mit 'l'?
items.e: 'Il-' is the form of 'in-' used before 'l': illegal, illegible, illiterate. Before 'p/b': im-. Before 'r': ir-. Otherwise: in-.
items.ed: 'Il-' ist die Form von 'in-', die vor 'l' verwendet wird: illegal, illegible, illiterate. Vor 'p/b': im-. Vor 'r': ir-. Sonst: in-.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: It's very unpossible to finish this in one hour.
items.c: It's very impossible to finish this in one hour.
a: It's very impossible to finish this in one hour.
It's very impossible to finish this in one hour
It's impossible to finish this in one hour.
items.h: 'Possible' doesn't take the prefix 'un-'. Before 'p', we use a different prefix.
items.hd: 'Possible' nimmt nicht das Praefix 'un-'. Vor 'p' verwenden wir ein anderes Praefix.
items.e: 'Possible' takes 'im-' (not 'un-'): 'impossible'. The prefix 'in-' changes to 'im-' before 'p' and 'b'.
items.ed: 'Possible' nimmt 'im-' (nicht 'un-'): 'impossible'. Das Praefix 'in-' aendert sich zu 'im-' vor 'p' und 'b'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: Her kindful nature makes everyone feel welcome.
items.c: Her kind nature makes everyone feel welcome.
a: Her kind nature makes everyone feel welcome.
Her kind nature makes everyone feel welcome
Her kindness makes everyone feel welcome.
items.h: 'Kind' is already an adjective. Does it need the suffix '-ful'?
items.hd: 'Kind' ist schon ein Adjektiv. Braucht es das Suffix '-ful'?
items.e: 'Kind' is already an adjective — 'kindful' doesn't exist. Use 'kind' directly, or the noun form 'kindness'.
items.ed: 'Kind' ist schon ein Adjektiv — 'kindful' existiert nicht. Verwende 'kind' direkt oder die Nomenform 'kindness'.

items.p: Find and fix the mistake: I think it's very inresponsible to drive without a seatbelt.
items.c: I think it's very irresponsible to drive without a seatbelt.
a: I think it's very irresponsible to drive without a seatbelt.
I think it's very irresponsible to drive without a seatbelt
items.h: Before words starting with 'r', the prefix 'in-' changes to 'ir-'.
items.hd: Vor Woertern, die mit 'r' beginnen, aendert sich das Praefix 'in-' zu 'ir-'.
items.e: Before 'r', 'in-' becomes 'ir-': irresponsible, irregular, irrelevant. This is called assimilation.
items.ed: Vor 'r' wird 'in-' zu 'ir-': irresponsible, irregular, irrelevant. Das nennt man Assimilation.

items.p: Rewrite the sentence using the word in brackets: That word doesn't make any sense. (meaningless) → That word is ___.
a: meaningless
That word is meaningless.
That word is meaningless
items.h: 'Doesn't make any sense' = 'without meaning'. Which suffix means 'without'?
items.hd: 'Doesn't make any sense' = 'ohne Bedeutung'. Welches Suffix bedeutet 'ohne'?
items.e: 'Doesn't make any sense' = 'meaningless'. The suffix '-less' means 'without': meaning + less = meaningless.
items.ed: 'Doesn't make any sense' = 'meaningless'. Das Suffix '-less' bedeutet 'ohne': meaning + less = meaningless.

items.p: Rewrite using the word in brackets: I don't agree with what you said. (disagree) → I ___ with what you said.
a: disagree
I disagree with what you said.
I disagree with what you said
items.h: The prefix 'dis-' makes the verb negative: agree → disagree.
items.hd: Das Praefix 'dis-' verneint das Verb: agree → disagree.
items.e: 'Don't agree' = 'disagree'. The prefix 'dis-' creates the opposite: agree → disagree, like → dislike.
items.ed: 'Don't agree' = 'disagree'. Das Praefix 'dis-' bildet das Gegenteil: agree → disagree, like → dislike.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Sein Verhalten war sehr unhöflich.
a: His behaviour was very impolite.
His behaviour was very impolite
His behavior was very impolite.
His behaviour was very rude.
items.h: 'Unhoeflich' = the opposite of 'polite'. 'Polite' starts with 'p', so which prefix do you use?
items.hd: 'Unhoeflich' = das Gegenteil von 'polite'. 'Polite' beginnt mit 'p', welches Praefix verwendest du?
items.e: 'Unhoeflich' = 'impolite' (not 'unpolite'). Before 'p', use the prefix 'im-': impolite, impossible, impatient.
items.ed: 'Unhoeflich' = 'impolite' (nicht 'unpolite'). Vor 'p' verwende das Praefix 'im-': impolite, impossible, impatient.

items.p: 🇩🇪 Seine Sorglosigkeit hat zu einem schweren Unfall gefuehrt.
items.c: His carelessness led to a serious accident.
a: His carelessness led to a serious accident.
His carelessness led to a serious accident
His carelessness caused a serious accident.
His carelessness resulted in a serious accident.
items.h: 'Sorglosigkeit' = noun from 'careless'. 'Careless' + which suffix makes it a noun?
items.hd: 'Sorglosigkeit' = Nomen von 'careless'. 'Careless' + welches Suffix macht daraus ein Nomen?
items.e: 'Sorglosigkeit' = 'carelessness'. Double suffix: care + -less (adjective) + -ness (noun). 'Led to' = 'fuehrte zu'.
items.ed: 'Sorglosigkeit' = 'carelessness'. Doppeltes Suffix: care + -less (Adjektiv) + -ness (Nomen). 'Led to' = 'fuehrte zu'.

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: is / behaviour / his / unacceptable / totally
a: His behaviour is totally unacceptable.
His behaviour is totally unacceptable
His behavior is totally unacceptable.
items.h: Start with the possessive + noun, then verb, then adverb + adjective.
items.hd: Beginne mit Possessiv + Nomen, dann Verb, dann Adverb + Adjektiv.
items.e: His behaviour (subject) + is (verb) + totally (adverb) + unacceptable (adjective with prefix 'un-').
items.ed: His behaviour (Subjekt) + is (Verb) + totally (Adverb) + unacceptable (Adjektiv mit Praefix 'un-').

items.p: Put the words in the correct order: her / the / of / happiness / made / gift / filled / with
items.c: The gift made her filled with happiness.
a: The gift made her filled with happiness.
The gift filled her with happiness.
items.h: Start with 'The gift' as the subject. 'Happiness' is the noun form of 'happy' (happy + -ness).
items.hd: Beginne mit 'The gift' als Subjekt. 'Happiness' ist die Nomenform von 'happy' (happy + -ness).
items.e: 'The gift filled her with happiness.' 'Happiness' = happy + -ness (adjective → noun with suffix '-ness').
items.ed: 'The gift filled her with happiness.' 'Happiness' = happy + -ness (Adjektiv → Nomen mit Suffix '-ness').

items.p: Match each word with its correct negative form. 1: happy 2: possible 3: legal 4: responsible 5: agree 6: understand
items.h: Remember: 'im-' before p/b, 'il-' before l, 'ir-' before r, 'dis-' for some verbs, 'mis-' for 'wrongly'.
items.hd: Merke: 'im-' vor p/b, 'il-' vor l, 'ir-' vor r, 'dis-' fuer manche Verben, 'mis-' fuer 'falsch'.
items.e: 1+c: happy → unhappy. 2+b: possible → impossible. 3+d: legal → illegal. 4+e: responsible → irresponsible. 5+a: agree → disagree. 6+f: understand → misunderstand. a: dis-. b: im-. c: un-. d: il-. e: ir-. f: mis-.
items.ed: 1+c: happy → unhappy. 2+b: possible → impossible. 3+d: legal → illegal. 4+e: responsible → irresponsible. 5+a: agree → disagree. 6+f: understand → misunderstand.

items.p: Your friend says driving without a licence is acceptable. You disagree! Form a question to challenge this: Is it really ___ to drive without a licence? (Use the opposite of 'acceptable')
items.c: Is it really unacceptable to drive without a licence?
a: Is it really unacceptable to drive without a licence?
Is it really unacceptable to drive without a licence
Is it really unacceptable to drive without a license?
Isn't it unacceptable to drive without a licence?
items.h: The opposite of 'acceptable' uses the prefix 'un-': ___acceptable.
items.hd: Das Gegenteil von 'acceptable' verwendet das Praefix 'un-': ___acceptable.
items.e: 'Acceptable' → 'unacceptable' (prefix 'un-'). The question challenges the idea by using the negative form.
items.ed: 'Acceptable' → 'unacceptable' (Praefix 'un-'). Die Frage stellt die Idee in Frage, indem sie die negative Form verwendet.
